namespace BernoulliCost
variable {n : ℕ}
def fixedSizeRestrProb (event : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred event]
    (k : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  ↑((fixedSizeRestrs n k).filter (fun ρ => event ρ)).card / ↑(fixedSizeRestrs n k).card
end BernoulliCost

namespace BernoulliCost
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma fixedSizeRestrProb_nonneg (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] (k : ℕ) :
    0 ≤ fixedSizeRestrProb event k := sorry
end BernoulliCost
title: Fixed-size restriction probability is nonnegative
statement_informal:
Fixed-size restriction probability is nonnegative. For any event, the fixed-size restriction probability satisfies
$0 \le \mathrm{fixedSizeRestrProb}\,(\mathrm{event})\,k$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Restrictions with a fixed number of free variables
definitions.informal:
For natural numbers $n$ and $k$, this is the finite set of restrictions on $n$ variables
whose set of free variables has cardinality exactly $k$.

definitions.title: Fixed-size restriction probability $R_k$
definitions.informal:
The probability of an event under the fixed-size restriction model $R_k$, which is uniform
over restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables. It is defined as the fraction of
restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables that satisfy the event.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $B$-reasonable random variable. A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely
\[
\E[X^4] \le B \cdot \bigl(\E[X^2]\bigr)^2 .
\]

Statement: Anti-concentration for $B$-reasonable variables. If $X$ is $B$-reasonable and not equivalent to $0$, then for every $t \in [0,1]$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,|X| > t\,\norm{X}_2\,\bigr] \ge \frac{(1-t^2)^2}{B}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace Bonami
def IsBReasonable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] (X : Ω → ℝ) (P : Measure Ω) (B : ℝ) : Prop :=
  moment X 4 P ≤ B * (moment X 2 P) ^ 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma b_reasonable_anticon_zero -- anticoncentration bound with theta = 0; general result after
  {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
  {X : Ω → ℝ} {B : ℝ} (hB : IsBReasonable X P B)
  (hX_meas : Measurable X)
  (hX_int_sq : Integrable (fun ω ↦ X ω ^ 2) P)
  (hX_int_4 : Integrable (fun ω ↦ X ω ^ 4) P)
  (hX_pos_2 : 0 < moment X 2 P)/-  -/
  {t : ℝ} (ht_nonneg : 0 ≤ t) (ht_le_one : t ≤ 1) :
  (1 - t^2)^2 / B ≤ (P {ω | t^2 * moment X 2 P < X ω ^ 2}).toReal := sorry
end Bonami
title: Anti-concentration for $B$-reasonable variables
statement_informal:
Anti-concentration for $B$-reasonable variables. If $X$ is $B$-reasonable and not equivalent to $0$, then for every $t \in [0,1]$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,|X| > t\,\norm{X}_2\,\bigr] \ge \frac{(1-t^2)^2}{B}.
\]

definitions.title: $B$-reasonable random variable
definitions.informal:
A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely
\[
\E[X^4] \le B \cdot \bigl(\E[X^2]\bigr)^2 .
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $B$-reasonable random variable. A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely
\[
\E[X^4] \le B \cdot \bigl(\E[X^2]\bigr)^2 .
\]

Statement: Tail bound for $B$-reasonable variables. If $X$ is $B$-reasonable and not equivalent to $0$ (its second moment is positive), then for
every $t > 0$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,|X| \ge t\,\norm{X}_2\,\bigr] \le \frac{B}{t^4}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace Bonami
def IsBReasonable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] (X : Ω → ℝ) (P : Measure Ω) (B : ℝ) : Prop :=
  moment X 4 P ≤ B * (moment X 2 P) ^ 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
lemma b_reasonable_tail_bound
  {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
  {X : Ω → ℝ} {B : ℝ} (hB : IsBReasonable X P B)
  (t : ℝ) (ht : 0 < t) (hX_pos : 0 < moment X 2 P) -- True if X isn't equivalent to 0
  (hX_int : Integrable (fun ω ↦ X ω ^ 4) P) : -- probably don't need; target for rewrite
  -- Below I already applied the power of 4; might want to rewrite later
  (P {ω | X ω ^ 4 ≥ t ^ 4 * (moment X 2 P) ^ 2}).toReal ≤ B / t ^ 4 := sorry
end Bonami
title: Tail bound for $B$-reasonable variables
statement_informal:
Tail bound for $B$-reasonable variables. If $X$ is $B$-reasonable and not equivalent to $0$ (its second moment is positive), then for
every $t > 0$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,|X| \ge t\,\norm{X}_2\,\bigr] \le \frac{B}{t^4}.
\]

definitions.title: $B$-reasonable random variable
definitions.informal:
A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely
\[

\]

informal_statement:
Algebraic inductive step for Bonami. The key algebraic inequality driving the inductive step of the Bonami lemma: for
$a, b, A, B, C \ge 0$ (with $a, b, B, C$ nonnegative) satisfying
$A \le 9^{m+1} a^2$, $B \le 9^m b^2$, and $C^2 \le A\cdot B$, one has
\[
A + 6C + B \le 9^{m+1}\,(a+b)^2.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma bonami_algebra {m : ℕ} {a b A B C : ℝ}
    (ha : 0 ≤ a) (hb : 0 ≤ b) (hB : 0 ≤ B) (hC : 0 ≤ C)
    (hA_bound : A ≤ 9 ^ (m + 1) * a ^ 2)
    (hB_bound : B ≤ 9 ^ m * b ^ 2)
    (hC_bound : C ^ 2 ≤ A * B) :
    A + 6 * C + B ≤ 9 ^ (m + 1) * (a + b) ^ 2 := sorry
end Bonami
title: Algebraic inductive step for Bonami
statement_informal:
Algebraic inductive step for Bonami. The key algebraic inequality driving the inductive step of the Bonami lemma: for
$a, b, A, B, C \ge 0$ (with $a, b, B, C$ nonnegative) satisfying
$A \le 9^{m+1} a^2$, $B \le 9^m b^2$, and $C^2 \le A\cdot B$, one has
\[
A + 6C + B \le 9^{m+1}\,(a+b)^2.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Degree at most $k$. A Boolean function $f$ \emph{has degree at most $k$} if every nonzero Fourier
coefficient is supported on a set of size at most $k$: for all $S$, if
$\hat f(S)\ne 0$ then $|S|\le k$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

Statement: Bonami inequality in expectation form. The main Bonami inequality stated through the expectation operator: if a Boolean function $f$
has degree at most $k$, then
\[
\E[f^4] \le 9^{k}\,\bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^2.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def has_degree_at_most {n : ℕ} (f : (BooleanFunc n)) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ S, f ̂( S ) ≠ 0 → S.card ≤ k
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma bonami_expect {n : ℕ} (k : ℕ) (f : BooleanFunc n)
    (hf : has_degree_at_most f k) :
    expect (fun x ↦ f x ^ 4) ≤ (9 : ℝ) ^ k * (expect (fun x ↦ f x ^ 2)) ^ 2 := sorry
end Bonami
title: Bonami inequality in expectation form
statement_informal:
Bonami inequality in expectation form. The main Bonami inequality stated through the expectation operator: if a Boolean function $f$
has degree at most $k$, then
\[
\E[f^4] \le 9^{k}\,\bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^2.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- $B$-reasonable random variable. A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is

- Uniform measure on the hypercube. The canonical uniform probability measure on the Boolean hypercube $\{0,1\}^n$.
- Degree at most $k$. A Boolean function $f$ \emph{has degree at most $k$} if every nonzero Fourier
coefficient is supported on a set of size at most $k$: for all $S$, if

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
def IsBReasonable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] (X : Ω → ℝ) (P : Measure Ω) (B : ℝ) : Prop :=
  moment X 4 P ≤ B * (moment X 2 P) ^ 2
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def uniformMeasure (n : ℕ) : Measure (BoolCube n) :=
  (PMF.uniformOfFintype (BoolCube n)).toMeasure
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def has_degree_at_most {n : ℕ} (f : (BooleanFunc n)) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ S, f ̂( S ) ≠ 0 → S.card ≤ k
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma bonami_lemma {n : ℕ} (k : ℕ) (f : BooleanFunc n)
    (hf : has_degree_at_most f k) :
    IsBReasonable f (uniformMeasure n) ((9 : ℝ) ^ k) := sorry
end Bonami
title: Bonami lemma
statement_informal:
Bonami lemma. A Boolean function $f$ of degree at most $k$ is $9^{k}$-reasonable under the uniform measure on
the hypercube.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: $B$-reasonable random variable
definitions.informal:
A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely

\E[X^4] \le B \cdot \bigl(\E[X^2]\bigr)^2 .
\]

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Uniform measure on the hypercube
definitions.informal: The canonical uniform probability measure on the Boolean hypercube $\{0,1\}^n$.

definitions.title: Degree at most $k$
definitions.informal:
A Boolean function $f$ \emph{has degree at most $k$} if every nonzero Fourier
coefficient is supported on a set of size at most $k$: for all $S$, if
$\hat f(S)\ne 0$ then $|S|\le k$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Degree at most $k$. A Boolean function $f$ \emph{has degree at most $k$} if every nonzero Fourier
coefficient is supported on a set of size at most $k$: for all $S$, if
$\hat f(S)\ne 0$ then $|S|\le k$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Degree bound for the average. If $f$ has degree at most $k$, then its average over the last coordinate $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$
also has degree at most $k$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def has_degree_at_most {n : ℕ} (f : (BooleanFunc n)) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ S, f ̂( S ) ≠ 0 → S.card ≤ k
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x + restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma degree_avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (k : ℕ)
    (hf : has_degree_at_most f k) :
    has_degree_at_most (avgLast f) k := sorry
end Bonami

statement_informal:

also has degree at most $k$.

coefficient is supported on a set of size at most $k$: for all $S$, if
$\hat f(S)\ne 0$ then $|S|\le k$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Degree bound for the half-difference. If $f$ has degree at most $k$, then its half-difference over the last coordinate
$\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ has degree at most $k-1$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def has_degree_at_most {n : ℕ} (f : (BooleanFunc n)) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ S, f ̂( S ) ≠ 0 → S.card ≤ k
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x - restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma degree_diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (k : ℕ)
    (hf : has_degree_at_most f k) :
    has_degree_at_most (diffLast f) (k - 1) := sorry
end Bonami

statement_informal:
Degree bound for the half-difference. If $f$ has degree at most $k$, then its half-difference over the last coordinate
$\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ has degree at most $k-1$.

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def has_degree_at_most {n : ℕ} (f : (BooleanFunc n)) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ S, f ̂( S ) ≠ 0 → S.card ≤ k
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma degree_zero_fourth_moment {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc n) (hf : has_degree_at_most f 0) :
    expect (fun x => f x ^ 4) = (expect (fun x => f x ^ 2)) ^ 2 := sorry
end Bonami

statement_informal:
Fourth moment of a constant function. For a Boolean function $f$ of degree at most $0$ (hence constant),
$\E[f^4] = \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^2$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma expect_sq_nonneg_prod {n : ℕ} (g h : BooleanFunc n) :
    0 ≤ expect (fun x => g x ^ 2 * h x ^ 2) := sorry
end Bonami
title: Nonnegativity of $\E[g^2 h^2
statement_informal: Nonnegativity of $\E[g^2 h^2. For Boolean functions $g, h$, $0 \le \E[g^2 h^2]$.

\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.

Statement: Expectation splits over the last coordinate. The expectation of $\varphi$ on the cube of dimension $n+1$ is the average of the expectations

\[
\E[\varphi] = \tfrac12\bigl(\E[\mathrm{restrictLast}\,\varphi\,\mathrm{false}] + \E[\mathrm{restrictLast}\,\varphi\,\mathrm{true}]\bigr).

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma expect_succ_eq {n : ℕ} (φ : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) :
    expect φ = (expect (restrictLast φ false) + expect (restrictLast φ true)) / 2 := sorry
end Bonami
title: Expectation splits over the last coordinate
statement_informal:
Expectation splits over the last coordinate. The expectation of $\varphi$ on the cube of dimension $n+1$ is the average of the expectations
of its two restrictions:
\[
\E[\varphi] = \tfrac12\bigl(\E[\mathrm{restrictLast}\,\varphi\,\mathrm{false}] + \E[\mathrm{restrictLast}\,\varphi\,\mathrm{true}]\bigr).
\]

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction of the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Fourier coefficient of the average. For each $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the Fourier coefficient of $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ at $S$
equals the Fourier coefficient of $f$ at the image of $S$ under \texttt{castSucc}:
$\widehat{\mathrm{avgLast}\,f}(S) = \widehat{f}(\mathrm{castSucc}(S))$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x + restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma fourierCoeff_avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff (avgLast f) S = BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff f (S.image Fin.castSucc) := sorry
end Bonami
title: Fourier coefficient of the average
statement_informal:
Fourier coefficient of the average. For each $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the Fourier coefficient of $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ at $S$
equals the Fourier coefficient of $f$ at the image of $S$ under \texttt{castSucc}:
$\widehat{\mathrm{avgLast}\,f}(S) = \widehat{f}(\mathrm{castSucc}(S))$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Fourier coefficient of the half-difference. For each $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the Fourier coefficient of $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ at $S$
equals the Fourier coefficient of $f$ at the image of $S$ under \texttt{castSucc} together with
the last index: $\widehat{\mathrm{diffLast}\,f}(S) = \widehat{f}\bigl(\mathrm{castSucc}(S) \cup \{\mathrm{last}\,n\}\bigr)$.

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x - restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma fourierCoeff_diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff (diffLast f) S = BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff f (S.image Fin.castSucc ∪ {Fin.last n}) := sorry
end Bonami

the last index: $\widehat{\mathrm{diffLast}\,f}(S) = \widehat{f}\bigl(\mathrm{castSucc}(S) \cup \{\mathrm{last}\,n\}\bigr)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

Statement: Fourth-moment decomposition. Writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and $h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the fourth moment of $f$
decomposes as
\[
\E[f^4] = \E[g^4] + 6\,\E[g^2 h^2] + \E[h^4].
\]

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x + restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x - restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma fourth_moment_decomp {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) :
    expect (fun x => f x ^ 4) =
    expect (fun x => (avgLast f x) ^ 4) +
    6 * expect (fun x => (avgLast f x) ^ 2 * (diffLast f x) ^ 2) +
    expect (fun x => (diffLast f x) ^ 4) := sorry
end Bonami
title: Fourth-moment decomposition
statement_informal:
Fourth-moment decomposition. Writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and $h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the fourth moment of $f$
decomposes as
\[
\E[f^4] = \E[g^4] + 6\,\E[g^2 h^2] + \E[h^4].
\]

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction of the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.

definitions.title: Average over the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

definitions.title: Half-difference over the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

informal_statement: Fourth-power algebraic identity. For all real $a, b$, $(a+b)^4 + (a-b)^4 = 2\,(a^4 + 6\,a^2 b^2 + b^4)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma fourth_pow_sum (a b : ℝ) :
    (a + b) ^ 4 + (a - b) ^ 4 = 2 * (a ^ 4 + 6 * a ^ 2 * b ^ 2 + b ^ 4) := sorry
end Bonami
title: Fourth-power algebraic identity
statement_informal: Fourth-power algebraic identity. For all real $a, b$, $(a+b)^4 + (a-b)^4 = 2\,(a^4 + 6\,a^2 b^2 + b^4)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $B$-reasonable random variable. A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely
\[
\E[X^4] \le B \cdot \bigl(\E[X^2]\bigr)^2 .
\]

Statement: $B$-reasonability from minimal probability. Let $X$ be a discrete random variable on a finite space whose law is a PMF $\pi$. If
$\mu > 0$ is a lower bound on every atom probability $\pi(\omega)$, then $X$ is
$(1/\mu)$-reasonable.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace Bonami
def IsBReasonable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] (X : Ω → ℝ) (P : Measure Ω) (B : ℝ) : Prop :=
  moment X 4 P ≤ B * (moment X 2 P) ^ 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
lemma min_prob_b_reasonable
  {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] [Fintype Ω] [DiscreteMeasurableSpace Ω]
  {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
  {X : Ω → ℝ} {π : PMF Ω} (hP : P = π.toMeasure)
  {μ : ℝ} (hμ_pos : 0 < μ) (hμ_min : ∀ ω, μ ≤ (π ω).toReal) :
  IsBReasonable X P (1 / μ) := sorry
end Bonami
title: $B$-reasonability from minimal probability
statement_informal:
$B$-reasonability from minimal probability. Let $X$ be a discrete random variable on a finite space whose law is a PMF $\pi$. If
$\mu > 0$ is a lower bound on every atom probability $\pi(\omega)$, then $X$ is
$(1/\mu)$-reasonable.

definitions.title: $B$-reasonable random variable
definitions.informal:
A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely
\[
\E[X^4] \le B \cdot \bigl(\E[X^2]\bigr)^2 .
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

Statement: Moments equal expectations under the uniform law. If $P$ is a probability measure on the cube assigning each point the uniform weight, then the
$p$-th moment of $f$ under $P$ coincides with the expectation $\E[f^p]$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma moment_eq_expect {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc n) (p : ℕ)
    (P : Measure (BoolCube n)) [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    (hP_unif : ∀ x, (P {x}).toReal = uniformWeight n) :
    moment f p P = expect (fun x ↦ f x ^ p) := sorry
end Bonami
title: Moments equal expectations under the uniform law
statement_informal:
Moments equal expectations under the uniform law. If $P$ is a probability measure on the cube assigning each point the uniform weight, then the
$p$-th moment of $f$ under $P$ coincides with the expectation $\E[f^p]$.

informal_statement:
Paley--Zygmund inequality. Let $Z \ge 0$ be a nonnegative random variable with finite first and second moments and
positive mean. For every $\theta$ with $0 \le \theta \le 1$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,Z > \theta\,\E[Z]\,\bigr] \ge \frac{(1-\theta)^2\,(\E[Z])^2}{\E[Z^2]}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
lemma paley_zygmund_ineq
  {Z : Ω → ℝ}
  (h_meas : Measurable Z)
  (h_nonneg : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, 0 ≤ Z ω)
  (h_int : Integrable Z μ)
  (h_int_sq : Integrable (fun ω ↦ Z ω ^ 2) μ)
  {θ : ℝ} (hθ_pos : 0 ≤ θ) (hθ_le_one : θ ≤ 1)
  (hZ_pos : 0 < moment Z 1 μ) :
  (1 - θ)^2 * (moment Z 1 μ)^2 / moment Z 2 μ ≤ (μ {ω | θ * moment Z 1 μ < Z ω}).toReal := sorry
end Bonami
title: Paley--Zygmund inequality
statement_informal:
Paley--Zygmund inequality. Let $Z \ge 0$ be a nonnegative random variable with finite first and second moments and
positive mean. For every $\theta$ with $0 \le \theta \le 1$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,Z > \theta\,\E[Z]\,\bigr] \ge \frac{(1-\theta)^2\,(\E[Z])^2}{\E[Z^2]}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

Statement: Restriction at false. For every $x$, the restriction of $f$ fixing the last coordinate to false equals the sum of
the average and the half-difference: $\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false}\,x = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f\,x + \mathrm{diffLast}\,f\,x$.

title: Second-power algebraic identity
statement_informal: Second-power algebraic identity. For all real $a, b$, $(a+b)^2 + (a-b)^2 = 2\,(a^2 + b^2)$.

represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

Statement: Sum over the hypercube splits on the last bit. For any $\varphi$ on the cube of dimension $n+1$, the total sum decomposes according to the
value of the last coordinate:
\[
\sum_{x \in \{0,1\}^{n+1}} \varphi(x) = \sum_{x \in \{0,1\}^{n}} \varphi(x,\mathrm{false}) + \sum_{x \in \{0,1\}^{n}} \varphi(x,\mathrm{true}).
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

Statement: Custom induction principle for circuits. An induction principle for \texttt{Circuit} stating that to prove a predicate
$\mathrm{motive}$ holds for every circuit it suffices to prove it for every literal and,
in the node case, to prove $\mathrm{motive}(\texttt{node}\,isAnd\,cs)$ assuming
$\mathrm{motive}\,c$ for every child $c \in cs$. This works around the lack of native
support for nested inductives.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem Circuit.ind {n : Nat} {motive : Circuit n → Prop}
    (hlit : ∀ l, motive (.lit l))
    (hnode : ∀ isAnd cs, (∀ c ∈ cs, motive c) → motive (.node isAnd cs)) :
    ∀ c, motive c := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Custom induction principle for circuits
statement_informal:
Custom induction principle for circuits. An induction principle for \texttt{Circuit} stating that to prove a predicate
$\mathrm{motive}$ holds for every circuit it suffices to prove it for every literal and,
in the node case, to prove $\mathrm{motive}(\texttt{node}\,isAnd\,cs)$ assuming
$\mathrm{motive}\,c$ for every child $c \in cs$. This works around the lack of native
support for nested inductives.

For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Re-indexing a circuit. Given a circuit $c$ on $m$ variables and a function $f : \mathrm{Fin}\,m \to \mathrm{Fin}\,m'$,
the re-indexed circuit $\mathrm{reidx}(c, f)$ on $m'$ variables is obtained by replacing
each literal index $i$ by $f(i)$ (keeping its sign) and recursing structurally through the
$\mathrm{And}/\mathrm{Or}$ nodes.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

Statement: Re-indexing preserves depth. For every circuit $c$ on $m$ variables and every $f : \mathrm{Fin}\,m \to \mathrm{Fin}\,m'$,
the re-indexed circuit has the same depth as $c$, i.e. $\mathrm{depth}(\mathrm{reidx}(c, f)) = \mathrm{depth}(c)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.reidx {m m' : ℕ} : Circuit m → (Fin m → Fin m') → Circuit m'
  | .lit l, f => .lit ⟨f l.idx, l.sign⟩
  | .node isAnd cs, f => .node isAnd (cs.map (fun c => Circuit.reidx c f))
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem Circuit.reidx_depth {m m' : ℕ} (c : Circuit m) (f : Fin m → Fin m') :
    (Circuit.reidx c f).depth = c.depth := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Re-indexing preserves depth
statement_informal:
Re-indexing preserves depth. For every circuit $c$ on $m$ variables and every $f : \mathrm{Fin}\,m \to \mathrm{Fin}\,m'$,
the re-indexed circuit has the same depth as $c$, i.e. $\mathrm{depth}(\mathrm{reidx}(c, f)) = \mathrm{depth}(c)$.

applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

each literal index $i$ by $f(i)$ (keeping its sign) and recursing structurally through the
$\mathrm{And}/\mathrm{Or}$ nodes.

has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Re-indexing a circuit. Given a circuit $c$ on $m$ variables and a function $f : \mathrm{Fin}\,m \to \mathrm{Fin}\,m'$,
the re-indexed circuit $\mathrm{reidx}(c, f)$ on $m'$ variables is obtained by replacing
each literal index $i$ by $f(i)$ (keeping its sign) and recursing structurally through the
$\mathrm{And}/\mathrm{Or}$ nodes.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

Statement: Re-indexing commutes with evaluation. For every circuit $c$ on $m$ variables, every $f : \mathrm{Fin}\,m \to \mathrm{Fin}\,m'$,
and every assignment $g : \mathrm{Fin}\,m' \to \mathrm{Bool}$, evaluating the re-indexed
circuit under $g$ equals evaluating $c$ under the pulled-back assignment $g \circ f$, i.e.
$\mathrm{eval}(\mathrm{reidx}(c, f), g) = \mathrm{eval}(c, g \circ f)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.reidx {m m' : ℕ} : Circuit m → (Fin m → Fin m') → Circuit m'
  | .lit l, f => .lit ⟨f l.idx, l.sign⟩
  | .node isAnd cs, f => .node isAnd (cs.map (fun c => Circuit.reidx c f))
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem Circuit.reidx_eval {m m' : ℕ} (c : Circuit m) (f : Fin m → Fin m')
    (g : Fin m' → Bool) :
    (Circuit.reidx c f).eval g = c.eval (g ∘ f) := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Re-indexing commutes with evaluation
statement_informal:
Re-indexing commutes with evaluation. For every circuit $c$ on $m$ variables, every $f : \mathrm{Fin}\,m \to \mathrm{Fin}\,m'$,
and every assignment $g : \mathrm{Fin}\,m' \to \mathrm{Bool}$, evaluating the re-indexed
circuit under $g$ equals evaluating $c$ under the pulled-back assignment $g \circ f$, i.e.
$\mathrm{eval}(\mathrm{reidx}(c, f), g) = \mathrm{eval}(c, g \circ f)$.

informal_statement:
Equal indices imply equal literals in a nodup clause. In a clause whose variable indices are pairwise distinct, any two literals
$l_1, l_2 \in lits$ that share the same index ($l_1.\mathrm{idx} = l_2.\mathrm{idx}$) are
in fact equal, $l_1 = l_2$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

Statement: Conversion preserves literal evaluation. For every literal $l$ and input $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the circuit
evaluation $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ equals the evaluation of its converted literal
$l.\mathrm{toLiteral}.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

theorem BoolCircuit.Lit.eval_eq_toLiteral_eval {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n)
    (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    l.eval x = l.toLiteral.eval x := sorry

Conversion preserves literal evaluation. For every literal $l$ and input $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the circuit
evaluation $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ equals the evaluation of its converted literal
$l.\mathrm{toLiteral}.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.

of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of

Statement: Variable-injectivity of a converted AND-clause. If the literal indices of an AND-clause are distinct, then within the converted term any
two literals sharing the same variable are equal.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm : NAndCircuit n → Term n
  | .clause lits _ => lits.map Lit.toLiteral
  | .node _ => []
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm_var_inj (lits : List (Lit n))
    (h : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup) :
    ∀ l₁ ∈ (NAndCircuit.clause lits h).clauseToTerm,
    ∀ l₂ ∈ (NAndCircuit.clause lits h).clauseToTerm,
    l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂ := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Variable-injectivity of a converted AND-clause
statement_informal:
Variable-injectivity of a converted AND-clause. If the literal indices of an AND-clause are distinct, then within the converted term any
two literals sharing the same variable are equal.

definitions.title: Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal
definitions.informal:

switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.

definitions.title: Normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.

definitions.title: AND-clause to a term
definitions.informal:
Sends an AND-clause $NAndCircuit.\mathrm{clause}\,lits$ to the
$Term$ (a conjunction of literals) obtained by mapping each circuit literal
through Lit.toLiteral.

Statement: Clause indices are nodup (AND). If a normal-form AND circuit $c$ equals a clause built from literals $lits$ with a
duplication-free proof $h$, then the variable indices $lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$
are pairwise distinct.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

theorem NAndCircuit.clause_nodup {n : Nat} {c : NAndCircuit n} {lits : List (Lit n)}
    {h : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup}
    (_ : c = NAndCircuit.clause lits h) : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup := sorry
title: Clause indices are nodup (AND)
statement_informal:
Clause indices are nodup (AND). If a normal-form AND circuit $c$ equals a clause built from literals $lits$ with a
duplication-free proof $h$, then the variable indices $lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$
are pairwise distinct.

definitions.title: Normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Normal-form AND circuit. A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.
- OR-clause to a term. Sends an OR-clause $NOrCircuit.\mathrm{clause}\,lits$ to the $Term$
obtained by mapping each circuit literal through Lit.toLiteral; this term plays
the role of one CNF clause (a disjunction of literals).
- Depth-2 AND-circuit to a CNF. Converts a depth-2 $NAndCircuit$ (an AND of OR-clauses) into a $CNF$, the
list of clauses obtained by converting each OR-clause via NOrCircuit.clauseToTerm;
a bare clause maps to the empty CNF.
- Evaluation of a normal-form AND circuit. Evaluates a normal-form AND circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the conjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{true}), and a node is the conjunction of its child
OR circuits' values.
- Evaluation of a normal-form OR circuit. Evaluates a normal-form OR circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the disjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{false}), and a node is the disjunction of its child
AND circuits' values.

Statement: Depth-2 AND-circuit evaluates as its CNF. If every child $c$ of an $NAndCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs$ is an OR-clause, then for
all $x$ the circuit evaluation $(NAndCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,x$
equals the CNF evaluation $CNF.eval\,((NAndCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{toCNF})\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def NOrCircuit.clauseToTerm : NOrCircuit n → Term n
  | .clause lits _ => lits.map Lit.toLiteral
  | .node _ => []
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def NAndCircuit.toCNF : NAndCircuit n → CNF n
  | .clause _ _ => []
  | .node cs => cs.map NOrCircuit.clauseToTerm
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

def NAndCircuit.eval : NAndCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x && acc) true
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true

def NOrCircuit.eval : NOrCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x || acc) false
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem NAndCircuit.node_eval_eq_toCNF_eval
    (cs : List (NOrCircuit n)) (x : Fin n → Bool)
    (h_clauses : ∀ c ∈ cs, ∃ lits h, c = NOrCircuit.clause lits h) :
    (NAndCircuit.node cs).eval x = CNF.eval (NAndCircuit.node cs).toCNF x := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Depth-2 AND-circuit evaluates as its CNF
statement_informal:
Depth-2 AND-circuit evaluates as its CNF. If every child $c$ of an $NAndCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs$ is an OR-clause, then for
all $x$ the circuit evaluation $(NAndCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,x$
equals the CNF evaluation $CNF.eval\,((NAndCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{toCNF})\,x$.

Statement: Width bound for the CNF of a depth-2 AND-circuit. If every OR-clause child has at most $w$ literals (with distinct indices), then the CNF
$(NAndCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{toCNF}$ has width at most $w$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def NOrCircuit.clauseToTerm : NOrCircuit n → Term n
  | .clause lits _ => lits.map Lit.toLiteral
  | .node _ => []
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace BoolCircuit
def NAndCircuit.toCNF : NAndCircuit n → CNF n
  | .clause _ _ => []
  | .node cs => cs.map NOrCircuit.clauseToTerm
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem NAndCircuit.toCNF_width_bounded (cs : List (NOrCircuit n)) (w : ℕ)
    (h_clauses : ∀ c ∈ cs, ∃ (lits : List (Lit n)) (h : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup),
      c = NOrCircuit.clause lits h ∧ lits.length ≤ w) :
    CNF.width (NAndCircuit.node cs).toCNF ≤ w := sorry
end BoolCircuit

Statement: Variable-injectivity of a converted OR-clause. If the literal indices of an OR-clause are distinct, then within the converted term any
two literals sharing the same variable are equal.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace BoolCircuit
def NOrCircuit.clauseToTerm : NOrCircuit n → Term n
  | .clause lits _ => lits.map Lit.toLiteral
  | .node _ => []
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem NOrCircuit.clauseToTerm_var_inj (lits : List (Lit n))
    (h : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup) :
    ∀ l₁ ∈ (NOrCircuit.clause lits h).clauseToTerm,
    ∀ l₂ ∈ (NOrCircuit.clause lits h).clauseToTerm,
    l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂ := sorry
end BoolCircuit

informal_statement:
Clause indices are nodup (OR). If a normal-form OR circuit $c$ equals a clause built from literals $lits$ with a
duplication-free proof $h$, then the variable indices $lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$
are pairwise distinct.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

theorem NOrCircuit.clause_nodup {n : Nat} {c : NOrCircuit n} {lits : List (Lit n)}
    {h : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup}
    (_ : c = NOrCircuit.clause lits h) : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup := sorry
title: Clause indices are nodup (OR)
statement_informal:
Clause indices are nodup (OR). If a normal-form OR circuit $c$ equals a clause built from literals $lits$ with a
duplication-free proof $h$, then the variable indices $lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$
are pairwise distinct.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Normal-form AND circuit. A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.
- AND-clause to a term. Sends an AND-clause $NAndCircuit.\mathrm{clause}\,lits$ to the
$Term$ (a conjunction of literals) obtained by mapping each circuit literal
through Lit.toLiteral.
- Depth-2 OR-circuit to a DNF. Converts a depth-2 $NOrCircuit$ (an OR of AND-clauses) into a $DNF$, the
list of terms obtained by converting each AND-clause via NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm;
a bare clause maps to the empty DNF.
- Evaluation of a normal-form AND circuit. Evaluates a normal-form AND circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the conjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{true}), and a node is the conjunction of its child
OR circuits' values.
- Evaluation of a normal-form OR circuit. Evaluates a normal-form OR circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the disjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{false}), and a node is the disjunction of its child
AND circuits' values.

Statement: Depth-2 OR-circuit evaluates as its DNF. If every child $c$ of an $NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs$ is an AND-clause, then for
all $x$ the circuit evaluation $(NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,x$
equals the DNF evaluation $DNF.eval\,((NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{toDNF})\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm : NAndCircuit n → Term n
  | .clause lits _ => lits.map Lit.toLiteral
  | .node _ => []
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def NOrCircuit.toDNF : NOrCircuit n → DNF n
  | .clause _ _ => []
  | .node cs => cs.map NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

def NAndCircuit.eval : NAndCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x && acc) true
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true

def NOrCircuit.eval : NOrCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x || acc) false
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem NOrCircuit.node_eval_eq_toDNF_eval
    (cs : List (NAndCircuit n)) (x : Fin n → Bool)
    (h_clauses : ∀ c ∈ cs, ∃ lits h, c = NAndCircuit.clause lits h) :
    (NOrCircuit.node cs).eval x = DNF.eval (NOrCircuit.node cs).toDNF x := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Depth-2 OR-circuit evaluates as its DNF
statement_informal:
Depth-2 OR-circuit evaluates as its DNF. If every child $c$ of an $NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs$ is an AND-clause, then for
all $x$ the circuit evaluation $(NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,x$
equals the DNF evaluation $DNF.eval\,((NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{toDNF})\,x$.

- AND-clause to a term. Sends an AND-clause $NAndCircuit.\mathrm{clause}\,lits$ to the
$Term$ (a conjunction of literals) obtained by mapping each circuit literal
through Lit.toLiteral.
- Depth-2 OR-circuit to a DNF. Converts a depth-2 $NOrCircuit$ (an OR of AND-clauses) into a $DNF$, the
list of terms obtained by converting each AND-clause via NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm;
a bare clause maps to the empty DNF.

Statement: Variable-injectivity of the DNF of a depth-2 OR-circuit. If every child of an $NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs$ is an AND-clause with distinct
literal indices, then in every term of the resulting DNF any two literals on the same
variable are equal.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm : NAndCircuit n → Term n
  | .clause lits _ => lits.map Lit.toLiteral
  | .node _ => []
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def NOrCircuit.toDNF : NOrCircuit n → DNF n
  | .clause _ _ => []
  | .node cs => cs.map NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem NOrCircuit.toDNF_var_inj (cs : List (NAndCircuit n))
    (h_clauses : ∀ c ∈ cs, ∃ (lits : List (Lit n)) (h : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup),
      c = NAndCircuit.clause lits h) :
    ∀ t ∈ (NOrCircuit.node cs).toDNF,
      ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂ := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Variable-injectivity of the DNF of a depth-2 OR-circuit
statement_informal:
