literal indices, then in every term of the resulting DNF any two literals on the same
variable are equal.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding

Statement: Width bound for the DNF of a depth-2 OR-circuit. If every AND-clause child has at most $w$ literals (with distinct indices), then the DNF
$(NOrCircuit.\mathrm{node}\,cs).\mathrm{toDNF}$ has width at most $w$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm : NAndCircuit n → Term n
  | .clause lits _ => lits.map Lit.toLiteral
  | .node _ => []
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace BoolCircuit
def NOrCircuit.toDNF : NOrCircuit n → DNF n
  | .clause _ _ => []
  | .node cs => cs.map NAndCircuit.clauseToTerm
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem NOrCircuit.toDNF_width_bounded (cs : List (NAndCircuit n)) (w : ℕ)
    (h_clauses : ∀ c ∈ cs, ∃ (lits : List (Lit n)) (h : (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup),
      c = NAndCircuit.clause lits h ∧ lits.length ≤ w) :
    DNF.width (NOrCircuit.node cs).toDNF ≤ w := sorry
end BoolCircuit

informal_statement:
Fold commutes with map (addition). A technical lemma: if $g(h\,c) = f\,c$ for all $c \in cs$, then the additive
\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ equals the additive \texttt{foldr} of $f$
over $cs$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
private theorem foldr_add_map {f : α → Nat} {g : β → Nat} {h : α → β}
    {cs : List α}
    (heq : ∀ c ∈ cs, g (h c) = f c) :
    (cs.map h).foldr (fun c acc => g c + acc) 0 =
    cs.foldr (fun c acc => f c + acc) 0 := sorry
title: Fold commutes with map (addition)
statement_informal:
Fold commutes with map (addition). A technical lemma: if $g(h\,c) = f\,c$ for all $c \in cs$, then the additive
\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ equals the additive \texttt{foldr} of $f$
over $cs$.

informal_statement:
Fold inequality under map (addition). A technical lemma: if $g(h\,c) \le k \cdot f\,c$ for all $c \in cs$, then the additive
\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ is at most $k$ times the additive
\texttt{foldr} of $f$ over $cs$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
private theorem foldr_add_map_le {f : α → Nat} {g : β → Nat} {h : α → β}
    {cs : List α} {k : Nat}
    (heq : ∀ c ∈ cs, g (h c) ≤ k * f c) :
    (cs.map h).foldr (fun c acc => g c + acc) 0 ≤
    k * cs.foldr (fun c acc => f c + acc) 0 := sorry

\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ is at most $k$ times the additive
\texttt{foldr} of $f$ over $cs$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

Statement: Conjunction of literals evaluates as a term. For any list of literals $lits$ and input $x$, the right fold of $\&\&$ over the literal
evaluations (starting from $\mathrm{true}$) equals the term evaluation
$Term.eval\,(lits.\mathrm{map}\,Lit.toLiteral)\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem foldr_and_lits_eq_term_eval (lits : List (Lit n)) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x && acc) true =
    Term.eval (lits.map Lit.toLiteral) x := sorry
end BoolCircuit

statement_informal:
Conjunction of literals evaluates as a term. For any list of literals $lits$ and input $x$, the right fold of $\&\&$ over the literal
evaluations (starting from $\mathrm{true}$) equals the term evaluation
$Term.eval\,(lits.\mathrm{map}\,Lit.toLiteral)\,x$.

Fold commutes with map (conjunction). A technical lemma: if $g(h\,c) = f\,c$ for all $c \in cs$, then the conjunctive
\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ equals the conjunctive \texttt{foldr} of
$f$ over $cs$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
private theorem foldr_and_map {f : α → Bool} {g : β → Bool} {h : α → β}
    {cs : List α}
    (heq : ∀ c ∈ cs, g (h c) = f c) :
    (cs.map h).foldr (fun c acc => g c && acc) true =
    cs.foldr (fun c acc => f c && acc) true := sorry

Fold commutes with map (conjunction). A technical lemma: if $g(h\,c) = f\,c$ for all $c \in cs$, then the conjunctive
\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ equals the conjunctive \texttt{foldr} of

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

Statement: Disjunction of literals evaluates as a CNF clause. For any list of literals $lits$ and input $x$, the right fold of $||$ over the literal
evaluations (starting from $\mathrm{false}$) equals the CNF clause evaluation
$CNF.evalClause\,(lits.\mathrm{map}\,Lit.toLiteral)\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
theorem foldr_or_lits_eq_clause_eval (lits : List (Lit n)) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x || acc) false =
    CNF.evalClause (lits.map Lit.toLiteral) x := sorry
end BoolCircuit
title: Disjunction of literals evaluates as a CNF clause
statement_informal:
Disjunction of literals evaluates as a CNF clause. For any list of literals $lits$ and input $x$, the right fold of $||$ over the literal
evaluations (starting from $\mathrm{false}$) equals the CNF clause evaluation
$CNF.evalClause\,(lits.\mathrm{map}\,Lit.toLiteral)\,x$.

Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.

definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

informal_statement:
Fold commutes with map (disjunction). A technical lemma: if $g(h\,c) = f\,c$ for all $c \in cs$, then the disjunctive
\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ equals the disjunctive \texttt{foldr} of
$f$ over $cs$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
private theorem foldr_or_map {f : α → Bool} {g : β → Bool} {h : α → β}
    {cs : List α}
    (heq : ∀ c ∈ cs, g (h c) = f c) :
    (cs.map h).foldr (fun c acc => g c || acc) false =
    cs.foldr (fun c acc => f c || acc) false := sorry
title: Fold commutes with map (disjunction)
statement_informal:
Fold commutes with map (disjunction). A technical lemma: if $g(h\,c) = f\,c$ for all $c \in cs$, then the disjunctive
\texttt{foldr} of $g$ over $cs.\mathrm{map}\;h$ equals the disjunctive \texttt{foldr} of
$f$ over $cs$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Normal-form AND circuit. A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Normalize a circuit to AND-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form AND circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an AND node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNOr}$; and an OR node is wrapped
as a single OR node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNAnd}$ images, enforcing
alternation.
- Normalize a circuit to OR-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form OR circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an OR node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNAnd}$; and an AND node is wrapped
as a single AND node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNOr}$ images, enforcing
alternation.
- Evaluation of a normal-form AND circuit. Evaluates a normal-form AND circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the conjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{true}), and a node is the conjunction of its child
OR circuits' values.
- Evaluation of a normal-form OR circuit. Evaluates a normal-form OR circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the disjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{false}), and a node is the disjunction of its child
AND circuits' values.

Statement: AND-normalization preserves semantics. For every circuit $c$ and assignment $x$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNAnd : Circuit n → NAndCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node true  cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)
  | .node false cs  => .node [NOrCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)]

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.eval : NAndCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x && acc) true
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true

def NOrCircuit.eval : NOrCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x || acc) false
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNAnd_eval (c : Circuit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    (c.toNAnd).eval x = c.eval x := sorry
title: AND-normalization preserves semantics
statement_informal:
AND-normalization preserves semantics. For every circuit $c$ and assignment $x$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Normalize a circuit to AND-normal form
definitions.informal:
Converts a general circuit into a normal-form AND circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an AND node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNOr}$; and an OR node is wrapped
as a single OR node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNAnd}$ images, enforcing
alternation.

definitions.title: Normalize a circuit to OR-normal form
definitions.informal:
Converts a general circuit into a normal-form OR circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an OR node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNAnd}$; and an AND node is wrapped
as a single AND node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNOr}$ images, enforcing
alternation.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a normal-form AND circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the conjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{true}), and a node is the conjunction of its child
OR circuits' values.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a normal-form OR circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a normal-form OR circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the disjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{false}), and a node is the disjunction of its child
AND circuits' values.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is

- Normal-form AND circuit. A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.
- Literal count of a circuit. The number of literal occurrences in a circuit: a literal contributes $1$ and a node
contributes the sum of the literal counts of its children.
- Normalize a circuit to AND-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form AND circuit: a literal becomes a singleton

- Normalize a circuit to OR-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form OR circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an OR node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNAnd}$; and an AND node is wrapped
as a single AND node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNOr}$ images, enforcing
alternation.
- Literal count of a normal-form AND circuit. The number of literal occurrences in a normal-form AND circuit: a clause contributes the
length of its literal list, and a node contributes the sum over its child OR circuits.
- Literal count of a normal-form OR circuit. The number of literal occurrences in a normal-form OR circuit: a clause contributes the

Statement: AND-normalization preserves literal count. For every circuit $c$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{litCount} = c.\mathrm{litCount}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.litCount : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.litCount + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNAnd : Circuit n → NAndCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node true  cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)
  | .node false cs  => .node [NOrCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)]

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.litCount : NAndCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause lits _ => lits.length
  | .node cs       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.litCount + acc) 0

def NOrCircuit.litCount : NOrCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause lits _ => lits.length
  | .node cs       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.litCount + acc) 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNAnd_litCount (c : Circuit n) :
    (c.toNAnd).litCount = c.litCount := sorry
title: AND-normalization preserves literal count
statement_informal:
AND-normalization preserves literal count. For every circuit $c$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{litCount} = c.\mathrm{litCount}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNAnd : Circuit n → NAndCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node true  cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)
  | .node false cs  => .node [NOrCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)]

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.size : NAndCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause _ _ => 1
  | .node cs    => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0

def NOrCircuit.size : NOrCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause _ _ => 1
  | .node cs    => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNAnd_size_le (c : Circuit n) :
    (c.toNAnd).size ≤ 2 * c.size := sorry

statement_informal:
AND-normalization at most doubles size. For every circuit $c$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{size} \le 2\,c.\mathrm{size}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Normal-form AND circuit. A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction

- Normalize a circuit to AND-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form AND circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an AND node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNOr}$; and an OR node is wrapped
as a single OR node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNAnd}$ images, enforcing
alternation.
- Normalize a circuit to OR-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form OR circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an OR node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNAnd}$; and an AND node is wrapped
as a single AND node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNOr}$ images, enforcing
alternation.
- Evaluation of a normal-form AND circuit. Evaluates a normal-form AND circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the conjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{true}), and a node is the conjunction of its child
OR circuits' values.
- Evaluation of a normal-form OR circuit. Evaluates a normal-form OR circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the disjunction

Statement: Normalization preserves semantics. For every circuit $c$ and assignment $x$, both normalizations agree with the original
on values: $(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ and
$(c.\mathrm{toNOr}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNAnd : Circuit n → NAndCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node true  cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)
  | .node false cs  => .node [NOrCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)]

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.eval : NAndCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x && acc) true
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true

def NOrCircuit.eval : NOrCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x || acc) false
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNAnd_toNOr_eval (c : Circuit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    (c.toNAnd).eval x = c.eval x ∧ (c.toNOr).eval x = c.eval x := sorry
title: Normalization preserves semantics
statement_informal:
Normalization preserves semantics. For every circuit $c$ and assignment $x$, both normalizations agree with the original
on values: $(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ and
$(c.\mathrm{toNOr}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.

def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.litCount : NAndCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause lits _ => lits.length
  | .node cs       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.litCount + acc) 0

def NOrCircuit.litCount : NOrCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause lits _ => lits.length
  | .node cs       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.litCount + acc) 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNAnd_toNOr_litCount (c : Circuit n) :
    (c.toNAnd).litCount = c.litCount ∧ (c.toNOr).litCount = c.litCount := sorry
title: Normalization preserves literal count
statement_informal:
Normalization preserves literal count. For every circuit $c$, both normalizations preserve the literal count:
$(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{litCount} = c.\mathrm{litCount}$ and
$(c.\mathrm{toNOr}).\mathrm{litCount} = c.\mathrm{litCount}$.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.

definitions.informal:
The number of literal occurrences in a circuit: a literal contributes $1$ and a node
contributes the sum of the literal counts of its children.

definitions.title: Normalize a circuit to AND-normal form

Converts a general circuit into a normal-form AND circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an AND node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNOr}$; and an OR node is wrapped
as a single OR node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNAnd}$ images, enforcing
alternation.

definitions.title: Normalize a circuit to OR-normal form

Converts a general circuit into a normal-form OR circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an OR node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNAnd}$; and an AND node is wrapped
as a single AND node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNOr}$ images, enforcing

definitions.title: Literal count of a normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
The number of literal occurrences in a normal-form AND circuit: a clause contributes the
length of its literal list, and a node contributes the sum over its child OR circuits.

definitions.title: Literal count of a normal-form OR circuit
definitions.informal:
The number of literal occurrences in a normal-form OR circuit: a clause contributes the
length of its literal list, and a node contributes the sum over its child AND circuits.

Statement: Normalization at most doubles size. For every circuit $c$, both normalizations have size at most twice the original:

formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNAnd : Circuit n → NAndCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node true  cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)
  | .node false cs  => .node [NOrCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)]

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.size : NAndCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause _ _ => 1
  | .node cs    => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0

def NOrCircuit.size : NOrCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause _ _ => 1
  | .node cs    => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNAnd_toNOr_size_le (c : Circuit n) :
    (c.toNAnd).size ≤ 2 * c.size ∧ (c.toNOr).size ≤ 2 * c.size := sorry
title: Normalization at most doubles size
statement_informal:
Normalization at most doubles size. For every circuit $c$, both normalizations have size at most twice the original:
$(c.\mathrm{toNAnd}).\mathrm{size} \le 2\,c.\mathrm{size}$ and
$(c.\mathrm{toNOr}).\mathrm{size} \le 2\,c.\mathrm{size}$.

has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

clause; an AND node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNOr}$; and an OR node is wrapped

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Normal-form AND circuit. A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Normalize a circuit to AND-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form AND circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an AND node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNOr}$; and an OR node is wrapped
as a single OR node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNAnd}$ images, enforcing
alternation.
- Normalize a circuit to OR-normal form. Converts a general circuit into a normal-form OR circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an OR node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNAnd}$; and an AND node is wrapped
as a single AND node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNOr}$ images, enforcing
alternation.
- Evaluation of a normal-form AND circuit. Evaluates a normal-form AND circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the conjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{true}), and a node is the conjunction of its child
OR circuits' values.
- Evaluation of a normal-form OR circuit. Evaluates a normal-form OR circuit under an assignment $x$: a clause is the disjunction
of its literals' values (unit \texttt{false}), and a node is the disjunction of its child
AND circuits' values.

Statement: OR-normalization preserves semantics. For every circuit $c$ and assignment $x$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNOr}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNAnd : Circuit n → NAndCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node true  cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)
  | .node false cs  => .node [NOrCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)]

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.eval : NAndCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x && acc) true
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true

def NOrCircuit.eval : NOrCircuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .clause lits _, x => lits.foldr (fun l acc => l.eval x || acc) false
  | .node cs, x       => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNOr_eval (c : Circuit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    (c.toNOr).eval x = c.eval x := sorry
title: OR-normalization preserves semantics
statement_informal:
OR-normalization preserves semantics. For every circuit $c$ and assignment $x$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNOr}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = c.\mathrm{eval}\,x$.

inductive NAndCircuit (n : Nat) where
  | clause : (lits : List (Lit n)) → (lits.map Lit.idx).Nodup → NAndCircuit n
  | node   : List (NOrCircuit n) → NAndCircuit n
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNAnd : Circuit n → NAndCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node true  cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)
  | .node false cs  => .node [NOrCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)]

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Circuit.toNOr : Circuit n → NOrCircuit n
  | .lit l          => .clause [l] (List.nodup_singleton _)
  | .node false cs  => .node (cs.map Circuit.toNAnd)
  | .node true  cs  => .node [NAndCircuit.node (cs.map Circuit.toNOr)]

def NAndCircuit.size : NAndCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause _ _ => 1
  | .node cs    => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0

def NOrCircuit.size : NOrCircuit n → Nat
  | .clause _ _ => 1
  | .node cs    => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
theorem toNOr_size_le (c : Circuit n) :
    (c.toNOr).size ≤ 2 * c.size := sorry
title: OR-normalization at most doubles size
statement_informal:
OR-normalization at most doubles size. For every circuit $c$,
$(c.\mathrm{toNOr}).\mathrm{size} \le 2\,c.\mathrm{size}$.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
A normal-form AND circuit on $n$ variables: either a base \texttt{clause} given by a list
of literals whose variable indices are pairwise distinct (a \texttt{Nodup} proof on
$lits.\mathrm{map}\;\mathrm{Lit.idx}$), or a \texttt{node} that ANDs together a list of
normal-form OR circuits. Together with $\mathrm{NOrCircuit}$ this forms a strictly
alternating AND/OR normal form.

definitions.title: Size of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

definitions.title: Normalize a circuit to AND-normal form
definitions.informal:
Converts a general circuit into a normal-form AND circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an AND node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNOr}$; and an OR node is wrapped
as a single OR node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNAnd}$ images, enforcing
alternation.

definitions.title: Normalize a circuit to OR-normal form
definitions.informal:
Converts a general circuit into a normal-form OR circuit: a literal becomes a singleton
clause; an OR node maps its children through $\mathrm{toNAnd}$; and an AND node is wrapped
as a single AND node built from the children's $\mathrm{toNOr}$ images, enforcing
alternation.

definitions.title: Size of a normal-form AND circuit
definitions.informal:
The total node count of a normal-form AND circuit: a clause has size $1$, and a node has
size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its child OR circuits.

definitions.title: Size of a normal-form OR circuit
definitions.informal:
The total node count of a normal-form OR circuit: a clause has size $1$, and a node has
size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its child AND circuits.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

Statement: Basic sign identities. $\sigma(\texttt{false}) = 1$, $\sigma(\texttt{true}) = -1$,
$\sigma(b)^2 = 1$, and $\sigma(\lnot b) = -\sigma(b)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

Statement: Count of Walsh characters. There are exactly $2^n$ Walsh characters, matching the dimension of
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n)$.

    (Finset.univ (α := Finset (Fin n))).card = 2 ^ n := sorry

Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Bit flip. The point $x^i\in\{0,1\}^n$ obtained from $x$ by flipping the $i$-th coordinate.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

Statement: Character under a single bit flip. Flipping coordinate $i$ negates $\chi_S$ when $i\in S$ and leaves it unchanged
otherwise:
\[
  \chi_S(x^i) \;=\;
  \begin{cases} -\chi_S(x) & i\in S,\\ \chi_S(x) & i\notin S. \end{cases}
\]

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def flipBit (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) : BoolCube n :=
  Function.update x i (!x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma chiS_flipBit (S : Finset (Fin n)) (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) :
    chiS S (flipBit x i) = if i ∈ S then -chiS S x else chiS S x := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis

title: Characters are multiplicative on disjoint sets
statement_informal: Characters are multiplicative on disjoint sets. If $S$ and $T$ are disjoint then $\chi_{S\cup T}(x) = \chi_S(x)\cdot\chi_T(x)$.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

Statement: Character under global bit flip. Flipping all bits multiplies the character by $(-1)^{|S|}$:
\[
  \chi_S(\lnot x) \;=\; (-1)^{|S|}\,\chi_S(x).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma chiS_neg (S : Finset (Fin n)) (x : BoolCube n) :
    chiS S (fun i => !x i) = (-1 : ℝ) ^ S.card * chiS S x := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Character under global bit flip
statement_informal:
Character under global bit flip. Flipping all bits multiplies the character by $(-1)^{|S|}$:
\[
  \chi_S(\lnot x) \;=\; (-1)^{|S|}\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Discrete derivative. The \emph{discrete derivative} of $f$ in direction $i$ is the Boolean function
\[
  (D_i f)(x) \;=\; \frac{f(x^{i\mapsto 0}) - f(x^{i\mapsto 1})}{2},
\]
where $x^{i\mapsto b}$ sets coordinate $i$ of $x$ to $b$.

Statement: Derivative is additive. The discrete derivative respects addition:
$D_i(f + g) = D_i f + D_i g$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def derivative {n : ℕ} (i : Fin n) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (f (Function.update x i false) - f (Function.update x i true)) / 2
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma derivative_add {n : ℕ} (i : Fin n) (f g : BooleanFunc n) :
    derivative i (f + g) = derivative i f + derivative i g := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Derivative is additive
statement_informal:
Derivative is additive. The discrete derivative respects addition:
$D_i(f + g) = D_i f + D_i g$.

Statement: Derivative commutes with scalars. The discrete derivative commutes with scalar multiplication:

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Dictator. The \emph{dictator function} for coordinate $i$ outputs the sign of the $i$-th
bit: $\mathrm{dict}_i(x) = (-1)^{x_i}$.  Its unique nonzero Fourier coefficient
is $\widehat{\mathrm{dict}_i}(\{i\}) = 1$.

Statement: Dictator equals singleton character. $\mathrm{dict}_i = \chi_{\{i\}}$.

end BooleanAnalysis

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation operator. The \emph{expectation operator} in direction $i$ averages $f$ over the two
values of coordinate $i$:
\[
  (E_i f)(x) \;=\; \frac{f(x^{i\mapsto 0}) + f(x^{i\mapsto 1})}{2}.
\]

Statement: Expectation operator is additive. The expectation operator respects addition:
$E_i(f + g) = E_i f + E_i g$.

Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Bit flip. The point $x^i\in\{0,1\}^n$ obtained from $x$ by flipping the $i$-th coordinate.

Statement: Flipping a bit twice is the identity. Flipping coordinate $i$ twice returns the original point:
$(x^i)^i = x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def flipBit (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) : BoolCube n :=
  Function.update x i (!x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma flipBit_flipBit (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) : flipBit (flipBit x i) i = x := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Flipping a bit twice is the identity
statement_informal:
Flipping a bit twice is the identity. Flipping coordinate $i$ twice returns the original point:
$(x^i)^i = x$.

abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Fourier coefficient at $\varnothing$ equals expectation. $\hat f(\varnothing) = \E[f]$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma fourierCoeff_empty (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    fourierCoeff f ∅ = expect f := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Fourier coefficient at $\varnothing$ equals expectation
statement_informal: Fourier coefficient at $\varnothing$ equals expectation. $\hat f(\varnothing) = \E[f]$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Odd function. A Boolean function $f$ is \emph{odd} if $f(\lnot x) = -f(x)$ for all $x$.
This models antisymmetry of pairwise preferences in social choice.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Odd functions have zero even-level Fourier coefficients. If $f$ is odd and $|S|$ is even, then $\hat f(S) = 0$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def isOddFunc (f : BooleanFunc n) : Prop :=
  ∀ x : BoolCube n, f (fun i => !x i) = -f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma fourierCoeff_odd_even (f : BooleanFunc n) (hodd : isOddFunc f)
    (S : Finset (Fin n)) (heven : Even S.card) :
    fourierCoeff f S = 0 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis

- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Bit flip. The point $x^i\in\{0,1\}^n$ obtained from $x$ by flipping the $i$-th coordinate.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Influence. The \emph{influence} of coordinate $i$ on $f$ measures how often flipping
bit $i$ changes the output:
\[
  \mathrm{Inf}_i[f]
  \;=\; \E\!\left[\frac{(f(x)-f(x^i))^2}{4}\right].
\]
For $\pm 1$-valued $f$ this equals $\Pr[f(x)\ne f(x^i)]$.

Statement: Influence of a Walsh character. $\mathrm{Inf}_i[\chi_S] = [i\in S]$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def flipBit (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) : BoolCube n :=
  Function.update x i (!x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def influence (i : Fin n) (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ (f x - f (flipBit x i)) ^ 2 / 4)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma influence_chi (i : Fin n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    influence i (chiS S) = if i ∈ S then 1 else 0 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Influence of a Walsh character
statement_informal: Influence of a Walsh character. $\mathrm{Inf}_i[\chi_S] = [i\in S]$.

  \;=\; \E\!\left[\frac{(f(x)-f(x^i))^2}{4}\right].
\]
For $\pm 1$-valued $f$ this equals $\Pr[f(x)\ne f(x^i)]$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Bit flip. The point $x^i\in\{0,1\}^n$ obtained from $x$ by flipping the $i$-th coordinate.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Influence. The \emph{influence} of coordinate $i$ on $f$ measures how often flipping
bit $i$ changes the output:
\[
  \mathrm{Inf}_i[f]
  \;=\; \E\!\left[\frac{(f(x)-f(x^i))^2}{4}\right].
\]
For $\pm 1$-valued $f$ this equals $\Pr[f(x)\ne f(x^i)]$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Influence via Fourier coefficients. \[
  \mathrm{Inf}_i[f] \;=\; \sum_{\substack{S\subseteq[n]\\ i\in S}} \hat f(S)^2.
\]

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def flipBit (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) : BoolCube n :=
  Function.update x i (!x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def influence (i : Fin n) (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ (f x - f (flipBit x i)) ^ 2 / 4)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem influence_eq_sum_fourier (i : Fin n) (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    influence i f = ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), if i ∈ S then fourierCoeff f S ^ 2 else 0 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis

statement_informal:
Influence via Fourier coefficients. \[
  \mathrm{Inf}_i[f] \;=\; \sum_{\substack{S\subseteq[n]\\ i\in S}} \hat f(S)^2.
\]

- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,

- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[

    0 ≤ innerProduct f f := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Inner product is nonneg on the diagonal
statement_informal: Inner product is nonneg on the diagonal. $\langle f, f\rangle \ge 0$.

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def isPmOne (f : BooleanFunc n) : Prop :=
  ∀ x : BoolCube n, f x = 1 ∨ f x = -1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

variable {n : ℕ}
lemma innerProduct_self_pm_one (f : BooleanFunc n) (hf : isPmOne f) :
    innerProduct f f = 1 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: $\pm 1$-valued function
definitions.informal: $f$ is \emph{$\pm 1$-valued} if $f(x)\in\{-1,1\}$ for all $x$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Bit flip. The point $x^i\in\{0,1\}^n$ obtained from $x$ by flipping the $i$-th coordinate.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Influence. The \emph{influence} of coordinate $i$ on $f$ measures how often flipping
bit $i$ changes the output:
\[
  \mathrm{Inf}_i[f]
  \;=\; \E\!\left[\frac{(f(x)-f(x^i))^2}{4}\right].
\]
For $\pm 1$-valued $f$ this equals $\Pr[f(x)\ne f(x^i)]$.
- Total influence. $I[f] = \sum_{i=1}^n \mathrm{Inf}_i[f]$.

Statement: Average influence lower bound. There exists a coordinate $i$ with $\mathrm{Inf}_i[f] \ge I[f]/n$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def flipBit (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) : BoolCube n :=
  Function.update x i (!x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def influence (i : Fin n) (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ (f x - f (flipBit x i)) ^ 2 / 4)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def totalInfluence (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  ∑ i : Fin n, influence i f
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma max_influence_lower_bound (f : BooleanFunc n) (hn : 0 < n) :
    ∃ i : Fin n, totalInfluence f / n ≤ influence i f := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Average influence lower bound
statement_informal: Average influence lower bound. There exists a coordinate $i$ with $\mathrm{Inf}_i[f] \ge I[f]/n$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

Statement: Moment equals power of expectation. Let $P$ be a probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ that assigns the uniform weight
to each singleton.  Then for any Boolean function $f$ and $p\in\mathbb{N}$, the
$p$-th moment of $f$ under $P$ equals the uniform expectation of $f^p$:
\[
  \mathbb{E}_P[f^p] \;=\; \E\!\left[f(x)^p\right].
\]

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma moment_eq_expect {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc n) (p : ℕ)
    (P : MeasureTheory.Measure (BoolCube n)) [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    (hP_unif : ∀ x, (P {x}).toReal = uniformWeight n) :
    ProbabilityTheory.moment f p P = expect (fun x ↦ f x ^ p) := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Moment equals power of expectation
statement_informal:
Moment equals power of expectation. Let $P$ be a probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ that assigns the uniform weight
to each singleton.  Then for any Boolean function $f$ and $p\in\mathbb{N}$, the
$p$-th moment of $f$ under $P$ equals the uniform expectation of $f^p$:
\[
  \mathbb{E}_P[f^p] \;=\; \E\!\left[f(x)^p\right].
\]

definitions.informal:

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Noise operator in the Fourier domain. \[
  \widehat{T_\rho f}(S) \;=\; \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem noiseOp_fourier (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    fourierCoeff (noiseOp ρ f) S = ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Noise operator in the Fourier domain
statement_informal:
Noise operator in the Fourier domain. \[
  \widehat{T_\rho f}(S) \;=\; \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S).
\]
