informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Noise operator is self-adjoint. The noise operator $T_\rho$ is self-adjoint with respect to the $L^2$ inner
product:
\[
  \langle T_\rho f, g\rangle \;=\; \langle f, T_\rho g\rangle.
\]

  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=

statement_informal:

\[
  \langle T_\rho f, g\rangle \;=\; \langle f, T_\rho g\rangle.

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Parity. The \emph{parity function} $f(x) = (-1)^{x_1+\cdots+x_n}$ satisfies
$\hat f([n]) = 1$ and $\hat f(S) = 0$ for $S\ne[n]$.

Statement: Parity equals full character. $\mathrm{parity}_n = \chi_{[n]}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def parity (n : ℕ) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i : Fin n, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma parity_eq_chi_univ : parity n = chiS Finset.univ := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Parity equals full character
statement_informal: Parity equals full character. $\mathrm{parity}_n = \chi_{[n]}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Parseval's identity. The squared $L^2$ norm of $f$ equals the sum of squared Fourier coefficients:
\[
  \|f\|^2 \;=\; \langle f, f\rangle \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \hat f(S)^2.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem parseval (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    innerProduct f f = ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), fourierCoeff f S ^ 2 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Parseval's identity
statement_informal:
Parseval's identity. The squared $L^2$ norm of $f$ equals the sum of squared Fourier coefficients:
\[
  \|f\|^2 \;=\; \langle f, f\rangle \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \hat f(S)^2.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- $\pm 1$-valued function. $f$ is \emph{$\pm 1$-valued} if $f(x)\in\{-1,1\}$ for all $x$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Parseval for $\pm 1$-valued functions. If $f$ is $\pm 1$-valued then $\sum_{S\subseteq[n]}\hat f(S)^2 = 1$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def isPmOne (f : BooleanFunc n) : Prop :=
  ∀ x : BoolCube n, f x = 1 ∨ f x = -1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma parseval_pm_one (f : BooleanFunc n) (hf : isPmOne f) :
    ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), fourierCoeff f S ^ 2 = 1 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Parseval for $\pm 1$-valued functions
statement_informal: Parseval for $\pm 1$-valued functions. If $f$ is $\pm 1$-valued then $\sum_{S\subseteq[n]}\hat f(S)^2 = 1$.

abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=

end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=

end BooleanAnalysis
title: Plancherel's identity
statement_informal:
Plancherel's identity. The inner product of two Boolean functions equals the sum of the products of
their Fourier coefficients:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \hat f(S)\,\hat g(S).
\]

$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Stability formula. \[
  \langle f, T_\rho f\rangle \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)^2.
\]

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem stability_formula (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    innerProduct f (noiseOp ρ f) = ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S ^ 2 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Stability formula
statement_informal:
Stability formula. \[
  \langle f, T_\rho f\rangle \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)^2.
\]

definitions.informal:

\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\

  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]

$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

uniform measure:

\]

\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).

Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees

Definitions:
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:

Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[

\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

Statement: Sum of a character over the cube. Summing the character $\chi_S$ over the whole hypercube yields
\[
  \sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} \chi_S(x) \;=\;
  \begin{cases} 2^n & S = \varnothing,\\ 0 & S\ne\varnothing. \end{cases}

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

Statement: Completeness kernel for the Walsh basis. For points $x, y\in\{0,1\}^n$,
\[
  \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \chi_S(x)\,\chi_S(y) \;=\;
  \begin{cases} 2^n & x = y,\\ 0 & x\ne y. \end{cases}
\]
This is the completeness kernel underlying Fourier inversion.

definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

\[

The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is

  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}

    ∏ i : Fin n, (1 + c i) := sorry

title: Sum over subsets as a product
statement_informal:
Sum over subsets as a product. For any $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \prod_{i\in S} c_i \;=\; \prod_{i=1}^n (1 + c_i).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Bit flip. The point $x^i\in\{0,1\}^n$ obtained from $x$ by flipping the $i$-th coordinate.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Influence. The \emph{influence} of coordinate $i$ on $f$ measures how often flipping
bit $i$ changes the output:
\[
  \mathrm{Inf}_i[f]
  \;=\; \E\!\left[\frac{(f(x)-f(x^i))^2}{4}\right].
\]
For $\pm 1$-valued $f$ this equals $\Pr[f(x)\ne f(x^i)]$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Total influence. $I[f] = \sum_{i=1}^n \mathrm{Inf}_i[f]$.

Statement: Total influence via Fourier coefficients. \[
  I[f] \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} |S|\,\hat f(S)^2.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def flipBit (x : BoolCube n) (i : Fin n) : BoolCube n :=
  Function.update x i (!x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def influence (i : Fin n) (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ (f x - f (flipBit x i)) ^ 2 / 4)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def totalInfluence (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  ∑ i : Fin n, influence i f
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem totalInfluence_eq_sum_sq_deg (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    totalInfluence f = ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card * fourierCoeff f S ^ 2 := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Total influence via Fourier coefficients

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[

                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Walsh expansion. Every Boolean function $f:\{0,1\}^n\to\bbr$ has the unique representation
\[
  f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
This is the Fourier inversion formula for the uniform measure on $\{0,1\}^n$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem walsh_expansion (f : BooleanFunc n) (x : BoolCube n) :
    f x = ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x := sorry
end BooleanAnalysis
title: Walsh expansion
statement_informal:
Walsh expansion. Every Boolean function $f:\{0,1\}^n\to\bbr$ has the unique representation
\[
  f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
This is the Fourier inversion formula for the uniform measure on $\{0,1\}^n$.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

Statement: Deep path length equals depth. For every decision tree $T$, the length of its deepest path equals its depth:
$T.\mathrm{deepPath}.\mathrm{length} = T.\mathrm{depth}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
lemma DecisionTree.length_deepPath {n : ℕ} (T : DecisionTree n) :
    T.deepPath.length = T.depth := sorry
title: Deep path length equals depth
statement_informal:
Deep path length equals depth. For every decision tree $T$, the length of its deepest path equals its depth:
$T.\mathrm{deepPath}.\mathrm{length} = T.\mathrm{depth}$.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Deepest path of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Negate decision tree leaves. Negates every leaf of a decision tree, leaving its branching structure unchanged.
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

Statement: negateLeaves preserves depth. $T.\mathrm{negateLeaves}.\mathrm{depth} = T.\mathrm{depth}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

lemma DecisionTree.negateLeaves_depth {n : ℕ} (T : DecisionTree n) :
    T.negateLeaves.depth = T.depth := sorry
title: negateLeaves preserves depth
statement_informal: negateLeaves preserves depth. $T.\mathrm{negateLeaves}.\mathrm{depth} = T.\mathrm{depth}$.

definitions.title: Negate decision tree leaves
definitions.informal: Negates every leaf of a decision tree, leaving its branching structure unchanged.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Negate decision tree leaves. Negates every leaf of a decision tree, leaving its branching structure unchanged.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

Statement: negateLeaves negates the value. $T.\mathrm{negateLeaves}.\mathrm{eval}\,x = \lnot\,(T.\mathrm{eval}\,x)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

lemma DecisionTree.negateLeaves_eval {n : ℕ} (T : DecisionTree n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    T.negateLeaves.eval x = !(T.eval x) := sorry
title: negateLeaves negates the value
statement_informal: negateLeaves negates the value. $T.\mathrm{negateLeaves}.\mathrm{eval}\,x = \lnot\,(T.\mathrm{eval}\,x)$.

definitions.title: Negate decision tree leaves
definitions.informal: Negates every leaf of a decision tree, leaving its branching structure unchanged.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Code. A (block) code of length $n$ over $\alpha$ is a finite set (Finset) of codewords.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Pointwise operations. Pointwise addition, subtraction, and the all-zero codeword.
- Linear code, existential form. Existential version: there exists some $m$ and generator matrix $G$ witnessing linearity.
- Distance predicate for a code. exist distinct codewords at distance exactly $d$, and no two distinct codewords
are closer than $d$.
- Weight. The Hamming weight of $c$ is $d(c, \mathbf{0})$.

Statement: Distance equals minimum nonzero weight. If $C$ is linear (witnessed by some generator matrix) and has minimum distance $d$, then:
\begin{itemize}
  \item every nonzero codeword in $C$ has weight at least $d$;
  \item some codeword in $C$ has weight exactly $d$.
\end{itemize}
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Code (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := Finset (Codeword n α)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def zero : Codeword n α := fun (_ : Fin n) ↦ 0
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def Linear_Code' (C : Code n α) (m : ℕ) :=
  ∃ (G : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) α),
    (∀ c' : Codeword m α, Matrix.mulVec G c' ∈ C) ∧
    (∀ c ∈ C, ∃ c' : Codeword m α, c = Matrix.mulVec G c')
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def distance (C : Code n α) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
  (∃ x ∈ C, ∃ y ∈ C, x ≠ y ∧ hamming_distance x y = d) ∧
  (∀ z ∈ C, ∀ w ∈ C, z ≠ w → hamming_distance z w ≥ d)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def weight (c : Codeword n α) : ℕ := hamming_distance c zero
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k m : ℕ}
lemma Linear_Code_dist_eq_min_weight {m d} (C : Code n α) (h_linear : Linear_Code' C m)
    (h : distance C d) :
    (∀ c ∈ C, c ≠ 0 → weight c ≥ d) ∧ (∃ c ∈ C, weight c = d) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Distance equals minimum nonzero weight

definitions.title: Codeword
definitions.informal: A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.

definitions.title: Code
definitions.informal: A (block) code of length $n$ over $\alpha$ is a finite set (Finset) of codewords.

definitions.title: Hamming distance
definitions.informal: Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.

definitions.title: Pointwise operations
definitions.informal: Pointwise addition, subtraction, and the all-zero codeword.

definitions.title: Linear code, existential form
definitions.informal: Existential version: there exists some $m$ and generator matrix $G$ witnessing linearity.

definitions.title: Distance predicate for a code
definitions.informal:
exist distinct codewords at distance exactly $d$, and no two distinct codewords
are closer than $d$.

definitions.title: Weight
definitions.informal: The Hamming weight of $c$ is $d(c, \mathbf{0})$.

informal_statement:
AM--GM for two nonnegative reals. For nonnegative reals $a,b\ge 0$, the geometric mean is at most the arithmetic mean:
\[
\sqrt{ab} \;\le\; \frac{a+b}{2}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
private lemma am_gm_sqrt_le_half_sum {a b : ℝ} (ha : 0 ≤ a) (hb : 0 ≤ b) :
    Real.sqrt (a * b) ≤ (a + b) / 2 := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: AM--GM for two nonnegative reals
statement_informal:
AM--GM for two nonnegative reals. For nonnegative reals $a,b\ge 0$, the geometric mean is at most the arithmetic mean:
\[
\sqrt{ab} \;\le\; \frac{a+b}{2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Binomial ratio bound. For $k\le M\le N$,
\[
\frac{\binom{N-k}{M-k}}{\binom{N}{M}} \;\le\; \Bigl(\frac{M}{N}\Bigr)^k.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
lemma binom_ratio_bound (N M k : ℕ) (hM : M ≤ N) (hk : k ≤ M) :
  (Nat.choose (N - k) (M - k) : ℝ) / (Nat.choose N M) ≤ ((M : ℝ) / N) ^ k := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Binomial ratio bound
statement_informal:
Binomial ratio bound. For $k\le M\le N$,
\[
\frac{\binom{N-k}{M-k}}{\binom{N}{M}} \;\le\; \Bigl(\frac{M}{N}\Bigr)^k.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $q$-ary entropy. \[
H_q(p) \;=\; p\log_q(q-1) - p\log_q p - (1-p)\log_q(1-p).
\]

Statement: Asymptotic lower bound on binomial term. For $0<p<1$ and $q\ge 2$, eventually
\[
\tbinom{n}{\lfloor np\rfloor}(q-1)^{pn}
\;\ge\;
q^{H_q(p)\,n - \varepsilon(n)},
\quad \varepsilon(n) = o(n).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
noncomputable def qaryEntropy (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) :=
  p * (Real.logb q (q-1)) - p * (Real.logb q p) - (1-p)*(Real.logb q (1 -p))
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
lemma binomial_coef_asymptotic_lower_bound' {q: ℕ} {p : ℝ} (hp : 0 < p ∧ p < 1) (hq : 2 ≤ q) :
    ∃ (ε : ℕ → ℝ),
      Asymptotics.IsLittleO atTop ε (fun n ↦ (n: ℝ)) ∧
      ∀ᶠ n in atTop,
        Nat.choose n (Nat.floor (n*p)) * (q - 1) ^ (p*n) ≥
          Real.rpow q ((qaryEntropy q p) * n - ε n) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Asymptotic lower bound on binomial term
statement_informal:
Asymptotic lower bound on binomial term. For $0<p<1$ and $q\ge 2$, eventually
\[
\tbinom{n}{\lfloor np\rfloor}(q-1)^{pn}
\;\ge\;
q^{H_q(p)\,n - \varepsilon(n)},
\quad \varepsilon(n) = o(n).
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Code. A (block) code of length $n$ over $\alpha$ is a finite set (Finset) of codewords.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Distance predicate for a code. exist distinct codewords at distance exactly $d$, and no two distinct codewords
are closer than $d$.

Statement: Distance is at most block length. If a code $C$ has minimum distance $d$, then $d \le n$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Code (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := Finset (Codeword n α)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def distance (C : Code n α) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
  (∃ x ∈ C, ∃ y ∈ C, x ≠ y ∧ hamming_distance x y = d) ∧
  (∀ z ∈ C, ∀ w ∈ C, z ≠ w → hamming_distance z w ≥ d)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
lemma dist_le_length (C : Code n α) (d : ℕ) (h : distance C d) : d ≤ n := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Distance is at most block length
statement_informal: Distance is at most block length. If a code $C$ has minimum distance $d$, then $d \le n$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Pointwise operations. Pointwise addition, subtraction, and the all-zero codeword.
- Hamming ball. The Hamming ball of radius $l$ around $c$:
\[
B_l(c) = \{c' : d(c',c) \le l\},
\]
implemented as a Finset.
- Weight. The Hamming weight of $c$ is $d(c, \mathbf{0})$.

Statement: Existence bound via union bound. The number of $n\times k$ matrices that send some nonzero message to a codeword of
weight $<d$ is at most
\[
(|\alpha|^k-1)\cdot|\alpha|^{nk-n}\cdot|B_{d-1}(\mathbf{0})|.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def zero : Codeword n α := fun (_ : Fin n) ↦ 0
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_ball (l : ℕ) (c : Codeword n α) : Finset (Codeword n α) :=
  {c' : Codeword n α | hamming_distance c' c ≤ l}.toFinset
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def weight (c : Codeword n α) : ℕ := hamming_distance c zero
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
theorem existence_bound (d: ℕ) (h_k : k ≥ 1) (h_d : d > 0) :
(Set.toFinset {G : (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin k) α) | ∃ (x : Codeword k α), x ≠ 0 ∧ weight (Matrix.mulVec G x) < d}).card ≤
((Fintype.card α)^k - 1) * (Fintype.card α)^(n*k - n) * ((hamming_ball (d-1) (zero : Codeword n α)).card) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Existence bound via union bound
statement_informal:
Existence bound via union bound. The number of $n\times k$ matrices that send some nonzero message to a codeword of
weight $<d$ is at most
\[
(|\alpha|^k-1)\cdot|\alpha|^{nk-n}\cdot|B_{d-1}(\mathbf{0})|.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Code. A (block) code of length $n$ over $\alpha$ is a finite set (Finset) of codewords.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Hamming ball. The Hamming ball of radius $l$ around $c$:
\[
B_l(c) = \{c' : d(c',c) \le l\},
\]
implemented as a Finset.
- List-decodable code. A code $C$ is $(\rho,L)$-list-decodable if every Hamming ball of radius
$\lfloor\rho n\rfloor$ contains at most $L$ codewords of $C$.

Statement: Existence of list-decodable codes. If the ball volume $V$ and code size $M$ satisfy the counting inequality
\[
|\alpha|^n\cdot\tbinom{V}{L+1}\cdot\tbinom{|\alpha|^n-(L+1)}{M-(L+1)}
\;<\;
\tbinom{|\alpha|^n}{M},
\]
then there exists a code $C$ of size $M$ that is $(p,L)$-list-decodable.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Code (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := Finset (Codeword n α)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_ball (l : ℕ) (c : Codeword n α) : Finset (Codeword n α) :=
  {c' : Codeword n α | hamming_distance c' c ≤ l}.toFinset
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def list_decodable (ρ : ℝ) (hρ₁: 0 ≤ ρ) (hρ₂: ρ ≤ 1) (n L : ℕ) (hL : L ≥ 1) (C : Code n α) : Prop :=
  (∀ y : Codeword n α, (hamming_ball (Nat.floor (ρ*n)) y ∩ C).card ≤ L)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
lemma exists_listDecodable_code (n L M : ℕ) (p : ℝ)
  (hp1 : 0 ≤ p) (hp2 : p ≤ 1) (hL : 1 ≤ L)
  (V : ℕ)
  (hV : ∀ y : Codeword n α, (hamming_ball (Nat.floor (p*n)) y).card ≤ V)
  (h_ineq : (Fintype.card α)^n * (Nat.choose V (L+1)) * (Nat.choose ((Fintype.card α)^n - (L+1)) (M - (L+1))) < Nat.choose ((Fintype.card α)^n) M)
  (hM_le_N : M ≤ (Fintype.card α)^n)
  (hL_lt_M : L < M) :
  ∃ C : Code n α, C.card = M ∧ list_decodable p hp1 hp2 n L hL C := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Existence of list-decodable codes
statement_informal:
Existence of list-decodable codes. If the ball volume $V$ and code size $M$ satisfy the counting inequality
\[
|\alpha|^n\cdot\tbinom{V}{L+1}\cdot\tbinom{|\alpha|^n-(L+1)}{M-(L+1)}
\;<\;
\tbinom{|\alpha|^n}{M},
\]
then there exists a code $C$ of size $M$ that is $(p,L)$-list-decodable.

Statement: Finiteness of constrained matrix set. For fixed $x\in\alpha^k$ and $v\in\alpha^n$, the set $\{G : Gx=v\}$ is finite.

import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]

variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k m : ℕ}
theorem finite_matrix_dist (n k : ℕ) (v : Codeword n α) (x : Codeword k α) :
    Set.Finite { G : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin k) α | Matrix.mulVec G x = v } := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

informal_statement: Positivity of $\lfloor np\rfloor$ for large $n$. Let $0<p$ and $0<1-p$, and set $N_2 = \lceil 2/(p(1-p))\rceil + 1$. If $N_2 \le n$, then $\lfloor np\rfloor > 0$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
private lemma floor_np_pos {p : ℝ} (hp : 0 < p) (h1p : 0 < 1 - p)
    {n : ℕ} {N₂ : ℕ} (hN₂_def : N₂ = Nat.ceil (2 / (p * (1 - p))) + 1)
    (hn_N2 : N₂ ≤ n) : 0 < ⌊(n : ℝ) * p⌋₊ := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Positivity of $\lfloor np\rfloor$ for large $n$
statement_informal: Positivity of $\lfloor np\rfloor$ for large $n$. Let $0<p$ and $0<1-p$, and set $N_2 = \lceil 2/(p(1-p))\rceil + 1$. If $N_2 \le n$, then $\lfloor np\rfloor > 0$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Pointwise operations. Pointwise addition, subtraction, and the all-zero codeword.
- Hamming ball. The Hamming ball of radius $l$ around $c$:
\[
B_l(c) = \{c' : d(c',c) \le l\},
\]
implemented as a Finset.
- Weight. The Hamming weight of $c$ is $d(c, \mathbf{0})$.

Statement: Gilbert--Varshamov bound. If $k \le n - \lceil\log_q|B_{d-1}(\mathbf{0})|\rceil - 1$, then there exists a
generator matrix $G$ such that every nonzero message is mapped to a codeword of
Hamming weight at least $d$.

import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
theorem gv_bound (n k q d : ℕ) (h_q : q = (Fintype.card α)) (h_k : k ≤ n - ((Nat.clog q) (hamming_ball (d-1) (zero : Codeword n α)).card) - 1):
(Set.toFinset {G : (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin k) α) | ∀ (x : Codeword k α), x ≠ 0 → weight (Matrix.mulVec G x) ≥ d}).card ≥ 1 := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Gilbert--Varshamov bound
statement_informal:
Gilbert--Varshamov bound. If $k \le n - \lceil\log_q|B_{d-1}(\mathbf{0})|\rceil - 1$, then there exists a
generator matrix $G$ such that every nonzero message is mapped to a codeword of
Hamming weight at least $d$.

lemma hamming_ball'_disjoint {d} (C : Code n α) (h : distance C d) (h' : 0 < d) :
    ∀ c₁ c₂ : Codeword n α, (c₁ ∈ C ∧ c₂ ∈ C ∧ c₁ ≠ c₂) →
      Disjoint (hamming_ball (Nat.floor (((d : ℝ) - 1)/2)) c₁)
               (hamming_ball (Nat.floor (((d : ℝ)-1)/2)) c₂) := sorry

Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Code. A (block) code of length $n$ over $\alpha$ is a finite set (Finset) of codewords.

\]

Statement: Decoding balls around distinct codewords are disjoint. If $C$ has minimum distance $d>0$, then for distinct $c_1,c_2\in C$,
\[
B_{\lfloor(d-1)/2\rfloor}(c_1)
\;\cap\;
B_{\lfloor(d-1)/2\rfloor}(c_2)
\;=\;\varnothing.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes

end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Code (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := Finset (Codeword n α)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]

  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def distance (C : Code n α) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
  (∃ x ∈ C, ∃ y ∈ C, x ≠ y ∧ hamming_distance x y = d) ∧
  (∀ z ∈ C, ∀ w ∈ C, z ≠ w → hamming_distance z w ≥ d)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]

end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
lemma hamming_ball_non_intersect {d} (C : Code n α) (h : distance C d) (h' : 0 < d) :
    ∀ c₁ c₂ : Codeword n α, (c₁ ∈ C ∧ c₂ ∈ C ∧ c₁ ≠ c₂) →
      ∀ c' : Codeword n α,
        c' ∈ (hamming_ball (Nat.floor (((d : ℝ)-1)/2)) c₁) →
        c' ∉  (hamming_ball (Nat.floor (((d : ℝ)-1)/2)) c₂) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Decoding balls around distinct codewords are disjoint
statement_informal:
Decoding balls around distinct codewords are disjoint. If $C$ has minimum distance $d>0$, then for distinct $c_1,c_2\in C$,
\[
B_{\lfloor(d-1)/2\rfloor}(c_1)
\;\cap\;
B_{\lfloor(d-1)/2\rfloor}(c_2)
\;=\;\varnothing.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Hamming ball. The Hamming ball of radius $l$ around $c$:
\[
B_l(c) = \{c' : d(c',c) \le l\},
\]
implemented as a Finset.

Statement: Cardinality of Hamming balls. For any center $c$ and radius $l$,
\[
|B_l(c)| \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{l} \binom{n}{i}\,(|\alpha|-1)^i.
\]

import Mathlib

variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]

end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_ball (l : ℕ) (c : Codeword n α) : Finset (Codeword n α) :=
  {c' : Codeword n α | hamming_distance c' c ≤ l}.toFinset
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
theorem hamming_ball_size (n l : ℕ) :
    ∀ c : Codeword n α,
      (hamming_ball l c).card =
        Finset.sum (Finset.range (l + 1)) (λ i => Nat.choose n i * (Fintype.card α - 1)^i) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- $q$-ary entropy. \[
H_q(p) \;=\; p\log_q(q-1) - p\log_q p - (1-p)\log_q(1-p).
\]
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Hamming ball. The Hamming ball of radius $l$ around $c$:
\[
B_l(c) = \{c' : d(c',c) \le l\},
\]
implemented as a Finset.

Statement: Entropy upper bound on Hamming balls. For $0<p\le 1-1/q$ and $q=|\alpha|$,
\[
|B_{\lfloor np\rfloor}(c)| \;\le\; q^{H_q(p)\,n}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
noncomputable def qaryEntropy (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) :=
  p * (Real.logb q (q-1)) - p * (Real.logb q p) - (1-p)*(Real.logb q (1 -p))
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_ball (l : ℕ) (c : Codeword n α) : Finset (Codeword n α) :=
  {c' : Codeword n α | hamming_distance c' c ≤ l}.toFinset
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
theorem hamming_ball_size_asymptotic_upper_bound (q n : ℕ) (p : ℝ)
    (hq : q = Fintype.card α) (hα : Nontrivial α)
    (hp : 0 < p ∧ p ≤ 1 - 1/q) :
    ∀ c : Codeword n α,
      (hamming_ball (Nat.floor (n*p)) c).card ≤ Real.rpow q ((qaryEntropy q p) * n) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Entropy upper bound on Hamming balls
statement_informal:
Entropy upper bound on Hamming balls. For $0<p\le 1-1/q$ and $q=|\alpha|$,
\[
|B_{\lfloor np\rfloor}(c)| \;\le\; q^{H_q(p)\,n}.
\]

definitions.title: Codeword
definitions.informal: A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.

definitions.title: Hamming distance
definitions.informal: Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Code. A (block) code of length $n$ over $\alpha$ is a finite set (Finset) of codewords.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Distance predicate for a code. exist distinct codewords at distance exactly $d$, and no two distinct codewords
are closer than $d$.

Statement: Hamming (sphere-packing) bound. Let $q=|\alpha|>1$.  If $C\subseteq\alpha^n$ has minimum distance $d>0$, then
\[
|C|
\;\le\;
\frac{q^n}{\displaystyle\sum_{i=0}^{\lfloor(d-1)/2\rfloor}\binom{n}{i}(q-1)^i}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Code (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := Finset (Codeword n α)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def distance (C : Code n α) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
  (∃ x ∈ C, ∃ y ∈ C, x ≠ y ∧ hamming_distance x y = d) ∧
  (∀ z ∈ C, ∀ w ∈ C, z ≠ w → hamming_distance z w ≥ d)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
theorem hamming_bound (n d : ℕ) (C : Code n α) (h : distance C d) (h'' : Fintype.card α > 1)
    (hd : 0 < d) :
    C.card ≤ Fintype.card α ^ n /
      (Finset.sum (Finset.range ((Nat.floor (((d : ℝ)-1)/2)) + 1))
        (λ i => Nat.choose n i * (Fintype.card α - 1)^i)) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Hamming (sphere-packing) bound
statement_informal:
Hamming (sphere-packing) bound. Let $q=|\alpha|>1$.  If $C\subseteq\alpha^n$ has minimum distance $d>0$, then
\[
|C|
\;\le\;
\frac{q^n}{\displaystyle\sum_{i=0}^{\lfloor(d-1)/2\rfloor}\binom{n}{i}(q-1)^i}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $q$-ary entropy. \[
H_q(p) \;=\; p\log_q(q-1) - p\log_q p - (1-p)\log_q(1-p).
\]

Statement: Counting inequality for list decoding. For the choice $r = 1-H_q(\rho)-1/L$, $M=\lfloor q^{rn}\rfloor$, and
$V=\lfloor q^{H_q(\rho)n}\rfloor$, whenever $L<M\le q^n$,
\[
q^n\cdot\tbinom{V}{L+1}\cdot\tbinom{q^n-(L+1)}{M-(L+1)}
\;<\;
\tbinom{q^n}{M}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
noncomputable def qaryEntropy (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) :=
  p * (Real.logb q (q-1)) - p * (Real.logb q p) - (1-p)*(Real.logb q (1 -p))
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
lemma listDecoding_counting_ineq
  (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) (n L : ℕ)
  (hq : 2 ≤ q)
  (hL : 1 ≤ L)
  (r : ℝ) (hr : r = 1 - (qaryEntropy q p) - 1 / (L : ℝ))
  (M : ℕ) (hM : M = Nat.floor ((q : ℝ) ^ (r * n)))
  (V : ℕ) (hV : V = Nat.floor (Real.rpow q ((qaryEntropy q p) * n)))
  (hM_pos : 0 < M)
  (hM_le : M ≤ q^n)
  (hL_lt_M : L < M) :
  (q : ℝ)^n * (Nat.choose V (L+1)) * (Nat.choose (q^n - (L+1)) (M - (L+1))) < Nat.choose (q^n) M := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Counting inequality for list decoding
statement_informal:
Counting inequality for list decoding. For the choice $r = 1-H_q(\rho)-1/L$, $M=\lfloor q^{rn}\rfloor$, and
$V=\lfloor q^{H_q(\rho)n}\rfloor$, whenever $L<M\le q^n$,
\[
q^n\cdot\tbinom{V}{L+1}\cdot\tbinom{q^n-(L+1)}{M-(L+1)}
\;<\;
\tbinom{q^n}{M}.
\]

informal_statement:
List-decoding capacity. For any $q=|\alpha|$, $0<\rho\le 1-1/q$, and $L\ge 1$, there exist codes of rate
$r = 1-H_q(\rho)-1/L$ that are $(\rho,L)$-list-decodable.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

variable {n k : ℕ}
theorem list_decoding_capacity
  (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) (hq : q = (Fintype.card α)) (hp : 0 < p ∧ p ≤ 1 - 1/q)
  (L : ℕ) (hL : 1 ≤ L) :
  let r := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: List-decoding capacity
statement_informal:
List-decoding capacity. For any $q=|\alpha|$, $0<\rho\le 1-1/q$, and $L\ge 1$, there exist codes of rate
$r = 1-H_q(\rho)-1/L$ that are $(\rho,L)$-list-decodable.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Pointwise operations. Pointwise addition, subtraction, and the all-zero codeword.
- Hamming ball. The Hamming ball of radius $l$ around $c$:
\[
B_l(c) = \{c' : d(c',c) \le l\},
\]
implemented as a Finset.
- Weight. The Hamming weight of $c$ is $d(c, \mathbf{0})$.

Statement: Probability of low-weight codeword. For any nonzero $x\in\alpha^k$,
\[
\frac{|\{G : \mathrm{wt}(Gx) < d\}|}{|\alpha|^{nk}}
\;\le\;
\frac{|B_{d-1}(\mathbf{0})|}{|\alpha|^n}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def zero : Codeword n α := fun (_ : Fin n) ↦ 0
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_ball (l : ℕ) (c : Codeword n α) : Finset (Codeword n α) :=
  {c' : Codeword n α | hamming_distance c' c ≤ l}.toFinset
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def weight (c : Codeword n α) : ℕ := hamming_distance c zero
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
theorem prob_leq_ball_size (x : Codeword k α) (d : ℕ) (h_k : k ≥ 1) (h_x : x ≠ 0) (h_d : d > 0) :
((Set.toFinset {G : (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin k) α) | weight (Matrix.mulVec G x) < d}).card : ℝ) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)^(n*k) ≤
((hamming_ball (d-1) (zero : Codeword n α)).card : ℝ) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)^n := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Probability of low-weight codeword

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $q$-ary entropy. \[
H_q(p) \;=\; p\log_q(q-1) - p\log_q p - (1-p)\log_q(1-p).
\]

Statement: Simplifying $q^{H_q(p)}$. For $q\ge 2$ and $0<p<1$,
\[
q^{H_q(p)} \;=\; (q-1)^p\,p^{-p}\,(1-p)^{-(1-p)}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
noncomputable def qaryEntropy (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) :=
  p * (Real.logb q (q-1)) - p * (Real.logb q p) - (1-p)*(Real.logb q (1 -p))
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
lemma q_pow_qary_entropy_simp {q : ℕ} {p : ℝ} (hq : 2 ≤ q) (hp : 0 < p ∧ p < 1) :
    Real.rpow q (qaryEntropy q p) =
      (q - 1)^p * p ^ (-p) * (1 - p)^(-(1 - p)) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Simplifying $q^{H_q(p)}$

informal_statement:

Statement: Same identity, alternate exponentiation. Variant using a different exponentiation operator.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

lemma q_pow_qary_entropy_simp' {q : ℕ} {p : ℝ} (hq : 2 ≤ q) (hp : 0 < p ∧ p < 1) :

informal_statement:
Expansion of the $q$-ary entropy exponent. Algebraic rewrite of the $q$-ary entropy exponent: for $q\in\mathbb{N}$ and $p\in\mathbb{R}$,
\[
p\,\log_q(q-1) - p\,\log_q p - (1-p)\,\log_q(1-p)
=
\log_q(q-1)\,p + \log_q p\,(-p) + \log_q(1-p)\,(-(1-p)).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
private lemma qary_entropy_logb_expand (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) :
    p * Real.logb (↑q) (↑q - 1) - p * Real.logb (↑q) p - (1 - p) * Real.logb (↑q) (1 - p) =
      Real.logb (↑q) (↑q - 1) * p + Real.logb (↑q) p * (-p) +
        Real.logb (↑q) (1 - p) * (-(1 - p)) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Expansion of the $q$-ary entropy exponent
statement_informal:
Expansion of the $q$-ary entropy exponent. Algebraic rewrite of the $q$-ary entropy exponent: for $q\in\mathbb{N}$ and $p\in\mathbb{R}$,
\[
p\,\log_q(q-1) - p\,\log_q p - (1-p)\,\log_q(1-p)
=
\log_q(q-1)\,p + \log_q p\,(-p) + \log_q(1-p)\,(-(1-p)).
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $q$-ary entropy. \[
H_q(p) \;=\; p\log_q(q-1) - p\log_q p - (1-p)\log_q(1-p).
\]

Statement: Positivity of $q$-ary entropy. For $q=|\alpha|$ and $0<p\le 1-1/q$,
\[
H_q(p) \;>\; 0.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
theorem qary_entropy_pos (q : ℕ) (p : ℝ) (hq : q = (Fintype.card α))
    (hp : 0 < p ∧ p ≤ 1 - 1 / (q : ℝ)) :
    0 < p * Real.logb (q : ℝ) ((q : ℝ) - 1) - p * Real.logb (q : ℝ) p -
        (1 - p) * Real.logb (q : ℝ) (1 - p) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Positivity of $q$-ary entropy
statement_informal:
Positivity of $q$-ary entropy. For $q=|\alpha|$ and $0<p\le 1-1/q$,
\[
H_q(p) \;>\; 0.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Code. A (block) code of length $n$ over $\alpha$ is a finite set (Finset) of codewords.
- Hamming distance. Hamming distance between two codewords, wrapping Mathlib's hammingDist.
- Distance predicate for a code. exist distinct codewords at distance exactly $d$, and no two distinct codewords
are closer than $d$.

Statement: Singleton bound. Assuming $\alpha$ is nontrivial, a code $C\subseteq\alpha^n$ with minimum distance $d$
satisfies
\[
|C| \;\le\; |\alpha|^{\,n-d+1}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option linter.unusedVariables false

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Code (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := Finset (Codeword n α)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def hamming_distance (c1 c2 : Codeword n α) : ℕ :=
  hammingDist c1 c2
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def distance (C : Code n α) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
  (∃ x ∈ C, ∃ y ∈ C, x ≠ y ∧ hamming_distance x y = d) ∧
  (∀ z ∈ C, ∀ w ∈ C, z ≠ w → hamming_distance z w ≥ d)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
theorem singleton_bound (C : Code n α) (d : ℕ) (h : distance C d) (hα : Nontrivial α) :
    C.card ≤ (Fintype.card α)^(n - d + 1) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Singleton bound
statement_informal:
Singleton bound. Assuming $\alpha$ is nontrivial, a code $C\subseteq\alpha^n$ with minimum distance $d$
satisfies
\[
|C| \;\le\; |\alpha|^{\,n-d+1}.
\]

Square-root/floor inequality. For $x\ge 0$,

\sqrt{x} - \sqrt{\lfloor x\rfloor} \;\le\; 1.

lemma sqrt_sub_sqrt_floor_le_one (hx : 0 ≤ x) : Real.sqrt x - Real.sqrt (Nat.floor x) ≤ 1 := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

informal_statement:
A Stirling square-root identity. For $n\ge 0$,
\[
\sqrt{2\pi n}\,\cdot\,\sqrt{\pi/2}\,\cdot\,\sqrt{n} \;=\; \pi n.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
private lemma sqrt_two_pi_mul_sqrt_pi_half (n : ℝ) (hn : 0 ≤ n) :
    Real.sqrt (2 * Real.pi * n) * Real.sqrt (Real.pi / 2) * Real.sqrt n = Real.pi * n := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: A Stirling square-root identity
statement_informal:
A Stirling square-root identity. For $n\ge 0$,
\[
\sqrt{2\pi n}\,\cdot\,\sqrt{\pi/2}\,\cdot\,\sqrt{n} \;=\; \pi n.
\]

informal_statement:
Stirling-based ratio bound for the binomial term. Let $a,b,n\in\mathbb{N}$ with $a,b>0$, $a+b=n$, and let $c>0$. Suppose the Stirling-type bound
\[
a!\,\cdot\,b! \;\le\; c\cdot\Big(\sqrt{2}\sqrt{a}\sqrt{\pi}\,(a/e)^a\cdot\sqrt{2}\sqrt{b}\sqrt{\pi}\,(b/e)^b\Big)
\]
holds. Then
\[
\frac{n^n}{a^a\,b^b}\,\Big/\,\big(c\,\sqrt{\pi/2}\,\sqrt{n}\big)
\;\le\;
\frac{n!}{a!\,b!}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
variable {x : ℝ}
private lemma stirling_comb_bound
    {a b n : ℕ} {c : ℝ}
    (ha_pos : 0 < (a : ℝ)) (hb_pos : 0 < (b : ℝ)) (hc_pos : 0 < c)
    (hab : a + b = n)
    (hN_n : (a.factorial : ℝ) * b.factorial ≤
      c * (Real.sqrt 2 * Real.sqrt ↑a * Real.sqrt Real.pi * (↑a / Real.exp 1) ^ a *
           (Real.sqrt 2 * Real.sqrt ↑b * Real.sqrt Real.pi * (↑b / Real.exp 1) ^ b))) :
    (n : ℝ) ^ n / ((a : ℝ) ^ a * (b : ℝ) ^ b) / (c * Real.sqrt (Real.pi / 2) * Real.sqrt n) ≤
    (n.factorial : ℝ) / ((a.factorial : ℝ) * b.factorial) := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Stirling-based ratio bound for the binomial term
statement_informal:
Stirling-based ratio bound for the binomial term. Let $a,b,n\in\mathbb{N}$ with $a,b>0$, $a+b=n$, and let $c>0$. Suppose the Stirling-type bound
\[
a!\,\cdot\,b! \;\le\; c\cdot\Big(\sqrt{2}\sqrt{a}\sqrt{\pi}\,(a/e)^a\cdot\sqrt{2}\sqrt{b}\sqrt{\pi}\,(b/e)^b\Big)
\]
holds. Then
\[
\frac{n^n}{a^a\,b^b}\,\Big/\,\big(c\,\sqrt{\pi/2}\,\sqrt{n}\big)
\;\le\;
\frac{n!}{a!\,b!}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Codeword. A codeword of length $n$ over alphabet $\alpha$ is a function $c : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \alpha$.
- Pointwise operations. Pointwise addition, subtraction, and the all-zero codeword.
- Distribution induced by random matrices. $\mu_x(v)$ is the fraction of $n\times k$ matrices $G$ satisfying $Gx=v$.
- Uniform distribution on vectors. The uniform probability mass function on $\alpha^n$, encoded as a function
$\alpha^n\to\mathbb{R}$ assigning $1/|\alpha|^n$ to each vector.

Statement: Uniformity of $Gx$ for nonzero $x$. If $x\neq 0$ and $k\ge 1$, then for a uniformly random $n\times k$ matrix $G$ over $\alpha$,
the product $Gx$ is uniformly distributed over $\alpha^n$:
\[
\Pr[Gx = v] = \frac{1}{|\alpha|^n}
\quad\text{for all }v\in\alpha^n.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
open Set Filter Asymptotics Finset

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
abbrev Codeword (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] := (i : Fin n) → α
end ErrorCorrectingCodes

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k : ℕ}
def zero : Codeword n α := fun (_ : Fin n) ↦ 0
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k m : ℕ}
noncomputable def matrix_dist (n k : ℕ) (x : Codeword k α) : (Codeword n α) → ℝ :=
  fun v => (Set.Finite.toFinset (finite_matrix_dist n k v x)).card / ((Fintype.card α) ^ (n * k))
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k m : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniform_vector_dist (n : ℕ) (α : Type*) [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] :
    (Codeword n α) → ℝ :=
  fun _ => 1 / ((Fintype.card α) ^ n)
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword

namespace ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] [DecidableEq α] [Field α]
variable {n k m : ℕ}
theorem uniformity_lemma (n k : ℕ) (x : Codeword k α) (h_x : x ≠ zero) (h_k : k ≥ 1) :
    matrix_dist n k x = uniform_vector_dist n α := sorry
end ErrorCorrectingCodes.Codeword
title: Uniformity of $Gx$ for nonzero $x$
statement_informal:
Uniformity of $Gx$ for nonzero $x$. If $x\neq 0$ and $k\ge 1$, then for a uniformly random $n\times k$ matrix $G$ over $\alpha$,
the product $Gx$ is uniformly distributed over $\alpha^n$:
\[
\Pr[Gx = v] = \frac{1}{|\alpha|^n}
\quad\text{for all }v\in\alpha^n.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

Statement: Depth-0 circuit is a literal. A circuit $c$ with $\mathrm{depth}\,c = 0$ is a literal: there exists $lr : \mathrm{Lit}\,m$
with $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,lr$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma Circuit.depth0_is_lit {m : ℕ} (c : Circuit m) (h : c.depth = 0) :
    ∃ lr : Lit m, c = Circuit.lit lr := sorry
end LMN
title: Depth-0 circuit is a literal
statement_informal:
Depth-0 circuit is a literal. A circuit $c$ with $\mathrm{depth}\,c = 0$ is a literal: there exists $lr : \mathrm{Lit}\,m$
with $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,lr$.
