definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

Statement: Depth-1 node has literal children. If a node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has depth at most $1$, then every
child $c \in cs$ is a literal, i.e.\ $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,lr$ for some $lr$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma Circuit.depth1_all_lits {m : ℕ} (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit m))
    (h : (Circuit.node isAnd cs).depth ≤ 1) :
    ∀ c ∈ cs, ∃ lr : Lit m, c = Circuit.lit lr := sorry
end LMN
title: Depth-1 node has literal children
statement_informal:
Depth-1 node has literal children. If a node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has depth at most $1$, then every
child $c \in cs$ is a literal, i.e.\ $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,lr$ for some $lr$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit

  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma Circuit.depth1_children_are_lits {m : ℕ} (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit m))
    (h : (Circuit.node isAnd cs).depth ≤ 1) :
    ∀ c ∈ cs, c.depth = 0 := sorry

namespace LMN

lemma Circuit.exists_node_of_depth_ge_one {m : ℕ} (c : Circuit m) (h : 1 ≤ c.depth) :

end LMN

$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is

$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

for the empty formula).

Statement: Absorb one level of the top circuit. The combined absorption step: for $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ of depth at least $1$ (with $0 < l$)
over $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$ gates having width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations, there are a
new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$ of depth

formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
structure Layer2Data (n : ℕ) where
  numGates : ℕ
  gates : Fin numGates → DNF n
  width : ℕ
  widthBound : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ width
  widthPos : 0 < width
  varInj : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂
  nodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma absorbOneLevel
    (data : Layer2Data n) (c_top : Circuit data.numGates)
    (l : ℕ) (hl : 0 < l)
    (h_depth : 1 ≤ c_top.depth)
    (hDNF_fn : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧
      (∀ x, φ.eval x = (data.gates i).eval x) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, t.Nodup))
    (hCNF_fn : ∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = (data.gates i).eval x) :
    ∃ (data' : Layer2Data n) (c_top' : Circuit data'.numGates),
      c_top'.depth ≤ c_top.depth - 1 ∧ data'.width ≤ l ∧
      (∀ x, c_top.eval (fun i => (data.gates i).eval x) =
            c_top'.eval (fun j => (data'.gates j).eval x)) := sorry
end LMN
title: Absorb one level of the top circuit
statement_informal:
Absorb one level of the top circuit. The combined absorption step: for $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ of depth at least $1$ (with $0 < l$)
over $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$ gates having width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations, there are a
new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$ of depth
at most $\mathrm{depth}\,c_{\mathrm{top}} - 1$ computing the same function. This dispatches
to the depth-1 case (giving depth $0$) and the general case (giving depth at most $1$).

($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Clean a DNF. $\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d$ first discards every contradictory term of the DNF $d$ and then
applies $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$ to each remaining term.
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Layer-2 data. A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
each term of each gate distinct literals have distinct variables.

Statement: Absorb a depth-1 top circuit. Let $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ have depth exactly $1$ over the gates of some $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$,
where (with $0 < l$) each gate has a width-$l$ DNF and CNF representation. Then there is a
new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$ of depth
$0$ over it computing the same function as $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ over the original gates.

namespace BoolCircuit

  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
structure Layer2Data (n : ℕ) where
  numGates : ℕ
  gates : Fin numGates → DNF n
  width : ℕ

  widthPos : 0 < width
  varInj : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma absorbOneLevel_depth1
    (data : Layer2Data n) (c_top : Circuit data.numGates)
    (l : ℕ) (hl : 0 < l)
    (h_depth_le : c_top.depth ≤ 1) (h_depth_ge : 1 ≤ c_top.depth)
    (hDNF_fn : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧
      (∀ x, φ.eval x = (data.gates i).eval x) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, t.Nodup))
    (hCNF_fn : ∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = (data.gates i).eval x) :
    ∃ (data' : Layer2Data n) (c_top' : Circuit data'.numGates),

- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Layer-2 data. A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
each term of each gate distinct literals have distinct variables.

Statement: Absorb a general top circuit (depth $\geq 2$). The $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$ wrapper of the general depth reduction: for $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ of
depth at least $2$ (with $0 < l$) over gates having width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations,
there are a new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$
of depth at most $\mathrm{depth}\,c_{\mathrm{top}} - 1$ computing the same function.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
structure Layer2Data (n : ℕ) where
  numGates : ℕ
  gates : Fin numGates → DNF n
  width : ℕ
  widthBound : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ width
  widthPos : 0 < width
  varInj : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂
  nodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma absorbOneLevel_general
    (data : Layer2Data n) (c_top : Circuit data.numGates)
    (l : ℕ) (hl : 0 < l)
    (h_depth : 2 ≤ c_top.depth)
    (hDNF_fn : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧
      (∀ x, φ.eval x = (data.gates i).eval x) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, t.Nodup))
    (hCNF_fn : ∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = (data.gates i).eval x) :
    ∃ (data' : Layer2Data n) (c_top' : Circuit data'.numGates),
      c_top'.depth ≤ c_top.depth - 1 ∧ data'.width ≤ l ∧
      (∀ x, c_top.eval (fun i => (data.gates i).eval x) =
            c_top'.eval (fun j => (data'.gates j).eval x)) := sorry
end LMN
title: Absorb a general top circuit (depth $\geq 2$)
statement_informal:
Absorb a general top circuit (depth $\geq 2$). The $\mathrm{Layer2Data}$ wrapper of the general depth reduction: for $c_{\mathrm{top}}$ of
depth at least $2$ (with $0 < l$) over gates having width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations,
there are a new $\mathrm{Layer2Data}'$ of width at most $l$ and a circuit $c_{\mathrm{top}}'$
of depth at most $\mathrm{depth}\,c_{\mathrm{top}} - 1$ computing the same function.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Layer-2 data
definitions.informal:
A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
each term of each gate distinct literals have distinct variables.

informal_statement:
Abstract iterative reduction. For $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$ with $0<s$ and $0<\varepsilon$, if each
per-stage bound satisfies
$\mathrm{per\_stage\_bound}(i)\le(\mathrm{layer\_size}(i))\cdot(1/2)^{\log_2(2s/\varepsilon)}$,
then $\sum_i \mathrm{per\_stage\_bound}(i)\le\varepsilon/2$. This packages the
union bound over all $d-2$ iterations as a purely arithmetic consequence of the
per-stage bounds.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem abstract_iterative_reduction
    (m : ℕ) (layer_size : Fin m → ℕ) (s : ℕ)
    (h_sum : ∑ i, layer_size i ≤ s) (hs : 0 < s)
    (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε)
    (per_stage_bound : Fin m → ℝ)
    (h_stage : ∀ i, per_stage_bound i ≤
      (layer_size i : ℝ) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ Real.logb 2 (2 * ↑s / ε)) :
    ∑ i, per_stage_bound i ≤ ε / 2 := sorry
end LMN
title: Abstract iterative reduction
statement_informal:
Abstract iterative reduction. For $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$ with $0<s$ and $0<\varepsilon$, if each
per-stage bound satisfies
$\mathrm{per\_stage\_bound}(i)\le(\mathrm{layer\_size}(i))\cdot(1/2)^{\log_2(2s/\varepsilon)}$,
then $\sum_i \mathrm{per\_stage\_bound}(i)\le\varepsilon/2$. This packages the
union bound over all $d-2$ iterations as a purely arithmetic consequence of the
per-stage bounds.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: All gates admit a small CNF under a good restriction. Given a family of $\mathrm{DNF}$ gates and a restriction $\rho$ under which every gate
satisfies $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho)\le l$, every gate can be replaced by a
$\mathrm{CNF}$ of width at most $l$ computing $\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ on all inputs.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem all_gates_have_small_cnf
    (gates : Fin s → DNF n) (l : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (h : ∀ i : Fin s, dtDepth (restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ) ≤ l) :
    ∀ i : Fin s, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, ψ.eval x = restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ x := sorry
end LMN
title: All gates admit a small CNF under a good restriction
statement_informal:
All gates admit a small CNF under a good restriction. Given a family of $\mathrm{DNF}$ gates and a restriction $\rho$ under which every gate
satisfies $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho)\le l$, every gate can be replaced by a
$\mathrm{CNF}$ of width at most $l$ computing $\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ on all inputs.

A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$

The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: AND of children has a small CNF. If every child $c \in cs$ satisfies $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho) \le l$,
then there is a CNF $\psi$ of width at most $l$ that computes the restriction of the AND node:
$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,\psi\,x = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs))\,\rho\,x$
for all $x$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ

  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}

  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma and_children_have_cnf (cs : List (Circuit n)) (ρ : Restriction n) (l : ℕ)
    (h_all : ∀ c ∈ cs, dtDepth (restrictFn c.eval ρ) ≤ l) :
    ∃ ψ : CNF n, CNF.width ψ ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = restrictFn (Circuit.eval (Circuit.node true cs)) ρ x := sorry
end LMN
title: AND of children has a small CNF
statement_informal:
AND of children has a small CNF. If every child $c \in cs$ satisfies $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho) \le l$,
then there is a CNF $\psi$ of width at most $l$ that computes the restriction of the AND node:
$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,\psi\,x = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs))\,\rho\,x$
for all $x$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$

Statement: AND of switched gates has a nice CNF. If each restricted gate $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{gates}\,i).\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1$ has
decision-tree depth $\le l$, then there is a CNF $\Psi$ of width $\le l$, with nodup and

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma and_of_gates_has_cnf
    (s₂ : ℕ) (gates : Fin s₂ → DNF n) (l : ℕ) (ρ₁ : Restriction n)
    (h_gates : ∀ i : Fin s₂, dtDepth (restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ₁) ≤ l) :
    ∃ Ψ : CNF n, CNF.width Ψ ≤ l ∧
      (∀ c ∈ Ψ, c.Nodup) ∧
      (∀ c ∈ Ψ, ∀ l₁ ∈ c, ∀ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ x, CNF.eval Ψ x = (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin s₂)).val.toList.all
        (fun i => restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ₁ x)) := sorry
end LMN
title: AND of switched gates has a nice CNF
statement_informal:
AND of switched gates has a nice CNF. If each restricted gate $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{gates}\,i).\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1$ has
decision-tree depth $\le l$, then there is a CNF $\Psi$ of width $\le l$, with nodup and
variable-injective clauses, whose evaluation equals the AND over all gates of their
restricted evaluations.

for the empty formula).

literal of every term and reading the result as a CNF. Concretely
$\neg\big(\bigvee_i \bigwedge_j \ell_{ij}\big) = \bigwedge_i \bigvee_j \neg\ell_{ij}$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$

Statement: AND of literal children has a width-$l$ CNF. Consider an AND node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs$ whose children are all
literals, where each gate function has a width-$l$ DNF and a width-$l$ CNF
representation. Then the function computed by the node has a width-$l$ CNF $\psi$ with

formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma and_of_lit_children_cnf
    {m : ℕ} (cs : List (Circuit m))
    (gates : Fin m → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (l : ℕ)
    (h_all_lits : ∀ c ∈ cs, ∃ lr : Lit m, c = Circuit.lit lr)
    (hDNF : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, φ.eval x = gates i x)
    (hCNF : ∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = gates i x) :
    ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = (Circuit.node true cs).eval (fun i => gates i x) := sorry
end LMN
title: AND of literal children has a width-$l$ CNF
statement_informal:
AND of literal children has a width-$l$ CNF. Consider an AND node $\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs$ whose children are all
literals, where each gate function has a width-$l$ DNF and a width-$l$ CNF
representation. Then the function computed by the node has a width-$l$ CNF $\psi$ with
$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,\psi\,x = (\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ for all $x$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Complement probability sums to one. For a Bernoulli restriction probability with parameter $0 \le p \le 1$ and a decidable
predicate $A$ on restrictions, $\Pr[A] + \Pr[\lnot A] = 1$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrProb_complement (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (A : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred A] :
    bernoulliRestrProb p A + bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => ¬ A ρ) = 1 := sorry
end LMN
title: Complement probability sums to one
statement_informal:
Complement probability sums to one. For a Bernoulli restriction probability with parameter $0 \le p \le 1$ and a decidable
predicate $A$ on restrictions, $\Pr[A] + \Pr[\lnot A] = 1$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Bernoulli restriction probability depends only on the function. If $f$ and $g$ agree pointwise, then for any $p\in\bbr$ and $t\in\bbn$ the Bernoulli
restriction probabilities of the events $\{\rho : \mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\}$
and $\{\rho : \mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,g\,\rho) > t\}$ are equal.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrProb_congr_fn {f g : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool}
    (h : ∀ x, f x = g x) (p : ℝ) (t : ℕ) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) =
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn g ρ) > t) := sorry
end LMN
title: Bernoulli restriction probability depends only on the function
statement_informal:
Bernoulli restriction probability depends only on the function. If $f$ and $g$ agree pointwise, then for any $p\in\bbr$ and $t\in\bbn$ the Bernoulli
restriction probabilities of the events $\{\rho : \mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\}$
and $\{\rho : \mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,g\,\rho) > t\}$ are equal.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Bernoulli depth-tail probability monotonicity. For every Boolean function $f$, threshold $t$, and parameters $p_1, p_2 \in (0,1]$, the
probability that the restricted depth exceeds $t$ under the stronger Bernoulli restriction
of parameter $p_1 p_2$ is at most that under parameter $p_1$:
\[
  \Pr_{p_1 p_2}\bigl[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\bigr] \le
  \Pr_{p_1}\bigl[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\bigr].
\]

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}

    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem bernoulliRestrProb_dtDepth_compose_le
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (t : ℕ)
    (p₁ p₂ : ℝ) (hp₁ : 0 < p₁) (hp₁₁ : p₁ ≤ 1) (hp₂ : 0 < p₂) (hp₂₁ : p₂ ≤ 1) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (p₁ * p₂) (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) ≤
    bernoulliRestrProb p₁ (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) := sorry
end LMN
title: Bernoulli depth-tail probability monotonicity
statement_informal:
Bernoulli depth-tail probability monotonicity. For every Boolean function $f$, threshold $t$, and parameters $p_1, p_2 \in (0,1]$, the
probability that the restricted depth exceeds $t$ under the stronger Bernoulli restriction
of parameter $p_1 p_2$ is at most that under parameter $p_1$:
\[
  \Pr_{p_1 p_2}\bigl[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\bigr] \le
  \Pr_{p_1}\bigl[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\bigr].
\]

definitions.title: Composition of two restrictions
definitions.informal:
Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Monotonicity in the depth threshold. For $0\le p\le 1$, a Boolean function $f$, and thresholds $l_1\le l_2$, the probability that
the restricted function exceeds depth $l_2$ is at most the probability that it exceeds depth
$l_1$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrProb_dtDepth_mono (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (l₁ l₂ : ℕ) (h : l₁ ≤ l₂) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > l₂) ≤
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > l₁) := sorry
end LMN
title: Monotonicity in the depth threshold
statement_informal:
Monotonicity in the depth threshold. For $0\le p\le 1$, a Boolean function $f$, and thresholds $l_1\le l_2$, the probability that
the restricted function exceeds depth $l_2$ is at most the probability that it exceeds depth
$l_1$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Union bound over a list of circuits. For $0\le p\le 1$, a list of circuits $\mathit{cs}$, and a predicate $\mathrm{bad}$ on circuits
and restrictions, the probability that some circuit in $\mathit{cs}$ is bad is bounded by the
folded sum of the per-circuit probabilities $\sum_{c\in \mathit{cs}}\Pr_{p}[\mathrm{bad}\,c]$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrProb_list_union_bound (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (cs : List (Circuit n))
    (bad : Circuit n → Restriction n → Prop)
    [inst : ∀ c, DecidablePred (bad c)] :
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => ∃ c ∈ cs, bad c ρ) ≤
    cs.foldr (fun c acc => bernoulliRestrProb p (bad c) + acc) 0 := sorry
end LMN
title: Union bound over a list of circuits
statement_informal:
Union bound over a list of circuits. For $0\le p\le 1$, a list of circuits $\mathit{cs}$, and a predicate $\mathrm{bad}$ on circuits
and restrictions, the probability that some circuit in $\mathit{cs}$ is bad is bounded by the
folded sum of the per-circuit probabilities $\sum_{c\in \mathit{cs}}\Pr_{p}[\mathrm{bad}\,c]$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[

\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor

- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by

Statement: Monotonicity of the Bernoulli restriction probability. Let $p\in[0,1]$ and let $A,B$ be predicates on restrictions. If $A(\rho)$ implies $B(\rho)$
for every restriction $\rho$, then
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,A \le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,B .
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrProb_mono (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (A B : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred A] [DecidablePred B]
    (h : ∀ ρ, A ρ → B ρ) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p A ≤ bernoulliRestrProb p B := sorry
end LMN
title: Monotonicity of the Bernoulli restriction probability
statement_informal:
Monotonicity of the Bernoulli restriction probability. Let $p\in[0,1]$ and let $A,B$ be predicates on restrictions. If $A(\rho)$ implies $B(\rho)$
for every restriction $\rho$, then
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,A \le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,B .
\]
