informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by

Statement: Union bound for restriction probabilities. For $0\le p\le 1$ and finitely many events $A_0,\dots,A_{m-1}$ on restrictions, the
Bernoulli$(p)$ probability that some $A_i$ holds is at most the sum of the
individual probabilities:
\[
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,(\exists i,\,A_i)\le
\sum_i \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,(A_i).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem bernoulliRestrProb_union_bound_fin
    (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (m : ℕ) (A : Fin m → Restriction n → Prop)
    [∀ i, DecidablePred (A i)] :
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => ∃ i, A i ρ) ≤
    ∑ i, bernoulliRestrProb p (A i) := sorry
end LMN
title: Union bound for restriction probabilities
statement_informal:
Union bound for restriction probabilities. For $0\le p\le 1$ and finitely many events $A_0,\dots,A_{m-1}$ on restrictions, the
Bernoulli$(p)$ probability that some $A_i$ holds is at most the sum of the
individual probabilities:
\[
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,(\exists i,\,A_i)\le
\sum_i \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,(A_i).
\]

definitions.informal:

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Per-variable Bernoulli weight. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$, the per-variable weight assigns $p$ to a free
coordinate (value \texttt{none}) and $(1-p)/2$ to a fixed coordinate (value
\texttt{some\_}), matching the marginal of the Bernoulli restriction model.
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

Statement: Bernoulli weight factors as a product. The Bernoulli restriction weight of $\rho$ at parameter $p$ equals the product over
all coordinates $i$ of the per-variable weights:
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho = \prod_{i : \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \mathrm{varWeight}\,p\,(\rho\,i).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def varWeight (p : ℝ) : Option Bool → ℝ
  | none => p
  | some _ => (1 - p) / 2
end LMN

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrWeight_eq_prod (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ = ∏ i : Fin n, varWeight p (ρ i) := sorry
end LMN
title: Bernoulli weight factors as a product
statement_informal:
Bernoulli weight factors as a product. The Bernoulli restriction weight of $\rho$ at parameter $p$ equals the product over
all coordinates $i$ of the per-variable weights:
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho = \prod_{i : \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \mathrm{varWeight}\,p\,(\rho\,i).
\]

definitions.title: Per-variable Bernoulli weight
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$, the per-variable weight assigns $p$ to a free
coordinate (value \texttt{none}) and $(1-p)/2$ to a fixed coordinate (value
\texttt{some\_}), matching the marginal of the Bernoulli restriction model.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

Statement: Build a node of signed literals. For any flag $\mathit{isAnd}$, arity $k$, and sign family $\mathit{signs} : \mathrm{Fin}\,k
\to \mathrm{Bool}$, there is a circuit $c'$ of depth at most $1$ that equals the node
$\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}$ over the literals $\mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,\langle j,
\mathit{signs}\,j\rangle$, evaluating identically on every input assignment $g$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma build_literal_circuit (isAnd : Bool) (k : ℕ)
    (signs : Fin k → Bool) :
    ∃ c' : Circuit k, c'.depth ≤ 1 ∧
      ∀ (g : Fin k → Bool),
        c'.eval g = (Circuit.node isAnd
          ((List.finRange k).map (fun j => Circuit.lit ⟨j, signs j⟩))).eval g := sorry
end LMN
title: Build a node of signed literals
statement_informal:
Build a node of signed literals. For any flag $\mathit{isAnd}$, arity $k$, and sign family $\mathit{signs} : \mathrm{Fin}\,k
\to \mathrm{Bool}$, there is a circuit $c'$ of depth at most $1$ that equals the node
$\mathrm{Circuit.node}\,\mathit{isAnd}$ over the literals $\mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,\langle j,
\mathit{signs}\,j\rangle$, evaluating identically on every input assignment $g$.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Clean a DNF. $\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d$ first discards every contradictory term of the DNF $d$ and then
applies $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$ to each remaining term.
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Depth-$\leq 1$ child has a signed width-$l$ DNF. A circuit $c_j$ of depth at most $1$ over gates that each have a width-$l$ DNF and CNF
representation admits a width-$l$ DNF $\varphi$ together with a sign bit such that, for
all $x$, the signed evaluation $\big(\mathrm{if}\ \mathit{sign}\ \mathrm{then}\
\varphi.\mathrm{eval}\,x\ \mathrm{else}\ \neg\varphi.\mathrm{eval}\,x\big)$ equals
$c_j.\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$; moreover the terms of $\varphi$
are duplicate-free with no repeated variable.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma child_depth_le1_has_signed_dnf
    {m : ℕ} (c_j : Circuit m) (gates : Fin m → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (l : ℕ)
    (hd : c_j.depth ≤ 1)
    (hDNF : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, φ.eval x = gates i x)
    (hCNF : ∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = gates i x) :
    ∃ (φ : DNF n) (sign : Bool), φ.width ≤ l ∧
      (∀ x, (if sign then φ.eval x else !(φ.eval x)) = c_j.eval (fun i => gates i x)) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, t.Nodup) := sorry
end LMN
title: Depth-$\leq 1$ child has a signed width-$l$ DNF
statement_informal:
Depth-$\leq 1$ child has a signed width-$l$ DNF. A circuit $c_j$ of depth at most $1$ over gates that each have a width-$l$ DNF and CNF
representation admits a width-$l$ DNF $\varphi$ together with a sign bit such that, for
all $x$, the signed evaluation $\big(\mathrm{if}\ \mathit{sign}\ \mathrm{then}\
\varphi.\mathrm{eval}\,x\ \mathrm{else}\ \neg\varphi.\mathrm{eval}\,x\big)$ equals
$c_j.\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$; moreover the terms of $\varphi$
are duplicate-free with no repeated variable.

Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are

applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is

- Size of a circuit. The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

Statement: Child size bounded by parent. If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has size at most $s$, then every
child $c \in cs$ has size at most $s - 1$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where

deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma child_size_le_parent (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit n)) (c : Circuit n) (hc : c ∈ cs)
    {s : ℕ} (hs : (Circuit.node isAnd cs).size ≤ s) :
    c.size ≤ s - 1 := sorry
end LMN

lemma children_depth_le (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit n)) (c : Circuit n) (hc : c ∈ cs)

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Maximum fanin of a circuit. The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.

Statement: Child fan-in bound. If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has maximum fan-in at most $w$, then
every child $c \in cs$ has maximum fan-in at most $w$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.maxFanin : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => max cs.length (cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.maxFanin acc) 0)
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma children_maxFanin_le (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit n)) (c : Circuit n) (hc : c ∈ cs)
    {w : ℕ} (hw : (Circuit.node isAnd cs).maxFanin ≤ w) :
    c.maxFanin ≤ w := sorry
end LMN
title: Child fan-in bound
statement_informal:
Child fan-in bound. If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has maximum fan-in at most $w$, then
every child $c \in cs$ has maximum fan-in at most $w$.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Maximum fanin of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Size of a circuit. The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

Statement: Sum of child sizes bound. If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has size at most $s$, then the sum of
the sizes of its children is at most $s - 1$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma children_size_sum_le (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit n))
    {s : ℕ} (hs : (Circuit.node isAnd cs).size ≤ s) :
    cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0 ≤ s - 1 := sorry
end LMN
title: Sum of child sizes bound
statement_informal:
Sum of child sizes bound. If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has size at most $s$, then the sum of
the sizes of its children is at most $s - 1$.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Size of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

Statement: Depth-zero circuit is a literal. If a circuit $c$ has depth $0$, then $c$ is a single literal: there exists a literal
$l$ with $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,l$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma circuit_depth_zero_is_lit (c : Circuit m) (h : c.depth = 0) :
    ∃ (l : BoolCircuit.Lit m), c = Circuit.lit l := sorry
end LMN
title: Depth-zero circuit is a literal
statement_informal:
Depth-zero circuit is a literal. If a circuit $c$ has depth $0$, then $c$ is a single literal: there exists a literal
$l$ with $c = \mathrm{Circuit.lit}\,l$.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Maximum fanin of a circuit. The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.
- Size of a circuit. The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Iterative reduction bound (relaxed form). Replacing the coefficient $s-1$ by $s$ in the previous bound: under the same hypotheses and
the parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le s(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.maxFanin : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => max cs.length (cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.maxFanin acc) 0)
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem circuit_reduction_aux
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d s w : ℕ) (l t : ℕ)
    (hd2 : 2 ≤ d) (hs_pos : 0 < s) (hw_pos : 0 < w) (hl_pos : 0 < l)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (h_circuit : ∃ c : Circuit n,
      c.depth ≤ d ∧ c.size ≤ s ∧ Circuit.maxFanin c ≤ w ∧ ∀ x, c.eval x = f x) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta w (↑l) d) (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) ≤
    ↑s * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑w))) +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Iterative reduction bound (relaxed form)
statement_informal:
Iterative reduction bound (relaxed form). Replacing the coefficient $s-1$ by $s$ in the previous bound: under the same hypotheses and
the parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le s(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.informal:

branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,

definitions.informal:

\[

definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},

Statement: Circuit reduction core bound. The user-facing form of the iterative reduction: for $f$ computed by a circuit of depth at
most $d\ge 2$, size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$, under $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[

  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.maxFanin : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => max cs.length (cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.maxFanin acc) 0)
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem circuit_reduction_core
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d s w : ℕ) (l t : ℕ)
    (hd2 : 2 ≤ d) (hs_pos : 0 < s) (hw_pos : 0 < w) (hl_pos : 0 < l) (hn : 0 < n)
    (h_circuit : ∃ c : Circuit n,
      c.depth ≤ d ∧ c.size ≤ s ∧ Circuit.maxFanin c ≤ w ∧ ∀ x, c.eval x = f x) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta w (↑l) d) (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) ≤
    ↑s * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑w))) +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Circuit reduction core bound
statement_informal:
Circuit reduction core bound. The user-facing form of the iterative reduction: for $f$ computed by a circuit of depth at
most $d\ge 2$, size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$, under $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le s(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Maximum fanin of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.

definitions.title: Size of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Depth-3 switching lemma, $\le \varepsilon$ version. For a depth-3 circuit in normal form, if $l$ and $t$ are chosen so that
$s_2 (1/2)^l \le \varepsilon/2$ and $(1/2)^t \le \varepsilon/2$, then under the
composed Bernoulli$(\mathrm{composedDelta}\,w\,l\,3)$ restriction (with $p_1 = 1/(40w)$,
$p_2 = 1/(40l)$),
\[
\Pr_\rho[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho) > t]
\le \varepsilon + s_2\exp(-np_1/3) + \exp(-np_2/3).
\]
The exponential tails vanish as $n \to \infty$, so the bound is $\le \varepsilon$
asymptotically.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem circuit_reduction_depth3_le_eps
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (s₂ : ℕ) (gates : Fin s₂ → DNF n) (w l t : ℕ)
    (h_f : ∀ x, f x = true ↔ ∀ i : Fin s₂, (gates i).eval x = true)
    (hw : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hnd : ∀ i, ∀ tm ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ tm, ∀ l₂ ∈ tm, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ i, ∀ tm ∈ gates i, tm.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n) (hl_pos : 0 < l)
    -- l and t are chosen so that the dominant terms sum to ≤ ε
    (ε : ℝ)
    (hl_bound : (s₂ : ℝ) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l ≤ ε / 2)
    (ht_bound : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t ≤ ε / 2) :
    let p₁ : ℝ := sorry
end LMN
title: Depth-3 switching lemma, $\le \varepsilon$ version
statement_informal:
Depth-3 switching lemma, $\le \varepsilon$ version. For a depth-3 circuit in normal form, if $l$ and $t$ are chosen so that
$s_2 (1/2)^l \le \varepsilon/2$ and $(1/2)^t \le \varepsilon/2$, then under the
composed Bernoulli$(\mathrm{composedDelta}\,w\,l\,3)$ restriction (with $p_1 = 1/(40w)$,
$p_2 = 1/(40l)$),
\[
\Pr_\rho[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\, f\,\rho) > t]
\le \varepsilon + s_2\exp(-np_1/3) + \exp(-np_2/3).
\]
The exponential tails vanish as $n \to \infty$, so the bound is $\le \varepsilon$
asymptotically.

Statement: Iterative reduction bound (tight form). By strong induction on the depth $d\ge 2$: if $f$ is computed by a circuit of depth at most
$d$, size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$ (with $s,w,l,n>0$), then under
$\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.maxFanin : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => max cs.length (cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.maxFanin acc) 0)
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem circuit_reduction_ind
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d s w : ℕ) (l t : ℕ)
    (hd2 : 2 ≤ d) (hs_pos : 0 < s) (hw_pos : 0 < w) (hl_pos : 0 < l)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (h_circuit : ∃ c : Circuit n,
      c.depth ≤ d ∧ c.size ≤ s ∧ Circuit.maxFanin c ≤ w ∧ ∀ x, c.eval x = f x) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta w (↑l) d) (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) ≤
    (↑s - 1) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t +
    (↑s - 1) * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑w))) +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Iterative reduction bound (tight form)
statement_informal:
Iterative reduction bound (tight form). By strong induction on the depth $d\ge 2$: if $f$ is computed by a circuit of depth at most
$d$, size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$ (with $s,w,l,n>0$), then under
$\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Maximum fanin of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.

definitions.title: Size of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:

- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree

the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value

- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor

- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via

unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction

- Maximum fanin of a circuit. The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.
- Size of a circuit. The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Inductive base case: depth-2 bound. The base case ($d=2$) of the inductive bound: if $f$ is computed by a depth-$2$ circuit of
size at most $s$ and fan-in at most $w$ (with $s,w,l>0$ and $n>0$), then under the composed
restriction parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,2)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.maxFanin : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => max cs.length (cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.maxFanin acc) 0)
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma circuit_reduction_ind_base
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (s w : ℕ) (l t : ℕ)
    (hs_pos : 0 < s) (hw_pos : 0 < w) (hl_pos : 0 < l)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (h_circuit : ∃ c : Circuit n,
      c.depth ≤ 2 ∧ c.size ≤ s ∧ Circuit.maxFanin c ≤ w ∧ ∀ x, c.eval x = f x) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta w (↑l) 2) (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) ≤
    (↑s - 1) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t +
    (↑s - 1) * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑w))) +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Inductive base case: depth-2 bound
statement_informal:
Inductive base case: depth-2 bound. The base case ($d=2$) of the inductive bound: if $f$ is computed by a depth-$2$ circuit of
size at most $s$ and fan-in at most $w$ (with $s,w,l>0$ and $n>0$), then under the composed
restriction parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,2)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]

restriction parameter is
\[

\]
