so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Maximum fanin of a circuit. The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.
- Size of a circuit. The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Inductive step: depth $d\ge 3$. The inductive step: for depth $d\ge 3$, assuming the bound holds for depth $d-1$ (the
hypothesis $\mathit{ih}$ applied to each child of the root), a circuit of depth at most $d$,
size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$ satisfies, under $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.maxFanin : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => max cs.length (cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.maxFanin acc) 0)
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma circuit_reduction_ind_step
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d s w : ℕ) (l t : ℕ)
    (hd3 : 3 ≤ d) (hs_pos : 0 < s) (hw_pos : 0 < w) (hl_pos : 0 < l)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (h_circuit : ∃ c : Circuit n,
      c.depth ≤ d ∧ c.size ≤ s ∧ Circuit.maxFanin c ≤ w ∧ ∀ x, c.eval x = f x)
    (ih : ∀ (g : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (s' : ℕ),
      0 < s' →
      (∃ c : Circuit n, c.depth ≤ d - 1 ∧ c.size ≤ s' ∧ Circuit.maxFanin c ≤ w ∧ ∀ x, c.eval x = g x) →
      bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta w (↑l) (d - 1)) (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn g ρ) > l) ≤
      (↑s' - 1) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l +
      (↑s' - 1) * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑w))) +
      ↑s' * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l)))) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta w (↑l) d) (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > t) ≤
    (↑s - 1) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t +
    (↑s - 1) * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑w))) +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Inductive step: depth $d\ge 3$
statement_informal:
Inductive step: depth $d\ge 3$. The inductive step: for depth $d\ge 3$, assuming the bound holds for depth $d-1$ (the
hypothesis $\mathit{ih}$ applied to each child of the root), a circuit of depth at most $d$,
size at most $s$, and fan-in at most $w$ satisfies, under $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$,
\[
  \Pr\!\left[\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) > t\right]
  \le (s-1)(1/2)^{l} + (1/2)^{t} + (s-1)\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120w}\right)
    + s\,\exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120l}\right).
\]

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Maximum fanin of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.

definitions.title: Size of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Tautological clause. A clause (a list of literals interpreted as their disjunction) is tautological if it
contains two literals on the same variable with opposite signs.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

Statement: A tautological clause evaluates to true. If a clause $c$ is tautological, then for every assignment $x$ the disjunction of its
literals evaluates to $\mathrm{true}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def clauseIsTaut (c : List (Literal n)) : Prop :=
  ∃ l₁ ∈ c, ∃ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var ∧ l₁.neg ≠ l₂.neg
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma clauseIsTaut_eval_true (c : List (Literal n)) (h : clauseIsTaut c)
    (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    c.any (fun l => l.eval x) = true := sorry
end LMN
title: A tautological clause evaluates to true
statement_informal:
A tautological clause evaluates to true. If a clause $c$ is tautological, then for every assignment $x$ the disjunction of its
literals evaluates to $\mathrm{true}$.

definitions.title: Tautological clause
definitions.informal:
A clause (a list of literals interpreted as their disjunction) is tautological if it
contains two literals on the same variable with opposite signs.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Tautological clause. A clause (a list of literals interpreted as their disjunction) is tautological if it
contains two literals on the same variable with opposite signs.
- Deduplicate clause variables. Removes duplicate variables from a clause, keeping the first occurrence of each
variable.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Clean a CNF. Cleans a CNF by removing all tautological clauses and then deduplicating the variables
within each remaining clause.

Statement: Cleaning preserves CNF evaluation. For every assignment $x$, the cleaned CNF $\mathrm{cleanCNF\_D3}\,\psi$ evaluates to the
same value as $\psi$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def clauseIsTaut (c : List (Literal n)) : Prop :=
  ∃ l₁ ∈ c, ∃ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var ∧ l₁.neg ≠ l₂.neg
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def dedupClauseVars (c : List (Literal n)) : List (Literal n) :=
  c.pwFilter (fun l₁ l₂ => decide (l₁.var ≠ l₂.var))
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def cleanCNF_D3 (ψ : CNF n) : CNF n :=
  (ψ.filter (fun c => ¬clauseIsTaut c)).map dedupClauseVars
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cleanCNF_D3_eval (ψ : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    CNF.eval (cleanCNF_D3 ψ) x = CNF.eval ψ x := sorry
end LMN
title: Cleaning preserves CNF evaluation
statement_informal:

same value as $\psi$.

assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and

within each remaining clause.

definitions.title: Tautological clause
definitions.informal:
A clause (a list of literals interpreted as their disjunction) is tautological if it
contains two literals on the same variable with opposite signs.

definitions.title: Deduplicate clause variables
definitions.informal:
Removes duplicate variables from a clause, keeping the first occurrence of each
variable.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Clean a CNF
definitions.informal:
Cleans a CNF by removing all tautological clauses and then deduplicating the variables
within each remaining clause.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).
- Clean a CNF. $\mathrm{cleanCNF}\,c$ filters out the contradictory (tautological) clauses of the CNF $c$
and then de-duplicates the literals of each remaining clause via $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$.

Statement: cleanCNF preserves evaluation. For every CNF $c$ and input $x$, cleaning preserves the computed function:
$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,(\mathrm{cleanCNF}\,c)\,x = \mathrm{CNF.eval}\,c\,x$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def dedupTermVar (t : Term n) : Term n :=
  t.foldr (fun l acc =>
    if acc.any (fun l' => decide (l'.var = l.var)) then acc
    else l :: acc) []
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def termHasContradiction (t : Term n) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l₁ => t.any (fun l₂ => decide (l₁.var = l₂.var) && decide (l₁.neg ≠ l₂.neg)))
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def cleanCNF (c : CNF n) : CNF n :=
  (c.filter (fun t => !termHasContradiction t)).map dedupTermVar
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cleanCNF_eval (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    CNF.eval (cleanCNF c) x = CNF.eval c x := sorry
end LMN

statement_informal:

$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,(\mathrm{cleanCNF}\,c)\,x = \mathrm{CNF.eval}\,c\,x$.

definitions.title: De-duplicate literals by variable
definitions.informal:
Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.

definitions.title: Contradictory term test
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).

definitions.title: Clean a DNF
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d$ first discards every contradictory term of the DNF $d$ and then
applies $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$ to each remaining term.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Clean a DNF. $\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d$ first discards every contradictory term of the DNF $d$ and then
applies $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$ to each remaining term.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: cleanDNF does not increase width. The cleaned DNF has width at most that of the original:
$(\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d).\mathrm{width} \le d.\mathrm{width}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def dedupTermVar (t : Term n) : Term n :=
  t.foldr (fun l acc =>
    if acc.any (fun l' => decide (l'.var = l.var)) then acc
    else l :: acc) []
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def termHasContradiction (t : Term n) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l₁ => t.any (fun l₂ => decide (l₁.var = l₂.var) && decide (l₁.neg ≠ l₂.neg)))
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def cleanDNF (d : DNF n) : DNF n :=
  (d.filter (fun t => !termHasContradiction t)).map dedupTermVar
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cleanDNF_width_le (d : DNF n) :
    (cleanDNF d).width ≤ d.width := sorry
end LMN
title: cleanDNF does not increase width
statement_informal:
cleanDNF does not increase width. The cleaned DNF has width at most that of the original:
$(\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d).\mathrm{width} \le d.\mathrm{width}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Concatenation of a list of lists. Flattens a list of lists $[\,\ell_1, \ell_2, \dots\,]$ into the single list
$\ell_1 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \ell_2 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \cdots$, defined by recursion on the
outer list. Used to merge the clause (or term) lists of several CNFs (or DNFs).
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

Statement: CNF concatenation evaluates as conjunction. For any input $x$, the value of the concatenated CNF at $x$ equals the conjunction over
all $\psi$ in the list of $\psi.\mathrm{eval}\,x$; that is, it holds iff every component
CNF holds at $x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def listConcat {α : Type u} : List (List α) → List α
  | [] => []
  | l :: ls => l ++ listConcat ls
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cnf_concat_eval (cnfs : List (CNF n)) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    CNF.eval (listConcat cnfs) x = cnfs.all (fun ψ => CNF.eval ψ x) := sorry
end LMN
title: CNF concatenation evaluates as conjunction
statement_informal:
CNF concatenation evaluates as conjunction. For any input $x$, the value of the concatenated CNF at $x$ equals the conjunction over
all $\psi$ in the list of $\psi.\mathrm{eval}\,x$; that is, it holds iff every component
CNF holds at $x$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Concatenation of a list of lists. Flattens a list of lists $[\,\ell_1, \ell_2, \dots\,]$ into the single list
$\ell_1 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \ell_2 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \cdots$, defined by recursion on the
outer list. Used to merge the clause (or term) lists of several CNFs (or DNFs).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: CNF concatenation preserves width. If CNFs $\psi_1, \dots, \psi_s$ each have width at most $\ell$, then the CNF obtained by
concatenating all their clause lists also has width at most $\ell$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def listConcat {α : Type u} : List (List α) → List α
  | [] => []
  | l :: ls => l ++ listConcat ls
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cnf_concat_width_le (cnfs : List (CNF n)) (l : ℕ)
    (h : ∀ ψ ∈ cnfs, CNF.width ψ ≤ l) :
    CNF.width (listConcat cnfs) ≤ l := sorry
end LMN
title: CNF concatenation preserves width
statement_informal:
CNF concatenation preserves width. If CNFs $\psi_1, \dots, \psi_s$ each have width at most $\ell$, then the CNF obtained by
concatenating all their clause lists also has width at most $\ell$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.

Statement: Pointwise characterization of composition. The composition $\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2$ equals $\sigma$ if and only if
for every coordinate $i$ the value $(\rho_1\,i)$, falling back to $(\rho_2\,i)$,
equals $\sigma\,i$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma composeRestr_eq_iff (ρ₁ ρ₂ σ : Restriction n) :
    composeRestr ρ₁ ρ₂ = σ ↔ ∀ i, (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i) = σ i := sorry
end LMN
title: Pointwise characterization of composition

Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first

Statement: Right identity for composition. Composing a restriction $\rho$ with the all-free restriction (which fixes no
variable) returns $\rho$ unchanged.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma composeRestr_id_right (ρ : Restriction n) :
    composeRestr ρ (fun _ => none) = ρ := sorry
end LMN
title: Right identity for composition
statement_informal:
Right identity for composition. Composing a restriction $\rho$ with the all-free restriction (which fixes no
variable) returns $\rho$ unchanged.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Composition of two restrictions
definitions.informal:
Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Per-variable Bernoulli weight. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$, the per-variable weight assigns $p$ to a free
coordinate (value \texttt{none}) and $(1-p)/2$ to a fixed coordinate (value
\texttt{some\_}), matching the marginal of the Bernoulli restriction model.
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

Statement: Fiber weight identity. Summing the product $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1 \cdot
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,q\,\rho_2$ over all pairs $(\rho_1, \rho_2)$ that
compose to $\sigma$ equals the Bernoulli weight of $\sigma$ at parameter $pq$:
\[
  \sum_{\rho_1}\sum_{\rho_2} \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1 \cdot
    \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,q\,\rho_2 \cdot
    [\,\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2 = \sigma\,]
  = \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,(pq)\,\sigma.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma compose_fiber_weight_eq (p q : ℝ) (σ : Restriction n) :
    ∑ ρ₁ : Restriction n, ∑ ρ₂ : Restriction n,
      bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ₁ * bernoulliRestrWeight q ρ₂ *
        (if composeRestr ρ₁ ρ₂ = σ then (1 : ℝ) else 0)
    = bernoulliRestrWeight (p * q) σ := sorry
end LMN
title: Fiber weight identity
statement_informal:
Fiber weight identity. Summing the product $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1 \cdot
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,q\,\rho_2$ over all pairs $(\rho_1, \rho_2)$ that
compose to $\sigma$ equals the Bernoulli weight of $\sigma$ at parameter $pq$:
\[
  \sum_{\rho_1}\sum_{\rho_2} \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1 \cdot
    \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,q\,\rho_2 \cdot
    [\,\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2 = \sigma\,]
  = \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,(pq)\,\sigma.
\]

definitions.title: Per-variable Bernoulli weight
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$, the per-variable weight assigns $p$ to a free
coordinate (value \texttt{none}) and $(1-p)/2$ to a fixed coordinate (value
\texttt{some\_}), matching the marginal of the Bernoulli restriction model.

assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction

For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

Statement: The composed parameter is at most one. For $w\ge 1$, $l\ge 1$, and $d\ge 2$, the composed restriction parameter satisfies
$\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)\le 1$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma composedDelta_le_one (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) (hw : 1 ≤ w) (hl : 1 ≤ l) (hd : 2 ≤ d) :
    composedDelta w l d ≤ 1 := sorry
end LMN
title: The composed parameter is at most one
statement_informal:
The composed parameter is at most one. For $w\ge 1$, $l\ge 1$, and $d\ge 2$, the composed restriction parameter satisfies
$\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)\le 1$.

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

Statement: Positivity of the composed parameter. For $w>0$ and $l>0$, the composed restriction parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$ is
positive.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma composedDelta_pos (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) (hw : 0 < w) (hl : 0 < l) :
    0 < composedDelta w l d := sorry
end LMN
title: Positivity of the composed parameter
statement_informal:
Positivity of the composed parameter. For $w>0$ and $l>0$, the composed restriction parameter $\mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d)$ is
positive.

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

Statement: Recursive step factorization (left). For natural numbers $w,l$ and depth $d\ge 3$,
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot \mathrm{composedDelta}(l,l,d-1).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma composedDelta_step (w l : ℕ) (d : ℕ) (hd : 3 ≤ d) :
    composedDelta w (↑l : ℝ) d = (1 / (40 * (↑w : ℝ))) * composedDelta l (↑l : ℝ) (d - 1) := sorry
end LMN
title: Recursive step factorization (left)
statement_informal:
Recursive step factorization (left). For natural numbers $w,l$ and depth $d\ge 3$,
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot \mathrm{composedDelta}(l,l,d-1).
\]

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

Statement: Recursive step factorization (right). For $d\ge 3$ and $l>0$, the composed parameter factors as the $(d-1)$ case times $1/(40l)$:
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d-1)\cdot\frac{1}{40l}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma composedDelta_step_right (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) (hd : 3 ≤ d) (hl : 0 < l) :
    composedDelta w l d = composedDelta w l (d - 1) * (1 / (40 * l)) := sorry
end LMN
title: Recursive step factorization (right)
statement_informal:
Recursive step factorization (right). For $d\ge 3$ and $l>0$, the composed parameter factors as the $(d-1)$ case times $1/(40l)$:
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d-1)\cdot\frac{1}{40l}.
\]

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Compression and switching for a node. Suppose $0 < l$, $0 < n$, and every child $c \in cs$ satisfies
$\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1) \le l$. Then under a further
Bernoulli random restriction $\rho_2$ with parameter $1/(40 l)$, the probability that the
restricted node $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs))\,(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)$
has decision-tree depth greater than $t$ is at most
\[
  \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^{t} + \exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120 l}\right).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma compress_and_switch (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit n))
    (ρ₁ : Restriction n) (l t : ℕ) (hl : 0 < l) (hn : 0 < n)
    (h_all : ∀ c ∈ cs, dtDepth (restrictFn c.eval ρ₁) ≤ l) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (1 / (40 * (↑l : ℝ)))
      (fun ρ₂ => dtDepth (restrictFn (Circuit.eval (Circuit.node isAnd cs)) (composeRestr ρ₁ ρ₂)) > t) ≤
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t + Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Compression and switching for a node
statement_informal:
Compression and switching for a node. Suppose $0 < l$, $0 < n$, and every child $c \in cs$ satisfies
$\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1) \le l$. Then under a further
Bernoulli random restriction $\rho_2$ with parameter $1/(40 l)$, the probability that the
restricted node $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs))\,(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)$
has decision-tree depth greater than $t$ is at most
\[
  \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^{t} + \exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120 l}\right).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Composition of two restrictions
definitions.informal:
Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Concatenation of a list of lists. Flattens a list of lists $[\,\ell_1, \ell_2, \dots\,]$ into the single list
$\ell_1 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \ell_2 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \cdots$, defined by recursion on the
outer list. Used to merge the clause (or term) lists of several CNFs (or DNFs).
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Circuit compression for CNFs under AND. Let an AND gate have a list of children, each of which computes a function expressible
by a CNF of width at most $\ell$. Then the AND of all children is itself computed by a
single CNF of width at most $\ell$, namely the concatenation of the children's clause
lists. This collapses the AND layer and the CNF layer into one.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem compression_and_of_cnfs
    (children : List ((Fin n → Bool) → Bool))
    (l : ℕ)
    (h_cnf : ∀ f ∈ children, ∃ ψ : CNF n, CNF.width ψ ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = f x) :
    ∃ ψ : CNF n, CNF.width ψ ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = children.all (fun f => f x) := sorry
end LMN

statement_informal:
Circuit compression for CNFs under AND. Let an AND gate have a list of children, each of which computes a function expressible
by a CNF of width at most $\ell$. Then the AND of all children is itself computed by a
single CNF of width at most $\ell$, namely the concatenation of the children's clause
lists. This collapses the AND layer and the CNF layer into one.

$\ell$, obtained by concatenating the children's term lists.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem compression_or_of_dnfs
    (children : List ((Fin n → Bool) → Bool))
    (l : ℕ)
    (h_dnf : ∀ f ∈ children, ∃ φ : DNF n, DNF.width φ ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, DNF.eval φ x = f x) :
    ∃ φ : DNF n, DNF.width φ ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, DNF.eval φ x = children.any (fun f => f x) := sorry
end LMN

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).

Statement: Contradictory clauses evaluate to true. If $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t = \texttt{true}$, then viewed as a clause $t$ is a
tautology: $\mathrm{CNF.evalClause}\,t\,x = \texttt{true}$ for every $x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def termHasContradiction (t : Term n) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l₁ => t.any (fun l₂ => decide (l₁.var = l₂.var) && decide (l₁.neg ≠ l₂.neg)))
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma contradiction_clause_eval_true (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool)
    (hc : termHasContradiction t = true) :
    CNF.evalClause t x = true := sorry
end LMN
title: Contradictory clauses evaluate to true
statement_informal:
Contradictory clauses evaluate to true. If $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t = \texttt{true}$, then viewed as a clause $t$ is a
tautology: $\mathrm{CNF.evalClause}\,t\,x = \texttt{true}$ for every $x$.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Contradictory term test
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).

Statement: Contradictory terms evaluate to false. If $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t = \texttt{true}$, then for every input $x$ the term
evaluates to false under conjunction: $\mathrm{Term.eval}\,t\,x = \texttt{false}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Tautological clause. A clause (a list of literals interpreted as their disjunction) is tautological if it
contains two literals on the same variable with opposite signs.
- Deduplicate clause variables. Removes duplicate variables from a clause, keeping the first occurrence of each
variable.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

Statement: Deduplication preserves evaluation of non-tautological clauses. If a clause $c$ is not tautological, then for every assignment $x$ the disjunction of
the literals of $\mathrm{dedupClauseVars}\,c$ has the same value as the disjunction of
the literals of $c$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def clauseIsTaut (c : List (Literal n)) : Prop :=
  ∃ l₁ ∈ c, ∃ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var ∧ l₁.neg ≠ l₂.neg
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def dedupClauseVars (c : List (Literal n)) : List (Literal n) :=
  c.pwFilter (fun l₁ l₂ => decide (l₁.var ≠ l₂.var))
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma dedupClauseVars_eval_of_not_taut (c : List (Literal n)) (h : ¬clauseIsTaut c)
    (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    (dedupClauseVars c).any (fun l => l.eval x) = c.any (fun l => l.eval x) := sorry
end LMN
title: Deduplication preserves evaluation of non-tautological clauses
statement_informal:
Deduplication preserves evaluation of non-tautological clauses. If a clause $c$ is not tautological, then for every assignment $x$ the disjunction of
the literals of $\mathrm{dedupClauseVars}\,c$ has the same value as the disjunction of
the literals of $c$.

assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

- Deduplicate clause variables. Removes duplicate variables from a clause, keeping the first occurrence of each

Statement: Deduplication does not increase length. The length of $\mathrm{dedupClauseVars}\,c$ is at most the length of $c$.

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.

Statement: De-duplication yields no duplicate literals. For every term $t$, the de-duplicated term $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ has no repeated
literals (it satisfies \texttt{Nodup}).

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def dedupTermVar (t : Term n) : Term n :=
  t.foldr (fun l acc =>
    if acc.any (fun l' => decide (l'.var = l.var)) then acc
    else l :: acc) []
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}

end LMN

literals (it satisfies \texttt{Nodup}).

variable already appeared.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Depth-$\le 2$ AND-top circuit to a CNF. Converts a list of children $cs$ of a top AND gate (of depth $\le 2$) into a CNF: each literal
child becomes a singleton clause, each OR child becomes the clause of its literals, and each
AND child contributes the singleton clauses of its literals.

Statement: depth2AndToCNF preserves evaluation. If the top AND node $\mathrm{node}\,\texttt{true}\,cs$ has depth at most $2$, then the CNF

import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def BoolCircuit.Lit.toLiteral {n : ℕ} (l : BoolCircuit.Lit n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.idx, !l.sign⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def depth2AndToCNF (cs : List (Circuit n)) : CNF n :=
  cs.flatMap (fun c =>
    match c with
    | .lit l => [[l.toLiteral]]
    | .node false cs' => [cs'.filterMap (fun c' =>
        match c' with
        | .lit l => some l.toLiteral
        | _ => none)]
    | .node true cs' => cs'.filterMap (fun c' =>
        match c' with
        | .lit l => some [l.toLiteral]
        | _ => none))
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma depth2AndToCNF_eval (cs : List (Circuit n))
    (hd : (Circuit.node true cs).depth ≤ 2)
    (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    CNF.eval (depth2AndToCNF cs) x = (Circuit.node true cs).eval x := sorry
end LMN
title: depth2AndToCNF preserves evaluation
statement_informal:
depth2AndToCNF preserves evaluation. If the top AND node $\mathrm{node}\,\texttt{true}\,cs$ has depth at most $2$, then the CNF
$\mathrm{depth2AndToCNF}\,cs$ computes the same Boolean function as the circuit:
$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,(\mathrm{depth2AndToCNF}\,cs)\,x = (\mathrm{node}\,\texttt{true}\,cs).\mathrm{eval}\,x$
for all $x$.

definitions.title: Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal
definitions.informal:
Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
switching-lemma $Literal\,n$, recording the same variable index and polarity.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Depth-$\le 2$ AND-top circuit to a CNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a list of children $cs$ of a top AND gate (of depth $\le 2$) into a CNF: each literal
child becomes a singleton clause, each OR child becomes the clause of its literals, and each
AND child contributes the singleton clauses of its literals.

- Convert a circuit literal to a switching-lemma literal. Maps a Boolean-circuit literal $l : BoolCircuit.Lit\,n$ to the corresponding
