Statement: Depth reduction for depth-1 circuits. A circuit $c$ of depth exactly $1$ over gates with width-$l$ DNF and CNF representations
collapses to a single signed gate: there exist a width-$l$ DNF $\varphi$ (with
duplicate-free, non-repeating-variable terms) and a sign bit whose signed evaluation
equals $c.\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ for all $x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma exists_circuit_depth_reduction_depth1
    {m : ℕ} (c : Circuit m) (gates : Fin m → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (l : ℕ) (h_depth : c.depth = 1)
    (hDNF : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, φ.eval x = gates i x)
    (hCNF : ∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = gates i x) :
    ∃ (φ : DNF n) (sign : Bool),
      φ.width ≤ l ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, t.Nodup) ∧
      (∀ x, (if sign then φ.eval x else !(φ.eval x)) = c.eval (fun i => gates i x)) := sorry
end LMN

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma exists_circuit_depth_reduction_depth2
    {m : ℕ} (c : Circuit m) (gates : Fin m → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (l : ℕ) (h_depth : c.depth = 2)
    (hDNF : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, φ.eval x = gates i x)

end LMN

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Tautological clause. A clause (a list of literals interpreted as their disjunction) is tautological if it
contains two literals on the same variable with opposite signs.
- Deduplicate clause variables. Removes duplicate variables from a clause, keeping the first occurrence of each
variable.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Clean a CNF. Cleans a CNF by removing all tautological clauses and then deduplicating the variables
within each remaining clause.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Every CNF has an equivalent nice CNF. For any CNF $\psi$ there exists a CNF $\psi'$ of width $\le$ that of $\psi$, evaluating
identically to $\psi$ on every input, with no duplicate literals in any clause and with
each clause variable-injective. Hence every CNF can be cleaned to satisfy the switching
lemma hypotheses.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem exists_nice_cnf_of_cnf (ψ : CNF n) :
    ∃ ψ' : CNF n, CNF.width ψ' ≤ CNF.width ψ ∧
    (∀ x, CNF.eval ψ' x = CNF.eval ψ x) ∧
    (∀ c ∈ ψ', c.Nodup) ∧
    (∀ c ∈ ψ', ∀ l₁ ∈ c, ∀ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) := sorry
end LMN
title: Every CNF has an equivalent nice CNF

Every CNF has an equivalent nice CNF. For any CNF $\psi$ there exists a CNF $\psi'$ of width $\le$ that of $\psi$, evaluating
identically to $\psi$ on every input, with no duplicate literals in any clause and with
each clause variable-injective. Hence every CNF can be cleaned to satisfy the switching
lemma hypotheses.

informal_statement:
Aggregating per-layer bounds. Given layer sizes summing to at most $s$, if each per-layer quantity is bounded by
$\mathrm{layerSize}(i)\cdot\alpha$ (with $\alpha\ge 0$) and a final term is bounded by $\beta$,
then the total $\sum_i \mathrm{per\_layer}(i) + \mathrm{final}$ is at most $s\cdot\alpha+\beta$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem full_iterative_bound (m : ℕ) (layerSize : Fin m → ℕ) (s : ℕ)
    (h_sum : ∑ i, layerSize i ≤ s) (α β : ℝ) (hα : 0 ≤ α)
    (per_layer : Fin m → ℝ) (h_per : ∀ i, per_layer i ≤ ↑(layerSize i) * α)
    (final : ℝ) (h_final : final ≤ β) :
    (∑ i, per_layer i) + final ≤ ↑s * α + β := sorry
end LMN
title: Aggregating per-layer bounds
statement_informal:
Aggregating per-layer bounds. Given layer sizes summing to at most $s$, if each per-layer quantity is bounded by
$\mathrm{layerSize}(i)\cdot\alpha$ (with $\alpha\ge 0$) and a final term is bounded by $\beta$,
then the total $\sum_i \mathrm{per\_layer}(i) + \mathrm{final}$ is at most $s\cdot\alpha+\beta$.

informal_statement:
Iterative dominant-term bound. For $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$ with $0<s$ and $0<\varepsilon$, setting
$l=\log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ gives
\[
\sum_i \bigl(\mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\bigr)\cdot (1/2)^l \le \varepsilon/2 .
\]

variable {n : ℕ}
theorem iterative_dominant_term_bound
    (m : ℕ) (layer_size : Fin m → ℕ) (s : ℕ)
    (h_sum : ∑ i, layer_size i ≤ s) (hs : 0 < s)
    (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) :
    let l := sorry
end LMN
title: Iterative dominant-term bound
statement_informal:
Iterative dominant-term bound. For $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$ with $0<s$ and $0<\varepsilon$, setting
$l=\log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ gives
\[
\sum_i \bigl(\mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\bigr)\cdot (1/2)^l \le \varepsilon/2 .
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Maximum fanin of a circuit. The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.
- Size of a circuit. The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Iterative circuit reduction bound with Chernoff tails. Let $c$ be a circuit on $n$ variables with $n > 0$, depth at most $d$, size at most
$s$, and fan-in at most $w$, where $d \ge 2$ and $s, w, l > 0$. Under a Bernoulli($\delta$)
random restriction $\rho$ with $\delta = \mathrm{composedDelta}\,w\,l\,d$, the probability
that the restricted function has decision-tree depth exceeding $t$ is bounded by
\[
  s \cdot \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^l + \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^t
  + s \cdot \exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120 w}\right)
  + s \cdot \exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120 l}\right).
\]
The exponential Chernoff tails vanish as $n \to \infty$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.maxFanin : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => max cs.length (cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.maxFanin acc) 0)
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma iterative_reduction_bound (c : Circuit n)
    (d s w : ℕ) (l t : ℕ)
    (hd : c.depth ≤ d) (hs : c.size ≤ s) (hw : c.maxFanin ≤ w)
    (hd2 : 2 ≤ d) (hs_pos : 0 < s) (hw_pos : 0 < w) (hl_pos : 0 < l)
    (hn : 0 < n) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta w (↑l) d)
      (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn (c.eval) ρ) > t) ≤
    ↑s * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑w))) +
    ↑s * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Iterative circuit reduction bound with Chernoff tails
statement_informal:
Iterative circuit reduction bound with Chernoff tails. Let $c$ be a circuit on $n$ variables with $n > 0$, depth at most $d$, size at most
$s$, and fan-in at most $w$, where $d \ge 2$ and $s, w, l > 0$. Under a Bernoulli($\delta$)
random restriction $\rho$ with $\delta = \mathrm{composedDelta}\,w\,l\,d$, the probability
that the restricted function has decision-tree depth exceeding $t$ is bounded by
\[
  s \cdot \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^l + \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^t
  + s \cdot \exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120 w}\right)
  + s \cdot \exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120 l}\right).
\]
The exponential Chernoff tails vanish as $n \to \infty$.

definitions.title: Composed restriction parameter
definitions.informal:
For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Depth of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Maximum fanin of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The maximum fanin of a circuit: a literal has fanin $0$, and a node's value is the
maximum of the number of its children and the maximal fanin occurring recursively among
its children.

definitions.title: Size of a circuit
definitions.informal:
The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

\[

\]

$(1-p)/2$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the

- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Simplified layer-2 CNF replaceability bound. Under the same hypotheses as the previous theorem, fix $\varepsilon>0$ and $s_2>0$, and suppose
$n$ is large enough that $\exp\!\bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\bigr)\le \tfrac{\varepsilon}{s_2}$. Then the
probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction $\rho$ that some layer-2 gate fails to admit a
width-$l$ $\mathrm{CNF}$ representation of $\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ is at most
\[
  s_2\cdot\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \varepsilon .
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem layer2_cnf_replaceability_simplified
    (gates : Fin s₂ → DNF n) (w l : ℕ)
    (hw : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hnd : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑w)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (ε : ℝ) (_hε : 0 < ε)
    (hn_large : Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3)) ≤ ε / ↑s₂)
    (hs₂_pos : 0 < s₂) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ => ∃ i : Fin s₂,
        ¬ ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, ψ.eval x = restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ x) ≤
    ↑s₂ * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + ε := sorry
end LMN
title: Simplified layer-2 CNF replaceability bound
statement_informal:
Simplified layer-2 CNF replaceability bound. Under the same hypotheses as the previous theorem, fix $\varepsilon>0$ and $s_2>0$, and suppose
$n$ is large enough that $\exp\!\bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\bigr)\le \tfrac{\varepsilon}{s_2}$. Then the
probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction $\rho$ that some layer-2 gate fails to admit a
width-$l$ $\mathrm{CNF}$ representation of $\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ is at most
\[
  s_2\cdot\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \varepsilon .
\]

Statement: Union bound for layer-2 CNF replaceability. Let $\mathrm{gates}_0,\dots,\mathrm{gates}_{s_2-1}$ be the layer-2 $\mathrm{DNF}$ gates, each of
width at most $w>0$ (with distinct variables per term and no duplicate literals), let $n>0$,
and let $0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ with $p\le 1$. Then the probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$
restriction $\rho$ that some gate fails to admit a width-$l$ $\mathrm{CNF}$ representation of
$\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ is at most
\[
  s_2\cdot\Bigl(\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr)\Bigr).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem layer2_cnf_replaceability_union_bound
    (gates : Fin s₂ → DNF n) (w l : ℕ)
    (hw : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hnd : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑w)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ => ∃ i : Fin s₂,
        ¬ ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, ψ.eval x = restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ x) ≤
    ↑s₂ * ((1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Union bound for layer-2 CNF replaceability
statement_informal:
Union bound for layer-2 CNF replaceability. Let $\mathrm{gates}_0,\dots,\mathrm{gates}_{s_2-1}$ be the layer-2 $\mathrm{DNF}$ gates, each of
width at most $w>0$ (with distinct variables per term and no duplicate literals), let $n>0$,
and let $0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ with $p\le 1$. Then the probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$
restriction $\rho$ that some gate fails to admit a width-$l$ $\mathrm{CNF}$ representation of
$\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ is at most
\[
  s_2\cdot\Bigl(\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr)\Bigr).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Composed restriction parameter. For a fan-in bound $w$, a width parameter $l$, and a depth $d$, the composed Bernoulli
restriction parameter is
\[
  \mathrm{composedDelta}(w,l,d) = \frac{1}{40w}\cdot\left(\frac{1}{40l}\right)^{d-2}.
\]
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Size of a circuit. The total number of nodes of a circuit (internal gates plus literal leaves): a literal
has size $1$ and a node has size $1$ plus the sum of the sizes of its children.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Layer-2 data. A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
each term of each gate distinct literals have distinct variables.

Statement: Layer-2 composed bound, base case. Base case ($d_{\mathrm{inner}} = 2$) of the composed layer-2 bound. Here the top circuit
$c_{\mathrm{top}}$ has depth $0$, hence is a single literal, so the composed function is one
DNF gate or its negation. Under a Bernoulli random restriction with parameter
$\mathrm{composedDelta}\,l\,l\,2$, the probability that the restricted function has
decision-tree depth exceeding $t$ is at most
\[
s_{\mathrm{rem}}\cdot\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^{l} + \Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^{t}
+ s_{\mathrm{rem}}\cdot e^{-n/(120 l)} + e^{-n/(120 l)},
\]
under the hypotheses $0 < l$, $0 < n$, $c_{\mathrm{top}}.\mathrm{depth}+2\le 2$,
$c_{\mathrm{top}}.\mathrm{size}\le s_{\mathrm{rem}}$, $\mathrm{data.width}\le l$, and
$0 < s_{\mathrm{rem}}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composedDelta (w : ℕ) (l : ℝ) (d : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  (1 / (40 * ↑w)) * (1 / (40 * l)) ^ (d - 2)
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.size : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 1
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.size + acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
structure Layer2Data (n : ℕ) where
  numGates : ℕ
  gates : Fin numGates → DNF n
  width : ℕ
  widthBound : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ width
  widthPos : 0 < width
  varInj : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂
  nodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma layer2_composed_bound_base (data : Layer2Data n) (c_top : Circuit data.numGates)
    (s_rem l t : ℕ) (hl_pos : 0 < l) (hn : 0 < n)
    (hd_depth : c_top.depth + 2 ≤ 2)
    (hs : c_top.size ≤ s_rem) (hwl : data.width ≤ l) (hs_pos : 0 < s_rem) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (composedDelta l (↑l) 2)
      (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn (fun x => c_top.eval (fun i => (data.gates i).eval x)) ρ) > t) ≤
    ↑s_rem * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t +
    ↑s_rem * Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) + Real.exp (-(↑n / (120 * ↑l))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Layer-2 composed bound, base case
statement_informal:
Layer-2 composed bound, base case. Base case ($d_{\mathrm{inner}} = 2$) of the composed layer-2 bound. Here the top circuit
$c_{\mathrm{top}}$ has depth $0$, hence is a single literal, so the composed function is one
DNF gate or its negation. Under a Bernoulli random restriction with parameter
$\mathrm{composedDelta}\,l\,l\,2$, the probability that the restricted function has
decision-tree depth exceeding $t$ is at most
\[
s_{\mathrm{rem}}\cdot\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^{l} + \Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^{t}
+ s_{\mathrm{rem}}\cdot e^{-n/(120 l)} + e^{-n/(120 l)},
\]
under the hypotheses $0 < l$, $0 < n$, $c_{\mathrm{top}}.\mathrm{depth}+2\le 2$,
$c_{\mathrm{top}}.\mathrm{size}\le s_{\mathrm{rem}}$, $\mathrm{data.width}\le l$, and
$0 < s_{\mathrm{rem}}$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Layer-2 data
definitions.informal:
A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
each term of each gate distinct literals have distinct variables.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Depth of a circuit. The depth of a circuit (longest root-to-leaf path): a literal has depth $0$ and a node
has depth $1$ plus the maximum depth among its children.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Signed DNFs for a list of children. Given a list $cs$ of circuits each of depth at most $1$, over gates with width-$l$ DNF and
CNF representations, there are families $\varphi_s$ of width-$l$ DNFs (with duplicate-free,
non-repeating-variable terms) and signs indexed by $\mathrm{Fin}\,cs.\mathrm{length}$ such
that, for each index $j$, the signed evaluation of $\varphi_s\,j$ matches
$(cs.\mathrm{get}\,j).\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ on every $x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.depth : Circuit n → Nat
  | .lit _ => 0
  | .node _ cs => 1 + cs.foldr (fun c acc => max c.depth acc) 0
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma list_child_signed_dnfs
    {m : ℕ} (cs : List (Circuit m)) (gates : Fin m → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (l : ℕ)
    (h_depth : ∀ c ∈ cs, c.depth ≤ 1)
    (hDNF : ∀ i, ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, φ.eval x = gates i x)
    (hCNF : ∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = gates i x) :
    ∃ (φs : Fin cs.length → DNF n) (signs : Fin cs.length → Bool),
      (∀ j, (φs j).width ≤ l) ∧
      (∀ j, ∀ t ∈ φs j, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ j, ∀ t ∈ φs j, t.Nodup) ∧
      (∀ j x, (if signs j then (φs j).eval x else !((φs j).eval x)) =
        (cs.get j).eval (fun i => gates i x)) := sorry
end LMN
title: Signed DNFs for a list of children
statement_informal:
Signed DNFs for a list of children. Given a list $cs$ of circuits each of depth at most $1$, over gates with width-$l$ DNF and
CNF representations, there are families $\varphi_s$ of width-$l$ DNFs (with duplicate-free,
non-repeating-variable terms) and signs indexed by $\mathrm{Fin}\,cs.\mathrm{length}$ such
that, for each index $j$, the signed evaluation of $\varphi_s\,j$ matches
$(cs.\mathrm{get}\,j).\mathrm{eval}\,(\lambda i,\ \mathrm{gates}\,i\,x)$ on every $x$.

informal_statement:
Monotonicity of $\mathrm{logb}_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ in $s$. For $s \in \bbn$ with $0 < s$ and $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon$,
we have $\log_2(2/\varepsilon) \le \log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma logb_2_div_eps_le_l (s : ℕ) (hs : 0 < s)
    (ε : ℝ) (hε_pos : 0 < ε) :
    Real.logb 2 (2 / ε) ≤ Real.logb 2 (2 * ↑s / ε) := sorry
end LMN
title: Monotonicity of $\mathrm{logb}_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ in $s$
statement_informal:
Monotonicity of $\mathrm{logb}_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ in $s$. For $s \in \bbn$ with $0 < s$ and $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon$,
we have $\log_2(2/\varepsilon) \le \log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$.

informal_statement:
Lower bound on $\mathrm{logb}_2(2s/\varepsilon)$. For $s \in \bbn$ with $0 < s$ and $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon \le 1$,
the quantity $l = \log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ satisfies $1 \le l$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma logb_2s_div_eps_pos (s : ℕ) (hs : 0 < s) (ε : ℝ) (hε_pos : 0 < ε) (hε_le : ε ≤ 1) :
    1 ≤ Real.logb 2 (2 * ↑s / ε) := sorry
end LMN
title: Lower bound on $\mathrm{logb}_2(2s/\varepsilon)$
statement_informal:
Lower bound on $\mathrm{logb}_2(2s/\varepsilon)$. For $s \in \bbn$ with $0 < s$ and $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon \le 1$,
the quantity $l = \log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ satisfies $1 \le l$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Merge of two gate arrays. Given gate arrays $g_1 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1 \to \alpha$ and
$g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2 \to \alpha$, the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,(m_1+m_2) \to \alpha$ takes its values
from $g_1$ on indices $0,\dots,m_1-1$ and from $g_2$ on indices
$m_1,\dots,m_1+m_2-1$.

Statement: Left projection of merge. For every $i : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1$, evaluating the merged array at the left-embedded index
$\mathrm{castAdd}\,m_2\,i$ recovers $g_1\,i$, i.e.
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2\,(\mathrm{castAdd}\,m_2\,i) = g_1\,i$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def mergeGates {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) : Fin (m₁ + m₂) → α :=
  fun j => if h : j.val < m₁ then g₁ ⟨j.val, h⟩ else g₂ ⟨j.val - m₁, by omega⟩
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma mergeGates_castAdd {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) (i : Fin m₁) :
    mergeGates g₁ g₂ (Fin.castAdd m₂ i) = g₁ i := sorry
end LMN
title: Left projection of merge
statement_informal:
Left projection of merge. For every $i : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1$, evaluating the merged array at the left-embedded index
$\mathrm{castAdd}\,m_2\,i$ recovers $g_1\,i$, i.e.
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2\,(\mathrm{castAdd}\,m_2\,i) = g_1\,i$.

Statement: Right projection of merge. For every $i : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2$, evaluating the merged array at the right-embedded index
$\mathrm{natAdd}\,m_1\,i$ recovers $g_2\,i$, i.e.
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2\,(\mathrm{natAdd}\,m_1\,i) = g_2\,i$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def mergeGates {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) : Fin (m₁ + m₂) → α :=
  fun j => if h : j.val < m₁ then g₁ ⟨j.val, h⟩ else g₂ ⟨j.val - m₁, by omega⟩
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma mergeGates_natAdd {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) (i : Fin m₂) :
    mergeGates g₁ g₂ (Fin.natAdd m₁ i) = g₂ i := sorry
end LMN
title: Right projection of merge
statement_informal:
Right projection of merge. For every $i : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2$, evaluating the merged array at the right-embedded index
$\mathrm{natAdd}\,m_1\,i$ recovers $g_2\,i$, i.e.
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2\,(\mathrm{natAdd}\,m_1\,i) = g_2\,i$.

definitions.title: Merge of two gate arrays
definitions.informal:
Given gate arrays $g_1 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1 \to \alpha$ and
$g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2 \to \alpha$, the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,(m_1+m_2) \to \alpha$ takes its values
from $g_1$ on indices $0,\dots,m_1-1$ and from $g_2$ on indices

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Merge of two gate arrays. Given gate arrays $g_1 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1 \to \alpha$ and
$g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2 \to \alpha$, the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,(m_1+m_2) \to \alpha$ takes its values
from $g_1$ on indices $0,\dots,m_1-1$ and from $g_2$ on indices
$m_1,\dots,m_1+m_2-1$.

Statement: No-duplication preserved under merging. If every term of every DNF gate of $g_1$ and of $g_2$ has no duplicate literals
($\mathrm{Nodup}$), then every term of every gate of the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2$ likewise has no duplicate literals.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def mergeGates {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) : Fin (m₁ + m₂) → α :=
  fun j => if h : j.val < m₁ then g₁ ⟨j.val, h⟩ else g₂ ⟨j.val - m₁, by omega⟩
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma mergeGates_nodup {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → DNF n) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → DNF n)
    (h₁ : ∀ k, ∀ t ∈ g₁ k, t.Nodup)
    (h₂ : ∀ k, ∀ t ∈ g₂ k, t.Nodup)
    (k : Fin (m₁ + m₂)) :
    ∀ t ∈ mergeGates g₁ g₂ k, t.Nodup := sorry
end LMN

statement_informal:
No-duplication preserved under merging. If every term of every DNF gate of $g_1$ and of $g_2$ has no duplicate literals
($\mathrm{Nodup}$), then every term of every gate of the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2$ likewise has no duplicate literals.

definitions.title: Merge of two gate arrays
definitions.informal:
Given gate arrays $g_1 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1 \to \alpha$ and
$g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2 \to \alpha$, the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,(m_1+m_2) \to \alpha$ takes its values

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Merge of two gate arrays. Given gate arrays $g_1 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1 \to \alpha$ and
$g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2 \to \alpha$, the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,(m_1+m_2) \to \alpha$ takes its values

$m_1,\dots,m_1+m_2-1$.

Statement: Variable injectivity preserved under merging. If, in each DNF gate of $g_1$ and of $g_2$, any two literals of a common term that share
the same variable are equal, then the same holds for every gate of the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2$.

import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def mergeGates {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) : Fin (m₁ + m₂) → α :=
  fun j => if h : j.val < m₁ then g₁ ⟨j.val, h⟩ else g₂ ⟨j.val - m₁, by omega⟩
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma mergeGates_varInj {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → DNF n) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → DNF n)
    (h₁ : ∀ k, ∀ t ∈ g₁ k, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (h₂ : ∀ k, ∀ t ∈ g₂ k, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (k : Fin (m₁ + m₂)) :
    ∀ t ∈ mergeGates g₁ g₂ k, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂ := sorry
end LMN
title: Variable injectivity preserved under merging
statement_informal:
Variable injectivity preserved under merging. If, in each DNF gate of $g_1$ and of $g_2$, any two literals of a common term that share
the same variable are equal, then the same holds for every gate of the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2$.

the merged array $\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2$ has width at most $l$.

import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def mergeGates {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) : Fin (m₁ + m₂) → α :=
  fun j => if h : j.val < m₁ then g₁ ⟨j.val, h⟩ else g₂ ⟨j.val - m₁, by omega⟩

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma mergeGates_width {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → DNF n) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → DNF n) (l : ℕ)
    (h₁ : ∀ k, (g₁ k).width ≤ l) (h₂ : ∀ k, (g₂ k).width ≤ l)
    (k : Fin (m₁ + m₂)) :
    (mergeGates g₁ g₂ k).width ≤ l := sorry
end LMN

the merged array $\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2$ has width at most $l$.

definitions.title: Merge of two gate arrays
definitions.informal:
Given gate arrays $g_1 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1 \to \alpha$ and
$g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_2 \to \alpha$, the merged array
$\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2 : \mathrm{Fin}\,(m_1+m_2) \to \alpha$ takes its values
from $g_1$ on indices $0,\dots,m_1-1$ and from $g_2$ on indices
$m_1,\dots,m_1+m_2-1$.

Statement: Width bound on the left part of a merge. If every $g_1\,k$ has width at most $l$, then for each $i : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1$ the merged
gate at the left-embedded index satisfies
$(\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2\,(\mathrm{castAdd}\,m_2\,i)).\mathrm{width} \le l$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def mergeGates {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → α) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → α) : Fin (m₁ + m₂) → α :=
  fun j => if h : j.val < m₁ then g₁ ⟨j.val, h⟩ else g₂ ⟨j.val - m₁, by omega⟩
end LMN

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma mergeGates_width_left {m₁ m₂ : ℕ}
    (g₁ : Fin m₁ → DNF n) (g₂ : Fin m₂ → DNF n) (l : ℕ)
    (h₁ : ∀ k, (g₁ k).width ≤ l) (i : Fin m₁) :
    (mergeGates g₁ g₂ (Fin.castAdd m₂ i)).width ≤ l := sorry
end LMN
title: Width bound on the left part of a merge
statement_informal:
Width bound on the left part of a merge. If every $g_1\,k$ has width at most $l$, then for each $i : \mathrm{Fin}\,m_1$ the merged
gate at the left-embedded index satisfies
$(\mathrm{mergeGates}\,g_1\,g_2\,(\mathrm{castAdd}\,m_2\,i)).\mathrm{width} \le l$.

open BoolCircuit
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}

  deriving DecidableEq

informal_statement:
Multi-stage failure bound. If over $m$ stages each failure bound satisfies
$\mathrm{failure\_bound}(i)\le (\mathrm{layer\_size}(i))\cdot\alpha$ with
$\alpha\ge 0$, and $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$, then
$\sum_i \mathrm{failure\_bound}(i)\le s\cdot\alpha$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem multi_stage_failure_bound
    (m : ℕ) (layer_size : Fin m → ℕ) (s : ℕ)
    (h_sum : ∑ i, layer_size i ≤ s)
    (α : ℝ) (hα : 0 ≤ α)
    (failure_bound : Fin m → ℝ)
    (h_bound : ∀ i, failure_bound i ≤ (layer_size i : ℝ) * α) :
    ∑ i, failure_bound i ≤ (s : ℝ) * α := sorry
end LMN
title: Multi-stage failure bound
statement_informal:
Multi-stage failure bound. If over $m$ stages each failure bound satisfies
$\mathrm{failure\_bound}(i)\le (\mathrm{layer\_size}(i))\cdot\alpha$ with
$\alpha\ge 0$, and $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$, then
$\sum_i \mathrm{failure\_bound}(i)\le s\cdot\alpha$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

Statement: Node evaluation under finRange re-indexing. If each re-indexed child $\mathrm{new\_cs}\,j$ evaluated at $g'$ agrees with the original
child $cs.\mathrm{get}\,j$ evaluated at $g$, then the node over $cs$ at $g$ equals the node
over $(\mathrm{List.finRange}\,cs.\mathrm{length}).\mathrm{map}\,\mathrm{new\_cs}$ at $g'$,
for the same flag $\mathit{isAnd}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma node_eval_eq_of_finRange_map {m M : ℕ}
    (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit m))
    (new_cs : Fin cs.length → Circuit M)
    (g : Fin m → Bool) (g' : Fin M → Bool)
    (heval : ∀ j, (new_cs j).eval g' = (cs.get j).eval g) :
    (Circuit.node isAnd cs).eval g =
    (Circuit.node isAnd ((List.finRange cs.length).map new_cs)).eval g' := sorry
end LMN
title: Node evaluation under finRange re-indexing
statement_informal:
Node evaluation under finRange re-indexing. If each re-indexed child $\mathrm{new\_cs}\,j$ evaluated at $g'$ agrees with the original
child $cs.\mathrm{get}\,j$ evaluated at $g$, then the node over $cs$ at $g$ equals the node
over $(\mathrm{List.finRange}\,cs.\mathrm{length}).\mathrm{map}\,\mathrm{new\_cs}$ at $g'$,
for the same flag $\mathit{isAnd}$.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Layer-2 data. A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
each term of each gate distinct literals have distinct variables.

Statement: One-step CNF replaceability for layer-2 data. For layer-2 data and a Bernoulli parameter $p$ with $0<p\le 1/(40\cdot\mathrm{width})$ and
$p\le 1$, the probability that it fails that every gate's restricted function is computed by
some width-$l$ CNF is at most
\[
  \mathrm{numGates}\cdot\left((1/2)^{l} + \exp(-np/3)\right).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
structure Layer2Data (n : ℕ) where
  numGates : ℕ
  gates : Fin numGates → DNF n
  width : ℕ
  widthBound : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ width
  widthPos : 0 < width
  varInj : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂
  nodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem normalform_one_step_cnf_replaceability (data : Layer2Data n) (l : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑data.width)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => ¬ ∀ i : Fin data.numGates,
        ∃ ψ : CNF n, CNF.width ψ ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = restrictFn (data.gates i).eval ρ x) ≤
    ↑data.numGates * ((1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3))) := sorry
end LMN
title: One-step CNF replaceability for layer-2 data
statement_informal:
One-step CNF replaceability for layer-2 data. For layer-2 data and a Bernoulli parameter $p$ with $0<p\le 1/(40\cdot\mathrm{width})$ and
$p\le 1$, the probability that it fails that every gate's restricted function is computed by
some width-$l$ CNF is at most
\[
  \mathrm{numGates}\cdot\left((1/2)^{l} + \exp(-np/3)\right).
\]

assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction

The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula

The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function

Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$

definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Layer-2 data
definitions.informal:
A bundle describing the second layer of a circuit over $n$ variables: a number of gates,
a family of DNF formulas indexed by the gates, a common width bound $\mathrm{width}>0$ such
that every gate's width is at most $\mathrm{width}$, together with the hypothesis that within
