informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.
- Boolean circuit tree. A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Evaluation of a circuit. Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

Statement: Restricted evaluation of a node. The restriction of the evaluation of a node circuit equals the conjunction or disjunction,
according to $\mathit{isAnd}$, of the restricted evaluations of its children. That is,
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs))\,\rho$ sends $x$
to the fold-$\mathrm{and}$ over $cs$ of $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho\,x$ when
$\mathit{isAnd}$ is true, and to the corresponding fold-$\mathrm{or}$ otherwise.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace BoolCircuit
structure Lit (n : Nat) where
  idx : Fin n
  sign : Bool
deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BoolCircuit
def Lit.eval (l : Lit n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.sign then x l.idx else !x l.idx
end BoolCircuit

namespace BoolCircuit
inductive Circuit (n : Nat) where
  | lit  : Lit n → Circuit n
  | node : (isAnd : Bool) → List (Circuit n) → Circuit n
deriving Repr
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace BoolCircuit
def Circuit.eval : Circuit n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .lit l, x => l.eval x
  | .node true cs, x  => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x && acc) true
  | .node false cs, x => cs.foldr (fun c acc => c.eval x || acc) false
end BoolCircuit

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma restrictFn_node_eval (isAnd : Bool) (cs : List (Circuit n)) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    restrictFn (Circuit.eval (Circuit.node isAnd cs)) ρ =
    fun x => if isAnd
      then cs.foldr (fun c acc => restrictFn c.eval ρ x && acc) true
      else cs.foldr (fun c acc => restrictFn c.eval ρ x || acc) false := sorry
end LMN
title: Restricted evaluation of a node
statement_informal:
Restricted evaluation of a node. The restriction of the evaluation of a node circuit equals the conjunction or disjunction,
according to $\mathit{isAnd}$, of the restricted evaluations of its children. That is,
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs))\,\rho$ sends $x$
to the fold-$\mathrm{and}$ over $cs$ of $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho\,x$ when
$\mathit{isAnd}$ is true, and to the corresponding fold-$\mathrm{or}$ otherwise.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ on $n$ variables and an assignment $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \bbf_2$,
the value $l.\mathrm{eval}\,x$ is $x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is positive
($l.\mathrm{sign} = \mathrm{true}$) and $\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{idx}}$ when $l$ is negated.

definitions.title: Boolean circuit tree
definitions.informal:
A Boolean circuit on $n$ variables is a tree whose leaves are literals
(\texttt{lit}\,$l$) and whose internal nodes (\texttt{node}\,$isAnd$\,$children$) are
AND gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{true}$ and OR gates when $isAnd = \mathrm{false}$,
applied to a list of child circuits. No alternation or deduplication constraint is
imposed.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a circuit
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a circuit under an assignment $x$: a literal evaluates via
$\mathrm{Lit.eval}$, an AND node is the conjunction (\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{\&\&},
unit \texttt{true}) of its children's values, and an OR node is the disjunction
(\texttt{foldr} with \texttt{||}, unit \texttt{false}).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Small decision-tree depth yields a small CNF. If the restricted function $f|_\rho$ has decision-tree depth at most $l$, then there exists a
$\mathrm{CNF}$ $\psi$ of width at most $l$ that computes $f|_\rho$ on every input.

  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem restricted_has_small_cnf_of_dtDepth_le
    (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) (l : ℕ)
    (h : dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) ≤ l) :
    (∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, ψ.eval x = restrictFn f ρ x) := sorry
end LMN
title: Small decision-tree depth yields a small CNF
statement_informal:
Small decision-tree depth yields a small CNF. If the restricted function $f|_\rho$ has decision-tree depth at most $l$, then there exists a
$\mathrm{CNF}$ $\psi$ of width at most $l$ that computes $f|_\rho$ on every input.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Composition of Bernoulli random restrictions, equality. For $0 < p \le 1$, $0 < q \le 1$ and any event on restrictions, the Bernoulli($pq$)
probability of the event equals the weighted average, over Bernoulli($p$)
restrictions $\rho_1$, of the Bernoulli($q$) probability that the event holds after
composing with $\rho_1$:
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,(pq)\,\mathrm{event}
  = \sum_{\rho_1} \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1 \cdot
    \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,\bigl(\rho_2 \mapsto \mathrm{event}(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)\bigr).
\]
This says a Bernoulli($p$) restriction followed by a Bernoulli($q$) restriction on
the remaining free variables has the same distribution as a single Bernoulli($pq$)
restriction.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem restriction_compose_eq (p q : ℝ) (hp : 0 < p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (hq : 0 < q) (hq1 : q ≤ 1)
    (event : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred event] :
    bernoulliRestrProb (p * q) event =
    ∑ ρ₁ : Restriction n, bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ₁ *
      bernoulliRestrProb q (fun ρ₂ => event (composeRestr ρ₁ ρ₂)) := sorry
end LMN
title: Composition of Bernoulli random restrictions, equality
statement_informal:
Composition of Bernoulli random restrictions, equality. For $0 < p \le 1$, $0 < q \le 1$ and any event on restrictions, the Bernoulli($pq$)
probability of the event equals the weighted average, over Bernoulli($p$)
restrictions $\rho_1$, of the Bernoulli($q$) probability that the event holds after
composing with $\rho_1$:
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,(pq)\,\mathrm{event}
  = \sum_{\rho_1} \mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1 \cdot
    \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,\bigl(\rho_2 \mapsto \mathrm{event}(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)\bigr).
\]
This says a Bernoulli($p$) restriction followed by a Bernoulli($q$) restriction on
the remaining free variables has the same distribution as a single Bernoulli($pq$)
restriction.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Composition of two restrictions
definitions.informal:
Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Composition inequality. For $0 < p \le 1$ and $0 < q \le 1$, the Bernoulli($pq$) probability of an event is
at most the Bernoulli($p$) probability that some $\rho_2$ makes the event hold after
composition:
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,(pq)\,\mathrm{event}
  \le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,\bigl(\rho_1 \mapsto \exists \rho_2,\;
      \mathrm{event}(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)\bigr).
\]

import Mathlib

open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem restriction_compose_le (p q : ℝ) (hp : 0 < p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (hq : 0 < q) (hq1 : q ≤ 1)
    (event : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred event] :
    bernoulliRestrProb (p * q) event ≤
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ₁ => ∃ ρ₂ : Restriction n, event (composeRestr ρ₁ ρ₂)) := sorry
end LMN
title: Composition inequality
statement_informal:
Composition inequality. For $0 < p \le 1$ and $0 < q \le 1$, the Bernoulli($pq$) probability of an event is

\[

  \le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,\bigl(\rho_1 \mapsto \exists \rho_2,\;
      \mathrm{event}(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)\bigr).
\]

informal_statement:
Bound $s \cdot 2^{-l} \le \varepsilon/2$. For $s \in \bbn$ with $0 < s$ and $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon$,
taking $l = \log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ gives
\[
  s \cdot 2^{-l} \le \frac{\varepsilon}{2},
\]
since $2^{-l} = \varepsilon/(2s)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma size_times_two_pow_neg_l_le (s : ℕ) (hs : 0 < s)
    (ε : ℝ) (hε_pos : 0 < ε) :
    (s : ℝ) * (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ Real.logb 2 (2 * ↑s / ε) ≤ ε / 2 := sorry
end LMN
title: Bound $s \cdot 2^{-l} \le \varepsilon/2$
statement_informal:
Bound $s \cdot 2^{-l} \le \varepsilon/2$. For $s \in \bbn$ with $0 < s$ and $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon$,
taking $l = \log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ gives
\[
  s \cdot 2^{-l} \le \frac{\varepsilon}{2},
\]
since $2^{-l} = \varepsilon/(2s)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Decision-tree depth at subsequent layers. Under the same hypotheses, a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction $\rho$ leaves every one of
the $s_i$ width-$\le l$ gates with restricted function of decision-tree depth at
most $l$, with probability at least
$1-s_i\cdot\bigl((1/2)^l+\exp(-np/3)\bigr)$. This is the decision-tree-depth form
of the one-step bound applied with width $l$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem subsequent_step_dtDepth
    (gates : Fin s_i → DNF n) (l : ℕ) (hl : 0 < l)
    (hw : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ l)
    (hnd : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑l)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ => ∀ i : Fin s_i, dtDepth (restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ) ≤ l) ≥
    1 - ↑s_i * ((1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3))) := sorry
end LMN

of the one-step bound applied with width $l$.

Statement: Reduction at subsequent layers. Let $g_1,\dots,g_{s_i}$ be width-$\le l$ DNFs on $n$ variables (with $0<l$,
$0<n$), and let $0<p\le 1/(40l)$ with $p\le 1$. Under a Bernoulli$(p)$ random
restriction $\rho$, the probability that some restricted gate fails to be
representable by a width-$\le l$ CNF agreeing with it pointwise is at most
$s_i\cdot\bigl((1/2)^l+\exp(-np/3)\bigr)$. This is the one-step reduction bound
applied with width parameter $l$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem subsequent_step_reduction
    (gates : Fin s_i → DNF n) (l : ℕ) (hl : 0 < l)
    (hw : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ l)
    (hnd : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑l)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ => ¬ ∀ i : Fin s_i,
        ∃ ψ : CNF n, CNF.width ψ ≤ l ∧
          ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ x) ≤
    ↑s_i * ((1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Reduction at subsequent layers

restriction $\rho$, the probability that some restricted gate fails to be
representable by a width-$\le l$ CNF agreeing with it pointwise is at most
$s_i\cdot\bigl((1/2)^l+\exp(-np/3)\bigr)$. This is the one-step reduction bound
applied with width parameter $l$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Clean a DNF. $\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d$ first discards every contradictory term of the DNF $d$ and then
applies $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$ to each remaining term.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Switched gates yield new bounded-width DNFs. Suppose for each gate $i$ the restricted function $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{gates}\,i).\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1$
has decision-tree depth at most $l$. Then there exist DNFs $\mathrm{gates}'$ such that each
$(\mathrm{gates}'\,i)$ has width at most $l$, evaluates to the corresponding restricted gate
function on every input, and is clean: within each term every literal is determined by its
variable (injectivity on variables) and each term has no duplicate literals.

abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=

end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

variable {n : ℕ}

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma switched_gates_give_new_dnfs
    (m : ℕ) (gates : Fin m → DNF n) (ρ₁ : Restriction n) (l : ℕ)
    (h_switch : ∀ i : Fin m, dtDepth (restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ₁) ≤ l) :
    ∃ (gates' : Fin m → DNF n),
      (∀ i, (gates' i).width ≤ l) ∧
      (∀ i, ∀ x, (gates' i).eval x = restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ₁ x) ∧
      (∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates' i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates' i, t.Nodup) := sorry
end LMN
title: Switched gates yield new bounded-width DNFs
statement_informal:
Switched gates yield new bounded-width DNFs. Suppose for each gate $i$ the restricted function $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{gates}\,i).\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1$
has decision-tree depth at most $l$. Then there exist DNFs $\mathrm{gates}'$ such that each
$(\mathrm{gates}'\,i)$ has width at most $l$, evaluates to the corresponding restricted gate
function on every input, and is clean: within each term every literal is determined by its
variable (injectivity on variables) and each term has no duplicate literals.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: De-duplicate literals by variable
definitions.informal:
Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.

definitions.title: Contradictory term test
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Clean a DNF
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d$ first discards every contradictory term of the DNF $d$ and then
applies $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$ to each remaining term.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Switched gates have DNF and CNF. If, after applying a restriction $\rho$, every gate function

    (m : ℕ) (gates : Fin m → DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) (l : ℕ)
    (h_switch : ∀ i, dtDepth (restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ) ≤ l) :

      (∀ x, φ.eval x = restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ x) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) ∧
      (∀ t ∈ φ, t.Nodup)) ∧
    (∀ i, ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧
      ∀ x, CNF.eval ψ x = restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ x) := sorry
end LMN
title: Switched gates have DNF and CNF
statement_informal:

most $l$, then each gate admits both a width-$l$ DNF representation (whose terms are
duplicate-free and contain no two literals on the same variable) and a width-$l$ CNF
representation, each computing the same function.

evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- De-duplicate literals by variable. Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Clean a CNF. $\mathrm{cleanCNF}\,c$ filters out the contradictory (tautological) clauses of the CNF $c$
and then de-duplicates the literals of each remaining clause via $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: General switching lemma for CNFs. Let $f$ be a CNF of width at most $w$ with $0<w$ and $0<n$, and let $p\in\bbr$ satisfy
$0<p$, $p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ and $p\le 1$. Then for every $t\in\bbn$,
\[
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,\bigl(\rho \mapsto \mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,f)\,\rho) > t\bigr)
\;\le\; \bigl(\tfrac12\bigr)^{t} + \exp\!\bigl(-np/3\bigr),
\]
again without any non-duplication hypothesis on $f$.

  var : Fin n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf_general (f : CNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (hw : CNF.width f ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hn : 0 < n) (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑w)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (t : ℕ) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn (CNF.eval f) ρ) > t) ≤
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3)) := sorry
end LMN
title: General switching lemma for CNFs
statement_informal:
General switching lemma for CNFs. Let $f$ be a CNF of width at most $w$ with $0<w$ and $0<n$, and let $p\in\bbr$ satisfy
$0<p$, $p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ and $p\le 1$. Then for every $t\in\bbn$,
\[
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,\bigl(\rho \mapsto \mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,f)\,\rho) > t\bigr)
\;\le\; \bigl(\tfrac12\bigr)^{t} + \exp\!\bigl(-np/3\bigr),
\]
again without any non-duplication hypothesis on $f$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: De-duplicate literals by variable
definitions.informal:
Given a term $t$ (a list of literals), $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}\,t$ keeps each literal only
if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.

definitions.title: Contradictory term test
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Clean a CNF
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{cleanCNF}\,c$ filters out the contradictory (tautological) clauses of the CNF $c$
and then de-duplicates the literals of each remaining clause via $\mathrm{dedupTermVar}$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

informal_statement:

if no earlier-retained literal already uses the same variable, removing literals whose
variable already appeared.
- Contradictory term test. $\mathrm{termHasContradiction}\,t$ returns \texttt{true} iff the term $t$ contains two
literals on the same variable with opposite polarities (one negated, one not).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[

evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Clean a DNF. $\mathrm{cleanDNF}\,d$ first discards every contradictory term of the DNF $d$ and then

probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the

Statement: General switching lemma for DNFs. Let $f$ be a DNF of width at most $w$ with $0<w$ and $0<n$, and let $p\in\bbr$ satisfy
$0<p$, $p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ and $p\le 1$. Then for every $t\in\bbn$,
\[
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,\bigl(\rho \mapsto \mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho) > t\bigr)
\;\le\; \bigl(\tfrac12\bigr)^{t} + \exp\!\bigl(-np/3\bigr).
\]
Unlike the base version, no syntactic non-duplication hypothesis is required, since the

formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf_general (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hn : 0 < n) (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑w)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (t : ℕ) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f.eval ρ) > t) ≤
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3)) := sorry
end LMN

\;\le\; \bigl(\tfrac12\bigr)^{t} + \exp\!\bigl(-np/3\bigr).

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)

This is the switching lemma stated at the level of functions rather than formulas.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.informal:

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_bernoulli_gate_to_cnf (g : DNF n) (w l : ℕ)
    (hw : g.width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ g, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ g, t.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑w)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ => ¬ ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ l ∧ ∀ x, ψ.eval x = restrictFn g.eval ρ x) ≤
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3)) := sorry
end LMN
title: Restricted DNF gate has a small CNF, with high probability
statement_informal:

\[
  \Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr).
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Union bound on gates with large decision-tree depth. Let $\mathrm{gates}_0,\dots,\mathrm{gates}_{s-1}$ be $s$ many $\mathrm{DNF}$s, each of width at
most $w>0$ (with distinct variables per term and no duplicate literals), let $n>0$, and let
$0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ with $p\le 1$. Then the probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction
$\rho$ that some gate $\mathrm{gates}_i$ has $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho) > l$ is
at most
\[
  s\cdot\Bigl(\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr)\Bigr).
\]
