    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_bernoulli_union_bound
    (gates : Fin s → DNF n) (w l : ℕ)
    (hw : ∀ i, (gates i).width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hnd : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ i, ∀ t ∈ gates i, t.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑w)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ => ∃ i : Fin s, dtDepth (restrictFn (gates i).eval ρ) > l) ≤
    ↑s * ((1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ l + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3))) := sorry
end LMN
title: Union bound on gates with large decision-tree depth
statement_informal:
Union bound on gates with large decision-tree depth. Let $\mathrm{gates}_0,\dots,\mathrm{gates}_{s-1}$ be $s$ many $\mathrm{DNF}$s, each of width at
most $w>0$ (with distinct variables per term and no duplicate literals), let $n>0$, and let
$0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ with $p\le 1$. Then the probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction
$\rho$ that some gate $\mathrm{gates}_i$ has $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho) > l$ is
at most
\[
  s\cdot\Bigl(\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr)\Bigr).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

informal_statement:
Value of $2^{-\mathrm{logb}_2(2/\varepsilon)}$. For $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon$,
\[
  2^{-\log_2(2/\varepsilon)} = \frac{\varepsilon}{2}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma two_pow_neg_logb_2_div_eps (ε : ℝ) (hε_pos : 0 < ε) :
    (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ Real.logb 2 (2 / ε) = ε / 2 := sorry
end LMN
title: Value of $2^{-\mathrm{logb}_2(2/\varepsilon)}$
statement_informal:
Value of $2^{-\mathrm{logb}_2(2/\varepsilon)}$. For $\varepsilon \in \bbr$ with $0 < \varepsilon$,
\[
  2^{-\log_2(2/\varepsilon)} = \frac{\varepsilon}{2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: General two-stage bound. Let $0 < p_1,p_2 \le 1$, let $E$ be an event on restrictions and $A$ a ``failure''
event, and let $\beta \ge 0$. If for every $\rho_1$ with $\lnot A(\rho_1)$ the
conditional probability $\Pr_{p_2}[E(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)] \le \beta$, then
$\Pr_{p_1 p_2}[E] \le \Pr_{p_1}[A] + \beta$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem two_stage_bound
    (p₁ p₂ : ℝ) (hp₁ : 0 < p₁) (hp₁_1 : p₁ ≤ 1) (hp₂ : 0 < p₂) (hp₂_1 : p₂ ≤ 1)
    (E : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred E]
    (A : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred A]
    (β : ℝ) (hβ : 0 ≤ β)
    (h_bound : ∀ ρ₁, ¬A ρ₁ →
      bernoulliRestrProb p₂ (fun ρ₂ => E (composeRestr ρ₁ ρ₂)) ≤ β) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (p₁ * p₂) E ≤ bernoulliRestrProb p₁ A + β := sorry
end LMN
title: General two-stage bound
statement_informal:
General two-stage bound. Let $0 < p_1,p_2 \le 1$, let $E$ be an event on restrictions and $A$ a ``failure''
event, and let $\beta \ge 0$. If for every $\rho_1$ with $\lnot A(\rho_1)$ the
conditional probability $\Pr_{p_2}[E(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)] \le \beta$, then
$\Pr_{p_1 p_2}[E] \le \Pr_{p_1}[A] + \beta$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Composition of two restrictions. Given two restrictions $\rho_1, \rho_2$ on $n$ variables, their composition is the
restriction that on each coordinate $i$ uses the value fixed by $\rho_1$ if there is
one, and otherwise the value given by $\rho_2$. This models applying $\rho_1$ first
and then $\rho_2$ on the variables left free by $\rho_1$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Two-stage probability bound. Let $p_1,p_2\in(0,1]$, let $E$ and $A$ be predicates on restrictions, and let $\beta\ge 0$.
If for every $\rho_1$ with $\neg A(\rho_1)$ the conditional probability
$\Pr_{p_2}[E(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)]\le\beta$, then
\[
  \Pr_{p_1 p_2}[E] \le \Pr_{p_1}[A] + \beta.
\]

import Mathlib

open Classical
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def composeRestr (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n) : Restriction n :=
  fun i => (ρ₁ i).orElse (fun _ => ρ₂ i)
end LMN

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem two_stage_bound'
    (p₁ p₂ : ℝ) (hp₁ : 0 < p₁) (hp₁_1 : p₁ ≤ 1) (hp₂ : 0 < p₂) (hp₂_1 : p₂ ≤ 1)
    (E : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred E]
    (A : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred A]
    (β : ℝ) (hβ : 0 ≤ β)
    (h_bound : ∀ ρ₁, ¬A ρ₁ →
      bernoulliRestrProb p₂ (fun ρ₂ => E (composeRestr ρ₁ ρ₂)) ≤ β) :
    bernoulliRestrProb (p₁ * p₂) E ≤ bernoulliRestrProb p₁ A + β := sorry
end LMN
title: Two-stage probability bound
statement_informal:
Two-stage probability bound. Let $p_1,p_2\in(0,1]$, let $E$ and $A$ be predicates on restrictions, and let $\beta\ge 0$.
If for every $\rho_1$ with $\neg A(\rho_1)$ the conditional probability
$\Pr_{p_2}[E(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)]\le\beta$, then
\[
  \Pr_{p_1 p_2}[E] \le \Pr_{p_1}[A] + \beta.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Two-stage composed union bound. For $0\le p\le 1$ and $0\le q\le 1$, and decidable events $A,B$ on restrictions,
\[
\sum_{\rho_1}\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1\cdot
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,(A(\rho_1)\vee B(\rho_2))
\le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,A + \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,B,
\]
where the inner event combines $A$ on the first-stage restriction with $B$ on the
second-stage restriction. This bounds the failure of a composed two-stage
Bernoulli restriction by the sum of the two individual stage probabilities.

import Mathlib

open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem two_stage_composed_union_bound
    (p q : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) (hq : 0 ≤ q) (hq1 : q ≤ 1)
    (A : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred A]
    (B : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred B] :
    ∑ ρ₁ : Restriction n, bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ₁ *
      bernoulliRestrProb q (fun ρ₂ => A ρ₁ ∨ B ρ₂) ≤
    bernoulliRestrProb p A + bernoulliRestrProb q B := sorry
end LMN
title: Two-stage composed union bound
statement_informal:
Two-stage composed union bound. For $0\le p\le 1$ and $0\le q\le 1$, and decidable events $A,B$ on restrictions,
\[
\sum_{\rho_1}\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1\cdot
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,(A(\rho_1)\vee B(\rho_2))
\le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,A + \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,B,
\]
where the inner event combines $A$ on the first-stage restriction with $B$ on the
second-stage restriction. This bounds the failure of a composed two-stage
Bernoulli restriction by the sum of the two individual stage probabilities.

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Per-variable Bernoulli weight. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$, the per-variable weight assigns $p$ to a free
coordinate (value \texttt{none}) and $(1-p)/2$ to a fixed coordinate (value
\texttt{some\_}), matching the marginal of the Bernoulli restriction model.

Statement: Per-variable composition identity. For parameters $p, q \in \bbr$ and any outcome $c$, summing the products
$\mathrm{varWeight}\,p\,a \cdot \mathrm{varWeight}\,q\,b$ over all pairs $(a,b)$ whose
composition equals $c$ gives $\mathrm{varWeight}\,(pq)\,c$:
\[
  \sum_{a} \sum_{b} \mathrm{varWeight}\,p\,a \cdot \mathrm{varWeight}\,q\,b \cdot
    [\,a \,\triangleright\, b = c\,] = \mathrm{varWeight}\,(pq)\,c.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2
open Classical in

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
def varWeight (p : ℝ) : Option Bool → ℝ
  | none => p
  | some _ => (1 - p) / 2
end LMN

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma varWeight_compose_sum (p q : ℝ) (c : Option Bool) :
    ∑ a : Option Bool, ∑ b : Option Bool,
      varWeight p a * varWeight q b *
        (if a.orElse (fun _ => b) = c then (1 : ℝ) else 0)
    = varWeight (p * q) c := sorry
end LMN
title: Per-variable composition identity
statement_informal:
Per-variable composition identity. For parameters $p, q \in \bbr$ and any outcome $c$, summing the products
$\mathrm{varWeight}\,p\,a \cdot \mathrm{varWeight}\,q\,b$ over all pairs $(a,b)$ whose
composition equals $c$ gives $\mathrm{varWeight}\,(pq)\,c$:
\[
  \sum_{a} \sum_{b} \mathrm{varWeight}\,p\,a \cdot \mathrm{varWeight}\,q\,b \cdot
    [\,a \,\triangleright\, b = c\,] = \mathrm{varWeight}\,(pq)\,c.
\]

definitions.title: Per-variable Bernoulli weight
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$, the per-variable weight assigns $p$ to a free
coordinate (value \texttt{none}) and $(1-p)/2$ to a fixed coordinate (value
\texttt{some\_}), matching the marginal of the Bernoulli restriction model.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Bounded width iff all terms bounded. For a list of terms $ts$, the folded maximum of their widths is at most $\ell$ if and
only if every term $t \in ts$ satisfies $t.\mathrm{width} \le \ell$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open BoolCircuit SwitchingLemma2 SwitchingBernoulli LMN
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace LMN
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma width_le_iff_forall {ts : List (Term n)} {l : ℕ} :
    (ts.map Term.width).foldr max 0 ≤ l ↔ ∀ t ∈ ts, t.width ≤ l := sorry
end LMN
title: Bounded width iff all terms bounded
statement_informal:
Bounded width iff all terms bounded. For a list of terms $ts$, the folded maximum of their widths is at most $\ell$ if and
only if every term $t \in ts$ satisfies $t.\mathrm{width} \le \ell$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

Statement: flipNeg negates the literal value. $(l.\mathrm{flipNeg}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = \lnot\,(l.\mathrm{eval}\,x)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

lemma Literal.flipNeg_eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    l.flipNeg.eval x = !(l.eval x) := sorry
title: flipNeg negates the literal value
statement_informal: flipNeg negates the literal value. $(l.\mathrm{flipNeg}).\mathrm{eval}\,x = \lnot\,(l.\mathrm{eval}\,x)$.

import Mathlib

structure Literal (n : ℕ) where

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

Statement: Cauchy--Schwarz for the Boolean inner product. For $f, g : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \le \big(\E[f^2]\big)^{1/2}\,\big(\E[g^2]\big)^{1/2}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
lemma cauchy_schwarz_bool {n : ℕ} (f g : BooleanFunc n) :
    innerProduct f g ≤
    (expect (fun x => f x ^ 2)) ^ ((1:ℝ)/2) * (expect (fun x => g x ^ 2)) ^ ((1:ℝ)/2) := sorry
end OneBit
title: Cauchy--Schwarz for the Boolean inner product
statement_informal:
Cauchy--Schwarz for the Boolean inner product. For $f, g : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \le \big(\E[f^2]\big)^{1/2}\,\big(\E[g^2]\big)^{1/2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

Statement: Expected $p$-th power of $|f|$ on one bit. For a one-bit function $f$, writing $a = \widehat{f}(\emptyset)$ and $b = \widehat{f}(\{0\})$,
\[
  \E\big[|f|^p\big] = \frac{|a+b|^p + |a-b|^p}{2}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
lemma expect_abs_rpow_one_bit (p : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc 1) :
    BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => |f x| ^ p) =
    (|fourierCoeff f ∅ + fourierCoeff f {⟨0, by omega⟩}| ^ p +
     |fourierCoeff f ∅ - fourierCoeff f {⟨0, by omega⟩}| ^ p) / 2 := sorry
end OneBit
title: Expected $p$-th power of $|f|$ on one bit
statement_informal:
Expected $p$-th power of $|f|$ on one bit. For a one-bit function $f$, writing $a = \widehat{f}(\emptyset)$ and $b = \widehat{f}(\{0\})$,
\[
  \E\big[|f|^p\big] = \frac{|a+b|^p + |a-b|^p}{2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Expected square of the noised one-bit function. For a one-bit function $f$ and noise rate $\rho$,
\[
  \E\big[(T_\rho f)^2\big] = \widehat{f}(\emptyset)^2 + \rho^2\,\widehat{f}(\{0\})^2 .
\]

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
lemma expect_noiseOp_sq_one_bit (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc 1) :
    BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ f x) ^ 2) =
    (fourierCoeff f ∅) ^ 2 + ρ ^ 2 * (fourierCoeff f {⟨0, by omega⟩}) ^ 2 := sorry
end OneBit
title: Expected square of the noised one-bit function
statement_informal:
Expected square of the noised one-bit function. For a one-bit function $f$ and noise rate $\rho$,
\[
  \E\big[(T_\rho f)^2\big] = \widehat{f}(\emptyset)^2 + \rho^2\,\widehat{f}(\{0\})^2 .
\]

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

Statement: Expectation of a nonnegative function is nonnegative. If $f \ge 0$ pointwise, then $\E[f] \ge 0$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]

Statement: H\"older sharpness. For H\"older conjugate exponents $(p, q)$ and any function $u$, there exists a function $f$ with
$\norm{f}_p \le 1$ and $\norm{u}_q \le \langle f, u\rangle$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S

open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
lemma holder_sharpness {n : ℕ} {p q : ℝ}
    (hpq : Real.HolderConjugate p q)
    (u : BooleanFunc n) :
    ∃ f : BooleanFunc n,
    (expect (fun x => |f x| ^ p)) ^ (1 / p) ≤ 1 ∧
    (expect (fun x => |u x| ^ q)) ^ (1 / q) ≤ innerProduct f u := sorry
end OneBit
title: H\"older sharpness
statement_informal:
H\"older sharpness. For H\"older conjugate exponents $(p, q)$ and any function $u$, there exists a function $f$ with
$\norm{f}_p \le 1$ and $\norm{u}_q \le \langle f, u\rangle$.

Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

Statement: $L^p$ norm monotonicity (power-mean inequality). For $1 \le r \le s$ and any $f : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \big(\E[|f|^r]\big)^{1/r} \le \big(\E[|f|^s]\big)^{1/s}.
\]
This is the power-mean inequality for the uniform probability measure on the cube.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g

namespace BooleanAnalysis

end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}

end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis

end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
lemma lp_norm_mono {n : ℕ} (r s : ℝ) (hr : 1 ≤ r) (hrs : r ≤ s)
    (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    (expect (fun x => |f x| ^ r)) ^ (1/r) ≤
    (expect (fun x => |f x| ^ s)) ^ (1/s) := sorry
end OneBit
title: $L^p$ norm monotonicity (power-mean inequality)
statement_informal:
$L^p$ norm monotonicity (power-mean inequality). For $1 \le r \le s$ and any $f : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \big(\E[|f|^r]\big)^{1/r} \le \big(\E[|f|^s]\big)^{1/s}.
\]
This is the power-mean inequality for the uniform probability measure on the cube.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

$2^{-n}$ to each point.

\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

informal_statement:
Noise operator does not increase the absolute-value $L^2$ contribution. For real $a, b$ and $0 \le \rho \le 1$, writing $u = a+b$ and $v = a-b$,
\[
  a^2 + \rho^2 b^2 \le \Big(\tfrac{|u| + |v|}{2}\Big)^2 + \rho^2 \Big(\tfrac{|u| - |v|}{2}\Big)^2 .
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace OneBit
lemma noise_l2_abs_mono (a b ρ : ℝ) (hρ0 : 0 ≤ ρ) (hρ1 : ρ ≤ 1) :
    a ^ 2 + ρ ^ 2 * b ^ 2 ≤
    ((|a + b| + |a - b|) / 2) ^ 2 + ρ ^ 2 * ((|a + b| - |a - b|) / 2) ^ 2 := sorry
end OneBit
title: Noise operator does not increase the absolute-value $L^2$ contribution
statement_informal:
Noise operator does not increase the absolute-value $L^2$ contribution. For real $a, b$ and $0 \le \rho \le 1$, writing $u = a+b$ and $v = a-b$,
\[
  a^2 + \rho^2 b^2 \le \Big(\tfrac{|u| + |v|}{2}\Big)^2 + \rho^2 \Big(\tfrac{|u| - |v|}{2}\Big)^2 .
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Noise operator duality. If, for H\"older conjugate exponents $p$ and $p'$ with $1 \le p$, the noise operator with rate
$\sqrt{p-1}$ satisfies the $(p,2)$-hypercontractive bound for every one-bit function, then it also
satisfies the dual $(2,p')$-hypercontractive bound: for every $g$,
\[
  \big(\E[|T_{\sqrt{p-1}} g|^{p'}]\big)^{1/p'} \le \big(\E[g^2]\big)^{1/2}.
\]

import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
theorem noise_operator_duality
  {p p_conj : ℝ}
  (hp_conj : Real.HolderConjugate p p_conj)
  (h_p1 : 1 ≤ p)
  (h_hyp : ∀ f : BooleanFunc 1,
    (expect (fun x => |noiseOp (Real.sqrt (p - 1)) f x| ^ (2 : ℝ))) ^ (1 / 2 : ℝ) ≤
    (expect (fun x => |f x| ^ p)) ^ (1 / p)) :
  ∀ g : BooleanFunc 1,
    (expect (fun x => |noiseOp (Real.sqrt (p - 1)) g x| ^ p_conj)) ^ (1 / p_conj) ≤
    (expect (fun x => |g x| ^ (2 : ℝ))) ^ (1 / 2 : ℝ) := sorry
end OneBit
title: Noise operator duality
statement_informal:
Noise operator duality. If, for H\"older conjugate exponents $p$ and $p'$ with $1 \le p$, the noise operator with rate
$\sqrt{p-1}$ satisfies the $(p,2)$-hypercontractive bound for every one-bit function, then it also
satisfies the dual $(2,p')$-hypercontractive bound: for every $g$,
\[
  \big(\E[|T_{\sqrt{p-1}} g|^{p'}]\big)^{1/p'} \le \big(\E[g^2]\big)^{1/2}.
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[

with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability

\[

Statement: One-bit $(2,q)$-hypercontractivity. For $g : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,1 \to \bbr$ and $q \ge 2$,

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
theorem one_bit_2q_hypercontractivity (q : ℝ) (hq2 : 2 ≤ q) (g : BooleanFunc 1) :
    (expect (fun x => |noiseOp (1 / Real.sqrt (q - 1)) g x| ^ q)) ^ (1 / q) ≤
    (expect (fun x => |g x| ^ (2 : ℝ))) ^ (1 / 2 : ℝ) := sorry
end OneBit

\[
  \norm{T_{1/\sqrt{q-1}}\, g}_q \le \norm{g}_2 .
\]

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace OneBit
lemma one_bit_val_true (f : BooleanFunc 1) :
    f (fun _ => true) = fourierCoeff f ∅ - fourierCoeff f {⟨0, by omega⟩} := sorry
end OneBit
title: One-bit value at $\mathit{true}$
statement_informal:
One-bit value at $\mathit{true}$. For a one-bit function $f$, its value on the all-$\mathit{true}$ input is the difference of
its two Fourier coefficients:
$f(\mathit{true}) = \widehat{f}(\emptyset) - \widehat{f}(\{0\})$.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

informal_statement: Sign times value equals absolute value. For all real $x$, $\operatorname{sign}(x)\cdot x = |x|$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace OneBit
private lemma sign_mul_self (x : ℝ) : Real.sign x * x = |x| := sorry
end OneBit
title: Sign times value equals absolute value
statement_informal: Sign times value equals absolute value. For all real $x$, $\operatorname{sign}(x)\cdot x = |x|$.

informal_statement:
Two-point inequality (full version). For $1 \le p \le 2$, all $a, b \in \bbr$, and $\rho \ge 0$ with $\rho^2 \le p-1$,
\[
  \big(a^2 + \rho^2 b^2\big)^{1/2} \le \Big(\frac{|a+b|^p + |a-b|^p}{2}\Big)^{1/p}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace OneBit
theorem two_point_ineq (a b p ρ : ℝ) (hp1 : 1 ≤ p) (hp2 : p ≤ 2)
    (hρ0 : 0 ≤ ρ) (hρ : ρ ^ 2 ≤ p - 1) :
    (a ^ 2 + ρ ^ 2 * b ^ 2) ^ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ≤
    ((|a + b| ^ p + |a - b| ^ p) / 2) ^ ((1 : ℝ) / p) := sorry
end OneBit
title: Two-point inequality (full version)
statement_informal:
Two-point inequality (full version). For $1 \le p \le 2$, all $a, b \in \bbr$, and $\rho \ge 0$ with $\rho^2 \le p-1$,
\[
  \big(a^2 + \rho^2 b^2\big)^{1/2} \le \Big(\frac{|a+b|^p + |a-b|^p}{2}\Big)^{1/p}.
\]

informal_statement:
Two-point inequality: the $a=0$ case. For $1 \le p \le 2$ one has $(p-1)^{p/2} \le 1$, which yields the two-point inequality in the
case $a = 0$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace OneBit
lemma two_point_ineq_a_zero (p : ℝ) (hp1 : 1 ≤ p) (hp2 : p ≤ 2) :
    (p - 1) ^ (p / 2) ≤ 1 := sorry
end OneBit
title: Two-point inequality: the $a=0$ case
statement_informal:
Two-point inequality: the $a=0$ case. For $1 \le p \le 2$ one has $(p-1)^{p/2} \le 1$, which yields the two-point inequality in the
case $a = 0$.

informal_statement:
Two-point inequality on the unit interval. For $1 \le p \le 2$ and $0 \le b \le 1$,
\[
  \big(1 + (p-1)b^2\big)^{p/2} \le \frac{(1+b)^p + (1-b)^p}{2}.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
open BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami SimpleHypercontractivity

namespace OneBit
theorem two_point_ineq_unit (b p : ℝ) (hp1 : 1 ≤ p) (hp2 : p ≤ 2)
    (hb0 : 0 ≤ b) (hb1 : b ≤ 1) :
    (1 + (p - 1) * b ^ 2) ^ (p / 2) ≤ ((1 + b) ^ p + (1 - b) ^ p) / 2 := sorry
end OneBit
title: Two-point inequality on the unit interval
statement_informal:
Two-point inequality on the unit interval. For $1 \le p \le 2$ and $0 \le b \le 1$,
\[
  \big(1 + (p-1)b^2\big)^{p/2} \le \frac{(1+b)^p + (1-b)^p}{2}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Embedding of a non-identity Pauli. Embeds a non-identity Pauli ($X$, $Y$, or $Z$) from the three-element type
$\mathrm{PauliNZ}$ into the full Pauli basis $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$.

Statement: Embedded Paulis are non-identity. For every $a \in \mathrm{PauliNZ}$, its image $a.\mathrm{toBasis}$ in the Pauli basis is not the
identity element $I$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

def PauliNZ.toBasis : PauliNZ → PauliBasis
| .X => .X
| .Y => .Y
| .Z => .Z

lemma PauliNZ.toBasis_ne_I : ∀ a : PauliNZ, a.toBasis ≠ PauliBasis.I := sorry
title: Embedded Paulis are non-identity
statement_informal:
Embedded Paulis are non-identity. For every $a \in \mathrm{PauliNZ}$, its image $a.\mathrm{toBasis}$ in the Pauli basis is not the
identity element $I$.

definitions.title: Embedding of a non-identity Pauli
definitions.informal:
Embeds a non-identity Pauli ($X$, $Y$, or $Z$) from the three-element type
$\mathrm{PauliNZ}$ into the full Pauli basis $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$.

informal_statement:
Binomial coefficient bound. For natural numbers $k \ge 1$ and $j \le k$,
\[
  \binom{2k}{2j} \le \binom{k}{j}\,(2k-1)^j.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma binom_coeff_ineq (k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) (j : ℕ) (hj : j ≤ k) :
    Nat.choose (2 * k) (2 * j) ≤ Nat.choose k j * (2 * k - 1) ^ j := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Binomial coefficient bound
statement_informal:
Binomial coefficient bound. For natural numbers $k \ge 1$ and $j \le k$,
\[
  \binom{2k}{2j} \le \binom{k}{j}\,(2k-1)^j.
\]

informal_statement:
Cardinality of a lifted set. Lifting a set along $\mathrm{castSucc}$ preserves its cardinality:
$|\mathrm{castSucc}(S)| = |S|$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma card_image_castSucc {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    (S.image Fin.castSucc).card = S.card := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Cardinality of a lifted set
statement_informal:
Cardinality of a lifted set. Lifting a set along $\mathrm{castSucc}$ preserves its cardinality:
$|\mathrm{castSucc}(S)| = |S|$.

informal_statement:
Cardinality of a lifted set with the last coordinate. Adjoining the (necessarily new) last coordinate to a lifted set increases its
cardinality by one:
$|\mathrm{castSucc}(S)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}| = |S| + 1$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma card_image_castSucc_union_last {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    (S.image Fin.castSucc ∪ {Fin.last n}).card = S.card + 1 := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Cardinality of a lifted set with the last coordinate
statement_informal:
Cardinality of a lifted set with the last coordinate. Adjoining the (necessarily new) last coordinate to a lifted set increases its
cardinality by one:
$|\mathrm{castSucc}(S)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}| = |S| + 1$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

Statement: Character of a lifted set ignores the last coordinate. For a subset $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, a point $x \in \{0,1\}^n$, and a bit $b$,
the parity character of the lifted set equals the character of $S$ on $x$:
\[
  \chi_{\,\mathrm{castSucc}(S)}(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b) = \chi_S(x).
\]
That is, appending a last coordinate $b$ and not including it in the set leaves the
character unchanged.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma chiS_snoc_castSucc {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) (x : BoolCube n) (b : Bool) :
    chiS (S.image Fin.castSucc) (Fin.snoc x b) = chiS S x := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Character of a lifted set ignores the last coordinate
statement_informal:
Character of a lifted set ignores the last coordinate. For a subset $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, a point $x \in \{0,1\}^n$, and a bit $b$,
the parity character of the lifted set equals the character of $S$ on $x$:
\[
  \chi_{\,\mathrm{castSucc}(S)}(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b) = \chi_S(x).
\]
That is, appending a last coordinate $b$ and not including it in the set leaves the
character unchanged.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

Statement: Character of a lifted set with the last coordinate. For $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, a point $x$, and a bit $b$, adjoining the last
coordinate to the lifted set multiplies the character by the sign of $b$:
\[
  \chi_{\,\mathrm{castSucc}(S)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}}(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b)
  = (-1)^{b}\,\chi_S(x),
\]
where $(-1)^b$ denotes boolToSign\,$b$.

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma chiS_snoc_with_last {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) (x : BoolCube n) (b : Bool) :
    chiS (S.image Fin.castSucc ∪ {Fin.last n}) (Fin.snoc x b) = boolToSign b * chiS S x := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Character of a lifted set with the last coordinate
statement_informal:
Character of a lifted set with the last coordinate. For $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, a point $x$, and a bit $b$, adjoining the last
coordinate to the lifted set multiplies the character by the sign of $b$:
\[
  \chi_{\,\mathrm{castSucc}(S)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}}(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b)
  = (-1)^{b}\,\chi_S(x),
\]
where $(-1)^b$ denotes boolToSign\,$b$.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Contractivity ($q = 2$ case). For $\rho^2 \le 1$ and any Boolean function $f$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^2] \le \E[f^2].
\]
This is the $q = 2$ case of hypercontractivity.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
theorem contractivity (ρ : ℝ) (hρ : ρ ^ 2 ≤ 1) (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ f x) ^ 2) ≤ expect (fun x => f x ^ 2) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Contractivity ($q = 2$ case)
statement_informal:
Contractivity ($q = 2$ case). For $\rho^2 \le 1$ and any Boolean function $f$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^2] \le \E[f^2].
\]
This is the $q = 2$ case of hypercontractivity.

  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma expect_sq_noiseOp_nonneg (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    0 ≤ BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ f x) ^ 2) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Nonnegativity of the expected square of $T_\rho f$
statement_informal: Nonnegativity of the expected square of $T_\rho f$. For any $\rho$ and Boolean function $f$, $\E[(T_\rho f)^2] \ge 0$.

informal_statement:
Partition of a sum over subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}(n+1)$. For any $\varphi$ on subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}(n+1)$, the total sum splits according to
whether the last coordinate belongs to the subset:
\[
  \sum_{S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}(n+1)} \varphi(S)
  = \sum_{T \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \varphi(\mathrm{castSucc}(T))
  + \sum_{T \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \varphi(\mathrm{castSucc}(T)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}).
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma finset_fin_succ_sum_partition {n : ℕ} (φ : Finset (Fin (n + 1)) → ℝ) :
    ∑ S : Finset (Fin (n + 1)), φ S =
    ∑ T : Finset (Fin n), φ (T.image Fin.castSucc) +
    ∑ T : Finset (Fin n), φ (T.image Fin.castSucc ∪ {Fin.last n}) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Partition of a sum over subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}(n+1)$
statement_informal:
Partition of a sum over subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}(n+1)$. For any $\varphi$ on subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}(n+1)$, the total sum splits according to
whether the last coordinate belongs to the subset:
\[
  \sum_{S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}(n+1)} \varphi(S)
  = \sum_{T \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \varphi(\mathrm{castSucc}(T))
  + \sum_{T \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \varphi(\mathrm{castSucc}(T)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}).
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Fourth moment decomposition with the noise operator. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and
$h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the fourth moment of $T_\rho f$ decomposes as
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^4]
  = \E[(T_\rho g)^4] + 6\rho^2\,\E[(T_\rho g)^2 (T_\rho h)^2] + \rho^4\,\E[(T_\rho h)^4].
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x + restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x - restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma fourth_moment_noise_decomp {n : ℕ} (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) :
    expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ f x) ^ 4) =
    expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ (avgLast f) x) ^ 4) +
    6 * ρ ^ 2 * expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ (avgLast f) x) ^ 2 * (noiseOp ρ (diffLast f) x) ^ 2) +
    ρ ^ 4 * expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ (diffLast f) x) ^ 4) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Fourth moment decomposition with the noise operator
statement_informal:
Fourth moment decomposition with the noise operator. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and
$h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the fourth moment of $T_\rho f$ decomposes as
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^4]
  = \E[(T_\rho g)^4] + 6\rho^2\,\E[(T_\rho g)^2 (T_\rho h)^2] + \rho^4\,\E[(T_\rho h)^4].
\]

\[

\]

\[

Statement: $(2,2)$-Hypercontractivity. For $\rho^2 \le 1$ and any Boolean function $f$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^2] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^1,

the $q = 2$ instance, which is just contractivity.
