import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
theorem hypercontractivity_2_2 (ρ : ℝ) (hρ : ρ ^ 2 ≤ 1) (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ f x) ^ 2) ≤ (expect (fun x => f x ^ 2)) ^ 1 := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: $(2,2)$-Hypercontractivity
statement_informal:
$(2,2)$-Hypercontractivity. For $\rho^2 \le 1$ and any Boolean function $f$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^2] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^1,
\]
the $q = 2$ instance, which is just contractivity.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: $(2,2k)$-Hypercontractivity (Bonami--Beckner). For any Boolean function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$, integer $k \ge 1$, and noise
parameter $\rho$ with $\rho^2 \le 1/(2k-1)$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^{2k}] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^k,
\]
equivalently $\norm{T_\rho f}_{2k} \le \norm{f}_2$.

namespace BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis

  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: $(2,4)$-Hypercontractivity (Bonami--Beckner). For any Boolean function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$ and noise parameter $\rho$ with
$\rho^2 \le 1/3$ (equivalently $\abs{\rho} \le 1/\sqrt3$),
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^4] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^2,
\]
that is $\norm{T_\rho f}_4 \le \norm{f}_2$.

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction of the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Average over the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

definitions.title: Half-difference over the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

informal_statement:

\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\

- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]

- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

\[

- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\

- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).

- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle

\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via

\[

Statement: $(2,q)$-Hypercontractivity for even $q$. For an even integer $q \ge 2$ and noise parameter $\rho$ with $\rho^2 \le 1/(q-1)$,

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
theorem hypercontractivity_4_div_3_2 {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    (expect (fun x => (noiseOp (1 / Real.sqrt 3) f x) ^ 2)) ^ (1/2 : ℝ)
    ≤ (expect (fun x => |f x| ^ (4/3 : ℝ))) ^ (3/4 : ℝ) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: $(4/3,2)$-Hypercontractivity with $\rho = 1/\sqrt3$
statement_informal:
$(4/3,2)$-Hypercontractivity with $\rho = 1/\sqrt3$. Boolean functions are $(4/3, 2)$-hypercontractive with parameter $1/\sqrt3$: for any
$f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \bigl(\E[(T_{1/\sqrt3} f)^2]\bigr)^{1/2}
  \le \bigl(\E[\,\abs{f}^{4/3}\,]\bigr)^{3/4}.
\]

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

informal_statement:
Key algebraic recurrence inequality. Suppose $a,b \ge 0$, $B \ge 0$, $A \le a^2$, $B \le b^2$, $C^2 \le AB$, and
$\rho^2 \le 1/3$. Then
\[
  A + 6\rho^2 C + \rho^4 B \le (a+b)^2.
\]
This is the algebraic step that closes the induction in the $(2,4)$ theorem.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma hypercontractivity_algebra' {a b A B C ρ : ℝ}
    (ha : 0 ≤ a) (hb : 0 ≤ b) (hB_nn : 0 ≤ B)
    (hA_bound : A ≤ a ^ 2) (hB_bound : B ≤ b ^ 2)
    (hC_bound : C ^ 2 ≤ A * B) (hρ : ρ ^ 2 ≤ 1 / 3) :
    A + 6 * ρ ^ 2 * C + ρ ^ 4 * B ≤ (a + b) ^ 2 := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Key algebraic recurrence inequality
statement_informal:
Key algebraic recurrence inequality. Suppose $a,b \ge 0$, $B \ge 0$, $A \le a^2$, $B \le b^2$, $C^2 \le AB$, and
$\rho^2 \le 1/3$. Then
\[
  A + 6\rho^2 C + \rho^4 B \le (a+b)^2.
\]
This is the algebraic step that closes the induction in the $(2,4)$ theorem.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: $(p,2)$-Hypercontractivity via duality. Let $1 < p$, $q \ge 2$ with $1/p + 1/q = 1$. Given a Hölder inequality for the pair
$(p,q)$ and a $(2,q)$-hypercontractivity bound for $T_\rho$, one concludes the dual
$(p,2)$ bound:
\[
  \bigl(\E[(T_\rho f)^2]\bigr)^{1/2} \le \bigl(\E[\,\abs{f}^p\,]\bigr)^{1/p}.
\]

open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
theorem hypercontractivity_p_2_general
    {ρ : ℝ} {p q : ℝ}
    (hp : 1 < p) (hq : 2 ≤ q)
    (hpq : 1/p + 1/q = 1)
    (f : BooleanFunc n)
    (holder : ∀ (u v : BooleanFunc n),
      innerProduct u v ≤
      (BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => |u x| ^ p)) ^ (1/p) *
      (BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => |v x| ^ q)) ^ (1/q))
    (hyp_2_q : ∀ (g : BooleanFunc n),
      BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => |noiseOp ρ g x| ^ q) ≤
      (BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => g x ^ 2)) ^ (q/2)) :
    (BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ f x) ^ 2)) ^ (1/2 : ℝ) ≤
    (BooleanAnalysis.expect (fun x => |f x| ^ p)) ^ (1/p) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: $(p,2)$-Hypercontractivity via duality
statement_informal:
$(p,2)$-Hypercontractivity via duality. Let $1 < p$, $q \ge 2$ with $1/p + 1/q = 1$. Given a Hölder inequality for the pair
$(p,q)$ and a $(2,q)$-hypercontractivity bound for $T_\rho$, one concludes the dual
$(p,2)$ bound:
\[
  \bigl(\E[(T_\rho f)^2]\bigr)^{1/2} \le \bigl(\E[\,\abs{f}^p\,]\bigr)^{1/p}.
\]

Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

\[

\[

Statement: Inner product equals expected square. The self inner product of a Boolean function equals the expectation of its square:
$\langle f, f \rangle = \E[f^2]$.

variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma noiseOp_compose (ρ σ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    noiseOp ρ (noiseOp σ f) = noiseOp (ρ * σ) f := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Composition of noise operators
statement_informal:
Composition of noise operators. Composing two noise operators multiplies their parameters:
$T_\rho(T_\sigma f) = T_{\rho\sigma} f$.

definitions.informal:

definitions.title: Boolean function

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Sign encoding. The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Walsh character. The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- $L^2$ inner product. The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Fourier coefficient. The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
- Noise operator. The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

Statement: Noise operator decomposes along the last coordinate. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$ the noise operator splits over the last coordinate:
\[
  T_\rho f(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b)
  = T_\rho(\mathrm{avgLast}\,f)(x) + (-1)^b\,\rho\,T_\rho(\mathrm{diffLast}\,f)(x),
\]
where $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ are the average and difference of
$f$ over the last bit.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x + restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x - restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma noiseOp_snoc {n : ℕ} (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (x : BoolCube n) (b : Bool) :
    noiseOp ρ f (Fin.snoc x b) =
    noiseOp ρ (avgLast f) x + boolToSign b * ρ * noiseOp ρ (diffLast f) x := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: Noise operator decomposes along the last coordinate
statement_informal:
Noise operator decomposes along the last coordinate. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$ the noise operator splits over the last coordinate:
\[
  T_\rho f(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b)
  = T_\rho(\mathrm{avgLast}\,f)(x) + (-1)^b\,\rho\,T_\rho(\mathrm{diffLast}\,f)(x),
\]
where $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ are the average and difference of
$f$ over the last bit.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma noise_l2_fourier (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) :
    innerProduct (noiseOp ρ f) (noiseOp ρ f) =
    ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), (ρ ^ S.card) ^ 2 * BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff f S ^ 2 := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: $L^2$ norm of $T_\rho f$ in Fourier space
statement_informal:

\[
  \langle T_\rho f, T_\rho f \rangle
  = \sum_{S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \rho^{2\abs{S}}\,\widehat{f}(S)^2.
\]

Statement: $q$-th moment decomposition with the noise operator. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = T_\rho(\mathrm{avgLast}\,f)$ and
$h = T_\rho(\mathrm{diffLast}\,f)$, the $q$-th moment of $T_\rho f$ decomposes as
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^q]
  = \E\!\left[\frac{(g + \rho h)^q + (g - \rho h)^q}{2}\right].
\]

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000
open scoped BigOperators
scoped notation "⟪" f ", " g "⟫_𝔹" => innerProduct f g
scoped notation "χ_[" S "]" => chiS S
scoped notation f "̂(" S ")" => fourierCoeff f S
open BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter BooleanAnalysis
open MeasureTheory Set Filter ProbabilityTheory BooleanAnalysis Real
open MeasureTheory Set Filter BooleanAnalysis Real Bonami
set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
def boolToSign (b : Bool) : ℝ := if b then -1 else 1
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def uniformWeight (n : ℕ) : ℝ := (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ n
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def chiS (S : Finset (Fin n)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∏ i ∈ S, boolToSign (x i)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def innerProduct (f g : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  expect (fun x ↦ f x * g x)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x + restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x - restrictLast f true x) / 2
end Bonami

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def fourierCoeff (f : BooleanFunc n) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ℝ :=
  innerProduct f (chiS S)
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def noiseOp (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc n) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x ↦ ∑ S : Finset (Fin n), ρ ^ S.card * fourierCoeff f S * chiS S x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma noise_qth_moment_decomp (q : ℕ) (ρ : ℝ) (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) :
    expect (fun x => (noiseOp ρ f x) ^ q) =
    expect (fun x' => ((noiseOp ρ (avgLast f) x' + ρ * noiseOp ρ (diffLast f) x') ^ q +
                       (noiseOp ρ (avgLast f) x' - ρ * noiseOp ρ (diffLast f) x') ^ q) / 2) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: $q$-th moment decomposition with the noise operator
statement_informal:
$q$-th moment decomposition with the noise operator. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = T_\rho(\mathrm{avgLast}\,f)$ and
$h = T_\rho(\mathrm{diffLast}\,f)$, the $q$-th moment of $T_\rho f$ decomposes as
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^q]
  = \E\!\left[\frac{(g + \rho h)^q + (g - \rho h)^q}{2}\right].
\]

definitions.title: Boolean hypercube
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Sign encoding
definitions.informal:
The \emph{sign encoding} maps $\mathrm{Bool}$ to $\{-1,1\}\subset\bbr$:
\[
  \sigma(b) \;=\; \begin{cases} 1 & b = \texttt{false},\\
                                -1 & b = \texttt{true}. \end{cases}
\]

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function
definitions.informal: A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.

definitions.title: Walsh character
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Walsh--Fourier character} associated to $S\subseteq[n]$ is
\[
  \chi_S(x) \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}(-1)^{x_i}
  \;=\; \prod_{i\in S}\sigma(x_i).
\]
The family $\{\chi_S\}_{S\subseteq[n]}$ forms an orthonormal basis for
$L^2(\{0,1\}^n,\mathrm{uniform})$.

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction of the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.

definitions.title: $L^2$ inner product
definitions.informal:
The \emph{$L^2$ inner product} of two Boolean functions with respect to the
uniform measure:
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \;=\; \E[f\cdot g]
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,g(x).
\]

definitions.title: Average over the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

definitions.title: Half-difference over the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

definitions.title: Fourier coefficient
definitions.informal:
The \emph{Fourier--Walsh coefficient} of $f$ at frequency $S$ is
\[
  \hat f(S) \;=\; \langle f, \chi_S\rangle
  \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x)\,\chi_S(x).
\]

definitions.title: Noise operator
definitions.informal:
The \emph{noise operator} $T_\rho$ with parameter $\rho\in[-1,1]$, defined via
the Fourier domain by
\[
  T_\rho f(x) \;=\; \sum_{S\subseteq[n]} \rho^{|S|}\,\hat f(S)\,\chi_S(x).
\]
Probabilistically, $T_\rho f(x) = \E_y[f(y)]$ where each bit of $y$ agrees
with $x_i$ with probability $\tfrac{1+\rho}{2}$ and is flipped with probability
$\tfrac{1-\rho}{2}$, independently.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Boolean hypercube. The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.
- Uniform weight. The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.
- Boolean function. A \emph{Boolean function} of arity $n$ is a function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$.
- Expectation. The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]
- Restriction of the last coordinate. Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
- Average over the last coordinate. The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
- Half-difference over the last coordinate. The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

Statement: $q$-th moment decomposition along the last coordinate. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and
$h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the $q$-th moment decomposes as
\[
  \E[f^q] = \E\!\left[\frac{(g+h)^q + (g-h)^q}{2}\right].
\]

abbrev BoolCube (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}

end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
abbrev BooleanFunc (n : ℕ) := BoolCube n → ℝ
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace BooleanAnalysis
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def expect (f : BooleanFunc n) : ℝ :=
  uniformWeight n * ∑ x : BoolCube n, f x
end BooleanAnalysis

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def restrictLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) (b : Bool) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => f (Fin.snoc x b)

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def avgLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=
  fun x => (restrictLast f false x + restrictLast f true x) / 2

namespace Bonami
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
noncomputable def diffLast {n : ℕ} (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) : BooleanFunc n :=

namespace SimpleHypercontractivity
lemma qth_moment_decomp (q : ℕ) (f : BooleanFunc (n + 1)) :
    expect (fun x => f x ^ q) =
    expect (fun x' => ((avgLast f x' + diffLast f x') ^ q +
                       (avgLast f x' - diffLast f x') ^ q) / 2) := sorry
end SimpleHypercontractivity
title: $q$-th moment decomposition along the last coordinate
statement_informal:
$q$-th moment decomposition along the last coordinate. For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and
$h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the $q$-th moment decomposes as
\[

The \emph{Boolean hypercube} of dimension $n$ is $\{0,1\}^n$,
represented as the function type $\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$.

definitions.title: Uniform weight
definitions.informal:
The uniform probability measure on $\{0,1\}^n$ assigns weight
$2^{-n}$ to each point.

definitions.title: Boolean function

definitions.title: Expectation
definitions.informal:
The \emph{expectation} of $f$ under the uniform measure:
\[
  \E[f] \;=\; 2^{-n}\sum_{x\in\{0,1\}^n} f(x).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction of the last coordinate
definitions.informal:
Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.

The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Number of $s$-restrictions. The number of restrictions on $n$ variables leaving exactly $s$ free, namely
$\binom{n}{s}\, 2^{\,n-s}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Restrictions with a fixed number of free variables. For natural numbers $n$ and $k$, this is the finite set of restrictions on $n$ variables
whose set of free variables has cardinality exactly $k$.

Statement: Cardinality of fixed-size restrictions. For $k \le n$, the set of restrictions of $\mathrm{Fin}\,n$ leaving exactly $k$
variables free has cardinality $\binom{n}{k}\,2^{\,n-k}$, i.e.\ it equals
$\mathrm{numSRestrictions}\,n\,k$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open SwitchingLemma2
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
open SwitchingLemma2 BernoulliCost
open Classical in

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def numSRestrictions (n s : ℕ) : ℕ := n.choose s * 2 ^ (n - s)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace BernoulliCost
variable {n : ℕ}
def fixedSizeRestrs (n k : ℕ) : Finset (Restriction n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun ρ : Restriction n => ρ.freeVars.card = k)
end BernoulliCost

namespace SwitchingBernoulli
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma fixedSizeRestrs_card (k : ℕ) (hk : k ≤ n) :
    (fixedSizeRestrs n k).card = numSRestrictions n k := sorry
end SwitchingBernoulli
title: Cardinality of fixed-size restrictions
statement_informal:
Cardinality of fixed-size restrictions. For $k \le n$, the set of restrictions of $\mathrm{Fin}\,n$ leaving exactly $k$
variables free has cardinality $\binom{n}{k}\,2^{\,n-k}$, i.e.\ it equals
$\mathrm{numSRestrictions}\,n\,k$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Number of $s$-restrictions
definitions.informal:
The number of restrictions on $n$ variables leaving exactly $s$ free, namely
$\binom{n}{s}\, 2^{\,n-s}$.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Restrictions with a fixed number of free variables
definitions.informal:
For natural numbers $n$ and $k$, this is the finite set of restrictions on $n$ variables
whose set of free variables has cardinality exactly $k$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Restrictions with a fixed number of free variables. For natural numbers $n$ and $k$, this is the finite set of restrictions on $n$ variables
whose set of free variables has cardinality exactly $k$.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

Statement: Bad fixed-size restrictions match the counting filter. For a function $f$ and parameters $d, k$, the number of size-$k$ restrictions
$\rho$ with $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$ equals the number of restrictions
that are $k$-restrictions and are bad for $f$ at depth $d$ in the counting
switching-lemma sense.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open SwitchingLemma2
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
open SwitchingLemma2 BernoulliCost
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace BernoulliCost
variable {n : ℕ}
def fixedSizeRestrs (n k : ℕ) : Finset (Restriction n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun ρ : Restriction n => ρ.freeVars.card = k)
end BernoulliCost

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingBernoulli
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma fixedSizeRestrs_filter_bad_eq (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d k : ℕ) :
    ((fixedSizeRestrs n k).filter (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d)).card =
    (Finset.univ.filter (fun ρ : Restriction n =>
      IsRestriction k ρ ∧ IsBadRestriction f d ρ)).card := sorry
end SwitchingBernoulli
title: Bad fixed-size restrictions match the counting filter
statement_informal:
Bad fixed-size restrictions match the counting filter. For a function $f$ and parameters $d, k$, the number of size-$k$ restrictions
$\rho$ with $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$ equals the number of restrictions
that are $k$-restrictions and are bad for $f$ at depth $d$ in the counting
switching-lemma sense.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction

definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Bernoulli switching lemma for CNFs. The same statement for a width-$w$ CNF $f$: under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction
with $0 < p \le 1/(40w)$ and $p \le 1$,
\[
  \Pr_{R_p}\big[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\big]
    \;\le\; \left(\tfrac12\right)^t + e^{-np/3},
\]
obtained from the DNF version via the dual-DNF representation of $f$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open SwitchingLemma2 BernoulliCost
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingBernoulli
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf (f : CNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hnd : ∀ c ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ c, ∀ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ c ∈ f, c.Nodup)
    (hn : 0 < n)
    (p : ℝ) (hp_pos : 0 < p) (hp_le : p ≤ 1 / (40 * ↑w)) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (t : ℕ) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p
      (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn (CNF.eval f) ρ) > t) ≤
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ t + Real.exp (-(↑n * p / 3)) := sorry
end SwitchingBernoulli
title: Bernoulli switching lemma for CNFs
statement_informal:
Bernoulli switching lemma for CNFs. The same statement for a width-$w$ CNF $f$: under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction
with $0 < p \le 1/(40w)$ and $p \le 1$,
\[
  \Pr_{R_p}\big[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\big]
    \;\le\; \left(\tfrac12\right)^t + e^{-np/3},
\]
obtained from the DNF version via the dual-DNF representation of $f$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Flip a literal's polarity
definitions.informal: Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: CNF to De Morgan dual DNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

definitions.title: Bernoulli event probability
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Bernoulli switching lemma for DNFs. Let $f$ be a width-$w$ DNF ($0 < w$) with no repeated variables per term, and
$0 < n$. For a Bernoulli$(p)$ random restriction with $0 < p \le 1/(40w)$ and
$p \le 1$, and for every $t$,
\[
  \Pr_{R_p}\big[\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > t\big]

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2
open SwitchingLemma2 BernoulliCost
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingBernoulli
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ f, t.Nodup)

    (t : ℕ) :
    bernoulliRestrProb p

\]

reduction, and $e^{-np/3}$ is the Chernoff tail for leaving too many free
variables.

- Restrictions with a fixed number of free variables. For natural numbers $n$ and $k$, this is the finite set of restrictions on $n$ variables
whose set of free variables has cardinality exactly $k$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Fixed-size restriction probability $R_k$. The probability of an event under the fixed-size restriction model $R_k$, which is uniform
over restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables. It is defined as the fraction of
restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables that satisfy the event.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Fixed-size switching bound. For a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with $0 < w$, $0 < n$, and $k \le n$, the size-$k$
restriction probability that $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$ is at most
$\left(10kw/n\right)^d$. This removes the $5k \le n$ restriction by using the
trivial bound when $5k > n$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
open SwitchingLemma2
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
open SwitchingLemma2 BernoulliCost
open Classical in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace BernoulliCost
variable {n : ℕ}
def fixedSizeRestrs (n k : ℕ) : Finset (Restriction n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun ρ : Restriction n => ρ.freeVars.card = k)
end BernoulliCost

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BernoulliCost
variable {n : ℕ}
def fixedSizeRestrProb (event : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred event]
    (k : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  ↑((fixedSizeRestrs n k).filter (fun ρ => event ρ)).card / ↑(fixedSizeRestrs n k).card
end BernoulliCost

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingBernoulli
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma switching_fixedSize_bound (f : DNF n) (w k d : ℕ)
    (hn : 0 < n) (hw : f.width ≤ w) (hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (_hk : k ≤ n)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ f, t.Nodup) :
    fixedSizeRestrProb (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f.eval ρ) > d) k ≤
    (10 * ↑k * ↑w / ↑n) ^ d := sorry
end SwitchingBernoulli
title: Fixed-size switching bound
statement_informal:
Fixed-size switching bound. For a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with $0 < w$, $0 < n$, and $k \le n$, the size-$k$
restriction probability that $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$ is at most
$\left(10kw/n\right)^d$. This removes the $5k \le n$ restriction by using the
trivial bound when $5k > n$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Restrictions with a fixed number of free variables
definitions.informal:
For natural numbers $n$ and $k$, this is the finite set of restrictions on $n$ variables
whose set of free variables has cardinality exactly $k$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Fixed-size restriction probability $R_k$
definitions.informal:
The probability of an event under the fixed-size restriction model $R_k$, which is uniform
over restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables. It is defined as the fraction of
restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables that satisfy the event.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace BernoulliCost
variable {n : ℕ}
def fixedSizeRestrProb (event : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred event]
    (k : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  ↑((fixedSizeRestrs n k).filter (fun ρ => event ρ)).card / ↑(fixedSizeRestrs n k).card
end BernoulliCost

  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)
