def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingBernoulli
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma switching_fixedSize_bound_small (f : DNF n) (w k d : ℕ)
    (hn : 0 < n) (hw : f.width ≤ w)
    (hk : 5 * k ≤ n)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ f, t.Nodup) :
    fixedSizeRestrProb (fun ρ => dtDepth (restrictFn f.eval ρ) > d) k ≤
    (10 * ↑k * ↑w / ↑n) ^ d := sorry
end SwitchingBernoulli
title: Fixed-size switching bound for small $k$
statement_informal:
Fixed-size switching bound for small $k$. For a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with no repeated variables per term, $0 < n$, and
$5k \le n$, the probability over uniform size-$k$ restrictions that the
restricted function has decision-tree depth exceeding $d$ is at most
$\left(10kw/n\right)^d$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Restrictions with a fixed number of free variables
definitions.informal:
For natural numbers $n$ and $k$, this is the finite set of restrictions on $n$ variables
whose set of free variables has cardinality exactly $k$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Fixed-size restriction probability $R_k$
definitions.informal:
The probability of an event under the fixed-size restriction model $R_k$, which is uniform
over restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables. It is defined as the fraction of
restrictions with exactly $k$ free variables that satisfy the event.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

Statement: Fixed literal evaluates to true. If a literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$, then for every $x$ it evaluates to true on the
extended point: $l.\mathrm{eval}(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x) = \mathrm{true}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some (!l.neg)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma Literal.fixedBy_eval_true {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (h : Literal.fixedBy l ρ) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    l.eval (ρ.extend x) = true := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

extended point: $l.\mathrm{eval}(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x) = \mathrm{true}$.

A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Razborov encoding. The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

Statement: Cardinality bound on the aux image. The number of distinct aux lists produced by the Razborov encoder on bad
restrictions with a fixed first component $\gamma$ is at most $(4w)^d$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def razborovEncode {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (ρ : Restriction n) : Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool) :=
  let path := (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.take d
  razborovEncode.go f w (path.length + 1) path ρ ρ []
where
  /-- Main encoding loop: find the first non-killed clause, process ALL of its
      free literals against the path, emit a termination marker, and repeat. -/
  go (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ) :
      ℕ → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
      List (ℕ × Bool) → Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
    | _, [], _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | 0, _, _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | fuel + 1, step :: rest, ρ₀, σ, acc =>
      let path := step :: rest
      match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) with
      | none => (σ, acc)
      | some t =>
        let freeLitsIdx := (t.zipIdx).filter (fun ⟨l, _⟩ => decide (l.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))
        match freeLitsIdx with
        | [] => (σ, acc)
        | fl :: fls =>
          let r := processClauseLits (fl :: fls) path ρ₀ σ
          go f w fuel r.1 r.2.1 r.2.2.1 (acc ++ r.2.2.2 ++ [(w, false)])
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma aux_image_card_bound {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (γ : Restriction n) :
    (((Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n =>
        IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ ∧
        (razborovEncode f w d ρ).1 = γ).image
        (fun ρ => (razborovEncode f w d ρ).2)).card) ≤ (4 * w) ^ d := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Cardinality bound on the aux image
statement_informal:
Cardinality bound on the aux image. The number of distinct aux lists produced by the Razborov encoder on bad
restrictions with a fixed first component $\gamma$ is at most $(4w)^d$.

definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Bad restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

definitions.title: Razborov encoding
definitions.informal:
The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

Statement: No bad restrictions when $s \leq d$. If $s \leq d$, the set of restrictions that are simultaneously $s$-restrictions
and bad restrictions for depth $d$ is empty.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma bad_filter_empty_of_d_ge_s {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (d s : ℕ) (hds : s ≤ d) :
    (Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n =>
        IsRestriction s ρ ∧ IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ) = ∅ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: No bad restrictions when $s \leq d$
statement_informal:
No bad restrictions when $s \leq d$. If $s \leq d$, the set of restrictions that are simultaneously $s$-restrictions
and bad restrictions for depth $d$ is empty.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.
- Bernoulli event probability. For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the
probability that the predicate holds under a Bernoulli($p$) random restriction is the
sum over all restrictions $\rho$ of $\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ weighted by
the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

Statement: Bernoulli event probability is at most one. If $0 \le p \le 1$, then for every (decidable) event the Bernoulli probability
$\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}(p,\mathrm{event})$ is at most $1$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrProb (p : ℝ) (event : Restriction n → Prop)
    [DecidablePred event] : ℝ :=
  ∑ ρ : Restriction n,
    bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ * (if event ρ then 1 else 0)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrProb_le_one' (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (event : Restriction n → Prop) [DecidablePred event] :
    bernoulliRestrProb p event ≤ 1 := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Bernoulli event probability is at most one
statement_informal:
Bernoulli event probability is at most one. If $0 \le p \le 1$, then for every (decidable) event the Bernoulli probability
$\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}(p,\mathrm{event})$ is at most $1$.

weight is
\[

\]

For a parameter $p$ and a (decidable) predicate $\mathrm{event}$ on restrictions, the

the indicator of $\mathrm{event}(\rho)$.

- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,

- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices

import Mathlib

open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=

namespace SwitchingLemma2

end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrWeight_nonneg' (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1)
    (ρ : Restriction n) : 0 ≤ bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Nonnegativity of Bernoulli weights
statement_informal:
Nonnegativity of Bernoulli weights. If $0 \le p \le 1$, then for every restriction $\rho$ the weight
$\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ is nonnegative.

weight is
\[

\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Bernoulli weight of a restriction. For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor
$(1-p)/2$.

Statement: Bernoulli weights sum to one. If $0 \le p \le 1$, then the Bernoulli weights form a probability distribution: summing
$\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ over all restrictions $\rho$ on $n$ variables
yields $1$.

import Mathlib

open Classical
open Classical SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def bernoulliRestrWeight (p : ℝ) (ρ : Restriction n) : ℝ :=
  p ^ ρ.freeVars.card * ((1 - p) / 2) ^ (n - ρ.freeVars.card)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma bernoulliRestrWeight_sum_one (p : ℝ) (hp : 0 ≤ p) (hp1 : p ≤ 1) :
    ∑ ρ : Restriction n, bernoulliRestrWeight p ρ = 1 := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Bernoulli weights sum to one
statement_informal:
Bernoulli weights sum to one. If $0 \le p \le 1$, then the Bernoulli weights form a probability distribution: summing
$\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}(p,\rho)$ over all restrictions $\rho$ on $n$ variables
yields $1$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Bernoulli weight of a restriction
definitions.informal:
For a parameter $p \in \bbr$ and a restriction $\rho$ on $n$ variables, the Bernoulli
weight is
\[
  p^{\abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}} \cdot \left(\tfrac{1-p}{2}\right)^{\,n - \abs{\mathrm{freeVars}(\rho)}},
\]
so each free variable contributes a factor $p$ and each fixed variable a factor

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

Statement: Alive branch delegates to \texttt{termSubTree}. When not all terms are killed and none is fixed, and $t$ is the first non-killed clause,
$\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,(\mathit{fuel}+1)\,\rho$ unfolds to $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,t\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$,
where $\mathrm{cont}\,\rho'$ is $\mathrm{leaf}\ \mathrm{true}$ if $t$ is fixed by $\rho'$ and otherwise
$\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}\,\rho'$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some (!l.neg)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∀ l ∈ t, Literal.fixedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma canonicalDTree_alive_eq_termSubTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (fuel : ℕ)
    (h1 : ¬ ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ)
    (h2 : ¬ ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ)
    (t : Term n)
    (hfind : f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) = some t) :
    canonicalDTree.go f (fuel + 1) ρ =
      termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
        if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
        else canonicalDTree.go f fuel ρ') := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Alive branch delegates to \texttt{termSubTree}
statement_informal:
Alive branch delegates to \texttt{termSubTree}. When not all terms are killed and none is fixed, and $t$ is the first non-killed clause,
$\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,(\mathit{fuel}+1)\,\rho$ unfolds to $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,t\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$,
where $\mathrm{cont}\,\rho'$ is $\mathrm{leaf}\ \mathrm{true}$ if $t$ is fixed by $\rho'$ and otherwise
$\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}\,\rho'$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Literal fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.

definitions.title: Literal killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Sub-tree for a term
definitions.informal:
Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

definitions.title: Term fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.

definitions.title: Term killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

Statement: Alive branch delegates to \texttt{termSubTree} (top level). The top-level analogue of the previous lemma for $\mathrm{canonicalDTree}$ itself:
under the alive-branch hypotheses with first non-killed clause $t$, $\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho$
equals $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,t\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$ with the same fixed-or-recurse continuation
running at fuel $\rho.\mathrm{numFree}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some (!l.neg)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∀ l ∈ t, Literal.fixedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma canonicalDTree_alive_eq_termSubTree' {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (h1 : ¬ ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ)
    (h2 : ¬ ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ)
    (t : Term n)
    (hfind : f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) = some t) :
    canonicalDTree f ρ =
      termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
        if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
        else canonicalDTree.go f ρ.numFree ρ') := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Alive branch delegates to \texttt{termSubTree} (top level)
statement_informal:
Alive branch delegates to \texttt{termSubTree} (top level). The top-level analogue of the previous lemma for $\mathrm{canonicalDTree}$ itself:
under the alive-branch hypotheses with first non-killed clause $t$, $\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho$
equals $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,t\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$ with the same fixed-or-recurse continuation
running at fuel $\rho.\mathrm{numFree}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

Statement: Correctness of the canonical decision tree. For every $x$, the canonical decision tree evaluates correctly:
$(\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{eval}\,x = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(f.\mathrm{eval})\,\rho\,x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma canonicalDTree_correct {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    ∀ x, (canonicalDTree f ρ).eval x = restrictFn f.eval ρ x := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Correctness of the canonical decision tree
statement_informal:
Correctness of the canonical decision tree. For every $x$, the canonical decision tree evaluates correctly:
$(\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{eval}\,x = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(f.\mathrm{eval})\,\rho\,x$.

definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree

For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and

Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the

$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

Statement: Canonical deep path length lower bound. If $\rho$ is a bad restriction (so $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$), then the
deepest path of the canonical decision tree $\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho$
has length strictly greater than $d$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma canonicalDTree_deepPath_length_ge {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n)
    (ρ : Restriction n) (d : ℕ) (hbad : IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ) :
    d < (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.length := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Canonical deep path length lower bound
statement_informal:
Canonical deep path length lower bound. If $\rho$ is a bad restriction (so $\mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho) > d$), then the
deepest path of the canonical decision tree $\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho$
has length strictly greater than $d$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Deepest path of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Canonical decision tree
definitions.informal:
The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

definitions.title: Bad restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

Statement: Deep path matches free literals of the first alive clause. When $f.\mathrm{find?}$ returns an alive clause $t$ and $\mathrm{flis}$ is the
filtered (zipIdx) list of $t$'s free literals under $\rho$, the variable at
position $k$ of $(\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{deepPath}$ equals the
variable of the $k$-th free literal, since the canonical tree branches on the
free literals of $t$ in clause order.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma canonicalDTree_deepPath_match_freeLits {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n)
    (ρ : Restriction n) (t : Term n)
    (hfind : f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) = some t)
    (hnd : ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : t.Nodup)
    (k : ℕ)
    (flis : List (Literal n × ℕ))
    (hflis : flis = (t.zipIdx).filter (fun p => decide (p.1.var ∈ ρ.freeVars)))
    (hk_flis : k < flis.length)
    (hk_path : k < (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.length) :
    ((canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath[k]'hk_path).1 = (flis[k]'hk_flis).1.var := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Deep path matches free literals of the first alive clause
statement_informal:
Deep path matches free literals of the first alive clause. When $f.\mathrm{find?}$ returns an alive clause $t$ and $\mathrm{flis}$ is the
filtered (zipIdx) list of $t$'s free literals under $\rho$, the variable at
position $k$ of $(\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{deepPath}$ equals the
variable of the $k$-th free literal, since the canonical tree branches on the
free literals of $t$ in clause order.

Statement: Canonical tree depth bounds \texttt{dtDepth}. The depth of the canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$ is at least the decision-tree depth of the
restricted function: $(\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{depth} \ge \mathrm{dtDepth}\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(f.\mathrm{eval})\,\rho)$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma canonicalDTree_depth_ge {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (canonicalDTree f ρ).depth ≥ dtDepth (restrictFn f.eval ρ) := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

restricted function: $(\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{depth} \ge \mathrm{dtDepth}\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(f.\mathrm{eval})\,\rho)$.

namespace SwitchingLemma2

where

    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma canonicalDTree_depth_zero_of_fixed {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n)
    (ρ : Restriction n) (h : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ) :
    (canonicalDTree f ρ).depth = 0 := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Canonical tree has depth zero when a clause is fixed
statement_informal:
Canonical tree has depth zero when a clause is fixed. If some clause of $f$ is fixed (satisfied) by $\rho$, the canonical decision tree
for $f|_\rho$ is the leaf $\mathrm{true}$ and hence has depth $0$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Literal fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.

definitions.title: Literal killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Term fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.

definitions.title: Term killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Canonical decision tree
definitions.informal:
The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

Statement: Canonical tree has depth zero when all clauses killed. If every clause of $f$ is killed by $\rho$, the canonical decision tree for

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma canonicalDTree_depth_zero_of_killed {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n)
    (ρ : Restriction n) (h : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ) :
    (canonicalDTree f ρ).depth = 0 := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

$f|_\rho$ is the leaf $\mathrm{false}$ and hence has depth $0$.

definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

Statement: Correctness of the fueled construction. Provided the fuel exceeds $\rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, the tree $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}\,\rho$
computes the restricted function: for all $x$, its evaluation equals $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(f.\mathrm{eval})\,\rho\,x$.
