import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma canonicalDTree_go_correct {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (fuel : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (hfuel : ρ.numFree < fuel) :
    ∀ x, (canonicalDTree.go f fuel ρ).eval x = restrictFn f.eval ρ x := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Correctness of the fueled construction
statement_informal:
Correctness of the fueled construction. Provided the fuel exceeds $\rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, the tree $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}\,\rho$
computes the restricted function: for all $x$, its evaluation equals $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(f.\mathrm{eval})\,\rho\,x$.

Statement: Fuel invariance of \texttt{canonicalDTree.go}. Once the fuel exceeds $\rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, the resulting tree does not depend on the exact
fuel: for any $\mathit{fuel}_1, \mathit{fuel}_2 > \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}_1\,\rho = \mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}_2\,\rho$.

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}

  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/

variable {n : ℕ}

end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma canonicalDTree_go_fuel_invariant {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) :
    ∀ (k : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) (fuel₁ fuel₂ : ℕ)
      (_ : ρ.numFree = k) (_ : ρ.numFree < fuel₁) (_ : ρ.numFree < fuel₂),
      canonicalDTree.go f fuel₁ ρ = canonicalDTree.go f fuel₂ ρ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Fuel invariance of \texttt{canonicalDTree.go}
statement_informal:
Fuel invariance of \texttt{canonicalDTree.go}. Once the fuel exceeds $\rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, the resulting tree does not depend on the exact
fuel: for any $\mathit{fuel}_1, \mathit{fuel}_2 > \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}_1\,\rho = \mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\mathit{fuel}_2\,\rho$.

definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Canonical path predicate. $\mathrm{IsCanonicalPath}\,f\,\rho\,\mathrm{path}$ holds when $\mathrm{path}$ is an
initial segment of the canonical decision tree's deepest root-to-leaf path for
$f|_\rho$, i.e.\ $\mathrm{path} = (\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{deepPath}.\mathrm{take}\,(\mathrm{path.length})$.
This is the invariant carried by the Razborov encoder.

Statement: Canonical path preserved by processClauseLits. When the literals are the free literals of the first alive clause and the input
path is canonical, $\mathrm{IsCanonicalPath}$ is preserved by
$\mathrm{processClauseLits}$: the remaining path is again a canonical path for the
updated restriction, and its length is at most the depth of the updated canonical
tree.

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
      (Function.update σ l.var (some (!l.neg)))
    (r.1, r.2.1, r.2.2.1, (idx, dir) :: r.2.2.2)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def IsCanonicalPath {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (path : List (Fin n × Bool)) : Prop :=
  path = (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.take path.length
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma canonicalPath_preserve_processClauseLits {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n)
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ)) (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n)
    (hcanon : IsCanonicalPath f ρ₀ path)
    (_hmatch : ∀ (k : ℕ) (hk : k < min lits.length path.length),
      (lits[k]'(by omega)).1.var = (path[k]'(by omega)).1)
    -- Extra context for the proof:
    (t : Term n)
    (hfind : f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) = some t)
    (hnd_t : ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup_t : t.Nodup)
    (hdepth : path.length ≤ (canonicalDTree f ρ₀).depth)
    (hlits_eq : lits = (t.zipIdx).filter (fun p => decide (p.1.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))) :
    IsCanonicalPath f (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1
        (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).1 ∧
    (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).1.length ≤
      (canonicalDTree f (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1).depth := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Canonical path preserved by processClauseLits
statement_informal:
Canonical path preserved by processClauseLits. When the literals are the free literals of the first alive clause and the input
path is canonical, $\mathrm{IsCanonicalPath}$ is preserved by
$\mathrm{processClauseLits}$: the remaining path is again a canonical path for the
updated restriction, and its length is at most the depth of the updated canonical
tree.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

definitions.title: Literal fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.

definitions.title: Literal killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Sub-tree for a term
definitions.informal:
Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

definitions.title: Deepest path of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

definitions.title: Term fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.

definitions.title: Term killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Canonical decision tree
definitions.informal:
The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

definitions.title: Canonical path predicate
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{IsCanonicalPath}\,f\,\rho\,\mathrm{path}$ holds when $\mathrm{path}$ is an
initial segment of the canonical decision tree's deepest root-to-leaf path for
$f|_\rho$, i.e.\ $\mathrm{path} = (\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{deepPath}.\mathrm{take}\,(\mathrm{path.length})$.
This is the invariant carried by the Razborov encoder.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

Statement: Number of restrictions with $k$ free variables. The number of restrictions of $\{0,\dots,n-1\}$ with exactly $k$ free variables
equals $\binom{n}{k} \cdot 2^{\,n-k}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma card_filter_numFree_eq (n k : ℕ) :
    (Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n => ρ.numFree = k).card =
    n.choose k * 2 ^ (n - k) := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

equals $\binom{n}{k} \cdot 2^{\,n-k}$.

Statement: Continuation equals the canonical tree. For $t \in f$ and a restriction $\rho'$ with $\rho_{\mathrm{orig}}.\mathrm{numFree} \ge \rho'.\mathrm{numFree} + 1$,
the standard continuation $\bigl(\text{if } t \text{ fixed by } \rho' \text{ then } \mathrm{leaf}\ \mathrm{true}\ \text{else}\ \mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\rho_{\mathrm{orig}}.\mathrm{numFree}\,\rho'\bigr)$

where

variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cont_eq_canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ_orig : Restriction n)
    (t : Term n) (ht_mem : t ∈ f) (ρ' : Restriction n)
    (hfuel : ρ_orig.numFree ≥ ρ'.numFree + 1) :
    (if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then DecisionTree.leaf true
     else canonicalDTree.go f ρ_orig.numFree ρ') =
    canonicalDTree f ρ' := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Continuation equals the canonical tree
statement_informal:
Continuation equals the canonical tree. For $t \in f$ and a restriction $\rho'$ with $\rho_{\mathrm{orig}}.\mathrm{numFree} \ge \rho'.\mathrm{numFree} + 1$,
the standard continuation $\bigl(\text{if } t \text{ fixed by } \rho' \text{ then } \mathrm{leaf}\ \mathrm{true}\ \text{else}\ \mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}\,f\,\rho_{\mathrm{orig}}.\mathrm{numFree}\,\rho'\bigr)$
equals $\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho'$.

Statement: Decoder loop preserves none. If $\sigma\,v = \mathrm{none}$, then the decoder loop
$\sigma$ is still $\mathrm{none}$ at $v$.

lemma decode_go_preserves_none {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (fuel : ℕ) (σ ρ₀ : Restriction n) (aux : List (ℕ × Bool))

statement_informal:
Decoder loop preserves none. If $\sigma\,v = \mathrm{none}$, then the decoder loop
$\sigma$ is still $\mathrm{none}$ at $v$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

Statement: Tree depth bounds decision-tree depth. If a decision tree $T$ computes a function $f$ (i.e. $T.\mathrm{eval}\,x = f(x)$ for all $x$), then its
depth is at least the decision-tree depth of $f$: $T.\mathrm{depth} \ge \mathrm{dtDepth}\,f$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma depth_ge_dtDepth {n : ℕ} {f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool}
    (T : DecisionTree n) (heval : ∀ x, T.eval x = f x) :
    T.depth ≥ dtDepth f := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Tree depth bounds decision-tree depth
statement_informal:
Tree depth bounds decision-tree depth. If a decision tree $T$ computes a function $f$ (i.e. $T.\mathrm{eval}\,x = f(x)$ for all $x$), then its
depth is at least the decision-tree depth of $f$: $T.\mathrm{depth} \ge \mathrm{dtDepth}\,f$.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Decision tree to CNF. Converts a decision tree into a CNF whose clauses are the disjunctions of negated
literals along each root-to-leaf path ending in a $\mathrm{false}$ leaf.
- Decision tree to DNF. Converts a decision tree into a DNF whose terms are the conjunctions of literals
along each root-to-leaf path ending in a $\mathrm{true}$ leaf.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Small decision-tree depth yields small DNF and CNF. If $\mathrm{dtDepth}\,f \leq d$, then $f$ is computed by a DNF of width at most
$d$ and by a CNF of width at most $d$.
formal_statement:

  deriving DecidableEq

  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ)
    (h : dtDepth f ≤ d) :
    (∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, φ.eval x = f x) ∧
    (∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, ψ.eval x = f x) := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Small decision-tree depth yields small DNF and CNF
statement_informal:
Small decision-tree depth yields small DNF and CNF. If $\mathrm{dtDepth}\,f \leq d$, then $f$ is computed by a DNF of width at most
$d$ and by a CNF of width at most $d$.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Decision tree to CNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a decision tree into a CNF whose clauses are the disjunctions of negated
literals along each root-to-leaf path ending in a $\mathrm{false}$ leaf.

definitions.title: Decision tree to DNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a decision tree into a DNF whose terms are the conjunctions of literals
along each root-to-leaf path ending in a $\mathrm{true}$ leaf.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

Statement: Decision-tree depth bound from a witnessing tree. If a decision tree $T$ of depth at most $d$ computes $f$ (i.e.\ $T.\mathrm{eval}\,x = f(x)$
for all $x$), then the decision-tree depth of $f$ is at most $d$.

  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)

  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2

private lemma dtDepth_le_of_tree {n : ℕ} {f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool}
    (T : DecisionTree n) (d : ℕ) (hd : T.depth ≤ d)
    (heval : ∀ x, T.eval x = f x) : dtDepth f ≤ d := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Decision-tree depth bound from a witnessing tree
statement_informal:
Decision-tree depth bound from a witnessing tree. If a decision tree $T$ of depth at most $d$ computes $f$ (i.e.\ $T.\mathrm{eval}\,x = f(x)$
for all $x$), then the decision-tree depth of $f$ is at most $d$.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

Statement: Decision-tree depth bounded by free count. For any function $f$ and restriction $\rho$, the decision-tree depth of the restricted function is at
most the number of free variables: $\mathrm{dtDepth}\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) \le \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma dtDepth_restrictFn_le_numFree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (ρ : Restriction n) :
    dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) ≤ ρ.numFree := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

most the number of free variables: $\mathrm{dtDepth}\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho) \le \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$.

import Mathlib

open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where

Encoder $\gamma$ independent of the accumulator. The first component of the encoder loop SwitchingLemma2.razborovEncode's

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

Statement: Encoder first component agrees with recursive step. When the encoder finds $t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ as the first clause not killed by $\rho_0$ and
its filtered free-variable literals form a nonempty list $\mathit{fl}::\mathit{fls}$, the
first component of the encoder run with fuel $\mathrm{fuel}+1$ on path $\mathit{step}::\mathit{rest}$
equals the first component of the recursive encoder run with fuel $\mathrm{fuel}$ on the
processed state $\mathrm{pcl} = \mathrm{processClauseLits}\,(\mathit{fl}::\mathit{fls})\,(\mathit{step}::\mathit{rest})\,\rho_0\,\sigma$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma encode_go_fst_eq_rec {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w fuel : ℕ)
    (step : Fin n × Bool) (rest : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n)
    (t_clause : Term n)
    (hfind : f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) = some t_clause)
    (fl : Literal n × ℕ) (fls : List (Literal n × ℕ))
    (hfli_eq : List.filter (fun (x : Literal n × ℕ) => decide (x.1.var ∈ Restriction.freeVars ρ₀))
      (List.zipIdx t_clause) = fl :: fls) :
    let pcl := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Encoder first component agrees with recursive step
statement_informal:
Encoder first component agrees with recursive step. When the encoder finds $t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ as the first clause not killed by $\rho_0$ and
its filtered free-variable literals form a nonempty list $\mathit{fl}::\mathit{fls}$, the
first component of the encoder run with fuel $\mathrm{fuel}+1$ on path $\mathit{step}::\mathit{rest}$
equals the first component of the recursive encoder run with fuel $\mathrm{fuel}$ on the
processed state $\mathrm{pcl} = \mathrm{processClauseLits}\,(\mathit{fl}::\mathit{fls})\,(\mathit{step}::\mathit{rest})\,\rho_0\,\sigma$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

definitions.title: Literal killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Term killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

Statement: Encoder $\gamma$ preserves $\sigma$ at non-free variables. If $\rho_0\,v \neq \mathrm{none}$ (so $v$ is non-free), then the first component
$\gamma$ of the encoder loop SwitchingLemma2.razborovEncode's \texttt{go}
returns $\sigma\,v$ at $v$; the encoder does not alter $\sigma$ on non-free variables.

Definitions:
- Mark the last entry of a block. Builds a triple list from a $\mathrm{Fin}\,w \times \mathrm{Bool}$ block by
setting the marker flag to true on the last entry only and to false on all
others.
- Cast an in-range aux list into Fin blocks. Casts a list of $(\mathbb{N} \times \mathrm{Bool})$ entries, all of whose first
coordinates are $< w$, into a list of $(\mathrm{Fin}\,w \times \mathrm{Bool})$.
- Triples back to an aux list. The inverse direction of SwitchingLemma2.parseAux: converts a list of
triples back into an aux list of $(\mathbb{N} \times \mathrm{Bool})$ entries,
emitting a $(w, \mathrm{false})$ termination marker after each triple whose
marker flag is true.
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Encoder well-formedness invariant. The aux output of $\mathrm{razborovEncode.go}$ (started with empty accumulator)
can be written as $\mathrm{triplesToAux}\,w\,ts$ for some triple list $ts$ whose
length is bounded by the path length and whose last element, if any, has marker
flag true. This is the main structural invariant of the encoder.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def triplesToAux (w : ℕ) :
    List (Fin w × Bool × Bool) → List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [] => []
  | (pos, dir, true) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: (w, false) :: triplesToAux w rest
  | (pos, dir, false) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: triplesToAux w rest
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma encode_go_wellformed {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (_hw_pos : 0 < w)
    (fuel : ℕ) (path : List (Fin n × Bool)) (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n) :
    ∃ ts : List (Fin w × Bool × Bool),
      (razborovEncode.go f w fuel path ρ₀ σ []).2 = triplesToAux w ts ∧
      ts.length ≤ path.length ∧
      (∀ (hne : ts ≠ []), (ts.getLast hne).2.2 = true) := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Encoder well-formedness invariant
statement_informal:
Encoder well-formedness invariant. The aux output of $\mathrm{razborovEncode.go}$ (started with empty accumulator)
can be written as $\mathrm{triplesToAux}\,w\,ts$ for some triple list $ts$ whose
length is bounded by the path length and whose last element, if any, has marker
flag true. This is the main structural invariant of the encoder.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

Statement: A free variable exists when neither killed nor fixed. For a DNF $f$ and restriction $\rho$, if not every term is killed by $\rho$ and no term
is fixed by $\rho$, then $\rho$ has at least one free variable $v \in \rho.\mathrm{freeVars}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some (!l.neg)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∀ l ∈ t, Literal.fixedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma exists_free_of_not_killed_not_fixed {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (h1 : ¬∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ) (h2 : ¬∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ) :
    ∃ v : Fin n, v ∈ ρ.freeVars := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: A free variable exists when neither killed nor fixed
statement_informal:
A free variable exists when neither killed nor fixed. For a DNF $f$ and restriction $\rho$, if not every term is killed by $\rho$ and no term
is fixed by $\rho$, then $\rho$ has at least one free variable $v \in \rho.\mathrm{freeVars}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

Statement: Extending an updated restriction agrees with the original. If $v$ is free in $\rho$ and the input $x$ satisfies $x(v) = b$, then extending the updated
restriction $\rho[v \mapsto b]$ by $x$ yields the same total assignment as extending $\rho$ by $x$.
title: Extending an updated restriction agrees with the original
statement_informal:
Extending an updated restriction agrees with the original. If $v$ is free in $\rho$ and the input $x$ satisfies $x(v) = b$, then extending the updated
restriction $\rho[v \mapsto b]$ by $x$ yields the same total assignment as extending $\rho$ by $x$.

definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.
- Razborov encoding. The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

Statement: Fiber bound for the encoder. The number of bad $s$-restrictions $\rho$ mapping to a fixed $\gamma$ under the
encoder is at most $(4w)^d$, since each of the $d$ path steps carries
$w \cdot 2 \cdot 2 = 4w$ possibilities.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def razborovEncode {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (ρ : Restriction n) : Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool) :=
  let path := (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.take d
  razborovEncode.go f w (path.length + 1) path ρ ρ []
where
  /-- Main encoding loop: find the first non-killed clause, process ALL of its
      free literals against the path, emit a termination marker, and repeat. -/
  go (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ) :
      ℕ → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
      List (ℕ × Bool) → Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
    | _, [], _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | 0, _, _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | fuel + 1, step :: rest, ρ₀, σ, acc =>
      let path := step :: rest
      match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) with
      | none => (σ, acc)
      | some t =>
        let freeLitsIdx := (t.zipIdx).filter (fun ⟨l, _⟩ => decide (l.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))
        match freeLitsIdx with
        | [] => (σ, acc)
        | fl :: fls =>
          let r := processClauseLits (fl :: fls) path ρ₀ σ
          go f w fuel r.1 r.2.1 r.2.2.1 (acc ++ r.2.2.2 ++ [(w, false)])
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma fiber_bound {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w s d : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (_hd : d ≤ s)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (γ : Restriction n) :
    (Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n =>
        IsRestriction s ρ ∧ IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ ∧
        (razborovEncode f w d ρ).1 = γ).card ≤ (4 * w) ^ d := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Fiber bound for the encoder
statement_informal:
Fiber bound for the encoder. The number of bad $s$-restrictions $\rho$ mapping to a fixed $\gamma$ under the
encoder is at most $(4w)^d$, since each of the $d$ path steps carries
$w \cdot 2 \cdot 2 = 4w$ possibilities.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Deepest path of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Canonical decision tree
definitions.informal:
The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Bad restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

definitions.title: Restriction with $s$ free variables
definitions.informal:
The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

definitions.title: Razborov encoding
definitions.informal:
The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

Statement: Freeness is unchanged by an unrelated update. If every literal of $\mathit{rest}$ has variable distinct from $v$, then filtering $\mathit{rest}$ by
freeness under $\rho[v \mapsto b]$ gives the same list as filtering by freeness under $\rho$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma filter_free_update_eq {n : ℕ}
    (rest : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n) (v : Fin n) (b : Bool)
    (hdist : ∀ l ∈ rest, l.var ≠ v) :
    rest.filter (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ Restriction.freeVars (Function.update ρ v (some b)))) =
    rest.filter (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars)) := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Freeness is unchanged by an unrelated update
statement_informal:
Freeness is unchanged by an unrelated update. If every literal of $\mathit{rest}$ has variable distinct from $v$, then filtering $\mathit{rest}$ by
freeness under $\rho[v \mapsto b]$ gives the same list as filtering by freeness under $\rho$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

Statement: First non-killed clause preserved under encoding. For a DNF $f$ with no two literals in a term sharing a variable, suppose the encoder finds
$t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ as the first term not killed by $\rho_0$. Under the agreement
hypotheses relating $\rho_0$, $\sigma$, and the decoder restriction $\rho_{0,\mathrm{dec}}$
(agreeing with the encoder output on free variables and with $\rho_0$ on set variables),
the decoder restriction also finds $t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ as its first non-killed clause.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma find_clause_preserved_in_encode {n : ℕ}
    (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (enc_fuel : ℕ) (path : List (Fin n × Bool)) (ρ₀ σ ρ₀_dec : Restriction n)
    (t_clause : Term n)
    (hfind_enc : f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) = some t_clause)
    (hE : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none → σ v = none)
    (hB : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none →
      ρ₀_dec v = (razborovEncode.go f w enc_fuel path ρ₀ σ []).1 v)
    (hD : ∀ v, ρ₀ v ≠ none → ρ₀_dec v = ρ₀ v) :
    f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀_dec)) = some t_clause := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: First non-killed clause preserved under encoding
statement_informal:
First non-killed clause preserved under encoding. For a DNF $f$ with no two literals in a term sharing a variable, suppose the encoder finds
$t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ as the first term not killed by $\rho_0$. Under the agreement
hypotheses relating $\rho_0$, $\sigma$, and the decoder restriction $\rho_{0,\mathrm{dec}}$
(agreeing with the encoder output on free variables and with $\rho_0$ on set variables),
the decoder restriction also finds $t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ as its first non-killed clause.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Literal killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.

definitions.title: Term killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

Statement: First surviving clause is preserved under non-free agreement. Suppose $t$ is the first term of $f$ not killed by $\rho$, that $\sigma$ agrees with
$\rho$ on all non-free variables, and that $t$ is not killed by $\sigma$. Then $t$ is also
the first term of $f$ not killed by $\sigma$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma first_clause_preserved {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ σ : Restriction n)
    (t : Term n)
    (hfirst : f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) = some t)
    (hagree : ∀ v, ρ v ≠ none → σ v = ρ v)
    (ht_alive : ¬Term.killedBy t σ) :
    f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t σ)) = some t := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: First surviving clause is preserved under non-free agreement
statement_informal:
First surviving clause is preserved under non-free agreement. Suppose $t$ is the first term of $f$ not killed by $\rho$, that $\sigma$ agrees with
$\rho$ on all non-free variables, and that $t$ is not killed by $\sigma$. Then $t$ is also
the first term of $f$ not killed by $\sigma$.

definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
