killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

Statement: A fixed term forces zero decision-tree depth. If some term of a DNF $f$ is fixed by $\rho$, then the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho$ is identically true and hence has
decision-tree depth $0$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some (!l.neg)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∀ l ∈ t, Literal.fixedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma fixedTerm_implies_dtDepth_zero {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (h : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ) :
    dtDepth (restrictFn f.eval ρ) = 0 := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: A fixed term forces zero decision-tree depth
statement_informal:
A fixed term forces zero decision-tree depth. If some term of a DNF $f$ is fixed by $\rho$, then the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho$ is identically true and hence has
decision-tree depth $0$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Literal fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Term fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

Statement: $\rho_0$-foldl stable off targeted variables. Folding the decoder's $\rho_0$-update over a list of entries leaves $v$ unchanged when
no entry targets $v$; if for every entry the literal at its position in $t$ does not have
variable $v$, the folded $\rho_0$ agrees with the initial $\rho_0$ at $v$.

variable {n : ℕ}
lemma foldl_rho_stable {n : ℕ} (t : Term n)
    (entries : List (ℕ × Bool)) (ρ₀ : Restriction n) (v : Fin n)
    (hne : ∀ e ∈ entries, ∀ (l : Literal n) (rest : List (Literal n)),
      t.drop e.1 = l :: rest → l.var ≠ v) :
    entries.foldl (fun ρ₀ (e : ℕ × Bool) =>
      match t.drop e.1 with | [] => ρ₀ | l :: _ => Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some e.2)) ρ₀ v
    = ρ₀ v := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

Statement: $\sigma$-foldl preserves none. If $\sigma\,v = \mathrm{none}$, then folding the decoder's $\sigma$-update over any list
of entries still gives $\mathrm{none}$ at $v$; the fold never sets a previously unset
variable to a value via the $\sigma$-update.

    entries.foldl (fun σ (e : ℕ × Bool) =>
      match t.drop e.1 with | [] => σ | l :: _ => Function.update σ l.var none) σ v = none := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

$f$ with distinct variables per term, running the encoder on $\rho$ and then running the

    (enc_fuel : ℕ) (path : List (Fin n × Bool)) (ρ : Restriction n) :

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: Generalized go-level round-trip. The generalized round-trip statement: for a DNF $f$ of width at most $w$ with distinct
variables per term, given decoder states $\sigma_{\mathrm{dec}}$ and $\rho_{0,\mathrm{dec}}$
that satisfy the round-trip invariants relative to the encoder output and enough decoder
fuel, the first component of the decoder run on the encoder's output list equals the
original $\sigma$. This is the inductive core from which the closed round trip follows.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma go_roundtrip_gen {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ) (hw : f.width ≤ w)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (enc_fuel : ℕ) (path : List (Fin n × Bool)) (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n)
    (σ_dec ρ₀_dec : Restriction n) (dec_fuel : ℕ)
    (hE : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none → σ v = none)
    (hA : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none → σ_dec v = (razborovEncode.go f w enc_fuel path ρ₀ σ []).1 v)
    (hB : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none → ρ₀_dec v = (razborovEncode.go f w enc_fuel path ρ₀ σ []).1 v)
    (hC : ∀ v, ρ₀ v ≠ none → σ_dec v = σ v)
    (hD : ∀ v, ρ₀ v ≠ none → ρ₀_dec v = ρ₀ v)
    (hfuel : dec_fuel ≥ (razborovEncode.go f w enc_fuel path ρ₀ σ []).2.length + 1) :
    (razborovDecode.go f w dec_fuel σ_dec ρ₀_dec
      (razborovEncode.go f w enc_fuel path ρ₀ σ []).2).1 = σ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

that satisfy the round-trip invariants relative to the encoder output and enough decoder
fuel, the first component of the decoder run on the encoder's output list equals the
original $\sigma$. This is the inductive core from which the closed round trip follows.

Statement: All terms killed forces zero decision-tree depth. If every term of a DNF $f$ is killed by $\rho$, then the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho$ is identically false and hence has
decision-tree depth $0$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma killedAll_implies_dtDepth_zero {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (h : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ) :
    dtDepth (restrictFn f.eval ρ) = 0 := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: All terms killed forces zero decision-tree depth
statement_informal:
All terms killed forces zero decision-tree depth. If every term of a DNF $f$ is killed by $\rho$, then the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho$ is identically false and hence has
decision-tree depth $0$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Literal killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Term killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

Statement: Killing is preserved under agreement on non-free variables. If a term $t$ is killed by $\rho$ and a restriction $\sigma$ agrees with $\rho$ on every
non-free variable (i.e.\ $\sigma(v) = \rho(v)$ whenever $\rho(v) \neq \mathrm{none}$), then
$t$ is also killed by $\sigma$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma killedBy_of_nonfree_agree {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ σ : Restriction n)
    (hk : Term.killedBy t ρ) (hagree : ∀ v, ρ v ≠ none → σ v = ρ v) :
    Term.killedBy t σ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Killing is preserved under agreement on non-free variables
statement_informal:
Killing is preserved under agreement on non-free variables. If a term $t$ is killed by $\rho$ and a restriction $\sigma$ agrees with $\rho$ on every
non-free variable (i.e.\ $\sigma(v) = \rho(v)$ whenever $\rho(v) \neq \mathrm{none}$), then
$t$ is also killed by $\sigma$.

Definitions:

setting the marker flag to true on the last entry only and to false on all
others.

Statement: Last entry of markLast is marked. On a nonempty block, the last element of $\mathrm{markLast}\,block$ has marker
flag equal to true.

import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def markLast {w : ℕ} :
    List (Fin w × Bool) → List (Fin w × Bool × Bool)
  | [] => []
  | hd :: [] => [(hd.1, hd.2, true)]
  | hd :: (hd2 :: rest) => (hd.1, hd.2, false) :: markLast (hd2 :: rest)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma markLast_getLast_true {w : ℕ} :
    ∀ (block : List (Fin w × Bool)) (hne : markLast block ≠ []),
      ((markLast block).getLast hne).2.2 = true
  | [], hne => by simp [markLast] at hne
  | [hd], _ => by
      show (((hd.1, hd.2, true) : Fin w × Bool × Bool)).2.2 = true
      rfl
  | hd :: hd2 :: rest, hne => by
      have hne' : markLast (hd2 :: rest) ≠ [] := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Last entry of markLast is marked
statement_informal:
Last entry of markLast is marked. On a nonempty block, the last element of $\mathrm{markLast}\,block$ has marker
flag equal to true.

definitions.informal:

Statement: markLast preserves length. $(\mathrm{markLast}\,block).\mathrm{length} = block.\mathrm{length}$.

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def markLast {w : ℕ} :

  | [] => []
  | hd :: [] => [(hd.1, hd.2, true)]
  | hd :: (hd2 :: rest) => (hd.1, hd.2, false) :: markLast (hd2 :: rest)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma markLast_length {w : ℕ} :
    ∀ (block : List (Fin w × Bool)), (markLast block).length = block.length
  | [] => rfl
  | [_] => rfl
  | hd :: hd2 :: rest => by
      show ((hd.1, hd.2, false) :: markLast (hd2 :: rest)).length =
        (hd :: hd2 :: rest).length
      have ih := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,

Statement: Filtered free-variable literals stay in the index list. If a pair $p$ lies in the list obtained by filtering the indexed literals of
$t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ (its \texttt{zipIdx}) to those whose variable is free under $\rho_0$,
then $p$ is a member of $t_{\mathrm{clause}}.\mathrm{zipIdx}$ and its variable is free,
i.e.\ $\rho_0\,(p.1.\mathrm{var}) = \texttt{none}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma mem_filter_freeVars_zipIdx {n : ℕ} (ρ₀ : Restriction n)
    (t_clause : List (Literal n)) (p : Literal n × ℕ)
    (hp : p ∈ List.filter
      (fun (x : Literal n × ℕ) => decide (x.1.var ∈ Restriction.freeVars ρ₀))
      (List.zipIdx t_clause)) :
    p ∈ t_clause.zipIdx ∧ ρ₀ p.1.var = none := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Filtered free-variable literals stay in the index list
statement_informal:
Filtered free-variable literals stay in the index list. If a pair $p$ lies in the list obtained by filtering the indexed literals of
$t_{\mathrm{clause}}$ (its \texttt{zipIdx}) to those whose variable is free under $\rho_0$,
then $p$ is a member of $t_{\mathrm{clause}}.\mathrm{zipIdx}$ and its variable is free,
i.e.\ $\rho_0\,(p.1.\mathrm{var}) = \texttt{none}$.

Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,

Statement: Updating a free variable decreases numFree by one. If $\rho\,v = \mathrm{none}$, then updating $\rho$ at $v$ to $\mathrm{some}\,b$
decreases $\mathrm{numFree}$ by exactly one:
$\mathrm{numFree}(\rho[v \mapsto b]) + 1 = \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Parse an aux list into triples. Parses a low-level aux list of $(\mathbb{N} \times \mathrm{Bool})$ entries into
a list of triples $(\mathrm{pos}, \mathrm{dir}, \mathrm{hasMarker})$ with
$\mathrm{pos} : \mathrm{Fin}\,w$, where $\mathrm{hasMarker}$ records whether the
entry is immediately followed by a $(w, \mathrm{false})$ termination marker.
Entries with index $\geq w$ that do not follow a real entry are dropped.

  | [] => []

import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def parseAux (w : ℕ) (hw_pos : 0 < w) :
    List (ℕ × Bool) → List (Fin w × Bool × Bool)
  | [] => []
  | (idx, dir) :: rest =>
    if h : idx < w then
      match rest with
      | [] => [(⟨idx, h⟩, dir, false)]
      | (idx', dir') :: rest' =>
        if idx' ≥ w then
          (⟨idx, h⟩, dir, true) :: parseAux w hw_pos rest'
        else
          (⟨idx, h⟩, dir, false) :: parseAux w hw_pos ((idx', dir') :: rest')
    else
      parseAux w hw_pos rest
termination_by l => l.length
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def triplesToAux (w : ℕ) :
    List (Fin w × Bool × Bool) → List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [] => []
  | (pos, dir, true) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: (w, false) :: triplesToAux w rest
  | (pos, dir, false) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: triplesToAux w rest
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma parseAux_triplesToAux (w : ℕ) (hw_pos : 0 < w) :
    ∀ (ts : List (Fin w × Bool × Bool)),
      parseAux w hw_pos (triplesToAux w ts) = ts := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

$\mathrm{triplesToAux}$ and then $\mathrm{parseAux}$ recovers $ts$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

Statement: Free variable preserved by clause processing. If the restriction component $(\mathrm{processClauseLits}\ \mathit{lits}\ \mathit{path}\ \rho_0\ \sigma).2.1$
is unset at a variable $v$ (equal to \texttt{none}), then $\rho_0$ is already unset at $v$,
i.e.\ $\rho_0\,v = \texttt{none}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
      (Function.update σ l.var (some (!l.neg)))
    (r.1, r.2.1, r.2.2.1, (idx, dir) :: r.2.2.2)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma pcl_none_implies_rho_free {n : ℕ}
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ)) (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n) (v : Fin n)
    (hv' : (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 v = none) :
    ρ₀ v = none := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Free variable preserved by clause processing
statement_informal:
Free variable preserved by clause processing. If the restriction component $(\mathrm{processClauseLits}\ \mathit{lits}\ \mathit{path}\ \rho_0\ \sigma).2.1$
is unset at a variable $v$ (equal to \texttt{none}), then $\rho_0$ is already unset at $v$,
i.e.\ $\rho_0\,v = \texttt{none}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where

  neg : Bool

variable {n : ℕ}

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
      (Function.update σ l.var (some (!l.neg)))

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma processClauseLits_bound {n : ℕ}
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ)) (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n) :
    (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.2.2.length +
    2 * (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).1.length ≤ 2 * path.length := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

$\rho_0$-update over the auxiliary entries of

variable {n : ℕ}

    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma processClauseLits_foldl_rho_eq {n : ℕ} (t : Term n)
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ)) (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ ρ₀_dec : Restriction n) (v : Fin n)
    (hmem : ∀ p ∈ lits, p ∈ t.zipIdx)
    (hinit : ρ₀ v = ρ₀_dec v) :
    (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.2.2.foldl (fun ρ₀ (e : ℕ × Bool) =>
      match t.drop e.1 with | [] => ρ₀ | l :: _ => Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some e.2)) ρ₀_dec v
    = (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 v := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

routine at $v$.

lemma processClauseLits_foldl_rho_eq_of_set {n : ℕ} (t : Term n)
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ)) (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ ρ₀_dec : Restriction n) (v : Fin n)
    (hmem : ∀ p ∈ lits, p ∈ t.zipIdx)
    (hfree : ρ₀ v = none)
    (hset : (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 v ≠ none) :

      match t.drop e.1 with | [] => ρ₀ | l :: _ => Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some e.2)) ρ₀_dec v
    = (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 v := sorry

title: $\rho_0$-foldl agrees when $v$ is newly set

$v$.

abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma processClauseLits_freeLits_pairwise_var {n : ℕ}
    (t : Term n) (ρ₀ : Restriction n) (ht_nodup : t.Nodup)
    (hnd : ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) :
    ((t.zipIdx).filter
        (fun p => decide (p.1.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))).Pairwise
      (fun p q : Literal n × ℕ => p.1.var ≠ q.1.var) := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

title: Processing does not lengthen the path
statement_informal:
Processing does not lengthen the path. The remaining path returned by processClauseLits is no longer than the input
path: the length of the first component of $\mathrm{processClauseLits}\,(\mathit{lits},
\mathit{path}, \rho_0, \sigma)$ is at most the length of $\mathit{path}$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free

structure Literal (n : ℕ) where

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))

      path.length := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma processClauseLits_path_nil_of_rho_none_and_mem {n : ℕ}
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ)) (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n) (l : Literal n) (idx : ℕ)
    (hl : (l, idx) ∈ lits)
    (hnd : ∀ m ∈ lits, m.1.var = l.var → m.1 = l)
    (hfree : ρ₀ l.var = none)
    (h : (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 l.var = none) :
    (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).1 = [] := sorry

Statement: Remaining path bounded by remaining free variables. If the initial path length is at most $\rho_0.\mathrm{numFree}$, then after

$\rho_0$'s $\mathrm{numFree}$.

variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma processClauseLits_remaining_le_numFree {n : ℕ}
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ)) (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n)
    (h0 : path.length ≤ ρ₀.numFree) :
    (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).1.length ≤
      (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1.numFree := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

definitions.informal:

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

definitions.informal:

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

Statement: termSubTree deep path after dropping the free-literal prefix. Dropping the free-literal prefix from $(\mathrm{termSubTree}\,t\,\rho_0\,\mathrm{cont}).\mathrm{deepPath}$
yields the deep path of the continuation evaluated at the
$\mathrm{processClauseLits}$-updated restriction, connecting the canonical tree
structure to the encoder's restriction update.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
      (Function.update σ l.var (some (!l.neg)))
    (r.1, r.2.1, r.2.2.1, (idx, dir) :: r.2.2.2)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma processClauseLits_termSubTree_drop {n : ℕ} :
    ∀ (t : Term n) (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n)
      (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
      (_hdistinct : t.Pairwise (fun l₁ l₂ => l₁.var ≠ l₂.var))
      (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
      (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ))
      (hlits_len : lits.length = (t.filter (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))).length)
      (_hlits_match : ∀ k (hk : k < lits.length),
        (lits[k]'hk).1 = (t.filter (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars)))[k]'(by omega))
      (_hpath_take : path = (termSubTree t ρ₀ cont).deepPath.take path.length)
      (_hfreeLen_le : lits.length ≤ path.length)
      (_hdp_len : path.length ≤ (termSubTree t ρ₀ cont).deepPath.length),
    (termSubTree t ρ₀ cont).deepPath.drop lits.length =
      (cont (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1).deepPath := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: termSubTree deep path after dropping the free-literal prefix
statement_informal:
termSubTree deep path after dropping the free-literal prefix. Dropping the free-literal prefix from $(\mathrm{termSubTree}\,t\,\rho_0\,\mathrm{cont}).\mathrm{deepPath}$
yields the deep path of the continuation evaluated at the
$\mathrm{processClauseLits}$-updated restriction, connecting the canonical tree
structure to the encoder's restriction update.

Statement: Tight bound for non-empty inputs. On non-empty literal and path lists, SwitchingLemma2.processClauseLits satisfies
the strengthened bound: the auxiliary-entry length plus one, plus twice the remaining

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
      (Function.update σ l.var (some (!l.neg)))
    (r.1, r.2.1, r.2.2.1, (idx, dir) :: r.2.2.2)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma processClauseLits_tight {n : ℕ}
    (fl : Literal n × ℕ) (fls : List (Literal n × ℕ))
    (step : Fin n × Bool) (rest : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n) :
    (processClauseLits (fl :: fls) (step :: rest) ρ₀ σ).2.2.2.length + 1 +
    2 * (processClauseLits (fl :: fls) (step :: rest) ρ₀ σ).1.length ≤
    2 * (step :: rest).length := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Tight bound for non-empty inputs
statement_informal:
Tight bound for non-empty inputs. On non-empty literal and path lists, SwitchingLemma2.processClauseLits satisfies
the strengthened bound: the auxiliary-entry length plus one, plus twice the remaining
path length, is still at most twice the length of the input path.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

informal_statement:

- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free

branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,

Statement: processEntries on processClauseLits data. Characterizes the decoder's \texttt{processEntries} when run on the auxiliary entries of
\mathrm{false})$ and a remainder: the result is given by folding the decoder's $\sigma$-
and $\rho_0$-updates over those auxiliary entries, leaving the remainder unconsumed,
provided $t.\mathrm{length} \le w$ and every input literal occurs in $t.\texttt{zipIdx}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma processEntries_preserves_none {n : ℕ}
    (t : Term n) (w : ℕ) (σ ρ₀ : Restriction n) (aux : List (ℕ × Bool))
    (v : Fin n) (hv : σ v = none) :
    (razborovDecode.processEntries t w σ ρ₀ aux).1 v = none := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: processEntries preserves none
statement_informal:
processEntries preserves none. If $\sigma\,v = \mathrm{none}$, then the decoder's \texttt{processEntries} routine leaves
$v$ unset; the resulting $\sigma$ is still $\mathrm{none}$ at $v$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Razborov decoding. The inverse of the Razborov encoding: given the DNF $f$, parameter $w$, and a pair
$(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$ produced by the encoder, it recovers the original restriction
$\rho$. It repeatedly finds the first clause not killed by the current restriction,
processes that clause's auxiliary block (unfixing each recorded variable in $\sigma$
and recording its path direction in $\rho_0$ until a termination marker is reached),
and returns the resulting restriction.
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Razborov encoding. The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

Statement: Decode of encode recovers the restriction. For a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with distinct variables per term and a bad restriction $\rho$ for
$f.\mathrm{eval}$ at depth $d$, decoding the Razborov encoding of $\rho$ returns $\rho$:
$\mathrm{razborovDecode}\ f\ w\ (\mathrm{razborovEncode}\ f\ w\ d\ \rho) = \rho$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def razborovDecode {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (γ : Restriction n) (aux : List (ℕ × Bool)) : Restriction n :=
  (razborovDecode.go f w (aux.length + 1) γ γ aux).1
where
  /-- Process aux entries for one clause until termination marker. -/
  processEntries (t : Term n) (w : ℕ) :
      Restriction n → Restriction n → List (ℕ × Bool) →
      Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
    | σ, ρ₀, [] => (σ, ρ₀, [])
    | σ, ρ₀, (idx, dir) :: rest =>
      if idx ≥ w then
        (σ, ρ₀, rest)
      else
        match t.drop idx with
        | [] => (σ, ρ₀, rest)
        | l :: _ =>
          processEntries t w
            (Function.update σ l.var none)
            (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
            rest
  /-- Main decoding loop: find the clause, process its aux block, repeat. -/
  go (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ) :
      ℕ → Restriction n → Restriction n → List (ℕ × Bool) →
      Restriction n × Restriction n
    | _, σ, ρ₀, [] => (σ, ρ₀)
    | 0, σ, ρ₀, _ => (σ, ρ₀)
    | fuel + 1, σ, ρ₀, entry :: restAux =>
      let aux := entry :: restAux
      match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) with
      | none => (σ, ρ₀)
      | some t =>
        let (σ', ρ₀', aux') := processEntries t w σ ρ₀ aux
        go f w fuel σ' ρ₀' aux'
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def razborovEncode {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (ρ : Restriction n) : Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool) :=
  let path := (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.take d
  razborovEncode.go f w (path.length + 1) path ρ ρ []
where
  /-- Main encoding loop: find the first non-killed clause, process ALL of its
      free literals against the path, emit a termination marker, and repeat. -/
  go (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ) :
      ℕ → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
      List (ℕ × Bool) → Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
    | _, [], _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | 0, _, _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | fuel + 1, step :: rest, ρ₀, σ, acc =>
      let path := step :: rest
      match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) with
      | none => (σ, acc)
      | some t =>
        let freeLitsIdx := (t.zipIdx).filter (fun ⟨l, _⟩ => decide (l.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))
        match freeLitsIdx with
        | [] => (σ, acc)
        | fl :: fls =>
          let r := processClauseLits (fl :: fls) path ρ₀ σ
          go f w fuel r.1 r.2.1 r.2.2.1 (acc ++ r.2.2.2 ++ [(w, false)])
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma razborovDecode_encode {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (_hbad : IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ) (hw : f.width ≤ w)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) :
    razborovDecode f w (razborovEncode f w d ρ).1 (razborovEncode f w d ρ).2 = ρ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Decode of encode recovers the restriction
statement_informal:
Decode of encode recovers the restriction. For a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with distinct variables per term and a bad restriction $\rho$ for
$f.\mathrm{eval}$ at depth $d$, decoding the Razborov encoding of $\rho$ returns $\rho$:
$\mathrm{razborovDecode}\ f\ w\ (\mathrm{razborovEncode}\ f\ w\ d\ \rho) = \rho$.

$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.
- Canonical path predicate. $\mathrm{IsCanonicalPath}\,f\,\rho\,\mathrm{path}$ holds when $\mathrm{path}$ is an
initial segment of the canonical decision tree's deepest root-to-leaf path for
$f|_\rho$, i.e.\ $\mathrm{path} = (\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{deepPath}.\mathrm{take}\,(\mathrm{path.length})$.
This is the invariant carried by the Razborov encoder.
- Razborov encoding. The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

Statement: Encoder output is an $(s-d)$-restriction. For a bad $s$-restriction $\rho$ with $d \leq s$, the first component
(the $\gamma$-output) of $\mathrm{razborovEncode}\,f\,w\,d\,\rho$ is an
$(s-d)$-restriction, since the encoder fixes exactly $d$ previously free
variables.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def razborovEncode {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (ρ : Restriction n) : Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool) :=
  let path := (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.take d
  razborovEncode.go f w (path.length + 1) path ρ ρ []
where
  /-- Main encoding loop: find the first non-killed clause, process ALL of its
      free literals against the path, emit a termination marker, and repeat. -/
  go (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ) :
      ℕ → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
      List (ℕ × Bool) → Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
    | _, [], _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | 0, _, _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | fuel + 1, step :: rest, ρ₀, σ, acc =>
      let path := step :: rest
      match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) with
      | none => (σ, acc)
      | some t =>
        let freeLitsIdx := (t.zipIdx).filter (fun ⟨l, _⟩ => decide (l.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))
        match freeLitsIdx with
        | [] => (σ, acc)
        | fl :: fls =>
          let r := processClauseLits (fl :: fls) path ρ₀ σ
          go f w fuel r.1 r.2.1 r.2.2.1 (acc ++ r.2.2.2 ++ [(w, false)])
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma razborovEncode_fst_numFree_eq {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (ρ : Restriction n) (s : ℕ) (hρ : IsRestriction s ρ)
    (hbad : IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ) (hd : d ≤ s)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ f, t.Nodup) :
    IsRestriction (s - d) (razborovEncode f w d ρ).1 := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Encoder output is an $(s-d)$-restriction
statement_informal:
Encoder output is an $(s-d)$-restriction. For a bad $s$-restriction $\rho$ with $d \leq s$, the first component
(the $\gamma$-output) of $\mathrm{razborovEncode}\,f\,w\,d\,\rho$ is an
$(s-d)$-restriction, since the encoder fixes exactly $d$ previously free
variables.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Deepest path of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Canonical decision tree
definitions.informal:
The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

definitions.title: Bad restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

definitions.title: Restriction with $s$ free variables
definitions.informal:
The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

definitions.title: Canonical path predicate
definitions.informal:
$\mathrm{IsCanonicalPath}\,f\,\rho\,\mathrm{path}$ holds when $\mathrm{path}$ is an
initial segment of the canonical decision tree's deepest root-to-leaf path for
$f|_\rho$, i.e.\ $\mathrm{path} = (\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{deepPath}.\mathrm{take}\,(\mathrm{path.length})$.
This is the invariant carried by the Razborov encoder.

definitions.title: Razborov encoding
definitions.informal:
The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Canonical path predicate. $\mathrm{IsCanonicalPath}\,f\,\rho\,\mathrm{path}$ holds when $\mathrm{path}$ is an
initial segment of the canonical decision tree's deepest root-to-leaf path for
$f|_\rho$, i.e.\ $\mathrm{path} = (\mathrm{canonicalDTree}\,f\,\rho).\mathrm{deepPath}.\mathrm{take}\,(\mathrm{path.length})$.
This is the invariant carried by the Razborov encoder.

Statement: Encoder free-variable consumption invariant. When the canonical tree depth is at least the path length,
$\mathrm{razborovEncode.go}$ fully consumes the path (never terminating early),
and the resulting $\sigma$ has $\mathrm{numFree}$ decreased by exactly the initial
path length: $(\mathrm{go}\,\dots).1.\mathrm{numFree} + \mathrm{path.length} = \sigma.\mathrm{numFree}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def IsCanonicalPath {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (path : List (Fin n × Bool)) : Prop :=
  path = (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.take path.length
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma razborovEncode_go_numFree_invariant {n : ℕ}
    (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ f, t.Nodup)
    (fuel : ℕ) (path : List (Fin n × Bool)) (ρ₀ σ : Restriction n)
    (hagree : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none ↔ σ v = none)
    (hcanon : IsCanonicalPath f ρ₀ path)
    (hdepth : path.length ≤ (canonicalDTree f ρ₀).depth)
    (hfuel : path.length < fuel) :
    (razborovEncode.go f w fuel path ρ₀ σ []).1.numFree + path.length = σ.numFree := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

path length: $(\mathrm{go}\,\dots).1.\mathrm{numFree} + \mathrm{path.length} = \sigma.\mathrm{numFree}$.
