- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Deepest path of a decision tree. Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Razborov encoding. The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

Statement: Injectivity of the Razborov encoding. The Razborov encoding is injective on bad restrictions: for a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with
distinct variables per term, if $\rho_1$ and $\rho_2$ are both bad restrictions for
$f.\mathrm{eval}$ at depth $d$ and their encodings agree,
$\mathrm{razborovEncode}\ f\ w\ d\ \rho_1 = \mathrm{razborovEncode}\ f\ w\ d\ \rho_2$,
then $\rho_1 = \rho_2$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def canonicalDTree {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    DecisionTree n :=
  canonicalDTree.go f (ρ.numFree + 1) ρ
where
  go (f : DNF n) : ℕ → Restriction n → DecisionTree n
    | 0, _ => .leaf false
    | fuel + 1, ρ =>
      if _h1 : ∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ then .leaf false
      else if _h2 : ∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ then .leaf true
      else
        match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
        | none => .leaf false
        | some t =>
          termSubTree t ρ (fun ρ' =>
            if decide (Term.fixedBy t ρ') then .leaf true
            else go f fuel ρ')
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def razborovEncode {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (ρ : Restriction n) : Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool) :=
  let path := (canonicalDTree f ρ).deepPath.take d
  razborovEncode.go f w (path.length + 1) path ρ ρ []
where
  /-- Main encoding loop: find the first non-killed clause, process ALL of its
      free literals against the path, emit a termination marker, and repeat. -/
  go (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ) :
      ℕ → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
      List (ℕ × Bool) → Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
    | _, [], _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | 0, _, _, σ, acc => (σ, acc)
    | fuel + 1, step :: rest, ρ₀, σ, acc =>
      let path := step :: rest
      match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ₀)) with
      | none => (σ, acc)
      | some t =>
        let freeLitsIdx := (t.zipIdx).filter (fun ⟨l, _⟩ => decide (l.var ∈ ρ₀.freeVars))
        match freeLitsIdx with
        | [] => (σ, acc)
        | fl :: fls =>
          let r := processClauseLits (fl :: fls) path ρ₀ σ
          go f w fuel r.1 r.2.1 r.2.2.1 (acc ++ r.2.2.2 ++ [(w, false)])
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem razborovEncode_injective {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w d : ℕ)
    (ρ₁ ρ₂ : Restriction n)
    (hbad₁ : IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ₁) (hbad₂ : IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ₂)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (henc : razborovEncode f w d ρ₁ = razborovEncode f w d ρ₂) :
    ρ₁ = ρ₂ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Injectivity of the Razborov encoding
statement_informal:
Injectivity of the Razborov encoding. The Razborov encoding is injective on bad restrictions: for a width-$w$ DNF $f$ with
distinct variables per term, if $\rho_1$ and $\rho_2$ are both bad restrictions for
$f.\mathrm{eval}$ at depth $d$ and their encodings agree,
$\mathrm{razborovEncode}\ f\ w\ d\ \rho_1 = \mathrm{razborovEncode}\ f\ w\ d\ \rho_2$,
then $\rho_1 = \rho_2$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Deepest path of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Canonical decision tree
definitions.informal:
The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Bad restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

definitions.title: Razborov encoding
definitions.informal:
The Razborov encoding of a DNF $f$ and a restriction $\rho$ with parameters $w, d$.
It takes the first $d$ steps of the deepest path of the canonical decision tree for
$f|_\rho$, then repeatedly selects the first clause of $f$ not killed by the current
restriction, processes all of that clause's free literals against the path, and emits
a termination marker $(w, \mathrm{false})$. The result is a pair $(\gamma, \mathrm{aux})$,
where $\gamma$ extends $\rho$ by fixing $d$ variables to their satisfying directions
and $\mathrm{aux}$ consists of per-clause blocks separated by the termination markers.

- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

Statement: Round-trip base case. When the encoder returns $(\sigma, [])$ (an empty emitted list), the decoder loop
that $\sigma$ and the decoder's starting restriction $\sigma_{\mathrm{dec}}$ agree with
$\sigma$ on both free and non-free variables and that $\sigma$ is unset wherever
$\rho_0$ is unset.

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma roundtrip_base {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (w : ℕ)
    (ρ₀ σ σ_dec ρ₀_dec : Restriction n) (dec_fuel : ℕ)
    (hE : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none → σ v = none)
    (hA : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none → σ_dec v = σ v)
    (hC : ∀ v, ρ₀ v ≠ none → σ_dec v = σ v) :
    (razborovDecode.go f w dec_fuel σ_dec ρ₀_dec []).1 = σ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Round-trip base case
statement_informal:
Round-trip base case. When the encoder returns $(\sigma, [])$ (an empty emitted list), the decoder loop
that $\sigma$ and the decoder's starting restriction $\sigma_{\mathrm{dec}}$ agree with
$\sigma$ on both free and non-free variables and that $\sigma$ is unset wherever
$\rho_0$ is unset.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

Statement: Round-trip invariant for $\sigma$. Under the freeness and membership hypotheses on $\mathit{lits}$ and the agreement of
$\sigma_{\mathrm{dec}}$ with $\sigma$ on variables set by $\rho_0$, the $\sigma$-fold over
the aux entries (which clears $l.\mathrm{var}$ for each dropped head literal $l$) applied to
$\sigma_{\mathrm{dec}}$ agrees with $\sigma$ at every variable $v$ where the processed
restriction $(\mathrm{processClauseLits}\ \mathit{lits}\ \mathit{path}\ \rho_0\ \sigma).2.1$
is set.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
      (Function.update σ l.var (some (!l.neg)))
    (r.1, r.2.1, r.2.2.1, (idx, dir) :: r.2.2.2)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma roundtrip_inv_hC' {n : ℕ}
    (t_clause : Term n)
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ))
    (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ σ_dec : Restriction n)
    (hfree_lits : ∀ p ∈ lits, ρ₀ p.1.var = none)
    (hmem_zip : ∀ p ∈ lits, p ∈ t_clause.zipIdx)
    (hE : ∀ v, ρ₀ v = none → σ v = none)
    (hC : ∀ v, ρ₀ v ≠ none → σ_dec v = σ v)
    (v : Fin n)
    (hv : (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 v ≠ none) :
    (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.2.2.foldl
      (fun σ' (e : ℕ × Bool) =>
        match t_clause.drop e.1 with | [] => σ' | l :: _ => Function.update σ' l.var none)
      σ_dec v = σ v := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Round-trip invariant for $\sigma$
statement_informal:
Round-trip invariant for $\sigma$. Under the freeness and membership hypotheses on $\mathit{lits}$ and the agreement of
$\sigma_{\mathrm{dec}}$ with $\sigma$ on variables set by $\rho_0$, the $\sigma$-fold over
the aux entries (which clears $l.\mathrm{var}$ for each dropped head literal $l$) applied to
$\sigma_{\mathrm{dec}}$ agrees with $\sigma$ at every variable $v$ where the processed
restriction $(\mathrm{processClauseLits}\ \mathit{lits}\ \mathit{path}\ \rho_0\ \sigma).2.1$
is set.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Process one clause's free literals against the path. Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

Statement: Round-trip invariant for $\rho_0$. Under the freeness and membership hypotheses on $\mathit{lits}$ and the agreement of
$\rho_{0,\mathrm{dec}}$ with $\rho_0$ on variables set by $\rho_0$, the $\rho_0$-fold over
the aux entries (which sets $l.\mathrm{var}$ to $e.2$ for each dropped head literal $l$)
applied to $\rho_{0,\mathrm{dec}}$ agrees with the processed restriction
$(\mathrm{processClauseLits}\ \mathit{lits}\ \mathit{path}\ \rho_0\ \sigma).2.1$ at every
variable $v$ where that restriction is set.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def processClauseLits {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n × ℕ) → List (Fin n × Bool) → Restriction n → Restriction n →
    List (Fin n × Bool) × Restriction n × Restriction n × List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [], path, ρ₀, σ => (path, ρ₀, σ, [])
  | _, [], ρ₀, σ => ([], ρ₀, σ, [])
  | (l, idx) :: restLits, (_, dir) :: restPath, ρ₀, σ =>
    let r := processClauseLits restLits restPath
      (Function.update ρ₀ l.var (some dir))
      (Function.update σ l.var (some (!l.neg)))
    (r.1, r.2.1, r.2.2.1, (idx, dir) :: r.2.2.2)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma roundtrip_inv_hD' {n : ℕ}
    (t_clause : Term n)
    (lits : List (Literal n × ℕ))
    (path : List (Fin n × Bool))
    (ρ₀ σ ρ₀_dec : Restriction n)
    (hfree_lits : ∀ p ∈ lits, ρ₀ p.1.var = none)
    (hmem_zip : ∀ p ∈ lits, p ∈ t_clause.zipIdx)
    (hD : ∀ v, ρ₀ v ≠ none → ρ₀_dec v = ρ₀ v)
    (v : Fin n)
    (hv : (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 v ≠ none) :
    (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.2.2.foldl
      (fun ρ₀' (e : ℕ × Bool) =>
        match t_clause.drop e.1 with | [] => ρ₀' | l :: _ => Function.update ρ₀' l.var (some e.2))
      ρ₀_dec v = (processClauseLits lits path ρ₀ σ).2.1 v := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Round-trip invariant for $\rho_0$
statement_informal:
Round-trip invariant for $\rho_0$. Under the freeness and membership hypotheses on $\mathit{lits}$ and the agreement of
$\rho_{0,\mathrm{dec}}$ with $\rho_0$ on variables set by $\rho_0$, the $\rho_0$-fold over
the aux entries (which sets $l.\mathrm{var}$ to $e.2$ for each dropped head literal $l$)
applied to $\rho_{0,\mathrm{dec}}$ agrees with the processed restriction
$(\mathrm{processClauseLits}\ \mathit{lits}\ \mathit{path}\ \rho_0\ \sigma).2.1$ at every
variable $v$ where that restriction is set.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Process one clause's free literals against the path
definitions.informal:
Consumes the free literals of a single clause (each paired with its position index)
against the canonical decision-tree path, one path entry per literal. For each free
literal it fixes the literal's variable to its satisfying direction in $\sigma$,
records the path direction in $\rho_0$ (mirroring the canonical decision-tree
branching), and appends the pair (literal position in clause, path direction) to the
auxiliary data. It returns the remaining path together with the updated $\rho_0$,
$\sigma$, and the accumulated clause auxiliary data.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Literal fixed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.
- Literal killed by a restriction. A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Term fixed by a restriction. A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.
- Term killed by a restriction. A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Canonical decision tree. The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.
- Branch variable selection. Given a DNF $f$ and restriction $\rho$, returns an optional variable in $\mathrm{Fin}\,n$:
the variable on which the canonical tree should next branch, drawn from the free variables
of the first term not killed by $\rho$.

Statement: Specification of branch variable selection. When not every term of $f$ is killed by $\rho$ and no term is fixed by $\rho$, the function
$\mathrm{selectBranchVar}\,f\,\rho$ returns some variable $v$, and that $v$ is free in $\rho$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some (!l.neg)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Literal.killedBy {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ l.var = some l.neg
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.fixedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∀ l ∈ t, Literal.fixedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def Term.killedBy {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ∃ l ∈ t, Literal.killedBy l ρ
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def selectBranchVar {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Option (Fin n) :=
  match f.find? (fun t => decide (¬Term.killedBy t ρ)) with
  | none => none
  | some t =>
    match t.find? (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars)) with
    | none => none
    | some l => some l.var
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma selectBranchVar_spec {n : ℕ} (f : DNF n) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (h1 : ¬∀ t ∈ f, Term.killedBy t ρ) (h2 : ¬∃ t ∈ f, Term.fixedBy t ρ) :
    ∃ v, selectBranchVar f ρ = some v ∧ v ∈ ρ.freeVars := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Specification of branch variable selection
statement_informal:
Specification of branch variable selection. When not every term of $f$ is killed by $\rho$ and no term is fixed by $\rho$, the function
$\mathrm{selectBranchVar}\,f\,\rho$ returns some variable $v$, and that $v$ is free in $\rho$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Literal fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is fixed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ true; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,(\lnot\, l.\mathrm{neg})$.

definitions.title: Literal killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A literal $l$ is killed by $\rho$ when $\rho$ fixes its variable to the value that makes
$l$ false; concretely $\rho(l.\mathrm{var}) = \mathrm{some}\,l.\mathrm{neg}$.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Term fixed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term is fixed by $\rho$ when every one of its literals is fixed by $\rho$, forcing the
whole term to evaluate to true.

definitions.title: Term killed by a restriction
definitions.informal:
A term (conjunction of literals) is killed by $\rho$ when at least one of its literals is
killed by $\rho$, forcing the whole term to evaluate to false.

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Canonical decision tree
definitions.informal:
The canonical decision tree for $f|_\rho$, following Razborov's construction. It is defined
through a fuel-driven helper $\mathrm{canonicalDTree.go}$ initialized with fuel
$\rho.\mathrm{numFree} + \text{(positive)}$, repeatedly selecting the first non-killed term and
expanding its free variables.

definitions.title: Branch variable selection
definitions.informal:
Given a DNF $f$ and restriction $\rho$, returns an optional variable in $\mathrm{Fin}\,n$:
the variable on which the canonical tree should next branch, drawn from the free variables
of the first term not killed by $\rho$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Number of $s$-restrictions. The number of restrictions on $n$ variables leaving exactly $s$ free, namely
$\binom{n}{s}\, 2^{\,n-s}$.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

Statement: Switching corollary. Under the same hypotheses as Theorem~\ref{thm:switching_lemma},
\[
  \bigl|\{\rho : \mathrm{IsRestriction}\ s\ \rho,\ f|_\rho
    \text{ has no CNF of width}\le w\}\bigr| \cdot n^w
  \le \mathrm{numSRestrictions}(n,s)\,(10sw)^w.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def numSRestrictions (n s : ℕ) : ℕ := n.choose s * 2 ^ (n - s)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_corollary {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (f : DNF n) (w s : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (hs : 5 * s ≤ n)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ f, t.Nodup) :
    (Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n =>
        IsRestriction s ρ ∧
        ¬ ∃ ψ : CNF n, ψ.width ≤ w ∧
          ∀ x, ψ.eval x = restrictFn f.eval ρ x).card * n ^ w ≤
    numSRestrictions n s * (10 * s * w) ^ w := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Switching corollary
statement_informal:
Switching corollary. Under the same hypotheses as Theorem~\ref{thm:switching_lemma},
\[
  \bigl|\{\rho : \mathrm{IsRestriction}\ s\ \rho,\ f|_\rho
    \text{ has no CNF of width}\le w\}\bigr| \cdot n^w
  \le \mathrm{numSRestrictions}(n,s)\,(10sw)^w.
\]

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Restriction with $s$ free variables
definitions.informal:
The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Number of $s$-restrictions. The number of restrictions on $n$ variables leaving exactly $s$ free, namely
$\binom{n}{s}\, 2^{\,n-s}$.
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

Statement: Switching Lemma -- Lean statement. Let $n>0$, let $f:\{0,1\}^n\to\{0,1\}$ be a DNF formula, and fix $w,s,d\in\bbn$.
Assume
\begin{itemize}
  \item $\text{width}(f)\le w$ (width counted as list length, see
        Section~\ref{sec:faithfulness}),
  \item $5s \le n$,
  \item $f$ satisfies the \emph{non-contradictory clause} hypothesis
        \texttt{hnd}: for every term $t\in f$ and every pair of literals
        $\ell_1,\ell_2\in t$, if $\ell_1.\mathrm{var}=\ell_2.\mathrm{var}$ then
        $\ell_1=\ell_2$.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
  \bigl|\{\,\rho : \rho\text{ is an $s$-restriction and }
        \mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho)>d\,\}\bigr| \cdot n^d
  \;\le\;
  \mathrm{numSRestrictions}(n,s)\,\cdot\,(10\,s\,w)^d.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def numSRestrictions (n s : ℕ) : ℕ := n.choose s * 2 ^ (n - s)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_lemma {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (f : DNF n) (w s d : ℕ)
    (hw : f.width ≤ w) (hs : 5 * s ≤ n)
    (hnd : ∀ t ∈ f, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ t ∈ f, t.Nodup) :
    (Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n =>
        IsRestriction s ρ ∧ IsBadRestriction f.eval d ρ).card * n ^ d ≤
    numSRestrictions n s * (10 * s * w) ^ d := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Switching Lemma -- Lean statement
statement_informal:
Switching Lemma -- Lean statement. Let $n>0$, let $f:\{0,1\}^n\to\{0,1\}$ be a DNF formula, and fix $w,s,d\in\bbn$.
Assume
\begin{itemize}
  \item $\text{width}(f)\le w$ (width counted as list length, see
        Section~\ref{sec:faithfulness}),
  \item $5s \le n$,
  \item $f$ satisfies the \emph{non-contradictory clause} hypothesis
        \texttt{hnd}: for every term $t\in f$ and every pair of literals
        $\ell_1,\ell_2\in t$, if $\ell_1.\mathrm{var}=\ell_2.\mathrm{var}$ then
        $\ell_1=\ell_2$.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
  \bigl|\{\,\rho : \rho\text{ is an $s$-restriction and }
        \mathrm{dtDepth}(f|_\rho)>d\,\}\bigr| \cdot n^d
  \;\le\;
  \mathrm{numSRestrictions}(n,s)\,\cdot\,(10\,s\,w)^d.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

Statement: Unfolding \texttt{termSubTree} at a free head. When the head literal $l$ is free in $\rho$, $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,(l :: \mathit{rest})\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$
is a branch on $l.\mathrm{var}$ whose two children are the recursive $\mathrm{termSubTree}$ calls on
$\mathit{rest}$ with $\rho$ updated to $\mathrm{some}\ \mathrm{false}$ and $\mathrm{some}\ \mathrm{true}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma termSubTree_cons_free {n : ℕ}
    (l : Literal n) (rest : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
    (hfree : l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars) :
    termSubTree (l :: rest) ρ cont = .branch l.var
      (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
      (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont) := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Unfolding \texttt{termSubTree} at a free head
statement_informal:
Unfolding \texttt{termSubTree} at a free head. When the head literal $l$ is free in $\rho$, $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,(l :: \mathit{rest})\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$
is a branch on $l.\mathrm{var}$ whose two children are the recursive $\mathrm{termSubTree}$ calls on
$\mathit{rest}$ with $\rho$ updated to $\mathrm{some}\ \mathrm{false}$ and $\mathrm{some}\ \mathrm{true}$.

definitions.informal:
Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

Statement: Unfolding \texttt{termSubTree} at a non-free head. When the head literal $l$ is not free in $\rho$, it is skipped:
$\mathrm{termSubTree}\,(l :: \mathit{rest})\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont} = \mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{rest}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

  var : Fin n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma termSubTree_cons_nonfree {n : ℕ}
    (l : Literal n) (rest : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
    (hnfree : l.var ∉ ρ.freeVars) :
    termSubTree (l :: rest) ρ cont = termSubTree rest ρ cont := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: Unfolding \texttt{termSubTree} at a non-free head
statement_informal:
Unfolding \texttt{termSubTree} at a non-free head. When the head literal $l$ is not free in $\rho$, it is skipped:
$\mathrm{termSubTree}\,(l :: \mathit{rest})\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont} = \mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{rest}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$.

Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each

Statement: \texttt{termSubTree} is extensional in its continuation. If two continuations $\mathrm{cont}_1, \mathrm{cont}_2$ agree on all restrictions $\rho'$ with
$\rho'.\mathrm{numFree} \le \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, then $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}_1 = \mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}_2$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma termSubTree_cont_congr {n : ℕ} :
    ∀ (lits : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
      (cont₁ cont₂ : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
      (_ : ∀ ρ', ρ'.numFree ≤ ρ.numFree → cont₁ ρ' = cont₂ ρ'),
      termSubTree lits ρ cont₁ = termSubTree lits ρ cont₂
  | [], ρ, cont₁, cont₂, hcont => by
      show cont₁ ρ = cont₂ ρ
      exact hcont ρ (le_refl _)
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont₁, cont₂, hcont => by
      by_cases hfree : l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars
      · simp only [termSubTree, hfree, ↓reduceIte]
        congr 1
        · apply termSubTree_cont_congr rest _ cont₁ cont₂
          intro ρ' hρ'
          exact hcont ρ' (le_trans hρ'
            (numFree_update_le ρ l.var false))
        · apply termSubTree_cont_congr rest _ cont₁ cont₂
          intro ρ' hρ'
          exact hcont ρ' (le_trans hρ'
            (numFree_update_le ρ l.var true))
      · simp only [termSubTree, hfree, ↓reduceIte]
        exact termSubTree_cont_congr rest ρ cont₁ cont₂ hcont := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: \texttt{termSubTree} is extensional in its continuation
statement_informal:
\texttt{termSubTree} is extensional in its continuation. If two continuations $\mathrm{cont}_1, \mathrm{cont}_2$ agree on all restrictions $\rho'$ with
$\rho'.\mathrm{numFree} \le \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$, then $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}_1 = \mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}_2$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Sub-tree for a term
definitions.informal:
Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

Statement: \texttt{termSubTree} extensionality, strict version. If $\mathit{lits}$ contains at least one literal free in $\rho$, then the two continuations need
only agree on restrictions $\rho'$ with $\rho'.\mathrm{numFree} < \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$ for the two
$\mathrm{termSubTree}$ trees to be equal.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma termSubTree_cont_congr_strict {n : ℕ} :
    ∀ (lits : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
      (_ : ∃ l ∈ lits, l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars)
      (cont₁ cont₂ : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
      (_ : ∀ ρ', ρ'.numFree < ρ.numFree → cont₁ ρ' = cont₂ ρ'),
      termSubTree lits ρ cont₁ = termSubTree lits ρ cont₂
  | [], _, hex, _, _, _ => by
      obtain ⟨_, hl, _⟩ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: \texttt{termSubTree} extensionality, strict version
statement_informal:
\texttt{termSubTree} extensionality, strict version. If $\mathit{lits}$ contains at least one literal free in $\rho$, then the two continuations need
only agree on restrictions $\rho'$ with $\rho'.\mathrm{numFree} < \rho.\mathrm{numFree}$ for the two
$\mathrm{termSubTree}$ trees to be equal.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each

the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

Statement: \texttt{deepPath} length decomposition for \texttt{termSubTree}. For $\mathit{lits}$ with pairwise distinct variables there is a restriction $\rho'$, agreeing with
$\rho$ off the variables of $\mathit{lits}$, such that the deep-path length of
$\mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$ equals the number of free literals of $\mathit{lits}$
plus the deep-path length of $\mathrm{cont}\,\rho'$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma termSubTree_deepPath_append {n : ℕ} :
    ∀ (lits : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
      (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
      (_hdistinct : lits.Pairwise (fun l₁ l₂ => l₁.var ≠ l₂.var)),
      ∃ (ρ' : Restriction n),
        (∀ v, v ∉ (lits.map Literal.var).toFinset → (ρ' v = ρ v)) ∧
        (termSubTree lits ρ cont).deepPath.length =
          (lits.filter (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars))).length +
          (cont ρ').deepPath.length := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

plus the deep-path length of $\mathrm{cont}\,\rho'$.

Statement: \texttt{deepPath} head extraction for \texttt{termSubTree}. When the head literal $l$ is free in $\rho$, the deepest root-to-leaf path of
$\mathrm{termSubTree}\,(l :: \mathit{rest})\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$ begins with $(l.\mathrm{var}, b)$ for some
$b$ (the direction of the deeper child), followed by the deep path of the recursive call on
$\rho[l.\mathrm{var} \mapsto b]$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>

      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.deepPath {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → List (Fin n × Bool)
  | .leaf _ => []
  | .branch v lo hi =>
    if hi.depth ≥ lo.depth then
      (v, true) :: hi.deepPath
    else
      (v, false) :: lo.deepPath

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma termSubTree_deepPath_head_free {n : ℕ}
    (l : Literal n) (rest : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
    (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
    (hfree : l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars) :
    ∃ b, (termSubTree (l :: rest) ρ cont).deepPath =
      (l.var, b) :: (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some b)) cont).deepPath := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2

$b$ (the direction of the deeper child), followed by the deep path of the recursive call on

variable {n : ℕ}

    ∀ (lits : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
      (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)

    ∀ (lits : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n)
      (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n)
      (_hdistinct : lits.Pairwise (fun l₁ l₂ => l₁.var ≠ l₂.var))
      (k : ℕ)
      (hk : k < (lits.filter (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars))).length)
      (hk_path : k < (termSubTree lits ρ cont).deepPath.length),
      ((termSubTree lits ρ cont).deepPath[k]'hk_path).1 =
        ((lits.filter (fun l => decide (l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars)))[k]'hk).var := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: \texttt{deepPath} variables match the free literals
statement_informal:
\texttt{deepPath} variables match the free literals. For $\mathit{lits}$ with pairwise distinct variables, the $k$-th variable of the deep path of
$\mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$ equals the variable of the $k$-th literal in the
free-filtered list of $\mathit{lits}$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Sub-tree for a term
definitions.informal:
Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

definitions.title: Deepest path of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Extracts a deepest root-to-leaf path from a decision tree, at each branch descending into
the deeper subtree (ties broken toward the high subtree). It returns the list of
$(\text{queried variable}, \text{branch direction})$ pairs along that path.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Sub-tree for a term. Builds a complete sub-tree for a term, queried as a list of literals: it branches on each
free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

Statement: \texttt{termSubTree} preserves semantics. Evaluating $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$ at $x$ equals evaluating the
continuation at $x$, applied to the restriction obtained by folding $x$'s values into $\rho$ along
the free literals of $\mathit{lits}$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
noncomputable def termSubTree {n : ℕ} :
    List (Literal n) → Restriction n →
    (Restriction n → DecisionTree n) → DecisionTree n
  | [], ρ, cont => cont ρ
  | l :: rest, ρ, cont =>
    if l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars then
      .branch l.var
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some false)) cont)
        (termSubTree rest (Function.update ρ l.var (some true)) cont)
    else
      termSubTree rest ρ cont
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma termSubTree_eval {n : ℕ} (lits : List (Literal n))
    (ρ : Restriction n) (cont : Restriction n → DecisionTree n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    (termSubTree lits ρ cont).eval x =
    (cont (lits.foldl (fun ρ' l =>
      if l.var ∈ ρ'.freeVars then Function.update ρ' l.var (some (x l.var)) else ρ') ρ)).eval x := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: \texttt{termSubTree} preserves semantics
statement_informal:
\texttt{termSubTree} preserves semantics. Evaluating $\mathrm{termSubTree}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho\,\mathrm{cont}$ at $x$ equals evaluating the
continuation at $x$, applied to the restriction obtained by folding $x$'s values into $\rho$ along
the free literals of $\mathit{lits}$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

Statement: \texttt{termSubTree} fold preserves extension. After $\mathrm{termSubTree}$ assigns all free variables of $\mathit{lits}$ from $x$, the resulting
restriction extends by $x$ to the same total assignment as $\rho$ does: $(\mathrm{fold}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho).\mathrm{extend}\,x = \rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma termSubTree_extend_eq {n : ℕ} (lits : List (Literal n))
    (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    (lits.foldl (fun (ρ' : Restriction n) l =>
      if l.var ∈ ρ'.freeVars then Function.update ρ' l.var (some (x l.var))
      else ρ') ρ).extend x = ρ.extend x := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: \texttt{termSubTree} fold preserves extension
statement_informal:
\texttt{termSubTree} fold preserves extension. After $\mathrm{termSubTree}$ assigns all free variables of $\mathit{lits}$ from $x$, the resulting
restriction extends by $x$ to the same total assignment as $\rho$ does: $(\mathrm{fold}\,\mathit{lits}\,\rho).\mathrm{extend}\,x = \rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$.

definitions.informal:

- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,

Statement: Fold strictly decreases the free count. When at least one literal of $\mathit{lits}$ has a variable free in $\rho$, the $\mathrm{termSubTree}$
fold strictly decreases $\mathrm{numFree}$.

variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma termSubTree_foldl_numFree_lt {n : ℕ}
    (lits : List (Literal n)) (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool)
    (l : Literal n) (hl : l ∈ lits) (hfree : l.var ∈ ρ.freeVars) :
    (lits.foldl (fun (ρ' : Restriction n) (lit : Literal n) =>
      if lit.var ∈ Restriction.freeVars ρ' then Function.update ρ' lit.var (some (x lit.var))
      else ρ') ρ).numFree < ρ.numFree := sorry

free variable of the term in order, and at every leaf invokes a continuation
$\mathrm{cont} : \mathrm{Restriction}\,n \to \mathrm{DecisionTree}\,n$ with the restriction updated
along that root-to-leaf path. Non-free literals are skipped.

coordinates are $< w$, into a list of $(\mathrm{Fin}\,w \times \mathrm{Bool})$.

Statement: toFinBlock preserves length. $(\mathrm{toFinBlock}\,w\,l\,h).\mathrm{length} = l.\mathrm{length}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def toFinBlock (w : ℕ) :
    ∀ (l : List (ℕ × Bool)) (_ : ∀ e ∈ l, e.1 < w), List (Fin w × Bool)
  | [], _ => []
  | (idx, dir) :: rest, h =>
    (⟨idx, h (idx, dir) (List.mem_cons_self)⟩, dir) ::
      toFinBlock w rest (fun e he => h e (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ he))
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma toFinBlock_length (w : ℕ) (l : List (ℕ × Bool))
    (h : ∀ e ∈ l, e.1 < w) :
    (toFinBlock w l h).length = l.length := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: toFinBlock preserves length
statement_informal: toFinBlock preserves length. $(\mathrm{toFinBlock}\,w\,l\,h).\mathrm{length} = l.\mathrm{length}$.

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Triples back to an aux list. The inverse direction of SwitchingLemma2.parseAux: converts a list of
triples back into an aux list of $(\mathbb{N} \times \mathrm{Bool})$ entries,
emitting a $(w, \mathrm{false})$ termination marker after each triple whose
marker flag is true.

Statement: triplesToAux distributes over append. $\mathrm{triplesToAux}\,(ts_1 \mathbin{+\!\!+} ts_2) = \mathrm{triplesToAux}\,ts_1 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \mathrm{triplesToAux}\,ts_2$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def triplesToAux (w : ℕ) :
    List (Fin w × Bool × Bool) → List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [] => []
  | (pos, dir, true) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: (w, false) :: triplesToAux w rest
  | (pos, dir, false) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: triplesToAux w rest
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma triplesToAux_append (w : ℕ)
    (ts₁ ts₂ : List (Fin w × Bool × Bool)) :
    triplesToAux w (ts₁ ++ ts₂) = triplesToAux w ts₁ ++ triplesToAux w ts₂ := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: triplesToAux distributes over append
statement_informal: triplesToAux distributes over append. $\mathrm{triplesToAux}\,(ts_1 \mathbin{+\!\!+} ts_2) = \mathrm{triplesToAux}\,ts_1 \mathbin{+\!\!+} \mathrm{triplesToAux}\,ts_2$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Mark the last entry of a block. Builds a triple list from a $\mathrm{Fin}\,w \times \mathrm{Bool}$ block by
setting the marker flag to true on the last entry only and to false on all
others.
- Triples back to an aux list. The inverse direction of SwitchingLemma2.parseAux: converts a list of
triples back into an aux list of $(\mathbb{N} \times \mathrm{Bool})$ entries,
emitting a $(w, \mathrm{false})$ termination marker after each triple whose
marker flag is true.

Statement: triplesToAux of markLast. On a nonempty block, $\mathrm{triplesToAux}(\mathrm{markLast}\,block)$ equals the
block mapped into $\mathbb{N} \times \mathrm{Bool}$ followed by a single
$(w, \mathrm{false})$ termination marker.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def markLast {w : ℕ} :
    List (Fin w × Bool) → List (Fin w × Bool × Bool)
  | [] => []
  | hd :: [] => [(hd.1, hd.2, true)]
  | hd :: (hd2 :: rest) => (hd.1, hd.2, false) :: markLast (hd2 :: rest)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private def triplesToAux (w : ℕ) :
    List (Fin w × Bool × Bool) → List (ℕ × Bool)
  | [] => []
  | (pos, dir, true) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: (w, false) :: triplesToAux w rest
  | (pos, dir, false) :: rest =>
    (pos.val, dir) :: triplesToAux w rest
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
private lemma triplesToAux_markLast (w : ℕ)
    (block : List (Fin w × Bool)) (hne : block ≠ []) :
    triplesToAux w (markLast block) =
      block.map (fun p => (p.1.val, p.2)) ++ [(w, false)] := sorry
end SwitchingLemma2
title: triplesToAux of markLast
statement_informal:
triplesToAux of markLast. On a nonempty block, $\mathrm{triplesToAux}(\mathrm{markLast}\,block)$ equals the
block mapped into $\mathbb{N} \times \mathrm{Bool}$ followed by a single
$(w, \mathrm{false})$ termination marker.

end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2

private lemma zipIdx_filter_getElem_fst {n : ℕ} (t : Term n)
    (p : Literal n → Bool) (k : ℕ)

import Mathlib

lemma zipIdx_find_to_find {α : Type*} (l : List α) (p : α → Bool)
    (x : α) (idx : ℕ) (start : ℕ := 0)
    (h : (l.zipIdx start).find? (fun ⟨a, _⟩ => p a) = some ⟨x, idx⟩) :
    l.find? p = some x := sorry
