informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

Statement: Bad restriction is invariant under negation. $\mathrm{IsBadRestriction}(\lnot f)\,d\,\rho \iff \mathrm{IsBadRestriction}\,f\,d\,\rho$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemmaCNF
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma IsBadRestriction_neg {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    IsBadRestriction (fun x => !(f x)) d ρ ↔ IsBadRestriction f d ρ := sorry
end SwitchingLemmaCNF
title: Bad restriction is invariant under negation
statement_informal: Bad restriction is invariant under negation. $\mathrm{IsBadRestriction}(\lnot f)\,d\,\rho \iff \mathrm{IsBadRestriction}\,f\,d\,\rho$.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: Bad restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

Statement: Dual DNF preserves variable-distinctness. If within each clause of $\psi$ any two literals with the same variable are equal,
then the same holds within every term of $\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def Literal.flipNeg {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.var, !l.neg⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

def cnfToDualDNF {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n) : DNF n :=
  ψ.map (fun clause => clause.map Literal.flipNeg)

namespace SwitchingLemmaCNF
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cnfToDualDNF_inj {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n)
    (h : ∀ c ∈ ψ, ∀ l₁ ∈ c, ∀ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂) :
    ∀ t ∈ cnfToDualDNF ψ, ∀ l₁ ∈ t, ∀ l₂ ∈ t, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂ := sorry
end SwitchingLemmaCNF
title: Dual DNF preserves variable-distinctness
statement_informal:
Dual DNF preserves variable-distinctness. If within each clause of $\psi$ any two literals with the same variable are equal,
then the same holds within every term of $\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi$.

definitions.title: Flip a literal's polarity
definitions.informal: Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.

definitions.title: CNF to De Morgan dual DNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

Statement: Dual DNF preserves no-duplicate clauses. If every clause of $\psi$ has no duplicate literals, then every term of
$\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi$ has no duplicate literals.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def Literal.flipNeg {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.var, !l.neg⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

def cnfToDualDNF {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n) : DNF n :=
  ψ.map (fun clause => clause.map Literal.flipNeg)

namespace SwitchingLemmaCNF
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma cnfToDualDNF_nodup {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n)
    (h : ∀ c ∈ ψ, c.Nodup) :
    ∀ t ∈ cnfToDualDNF ψ, t.Nodup := sorry
end SwitchingLemmaCNF
title: Dual DNF preserves no-duplicate clauses
statement_informal:
Dual DNF preserves no-duplicate clauses. If every clause of $\psi$ has no duplicate literals, then every term of
$\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi$ has no duplicate literals.

definitions.title: Flip a literal's polarity
definitions.informal: Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.

definitions.title: CNF to De Morgan dual DNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

Statement: Restriction commutes with negation. $\mathrm{restrictFn}(\lnot f)\,\rho = \lnot\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho)$,
pointwise.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemmaCNF
variable {n : ℕ}
lemma restrictFn_neg {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    restrictFn (fun x => !(f x)) ρ = fun x => !(restrictFn f ρ x) := sorry
end SwitchingLemmaCNF
title: Restriction commutes with negation
statement_informal:
Restriction commutes with negation. $\mathrm{restrictFn}(\lnot f)\,\rho = \lnot\,(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho)$,
pointwise.

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Number of $s$-restrictions. The number of restrictions on $n$ variables leaving exactly $s$ free, namely
$\binom{n}{s}\, 2^{\,n-s}$.
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

Statement: Switching Lemma Corollary for CNFs. For a CNF $\psi$ of width at most $w$ with $5s \leq n$ and well-formed clauses, the
number of $s$-restrictions $\rho$ for which $\psi|_\rho$ cannot be represented by a
DNF of width $\leq w$ satisfies
\[
  \abs{\{\rho : \mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho \wedge \lnot\,\exists \varphi,\ \varphi.\mathrm{width} \leq w \wedge \varphi \equiv \psi|_\rho\}} \cdot n^w
  \;\leq\; \mathrm{numSRestrictions}\,n\,s \cdot (10\,s\,w)^w .
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def numSRestrictions (n s : ℕ) : ℕ := n.choose s * 2 ^ (n - s)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemmaCNF
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_corollary_cnf {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (ψ : CNF n) (w s : ℕ)
    (hw : ψ.width ≤ w) (hs : 5 * s ≤ n)
    (hnd : ∀ c ∈ ψ, ∀ l₁ ∈ c, ∀ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ c ∈ ψ, c.Nodup) :
    (Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n =>
        IsRestriction s ρ ∧
        ¬ ∃ φ : DNF n, φ.width ≤ w ∧
          ∀ x, φ.eval x = restrictFn ψ.eval ρ x).card * n ^ w ≤
    numSRestrictions n s * (10 * s * w) ^ w := sorry
end SwitchingLemmaCNF
title: Switching Lemma Corollary for CNFs
statement_informal:
Switching Lemma Corollary for CNFs. For a CNF $\psi$ of width at most $w$ with $5s \leq n$ and well-formed clauses, the
number of $s$-restrictions $\rho$ for which $\psi|_\rho$ cannot be represented by a
DNF of width $\leq w$ satisfies
\[
  \abs{\{\rho : \mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho \wedge \lnot\,\exists \varphi,\ \varphi.\mathrm{width} \leq w \wedge \varphi \equiv \psi|_\rho\}} \cdot n^w
  \;\leq\; \mathrm{numSRestrictions}\,n\,s \cdot (10\,s\,w)^w .
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Restriction. A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).
- Number of $s$-restrictions. The number of restrictions on $n$ variables leaving exactly $s$ free, namely
$\binom{n}{s}\, 2^{\,n-s}$.
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- Extension of a point along a restriction. Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.
- Free variables of a restriction. The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Number of free variables. The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.
- Restricted Boolean function. Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Bad restriction. A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.
- Restriction with $s$ free variables. The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

Statement: Håstad's Switching Lemma for CNFs. For a CNF $\psi$ of width at most $w$ on $n$ variables with $5s \leq n$ and clauses
free of duplicate or conflicting variables, the number of $s$-restrictions $\rho$
under which $\psi|_\rho$ has decision-tree depth $> d$ satisfies
\[
  \abs{\{\rho : \mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho \wedge \mathrm{IsBadRestriction}\,\psi.\mathrm{eval}\,d\,\rho\}} \cdot n^d
  \;\leq\; \mathrm{numSRestrictions}\,n\,s \cdot (10\,s\,w)^d .
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
abbrev Restriction (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Option Bool
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def numSRestrictions (n s : ℕ) : ℕ := n.choose s * 2 ^ (n - s)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def extend {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Fin n → Bool :=
  fun i => (ρ i).getD (x i)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def freeVars {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => (ρ i).isNone)
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

    variable index then they are identical. -/
noncomputable def dtDepth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) : ℕ := by
  classical
  exact Nat.find (p := fun d => ∃ T : DecisionTree n, T.depth ≤ d ∧ ∀ x, T.eval x = f x)
    ⟨n, buildFullDTree f 0 (fun _ => false),
     buildFullDTree_depth f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _,
     fun x => buildFullDTree_eval f 0 (Nat.zero_le n) _ x (fun _ hi => by omega)⟩

namespace SwitchingLemma2.Restriction
variable {n : ℕ}
def numFree {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : ℕ := ρ.freeVars.card
end SwitchingLemma2.Restriction

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def restrictFn {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    (Fin n → Bool) → Bool :=
  fun x => f (ρ.extend x)
end SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsBadRestriction {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool) (d : ℕ) (ρ : Restriction n) :
    Prop :=
  dtDepth (restrictFn f ρ) > d
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemma2
variable {n : ℕ}
def IsRestriction (s : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (ρ : Restriction n) : Prop :=
  ρ.numFree = s
end SwitchingLemma2

namespace SwitchingLemmaCNF
variable {n : ℕ}
theorem switching_lemma_cnf {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (ψ : CNF n) (w s d : ℕ)
    (hw : ψ.width ≤ w) (hs : 5 * s ≤ n)
    (hnd : ∀ c ∈ ψ, ∀ l₁ ∈ c, ∀ l₂ ∈ c, l₁.var = l₂.var → l₁ = l₂)
    (hnodup : ∀ c ∈ ψ, c.Nodup) :
    (Finset.univ.filter fun ρ : Restriction n =>
        IsRestriction s ρ ∧ IsBadRestriction ψ.eval d ρ).card * n ^ d ≤
    numSRestrictions n s * (10 * s * w) ^ d := sorry
end SwitchingLemmaCNF
title: Håstad's Switching Lemma for CNFs
statement_informal:
Håstad's Switching Lemma for CNFs. For a CNF $\psi$ of width at most $w$ on $n$ variables with $5s \leq n$ and clauses
free of duplicate or conflicting variables, the number of $s$-restrictions $\rho$
under which $\psi|_\rho$ has decision-tree depth $> d$ satisfies
\[
  \abs{\{\rho : \mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho \wedge \mathrm{IsBadRestriction}\,\psi.\mathrm{eval}\,d\,\rho\}} \cdot n^d
  \;\leq\; \mathrm{numSRestrictions}\,n\,s \cdot (10\,s\,w)^d .
\]

definitions.title: Restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction on $n$ variables is a map $\rho : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Option}\,\mathrm{Bool}$,
assigning each variable either a fixed Boolean value or leaving it free (none).

definitions.title: Number of $s$-restrictions
definitions.informal:
The number of restrictions on $n$ variables leaving exactly $s$ free, namely
$\binom{n}{s}\, 2^{\,n-s}$.

definitions.title: Flip a literal's polarity
definitions.informal: Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: Extension of a point along a restriction
definitions.informal:
Given a restriction $\rho$ and a point $x : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}$, the
extension $\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x$ is the Boolean assignment that uses the fixed value
$\rho(i)$ where it is set and falls back to $x(i)$ on the free variables.

definitions.title: Free variables of a restriction
definitions.informal:
The set of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the finite set of indices
$i \in \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ for which $\rho(i)$ is unset (none).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Number of free variables
definitions.informal:
The number of free variables of a restriction $\rho$ is the cardinality of its set of
free variables.

definitions.title: Restricted Boolean function
definitions.informal:
Given $f : (\mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{Bool}) \to \mathrm{Bool}$ and a restriction
$\rho$, the restricted function maps $x$ to $f(\rho.\mathrm{extend}\,x)$, i.e.\ $f$
evaluated on the free coordinates with the fixed coordinates set by $\rho$.

definitions.title: CNF to De Morgan dual DNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Width of a CNF formula
definitions.informal:
The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

definitions.title: Bad restriction
definitions.informal:
A restriction $\rho$ is bad for $f$ at depth $d$ when the restricted function
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,f\,\rho$ has decision-tree depth strictly greater than $d$.

definitions.title: Restriction with $s$ free variables
definitions.informal:
The predicate $\mathrm{IsRestriction}\,s\,\rho$ holds when the restriction $\rho$ has
exactly $s$ free variables.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

Statement: Full support is the whole space. $V_{\mathrm{univ}} = \top$, the entire space $V$.

import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Depth of a decision tree. The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

Statement: Depth bound for the full decision tree. For a function $f$, an index $k \le n$, and an accumulator assignment $acc$, the complete
decision tree $\mathrm{buildFullDTree}\,f\,k\,acc$ that queries variables
$k, k+1, \dots, n-1$ in order has depth at most $n - k$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.depth {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → ℕ
  | .leaf _          => 0
  | .branch _ lo hi  => 1 + max lo.depth hi.depth

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

    variable index then they are identical. -/
lemma buildFullDTree_depth {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (_ : k ≤ n) (acc : Fin n → Bool) :
    (buildFullDTree f k acc).depth ≤ n - k := sorry
title: Depth bound for the full decision tree
statement_informal:
Depth bound for the full decision tree. For a function $f$, an index $k \le n$, and an accumulator assignment $acc$, the complete
decision tree $\mathrm{buildFullDTree}\,f\,k\,acc$ that queries variables
$k, k+1, \dots, n-1$ in order has depth at most $n - k$.

definitions.title: Depth of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
The depth of a decision tree (maximum path length to a leaf): a leaf has depth $0$ and a
branch has depth $1$ plus the maximum of its two subtrees' depths.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Evaluation of a decision tree. Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

Statement: Correctness of the full decision tree. For a function $f$, an index $k \le n$, and assignments $acc, x$ agreeing on all
coordinates below $k$ (i.e. $acc\,i = x\,i$ whenever $i.\mathrm{val} < k$), the complete
decision tree $\mathrm{buildFullDTree}\,f\,k\,acc$ evaluates to $f\,x$ on $x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

    variable index then they are identical. -/
inductive DecisionTree (n : ℕ) where
  | leaf   (val : Bool)                            : DecisionTree n
  | branch (var : Fin n) (lo hi : DecisionTree n) : DecisionTree n

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DecisionTree.eval {n : ℕ} : DecisionTree n → (Fin n → Bool) → Bool
  | .leaf b,          _  => b
  | .branch i lo hi,  x  => if x i then hi.eval x else lo.eval x

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def buildFullDTree {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (acc : Fin n → Bool) : DecisionTree n :=
  if h : k < n then
    .branch ⟨k, h⟩
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ false))
      (buildFullDTree f (k + 1) (Function.update acc ⟨k, h⟩ true))
  else
    .leaf (f acc)
termination_by n - k

    variable index then they are identical. -/
lemma buildFullDTree_eval {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → Bool) → Bool)
    (k : ℕ) (hk : k ≤ n) (acc x : Fin n → Bool)
    (hinv : ∀ i : Fin n, i.val < k → acc i = x i) :
    (buildFullDTree f k acc).eval x = f x := sorry
title: Correctness of the full decision tree
statement_informal:
Correctness of the full decision tree. For a function $f$, an index $k \le n$, and assignments $acc, x$ agreeing on all
coordinates below $k$ (i.e. $acc\,i = x\,i$ whenever $i.\mathrm{val} < k$), the complete
decision tree $\mathrm{buildFullDTree}\,f\,k\,acc$ evaluates to $f\,x$ on $x$.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a decision tree
definitions.informal:
Evaluates a decision tree on an input $x$: a leaf returns its stored bit, and a branch
on variable $i$ follows the high subtree when $x_i$ is \texttt{true} and the low subtree
otherwise.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Pauli string. A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.
- Pauli error set. The set of all Pauli strings of weight at most $t$:
\[
\mathcal{E}(n,t) = \bigl\{p : \mathrm{PauliString}\,n \;\big|\; \mathrm{wt}(p) \le t\bigr\}.
\]

Statement: Cardinality of the Pauli error set. \[
|\mathcal{E}(n,t)| \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{t}\binom{n}{i}\,3^i.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

def PauliString (n : ℕ) := Fin n → PauliBasis

def support {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => p i ≠ PauliBasis.I)

def weight {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : ℕ :=
  (support p).card

def PauliErrorsLe (n t : ℕ) : Finset (PauliString n) :=
  Finset.filter (fun p => weight p ≤ t) Finset.univ

theorem card_pauliErrorsLe (n t : ℕ) :
    (PauliErrorsLe n t).card = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range (t + 1), n.choose j * 3 ^ j := sorry
title: Cardinality of the Pauli error set
statement_informal:
Cardinality of the Pauli error set. \[
|\mathcal{E}(n,t)| \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{t}\binom{n}{i}\,3^i.
\]

definitions.title: Pauli string
definitions.informal: A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.

definitions.title: Support and weight
definitions.informal:
The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

definitions.title: Support and weight
definitions.informal:
The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

definitions.title: Pauli error set
definitions.informal:
The set of all Pauli strings of weight at most $t$:
\[
\mathcal{E}(n,t) = \bigl\{p : \mathrm{PauliString}\,n \;\big|\; \mathrm{wt}(p) \le t\bigr\}.
\]

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Embedding of a non-identity Pauli. Embeds a non-identity Pauli ($X$, $Y$, or $Z$) from the three-element type
$\mathrm{PauliNZ}$ into the full Pauli basis $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$.
- Pauli string. A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.
- Pauli string with prescribed support. Given a finset $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ and an assignment $f : S \to \mathrm{PauliNZ}$,
constructs the Pauli string that equals $f(i).\mathrm{toBasis}$ on each $i \in S$ and equals $I$
elsewhere.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.
- Pauli strings with exact support. The finset of all Pauli strings on $n$ qubits whose support equals exactly the given
finset $S$.

Statement: Count of Pauli strings with exact support. The number of Pauli strings with support exactly $S$ is $3^{|S|}$, since each coordinate in $S$
is assigned one of the three non-identity Paulis.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

def PauliString (n : ℕ) := Fin n → PauliBasis

def support {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => p i ≠ PauliBasis.I)

def pauliStringsExactSupport {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Finset (PauliString n) :=
  Finset.filter (fun p => support p = S) Finset.univ

lemma card_pauliStringsExactSupport {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    (pauliStringsExactSupport (n:=n) S).card = 3 ^ S.card := sorry
title: Count of Pauli strings with exact support
statement_informal:
Count of Pauli strings with exact support. The number of Pauli strings with support exactly $S$ is $3^{|S|}$, since each coordinate in $S$
is assigned one of the three non-identity Paulis.

definitions.title: Embedding of a non-identity Pauli
definitions.informal:
Embeds a non-identity Pauli ($X$, $Y$, or $Z$) from the three-element type
$\mathrm{PauliNZ}$ into the full Pauli basis $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$.

definitions.title: Pauli string
definitions.informal: A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.

definitions.title: Pauli string with prescribed support
definitions.informal:
Given a finset $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ and an assignment $f : S \to \mathrm{PauliNZ}$,
constructs the Pauli string that equals $f(i).\mathrm{toBasis}$ on each $i \in S$ and equals $I$
elsewhere.

definitions.title: Support and weight
definitions.informal:
The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

definitions.title: Pauli strings with exact support
definitions.informal:
The finset of all Pauli strings on $n$ qubits whose support equals exactly the given
finset $S$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Complement of $E$. $E^c = \mathrm{Fin}\,n \setminus E$, the complement finset of $E$.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$. For a submodule $S \le V$ and $M \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the submodule $S_M = S \cap V_M$.
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Isotropic submodule. $S$ is \emph{isotropic} if $S \le S^{\perp_\omega}$, i.e.\ $\omega(u,v) = 0$
for all $u,v \in S$.
- Intersection $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$. The submodule $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$.
- Supportable logical operators count $g(M)$. $g(S,M) = \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S^{\perp}_M) - \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S_M)$.

Statement: Cleaning dimension identity. For isotropic $S$,
\[
g(S,M) + g(S,M^c) = 2n - 2\dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S).
\]

import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def E_c (E : Finset (Fin n)) : Finset (Fin n) := Eᶜ

#check E_c

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def S_M (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (M : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) M

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev sym_orth (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).orthogonal S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def IsIsotropic (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Prop :=
  S ≤ sym_orth S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def S_perp_M (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (M : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  sym_orth S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) M

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def g (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (M : Finset (Fin n)) : ℕ :=
  Module.finrank (F p) (S_perp_M S M) - Module.finrank (F p) (S_M S M)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma cleaning_dimension_identity (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (hS : IsIsotropic S) (M : Finset (Fin n)) :
    g S M + g S (E_c M) = 2 * n - 2 * Module.finrank (F p) S := sorry
title: Cleaning dimension identity
statement_informal:
Cleaning dimension identity. For isotropic $S$,
\[
g(S,M) + g(S,M^c) = 2n - 2\dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S).
\]

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Complement of $E$
definitions.informal: $E^c = \mathrm{Fin}\,n \setminus E$, the complement finset of $E$.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$
definitions.informal: For a submodule $S \le V$ and $M \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the submodule $S_M = S \cap V_M$.

definitions.title: Bundled bilinear form
definitions.informal: $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.

definitions.title: Symplectic orthogonal complement
definitions.informal:
For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.

definitions.title: Isotropic submodule
definitions.informal:
$S$ is \emph{isotropic} if $S \le S^{\perp_\omega}$, i.e.\ $\omega(u,v) = 0$
for all $u,v \in S$.

definitions.title: Intersection $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$
definitions.informal: The submodule $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$.

definitions.title: Supportable logical operators count $g(M)$
definitions.informal: $g(S,M) = \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S^{\perp}_M) - \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S_M)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- Evaluation of a literal. For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

Statement: cnfToDualDNF negates the value. $(\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi).\mathrm{eval}\,x = \lnot\,(\psi.\mathrm{eval}\,x)$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def Literal.flipNeg {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.var, !l.neg⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Literal.eval {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  if l.neg then !x l.var else x l.var

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.evalClause {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.any (fun l => l.eval x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.eval {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  t.all (fun l => l.eval x)

def cnfToDualDNF {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n) : DNF n :=
  ψ.map (fun clause => clause.map Literal.flipNeg)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.eval {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  c.all (fun t => CNF.evalClause t x)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.eval {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) : Bool :=
  d.any (fun t => t.eval x)

lemma cnfToDualDNF_eval {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n) (x : Fin n → Bool) :
    (cnfToDualDNF ψ).eval x = !(ψ.eval x) := sorry
title: cnfToDualDNF negates the value
statement_informal: cnfToDualDNF negates the value. $(\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi).\mathrm{eval}\,x = \lnot\,(\psi.\mathrm{eval}\,x)$.

definitions.title: Flip a literal's polarity
definitions.informal: Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.

definitions.title: Evaluation of a literal
definitions.informal:
For a literal $l$ with variable $l.\mathrm{var}$ and polarity $l.\mathrm{neg}$ and an
assignment $x$, the value is $x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a positive literal and
$\lnot x_{l.\mathrm{var}}$ for a negated one.

definitions.title: CNF to De Morgan dual DNF
definitions.informal:
Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Flip a literal's polarity. Negates a literal by flipping its polarity bit while keeping its variable.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- CNF to De Morgan dual DNF. Converts a CNF $\psi$ to its De Morgan dual DNF by negating every literal, so each
clause (a disjunction) becomes a term (a conjunction) of negated literals:
$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).
- Width of a CNF formula. The width of a CNF formula $c$, defined as the maximum width over its clauses (and $0$
for the empty formula).

Statement: cnfToDualDNF preserves width. $(\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi).\mathrm{width} = \psi.\mathrm{width}$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000
open Classical
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
open SwitchingLemma2

    variable index then they are identical. -/
structure Literal (n : ℕ) where
  var : Fin n
  neg : Bool
  deriving DecidableEq

def Literal.flipNeg {n : ℕ} (l : Literal n) : Literal n :=
  ⟨l.var, !l.neg⟩

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev Term (n : ℕ) := List (Literal n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev CNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
abbrev DNF (n : ℕ) := List (Term n)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def Term.width {n : ℕ} (t : Term n) : ℕ := t.length

def cnfToDualDNF {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n) : DNF n :=
  ψ.map (fun clause => clause.map Literal.flipNeg)

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def CNF.width {n : ℕ} (c : CNF n) : ℕ := (c.map Term.width).foldr max 0

    variable index then they are identical. -/
def DNF.width {n : ℕ} (d : DNF n) : ℕ := (d.map Term.width).foldr max 0

lemma cnfToDualDNF_width {n : ℕ} (ψ : CNF n) :
    (cnfToDualDNF ψ).width = ψ.width := sorry
title: cnfToDualDNF preserves width
statement_informal: cnfToDualDNF preserves width. $(\mathrm{cnfToDualDNF}\,\psi).\mathrm{width} = \psi.\mathrm{width}$.

$\lnot\bigl(\bigwedge_i \bigvee_j l_{ij}\bigr) = \bigvee_i \bigl(\bigwedge_j \lnot l_{ij}\bigr)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $n$-qubit Hilbert space. The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).
- Projection onto a code. The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism
$P_C : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.

Statement: Action of the code projection. For any $x \in \mathcal{H}_n$, the value $P_C\,x$ coincides with the orthogonal projection
$\Pi_C(x)$ of $x$ onto $C$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

abbrev Hn (n : ℕ) := EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2)

noncomputable def codeProj {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  (C.subtypeL.comp (Submodule.orthogonalProjection C)).toLinearMap

@[simp] lemma codeProj_apply {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) (x : Hn n) :
    codeProj C x = (Submodule.orthogonalProjection C x : Hn n) := sorry
title: Action of the code projection
statement_informal:
Action of the code projection. For any $x \in \mathcal{H}_n$, the value $P_C\,x$ coincides with the orthogonal projection
$\Pi_C(x)$ of $x$ onto $C$.

definitions.title: $n$-qubit Hilbert space
definitions.informal:
The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).

definitions.title: Projection onto a code
definitions.informal:
The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism
$P_C : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.

Definitions:
- $n$-qubit Hilbert space. The $n$-qubit Hilbert space

Statement: Projection fixes code vectors. If $x \in C$, then $P_C\,x = x$.

import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false

  (C.subtypeL.comp (Submodule.orthogonalProjection C)).toLinearMap

lemma codeProj_eq_self_of_mem {n : ℕ} {C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)} {x : Hn n} (hx : x ∈ C) :
    codeProj C x = x := sorry

Definitions:
- $n$-qubit Hilbert space. The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).
- Projection onto a code. The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism
$P_C : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.

Statement: Idempotence of the code projection. The code projection is idempotent: $P_C(P_C\,x) = P_C\,x$ for all $x \in \mathcal{H}_n$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

abbrev Hn (n : ℕ) := EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2)

noncomputable def codeProj {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  (C.subtypeL.comp (Submodule.orthogonalProjection C)).toLinearMap

lemma codeProj_idempotent {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) (x : Hn n) :
    codeProj C (codeProj C x) = codeProj C x := sorry
title: Idempotence of the code projection
statement_informal: Idempotence of the code projection. The code projection is idempotent: $P_C(P_C\,x) = P_C\,x$ for all $x \in \mathcal{H}_n$.

definitions.title: $n$-qubit Hilbert space
definitions.informal:
The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).

definitions.title: Projection onto a code

The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism
$P_C : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.

Definitions:
- $n$-qubit Hilbert space. The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).

import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

abbrev Hn (n : ℕ) := EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2)

noncomputable def codeProj {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  (C.subtypeL.comp (Submodule.orthogonalProjection C)).toLinearMap

lemma codeProj_mem {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) (x : Hn n) :
    codeProj C x ∈ C := sorry
title: Image of the code projection lies in $C$
statement_informal: Image of the code projection lies in $C$. For any $x \in \mathcal{H}_n$, the projected vector $P_C\,x$ belongs to the code subspace $C$.

definitions.title: $n$-qubit Hilbert space
definitions.informal:
The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).

definitions.title: Projection onto a code
definitions.informal:
The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Logical dimension. $k(S) = n - \dim(S)$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of
coordinates where either component of $v$ is nonzero;
the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(v) = |\mathrm{supp}(v)|$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of
coordinates where either component of $v$ is nonzero;
the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(v) = |\mathrm{supp}(v)|$.
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Isotropic submodule. $S$ is \emph{isotropic} if $S \le S^{\perp_\omega}$, i.e.\ $\omega(u,v) = 0$
for all $u,v \in S$.
- Quantum code distance. $d(S) = \min\{\mathrm{wt}(v) \mid v \in S^{\perp_\omega} \setminus S,\; \mathrm{wt}(v) \neq 0\}$
(or $0$ if no such $v$ exists).

Statement: Zero logical dimension forces zero distance. If $S$ is isotropic and $k(S) = 0$, then $d(S) = 0$.

abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def code_k (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : ℕ := n - Module.finrank (F p) S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def supp (v : V n p) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  {i | v.1 i ≠ 0 ∨ v.2 i ≠ 0}.toFinset

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def wt (v : V n p) : ℕ := (supp v).card

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev sym_orth (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).orthogonal S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def IsIsotropic (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Prop :=
  S ≤ sym_orth S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def code_dist (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : ℕ :=
  sInf {d | ∃ v ∈ sym_orth S, v ∉ S ∧ wt v = d}

lemma code_dist_eq_zero_of_code_k_eq_zero
    (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p))

    code_dist (n:=n) (p:=p) S = 0 := sorry

definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support and weight
Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$. For a submodule $S \le V$ and $M \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the submodule $S_M = S \cap V_M$.
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Intersection $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$. The submodule $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$.
- Erasure correctability. An erasure set $E \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ is \emph{correctable} for $S$ if
every $v \in V_E \cap S^{\perp_\omega}$ is already in $S$.
- Supportable logical operators count $g(M)$. $g(S,M) = \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S^{\perp}_M) - \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S_M)$.

Statement: Correctable sets have $g = 0$. If $M$ is correctable for $S$, then $g(S,M) = 0$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def S_M (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (M : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) M

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev sym_orth (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).orthogonal S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def S_perp_M (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (M : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  sym_orth S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) M

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def correctable (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (E : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
  sym_orth S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) E ≤ S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def g (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (M : Finset (Fin n)) : ℕ :=
  Module.finrank (F p) (S_perp_M S M) - Module.finrank (F p) (S_M S M)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma correctable_implies_g_zero (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (M : Finset (Fin n))
    (h : correctable S M) : g S M = 0 := sorry
title: Correctable sets have $g = 0$
statement_informal: Correctable sets have $g = 0$. If $M$ is correctable for $S$, then $g(S,M) = 0$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$
definitions.informal: For a submodule $S \le V$ and $M \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the submodule $S_M = S \cap V_M$.

definitions.title: Bundled bilinear form
definitions.informal: $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.

definitions.title: Symplectic orthogonal complement
definitions.informal:
For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.

definitions.title: Intersection $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$
definitions.informal: The submodule $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$.

definitions.title: Erasure correctability
definitions.informal:
An erasure set $E \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ is \emph{correctable} for $S$ if
every $v \in V_E \cap S^{\perp_\omega}$ is already in $S$.

definitions.title: Supportable logical operators count $g(M)$
definitions.informal: $g(S,M) = \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S^{\perp}_M) - \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S_M)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Complement of $E$. $E^c = \mathrm{Fin}\,n \setminus E$, the complement finset of $E$.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$. For a submodule $S \le V$ and $M \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the submodule $S_M = S \cap V_M$.

Statement: Sum of restricted dimensions is bounded. $\dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S_M) + \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S_{M^c}) \le \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S)$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Complement of $E$

definitions.title: Support submodule

For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}

definitions.title: Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

Statement: Dimension of support submodule. $\dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(V_C) = 2|C|$.

abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma dim_V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Module.finrank (F p) (V_sub (p:=p) C) = 2 * C.card := sorry
title: Dimension of support submodule
statement_informal: Dimension of support submodule. $\dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(V_C) = 2|C|$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Complement of $E$. $E^c = \mathrm{Fin}\,n \setminus E$, the complement finset of $E$.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$. For a submodule $S \le V$ and $M \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the submodule $S_M = S \cap V_M$.
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Isotropic submodule. $S$ is \emph{isotropic} if $S \le S^{\perp_\omega}$, i.e.\ $\omega(u,v) = 0$
for all $u,v \in S$.

Statement: Dimension inequality. For isotropic $S$, $\dim(S) + \dim(S_M) \le 2|M| + \dim(S_{M^c})$.

abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where

  zero_mem' := by
