lemma g_le_two_card_C (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (B C : Finset (Fin n))
    (h_disjoint : Disjoint B C) (hB : correctable S B) :
    g S (B ∪ C) ≤ 2 * C.card := sorry
title: Bound on $g$ for a correctable part plus a set
statement_informal:
Bound on $g$ for a correctable part plus a set. If $B$ and $C$ are disjoint and $B$ is correctable for $S$, then
$g(S, B \cup C) \le 2|C|$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Intersection $S_M = S \cap V_M$
definitions.informal: For a submodule $S \le V$ and $M \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the submodule $S_M = S \cap V_M$.

definitions.title: Restriction map $r_E$
definitions.informal:
The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.

definitions.title: Bundled bilinear form
definitions.informal: $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.

definitions.title: Symplectic orthogonal complement
definitions.informal:
For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.

definitions.title: Intersection $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$
definitions.informal: The submodule $S^{\perp}_M = S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$.

definitions.title: Erasure correctability
definitions.informal:
An erasure set $E \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ is \emph{correctable} for $S$ if
every $v \in V_E \cap S^{\perp_\omega}$ is already in $S$.

definitions.title: Supportable logical operators count $g(M)$
definitions.informal: $g(S,M) = \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S^{\perp}_M) - \dim_{\mathbb{F}_p}(S_M)$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Restriction map $r_E$. The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.

Statement: Kernel of $r_E$. $\ker(r_E) = V_{\mathrm{univ} \setminus E}$, the subspace supported on the complement of $E$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def r_E (E : Finset (Fin n)) : V n p →ₗ[F p] V_sub (p:=p) E where
  toFun v := ⟨(fun i => if i ∈ E then v.1 i else 0, fun i => if i ∈ E then v.2 i else 0), by
    exact fun i hi => by simp_all only [↓reduceIte, and_self];⟩
  map_add' := by
    intro x y
    simp_all only [Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, Prod.snd_add]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    obtain ⟨fst_1, snd_1⟩ := y
    simp_all only [AddMemClass.mk_add_mk, Prod.mk_add_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
  map_smul' := by
    intro m x
    simp_all only [Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Prod.smul_snd, RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    simp_all only [SetLike.mk_smul_mk, Prod.smul_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma ker_r_E (E : Finset (Fin n)) :
    LinearMap.ker (r_E (p:=p) E) = V_sub (p:=p) (Finset.univ \ E) := sorry
title: Kernel of $r_E$
statement_informal: Kernel of $r_E$. $\ker(r_E) = V_{\mathrm{univ} \setminus E}$, the subspace supported on the complement of $E$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Restriction map $r_E$

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Restriction map $r_E$. The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Restriction as an endomorphism of $V$. The composition $r_E^V : V \to V$ of $r_E$ with the inclusion $V_E \hookrightarrow V$.

Statement: Orthogonal intersection equals orthogonal of restricted image. $S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M = (r_M^V(S))^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def r_E (E : Finset (Fin n)) : V n p →ₗ[F p] V_sub (p:=p) E where
  toFun v := ⟨(fun i => if i ∈ E then v.1 i else 0, fun i => if i ∈ E then v.2 i else 0), by
    exact fun i hi => by simp_all only [↓reduceIte, and_self];⟩
  map_add' := by
    intro x y
    simp_all only [Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, Prod.snd_add]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    obtain ⟨fst_1, snd_1⟩ := y
    simp_all only [AddMemClass.mk_add_mk, Prod.mk_add_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
  map_smul' := by
    intro m x
    simp_all only [Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Prod.smul_snd, RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    simp_all only [SetLike.mk_smul_mk, Prod.smul_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev sym_orth (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).orthogonal S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def r_E_V (E : Finset (Fin n)) : V n p →ₗ[F p] V n p :=
  (V_sub (p:=p) E).subtype.comp (r_E E)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma orth_inter_eq_orth_map (M : Finset (Fin n)) (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) :
    sym_orth (n:=n) (p:=p) S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) M
      = sym_orth (n:=n) (p:=p) (S.map (r_E_V (n:=n) (p:=p) M)) ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) M := sorry
title: Orthogonal intersection equals orthogonal of restricted image
statement_informal: Orthogonal intersection equals orthogonal of restricted image. $S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M = (r_M^V(S))^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction map $r_E$
definitions.informal:
The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.

definitions.title: Bundled bilinear form

For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.

definitions.title: Restriction as an endomorphism of $V$
definitions.informal: The composition $r_E^V : V \to V$ of $r_E$ with the inclusion $V_E \hookrightarrow V$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Restriction map $r_E$. The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Restriction as an endomorphism of $V$. The composition $r_E^V : V \to V$ of $r_E$ with the inclusion $V_E \hookrightarrow V$.
- Restricted bundled form. The symplectic bilinear form $\mathrm{symB}$ restricted to the subspace $V_M$.
- Restricted symplectic form. An abbreviation for $\mathrm{symB\_sub}$, the symplectic form viewed as a bilinear form on $V_M$.

Statement: Orthogonal intersection as an image. $S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$ is the image under $V_M \hookrightarrow V$ of the orthogonal complement
of $r_M(S)$ with respect to the restricted form.

def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where

    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def r_E (E : Finset (Fin n)) : V n p →ₗ[F p] V_sub (p:=p) E where
  toFun v := ⟨(fun i => if i ∈ E then v.1 i else 0, fun i => if i ∈ E then v.2 i else 0), by
    exact fun i hi => by simp_all only [↓reduceIte, and_self];⟩
  map_add' := by
    intro x y
    simp_all only [Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, Prod.snd_add]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    obtain ⟨fst_1, snd_1⟩ := y
    simp_all only [AddMemClass.mk_add_mk, Prod.mk_add_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
  map_smul' := by
    intro m x
    simp_all only [Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Prod.smul_snd, RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    simp_all only [SetLike.mk_smul_mk, Prod.smul_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

abbrev sym_orth (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).orthogonal S

lemma orth_inter_eq_orth_sub_image (M : Finset (Fin n)) (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) :
    sym_orth S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) M = ((sym_form_sub (p:=p) M).orthogonal (S.map (r_E M))).map (V_sub (p:=p) M).subtype := sorry
title: Orthogonal intersection as an image
statement_informal:
Orthogonal intersection as an image. $S^{\perp_\omega} \cap V_M$ is the image under $V_M \hookrightarrow V$ of the orthogonal complement
of $r_M(S)$ with respect to the restricted form.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction map $r_E$
definitions.informal:
The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.

definitions.title: Bundled bilinear form
definitions.informal: $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.

definitions.title: Symplectic orthogonal complement
definitions.informal:
For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.

definitions.title: Restriction as an endomorphism of $V$
definitions.informal: The composition $r_E^V : V \to V$ of $r_E$ with the inclusion $V_E \hookrightarrow V$.

definitions.title: Restricted bundled form
definitions.informal: The symplectic bilinear form $\mathrm{symB}$ restricted to the subspace $V_M$.

definitions.title: Restricted symplectic form
definitions.informal: An abbreviation for $\mathrm{symB\_sub}$, the symplectic form viewed as a bilinear form on $V_M$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $n$-qubit Hilbert space. The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).
- Pauli string. A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.
- Pauli $I$ matrix. The $2\times 2$ identity matrix over $\mathbb{C}$, i.e.\ the Pauli operator $I$.
- Pauli $X$ matrix. The bit-flip Pauli matrix
$X = \begin{pmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.
- Pauli $Y$ matrix. The bit-phase-flip Pauli matrix
$Y = \begin{pmatrix} 0 & -i \\ i & 0 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.
- Pauli $Z$ matrix. The phase-flip Pauli matrix
$Z = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.
- Matrix of a Pauli basis element. Maps each Pauli basis element $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$ to its corresponding $2\times 2$ matrix
$\{\sigma_I,\sigma_X,\sigma_Y,\sigma_Z\}$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.
- Projection onto a code. The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism
$P_C : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.
- Matrix of a Pauli string. For a Pauli string $p$ on $n$ qubits, the $2^n\times 2^n$ matrix obtained as the
tensor product of the single-qubit Pauli matrices $\sigma_{p(i)}$ over all coordinates~$i$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.
- Pauli operator. For $p \in \mathrm{PauliString}\,n$, the associated Pauli operator
$\hat{p} : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.
- Adjoint of a Pauli operator. The adjoint (Hermitian conjugate) $\hat{p}^\dagger : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$ of the
Pauli operator associated with a Pauli string $p$.
- Knill--Laflamme condition. A subspace $C \le \mathcal{H}_n$ satisfies the \emph{Knill--Laflamme condition}
for $t$-error correction if for all Pauli strings $E,F$ with $\mathrm{wt}(E),\mathrm{wt}(F) \le t$
there exists $\lambda_{EF}\in\mathbb{C}$ such that
$P_C\,E^\dagger F\,P_C = \lambda_{EF}\,P_C$,
where $P_C$ is the orthogonal projection onto $C$.
- Non-degenerate code. A code is \emph{non-degenerate} if it satisfies the Knill--Laflamme condition
and additionally $P_C E^\dagger F P_C = 0$ whenever $E \neq F$.

Statement: Quantum Hamming bound. If $C \le \mathcal{H}_n$ is non-degenerate, then
\[
\left(\sum_{i=0}^{t}\binom{n}{i}\,3^i\right)\cdot\dim_{\mathbb{C}}(C)
\;\le\; 2^n.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

abbrev Hn (n : ℕ) := EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2)

def PauliString (n : ℕ) := Fin n → PauliBasis

def sigmaI : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![1, 0; 0, 1]

def sigmaX : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![0, 1; 1, 0]

def sigmaY : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![0, -I; I, 0]

def sigmaZ : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![1, 0; 0, -1]

def PauliBasis.toMatrix : PauliBasis → Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ
| PauliBasis.I => sigmaI
| PauliBasis.X => sigmaX
| PauliBasis.Y => sigmaY
| PauliBasis.Z => sigmaZ

def support {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => p i ≠ PauliBasis.I)

noncomputable def codeProj {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  (C.subtypeL.comp (Submodule.orthogonalProjection C)).toLinearMap

def pauliMatrix {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) :
    Matrix (Fin n → Fin 2) (Fin n → Fin 2) ℂ :=
  fun i j => ∏ k, (p k).toMatrix (i k) (j k)

def weight {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : ℕ :=
  (support p).card

noncomputable def pauliOp {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  Matrix.toLin' (pauliMatrix p)

noncomputable def pauliOpAdjoint {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  Matrix.toLin' (pauliMatrix p).conjTranspose

def KnillLaflamme (n : ℕ) (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) (t : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ E F : PauliString n, weight E ≤ t → weight F ≤ t →
    ∃ α : ℂ,
      (codeProj C).comp ((pauliOpAdjoint E).comp ((pauliOp F).comp (codeProj C))) =
      α • (codeProj C)

def IsNondegenerate (n : ℕ) (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) (t : ℕ) : Prop :=
  KnillLaflamme n C t ∧
  ∀ E F : PauliString n, weight E ≤ t → weight F ≤ t → E ≠ F →
    (codeProj C).comp ((pauliOpAdjoint E).comp ((pauliOp F).comp (codeProj C))) = 0

theorem quantum_hamming_bound
    (n k t : ℕ) (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n))
    (h_dim : Module.finrank ℂ C = 2^k)
    (h_nondeg : IsNondegenerate n C t)
    (hkn : k ≤ n) :
    ∑ j ∈ Finset.range (t + 1), n.choose j * 3 ^ j ≤ 2 ^ (n - k) := sorry
title: Quantum Hamming bound
statement_informal:
Quantum Hamming bound. If $C \le \mathcal{H}_n$ is non-degenerate, then
\[
\left(\sum_{i=0}^{t}\binom{n}{i}\,3^i\right)\cdot\dim_{\mathbb{C}}(C)
\;\le\; 2^n.
\]

The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- $n$-qubit Hilbert space. The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).
- Pauli string. A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.
- Pauli $I$ matrix. The $2\times 2$ identity matrix over $\mathbb{C}$, i.e.\ the Pauli operator $I$.
- Pauli $X$ matrix. The bit-flip Pauli matrix
$X = \begin{pmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.
- Pauli $Y$ matrix. The bit-phase-flip Pauli matrix
$Y = \begin{pmatrix} 0 & -i \\ i & 0 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.
- Pauli $Z$ matrix. The phase-flip Pauli matrix
$Z = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.
- Matrix of a Pauli basis element. Maps each Pauli basis element $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$ to its corresponding $2\times 2$ matrix
$\{\sigma_I,\sigma_X,\sigma_Y,\sigma_Z\}$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.
- Projection onto a code. The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism
$P_C : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.
- Matrix of a Pauli string. For a Pauli string $p$ on $n$ qubits, the $2^n\times 2^n$ matrix obtained as the
tensor product of the single-qubit Pauli matrices $\sigma_{p(i)}$ over all coordinates~$i$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.
- Pauli error set. The set of all Pauli strings of weight at most $t$:
\[
\mathcal{E}(n,t) = \bigl\{p : \mathrm{PauliString}\,n \;\big|\; \mathrm{wt}(p) \le t\bigr\}.
\]
- Pauli operator. For $p \in \mathrm{PauliString}\,n$, the associated Pauli operator
$\hat{p} : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.
- Adjoint of a Pauli operator. The adjoint (Hermitian conjugate) $\hat{p}^\dagger : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$ of the
Pauli operator associated with a Pauli string $p$.
- Knill--Laflamme condition. A subspace $C \le \mathcal{H}_n$ satisfies the \emph{Knill--Laflamme condition}
for $t$-error correction if for all Pauli strings $E,F$ with $\mathrm{wt}(E),\mathrm{wt}(F) \le t$
there exists $\lambda_{EF}\in\mathbb{C}$ such that
$P_C\,E^\dagger F\,P_C = \lambda_{EF}\,P_C$,
where $P_C$ is the orthogonal projection onto $C$.
- Error sphere. The \emph{error sphere} $\mathrm{ES}(C,t)$ is the subspace
$\bigvee_{\mathrm{wt}(p)\le t} \hat{p}(C)$, i.e.\ the supremum of the
Pauli images of $C$ over all $t$-errors.
- Non-degenerate code. A code is \emph{non-degenerate} if it satisfies the Knill--Laflamme condition
and additionally $P_C E^\dagger F P_C = 0$ whenever $E \neq F$.

Statement: Quantum Hamming bound, raw form. For a non-degenerate $[[n,k]]$ quantum code $C \le \mathcal{H}_n$ correcting $t$ errors, with
$\dim_{\mathbb{C}}(C) = 2^k$, the dimension of the error sphere does not exceed the ambient
dimension:
\[
\left(\sum_{j=0}^{t}\binom{n}{j}\,3^j\right)\cdot 2^k \;\le\; 2^n.
\]
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

abbrev Hn (n : ℕ) := EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2)

def PauliString (n : ℕ) := Fin n → PauliBasis

def sigmaI : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![1, 0; 0, 1]

def sigmaX : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![0, 1; 1, 0]

def sigmaY : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![0, -I; I, 0]

def sigmaZ : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ := !![1, 0; 0, -1]

def PauliBasis.toMatrix : PauliBasis → Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ
| PauliBasis.I => sigmaI
| PauliBasis.X => sigmaX
| PauliBasis.Y => sigmaY
| PauliBasis.Z => sigmaZ

def support {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => p i ≠ PauliBasis.I)

noncomputable def codeProj {n : ℕ} (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  (C.subtypeL.comp (Submodule.orthogonalProjection C)).toLinearMap

def pauliMatrix {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) :
    Matrix (Fin n → Fin 2) (Fin n → Fin 2) ℂ :=
  fun i j => ∏ k, (p k).toMatrix (i k) (j k)

def weight {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : ℕ :=
  (support p).card

noncomputable def pauliOp {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  Matrix.toLin' (pauliMatrix p)

noncomputable def pauliOpAdjoint {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Hn n →ₗ[ℂ] Hn n :=
  Matrix.toLin' (pauliMatrix p).conjTranspose

def KnillLaflamme (n : ℕ) (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) (t : ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ E F : PauliString n, weight E ≤ t → weight F ≤ t →
    ∃ α : ℂ,
      (codeProj C).comp ((pauliOpAdjoint E).comp ((pauliOp F).comp (codeProj C))) =
      α • (codeProj C)

def IsNondegenerate (n : ℕ) (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n)) (t : ℕ) : Prop :=
  KnillLaflamme n C t ∧
  ∀ E F : PauliString n, weight E ≤ t → weight F ≤ t → E ≠ F →
    (codeProj C).comp ((pauliOpAdjoint E).comp ((pauliOp F).comp (codeProj C))) = 0

theorem quantum_hamming_bound_raw
    (n k t : ℕ) (C : Submodule ℂ (Hn n))
    (h_dim : Module.finrank ℂ C = 2^k)
    (h_nondeg : IsNondegenerate n C t) :
    (∑ j ∈ Finset.range (t + 1), n.choose j * 3 ^ j) * 2 ^ k ≤ 2 ^ n := sorry
title: Quantum Hamming bound, raw form
statement_informal:
Quantum Hamming bound, raw form. For a non-degenerate $[[n,k]]$ quantum code $C \le \mathcal{H}_n$ correcting $t$ errors, with
$\dim_{\mathbb{C}}(C) = 2^k$, the dimension of the error sphere does not exceed the ambient
dimension:
\[
\left(\sum_{j=0}^{t}\binom{n}{j}\,3^j\right)\cdot 2^k \;\le\; 2^n.
\]

definitions.title: $n$-qubit Hilbert space
definitions.informal:
The $n$-qubit Hilbert space
$\mathcal{H}_n = \ell^2\!\bigl(\{0,1\}^n,\mathbb{C}\bigr)$,
implemented as EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n → Fin 2).

definitions.title: Pauli string
definitions.informal: A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.

definitions.title: Pauli $I$ matrix
definitions.informal: The $2\times 2$ identity matrix over $\mathbb{C}$, i.e.\ the Pauli operator $I$.

definitions.title: Pauli $X$ matrix
definitions.informal:
The bit-flip Pauli matrix
$X = \begin{pmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.

definitions.title: Pauli $Y$ matrix
definitions.informal:
The bit-phase-flip Pauli matrix
$Y = \begin{pmatrix} 0 & -i \\ i & 0 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.

definitions.title: Pauli $Z$ matrix
definitions.informal:
The phase-flip Pauli matrix
$Z = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{pmatrix}$
over $\mathbb{C}$.

definitions.title: Matrix of a Pauli basis element
definitions.informal:
Maps each Pauli basis element $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$ to its corresponding $2\times 2$ matrix
$\{\sigma_I,\sigma_X,\sigma_Y,\sigma_Z\}$.

definitions.title: Support and weight
definitions.informal:
The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

definitions.title: Projection onto a code
definitions.informal:
The orthogonal projection of $\mathcal{H}_n$ onto a subspace $C$, viewed as an endomorphism
$P_C : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.

definitions.title: Matrix of a Pauli string
definitions.informal:
For a Pauli string $p$ on $n$ qubits, the $2^n\times 2^n$ matrix obtained as the
tensor product of the single-qubit Pauli matrices $\sigma_{p(i)}$ over all coordinates~$i$.

definitions.title: Support and weight
definitions.informal:
The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

definitions.title: Pauli error set
definitions.informal:
The set of all Pauli strings of weight at most $t$:
\[
\mathcal{E}(n,t) = \bigl\{p : \mathrm{PauliString}\,n \;\big|\; \mathrm{wt}(p) \le t\bigr\}.
\]

definitions.title: Pauli operator
definitions.informal:
For $p \in \mathrm{PauliString}\,n$, the associated Pauli operator
$\hat{p} : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$.

definitions.title: Adjoint of a Pauli operator
definitions.informal:
The adjoint (Hermitian conjugate) $\hat{p}^\dagger : \mathcal{H}_n \to \mathcal{H}_n$ of the
Pauli operator associated with a Pauli string $p$.

definitions.title: Knill--Laflamme condition
definitions.informal:
A subspace $C \le \mathcal{H}_n$ satisfies the \emph{Knill--Laflamme condition}
for $t$-error correction if for all Pauli strings $E,F$ with $\mathrm{wt}(E),\mathrm{wt}(F) \le t$
there exists $\lambda_{EF}\in\mathbb{C}$ such that
$P_C\,E^\dagger F\,P_C = \lambda_{EF}\,P_C$,
where $P_C$ is the orthogonal projection onto $C$.

definitions.title: Error sphere
definitions.informal:
The \emph{error sphere} $\mathrm{ES}(C,t)$ is the subspace
$\bigvee_{\mathrm{wt}(p)\le t} \hat{p}(C)$, i.e.\ the supremum of the
Pauli images of $C$ over all $t$-errors.

definitions.title: Non-degenerate code
definitions.informal:
A code is \emph{non-degenerate} if it satisfies the Knill--Laflamme condition
and additionally $P_C E^\dagger F P_C = 0$ whenever $E \neq F$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Logical dimension. $k(S) = n - \dim(S)$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of
coordinates where either component of $v$ is nonzero;
the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(v) = |\mathrm{supp}(v)|$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of
coordinates where either component of $v$ is nonzero;
the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(v) = |\mathrm{supp}(v)|$.
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Isotropic submodule. $S$ is \emph{isotropic} if $S \le S^{\perp_\omega}$, i.e.\ $\omega(u,v) = 0$
for all $u,v \in S$.
- Quantum code distance. $d(S) = \min\{\mathrm{wt}(v) \mid v \in S^{\perp_\omega} \setminus S,\; \mathrm{wt}(v) \neq 0\}$
(or $0$ if no such $v$ exists).

Statement: Quantum Singleton bound. For any isotropic submodule $S \le V$,
\[
k(S) + 2(d(S) - 1) \;\le\; n.
\]

abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

def code_k (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : ℕ := n - Module.finrank (F p) S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def supp (v : V n p) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  {i | v.1 i ≠ 0 ∨ v.2 i ≠ 0}.toFinset

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def wt (v : V n p) : ℕ := (supp v).card

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev sym_orth (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).orthogonal S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def IsIsotropic (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Prop :=
  S ≤ sym_orth S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def code_dist (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : ℕ :=
  sInf {d | ∃ v ∈ sym_orth S, v ∉ S ∧ wt v = d}

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
theorem quantum_singleton_bound
    (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p))
    (hS : IsIsotropic (n:=n) (p:=p) S) :
    code_k (n:=n) (p:=p) S + 2 * (code_dist (n:=n) (p:=p) S - 1) ≤ n := sorry
title: Quantum Singleton bound
statement_informal:
Quantum Singleton bound. For any isotropic submodule $S \le V$,
\[
k(S) + 2(d(S) - 1) \;\le\; n.
\]

For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,

\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Extension from coordinates in $C$. The linear map $(C \to \mathbb{F}_p) \times (C \to \mathbb{F}_p) \to V_C$ that extends a pair of
functions on $C$ to a vector of $V$ by setting all coordinates outside $C$ to zero.
- Restriction to coordinates in $C$. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the linear map $V_C \to (C \to \mathbb{F}_p) \times (C \to \mathbb{F}_p)$
sending a vector supported on $C$ to its two component functions restricted to $C$.

Statement: $\mathrm{restrictToC}$ is a left inverse of $\mathrm{extendFromC}$. For every $x$, $\mathrm{restrictToC}_C(\mathrm{extendFromC}_C(x)) = x$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def extendFromC (C : Finset (Fin n)) :
    (C → F p) × (C → F p) →ₗ[F p] V_sub (p:=p) C where
  toFun := fun ⟨f, g⟩ =>
    ⟨ ((fun i => if h : i ∈ C then f ⟨i, h⟩ else 0),
       (fun i => if h : i ∈ C then g ⟨i, h⟩ else 0)),
      by
        intro j hj
        constructor <;> simp [hj] ⟩
  map_add' := by
    classical
    rintro ⟨f1, g1⟩ ⟨f2, g2⟩
    apply Subtype.ext
    ext i <;> by_cases hi : i ∈ C <;> simp [hi]
  map_smul' := by
    classical
    intro r x
    rcases x with ⟨f, g⟩
    apply Subtype.ext
    ext i <;> by_cases hi : i ∈ C <;> simp [hi]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def restrictToC (C : Finset (Fin n)) :
    V_sub (p:=p) C →ₗ[F p] (C → F p) × (C → F p) where
  toFun := fun ⟨v, _⟩ => (fun c => v.1 c.1, fun c => v.2 c.1)
  map_add' := by
    rintro ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩
    ext c <;> simp
  map_smul' := by
    intro r x
    rcases x with ⟨x, hx⟩
    ext c <;> simp

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma restrictToC_extendFromC (C : Finset (Fin n)) :
    ∀ x, restrictToC (p:=p) C (extendFromC (p:=p) C x) = x := sorry
title: $\mathrm{restrictToC}$ is a left inverse of $\mathrm{extendFromC}$
statement_informal: $\mathrm{restrictToC}$ is a left inverse of $\mathrm{extendFromC}$. For every $x$, $\mathrm{restrictToC}_C(\mathrm{extendFromC}_C(x)) = x$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Extension from coordinates in $C$
definitions.informal:
The linear map $(C \to \mathbb{F}_p) \times (C \to \mathbb{F}_p) \to V_C$ that extends a pair of
functions on $C$ to a vector of $V$ by setting all coordinates outside $C$ to zero.

definitions.title: Restriction to coordinates in $C$
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the linear map $V_C \to (C \to \mathbb{F}_p) \times (C \to \mathbb{F}_p)$
sending a vector supported on $C$ to its two component functions restricted to $C$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Embedding of a non-identity Pauli. Embeds a non-identity Pauli ($X$, $Y$, or $Z$) from the three-element type
$\mathrm{PauliNZ}$ into the full Pauli basis $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$.
- Pauli string. A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.
- Pauli string with prescribed support. Given a finset $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ and an assignment $f : S \to \mathrm{PauliNZ}$,
constructs the Pauli string that equals $f(i).\mathrm{toBasis}$ on each $i \in S$ and equals $I$
elsewhere.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

Statement: Support of \texttt{mkWithSupport}. The Pauli string $\mathrm{mkWithSupport}\,S\,f$ has support exactly $S$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped BigOperators
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open scoped Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
open Complex Matrix

def PauliNZ.toBasis : PauliNZ → PauliBasis
| .X => .X
| .Y => .Y
| .Z => .Z

def PauliString (n : ℕ) := Fin n → PauliBasis

def mkWithSupport {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) (f : S → PauliNZ) : PauliString n :=
  fun i => if h : i ∈ S then (f ⟨i, h⟩).toBasis else PauliBasis.I

def support {n : ℕ} (p : PauliString n) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  Finset.univ.filter (fun i => p i ≠ PauliBasis.I)

lemma support_mkWithSupport {n : ℕ} (S : Finset (Fin n)) (f : S → PauliNZ) :
    support (mkWithSupport S f) = S := sorry
title: Support of \texttt{mkWithSupport}
statement_informal: Support of \texttt{mkWithSupport}. The Pauli string $\mathrm{mkWithSupport}\,S\,f$ has support exactly $S$.

definitions.title: Embedding of a non-identity Pauli
definitions.informal:
Embeds a non-identity Pauli ($X$, $Y$, or $Z$) from the three-element type
$\mathrm{PauliNZ}$ into the full Pauli basis $\{I,X,Y,Z\}$.

definitions.title: Pauli string
definitions.informal: A \emph{Pauli string} of length $n$ is a function $p : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \mathrm{PauliBasis}$.

definitions.title: Pauli string with prescribed support
definitions.informal:
Given a finset $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ and an assignment $f : S \to \mathrm{PauliNZ}$,
constructs the Pauli string that equals $f(i).\mathrm{toBasis}$ on each $i \in S$ and equals $I$
elsewhere.

definitions.title: Support and weight
definitions.informal:
The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(p) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of coordinates
where $p(i) \neq I$; the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(p) = |\mathrm{supp}(p)|$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.

Statement: Evaluation of the bundled form. The bundled bilinear form $\mathrm{symB}$ agrees with $\omega$:
$\mathrm{symB}(x,y) = \omega(x,y)$ for all $x,y \in V$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
@[simp] lemma symB_apply (x y : V n p) :
    symB (n:=n) (p:=p) x y = sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y := sorry
title: Evaluation of the bundled form
statement_informal:
Evaluation of the bundled form. The bundled bilinear form $\mathrm{symB}$ agrees with $\omega$:
$\mathrm{symB}(x,y) = \omega(x,y)$ for all $x,y \in V$.

import Mathlib

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma sym_form_add_left (x y z : V n p) :
    sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) (x + y) z = sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x z + sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) y z := sorry

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Restriction map $r_E$. The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.
- Restriction as an endomorphism of $V$. The composition $r_E^V : V \to V$ of $r_E$ with the inclusion $V_E \hookrightarrow V$.

Statement: Restriction on the left argument, second form. If $v \in V_M$, then $\omega(r_M^V(s), v) = \omega(s, v)$ for all $s \in V$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real

set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def r_E (E : Finset (Fin n)) : V n p →ₗ[F p] V_sub (p:=p) E where
  toFun v := ⟨(fun i => if i ∈ E then v.1 i else 0, fun i => if i ∈ E then v.2 i else 0), by
    exact fun i hi => by simp_all only [↓reduceIte, and_self];⟩
  map_add' := by
    intro x y
    simp_all only [Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, Prod.snd_add]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    obtain ⟨fst_1, snd_1⟩ := y
    simp_all only [AddMemClass.mk_add_mk, Prod.mk_add_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
  map_smul' := by
    intro m x
    simp_all only [Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Prod.smul_snd, RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    simp_all only [SetLike.mk_smul_mk, Prod.smul_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
  lemma sym_form_left_restrict (M : Finset (Fin n)) (s v : V n p)
    (hv : v ∈ V_sub (p:=p) M) :
    sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) ((r_E_V (n:=n) (p:=p) M) s) v = sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) s v := sorry
title: Restriction on the left argument, second form
statement_informal: Restriction on the left argument, second form. If $v \in V_M$, then $\omega(r_M^V(s), v) = \omega(s, v)$ for all $s \in V$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction map $r_E$
definitions.informal:
The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.

definitions.title: Restriction as an endomorphism of $V$
definitions.informal: The composition $r_E^V : V \to V$ of $r_E$ with the inclusion $V_E \hookrightarrow V$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

Statement: Nondegeneracy. If $\omega(u,v) = 0$ for all $v$, then $u = 0$.

import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma sym_form_nondegenerate (u : V n p) (h : ∀ v, sym_form u v = 0) : u = 0 := sorry
title: Nondegeneracy
statement_informal: Nondegeneracy. If $\omega(u,v) = 0$ for all $v$, then $u = 0$.

  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def r_E (E : Finset (Fin n)) : V n p →ₗ[F p] V_sub (p:=p) E where
  toFun v := ⟨(fun i => if i ∈ E then v.1 i else 0, fun i => if i ∈ E then v.2 i else 0), by
    exact fun i hi => by simp_all only [↓reduceIte, and_self];⟩
  map_add' := by
    intro x y
    simp_all only [Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, Prod.snd_add]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    obtain ⟨fst_1, snd_1⟩ := y
    simp_all only [AddMemClass.mk_add_mk, Prod.mk_add_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.add_apply]
      split
      next h => simp_all only
      next h => simp_all only [add_zero]
  map_smul' := by
    intro m x
    simp_all only [Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Prod.smul_snd, RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨fst, snd⟩ := x
    simp_all only [SetLike.mk_smul_mk, Prod.smul_mk, Subtype.mk.injEq, Prod.mk.injEq]
    apply And.intro
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]
    · ext x : 1
      simp_all only [Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

def r_E_V (E : Finset (Fin n)) : V n p →ₗ[F p] V n p :=
  (V_sub (p:=p) E).subtype.comp (r_E E)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma sym_form_r_E_left (M : Finset (Fin n)) (s v : V n p)
    (hv : v ∈ V_sub (p:=p) M) :
    sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) ((r_E_V (n:=n) (p:=p) M) s) v
      = sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) s v := sorry
title: Restriction on the left argument
statement_informal: Restriction on the left argument. If $v \in V_M$, then $\omega(r_M^V(s), v) = \omega(s, v)$ for all $s \in V$.

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Restriction map $r_E$
definitions.informal:
The linear map $r_E : V \to V_E$ that zeroes out the coordinates of a vector outside $E$,
keeping those in $E$ unchanged.

definitions.title: Restriction as an endomorphism of $V$
definitions.informal: The composition $r_E^V : V \to V$ of $r_E$ with the inclusion $V_E \hookrightarrow V$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

Statement: Bilinearity. $\omega$ is bilinear: additive and scalar-homogeneous in each argument.

import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma sym_form_smul_left (c : F p) (x y : V n p) :
    sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) (c • x) y = c * sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y := sorry
title: Bilinearity
statement_informal: Bilinearity. $\omega$ is bilinear: additive and scalar-homogeneous in each argument.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Restricted bundled form. The symplectic bilinear form $\mathrm{symB}$ restricted to the subspace $V_M$.

Statement: Evaluation of the restricted form. For $x,y \in V_M$, $\mathrm{sym\_form\_sub}_M(x,y) = \omega(x,y)$ computed on the underlying vectors.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB_sub (M : Finset (Fin n)) : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V_sub (p:=p) M) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).comp (V_sub (p:=p) M).subtype (V_sub (p:=p) M).subtype

abbrev sym_form_sub (M : Finset (Fin n)) :

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
@[simp] lemma sym_form_sub_apply (M : Finset (Fin n))
    (x y : ↥(V_sub (p:=p) M)) :
    sym_form_sub (n:=n) (p:=p) M x y
      = sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) (x : V n p) (y : V n p) := sorry

Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}

  zero_mem' := by

  smul_mem' := by

      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Restricted bundled form. The symplectic bilinear form $\mathrm{symB}$ restricted to the subspace $V_M$.
- Restricted symplectic form. An abbreviation for $\mathrm{symB\_sub}$, the symplectic form viewed as a bilinear form on $V_M$.

formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def V_sub (C : Finset (Fin n)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ i ∉ C, v.1 i = 0 ∧ v.2 i = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b a_1 a_2
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_add, Pi.add_apply, not_false_eq_true, add_zero, Prod.snd_add, and_self,
      implies_true]
  zero_mem' := by
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.fst_zero, Pi.zero_apply, Prod.snd_zero, and_self, implies_true]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c x a
    simp_all only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Prod.smul_fst, Pi.smul_apply, not_false_eq_true, smul_eq_mul, mul_zero,
      Prod.smul_snd, and_self, implies_true]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def sym_form (u v : V n p) : F p :=
  Finset.univ.sum (fun i : Fin n => (u.1 i * v.2 i - u.2 i * v.1 i))

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
noncomputable def symB : LinearMap.BilinForm (F p) (V n p) :=
  LinearMap.mk₂ (F p) (fun x y => sym_form (n:=n) (p:=p) x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_left (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_left (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)
    (by intro x y z; simpa using sym_form_add_right (n:=n) (p:=p) x y z)
    (by intro c x y; simpa using sym_form_smul_right (n:=n) (p:=p) c x y)

Definitions:

import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support submodule. For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.
- Logical dimension. $k(S) = n - \dim(S)$.
- Symplectic form. For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]
- Bundled bilinear form. $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.
- Symplectic orthogonal complement. For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.
- Isotropic submodule. $S$ is \emph{isotropic} if $S \le S^{\perp_\omega}$, i.e.\ $\omega(u,v) = 0$
for all $u,v \in S$.
- Erasure correctability. An erasure set $E \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ is \emph{correctable} for $S$ if
every $v \in V_E \cap S^{\perp_\omega}$ is already in $S$.

Statement: Two disjoint correctable sets bound $k$. Let $S$ be isotropic.  If $A,B \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ are disjoint and both correctable, then
\[
k(S) \;\le\; n - |A| - |B|.
\]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev sym_orth (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Submodule (F p) (V n p) :=
  (symB (n:=n) (p:=p)).orthogonal S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def IsIsotropic (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) : Prop :=
  S ≤ sym_orth S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def correctable (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (E : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
  sym_orth S ⊓ V_sub (p:=p) E ≤ S

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
lemma two_disjoint_correctable_sets_bound_logical_dimension (S : Submodule (F p) (V n p)) (hS : IsIsotropic S)
    (A B : Finset (Fin n)) (h_disjoint : Disjoint A B)
    (hA : correctable S A) (hB : correctable S B) :
    code_k S ≤ (Finset.univ \ (A ∪ B)).card := sorry
title: Two disjoint correctable sets bound $k$
statement_informal:
Two disjoint correctable sets bound $k$. Let $S$ be isotropic.  If $A,B \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ are disjoint and both correctable, then
\[
k(S) \;\le\; n - |A| - |B|.
\]

definitions.title: Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$
definitions.informal: The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.

definitions.title: Symplectic vector space
definitions.informal: The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.

definitions.title: Support submodule
definitions.informal:
For $C \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the \emph{support submodule}
$V_C = \{v \in V \mid \mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq C\}$.

definitions.title: Logical dimension
definitions.informal: $k(S) = n - \dim(S)$.

definitions.title: Symplectic form
definitions.informal:
For $u = (x,z), v = (x',z') \in V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$,
\[
\omega(u,v) \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_i z'_i - z_i x'_i).
\]

definitions.title: Bundled bilinear form
definitions.informal: $\omega$ packaged as a LinearMap.BilinForm.

definitions.title: Symplectic orthogonal complement
definitions.informal:
For a submodule $S \le V$,
$S^{\perp_\omega} = \{v \in V \mid \forall s \in S,\; \omega(v,s) = 0\}$.

definitions.title: Isotropic submodule
definitions.informal:
$S$ is \emph{isotropic} if $S \le S^{\perp_\omega}$, i.e.\ $\omega(u,v) = 0$
for all $u,v \in S$.

definitions.title: Erasure correctability
definitions.informal:
An erasure set $E \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ is \emph{correctable} for $S$ if
every $v \in V_E \cap S^{\perp_\omega}$ is already in $S$.

informal_statement:
Definitions:
- Prime field $\mathbb{F}_p$. The prime field $\mathrm{GF}(p)$, realized as $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$ via ZMod.
- Symplectic vector space. The ambient space $V = \mathbb{F}_p^n \times \mathbb{F}_p^n$ of pairs of coordinate vectors.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of
coordinates where either component of $v$ is nonzero;
the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(v) = |\mathrm{supp}(v)|$.
- Support and weight. The \emph{support} $\mathrm{supp}(v) \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$ consists of
coordinates where either component of $v$ is nonzero;
the \emph{weight} is $\mathrm{wt}(v) = |\mathrm{supp}(v)|$.

Statement: Weight bound. $\mathrm{wt}(v) \le n$ for all $v \in V$.
formal_statement:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators
set_option linter.mathlibStandardSet false
open scoped Real
open scoped Nat
open Classical
open scoped Pointwise
set_option maxHeartbeats 0
set_option maxRecDepth 4000
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 20000
set_option synthInstance.maxSize 128
set_option relaxedAutoImplicit false
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.unnecessarySimpa false

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev F (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := ZMod p

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
abbrev V (n p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] := (Fin n → F p) × (Fin n → F p)

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def supp (v : V n p) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  {i | v.1 i ≠ 0 ∨ v.2 i ≠ 0}.toFinset

variable {n : ℕ} {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime]
def wt (v : V n p) : ℕ := (supp v).card

lemma wt_le_n (v : V n p) : wt (n:=n) (p:=p) v ≤ n := sorry
title: Weight bound
statement_informal: Weight bound. $\mathrm{wt}(v) \le n$ for all $v \in V$.
